Winter_Is_Coming.action: Ser Waymar Royce, Will, and Gared depart from the Wall to investigate reports of wildlings in the Haunted Forest
Will discovers a number of wildling corpses dismembered and arranged in a strange, ritualistic fashion on the ground
Will finds a dead wildling girl pinned to a tree
Will reports back to the others
Ser Waymar overrides Gared's advice to return to Castle Black and report to Lord Commander Jeor Mormont
Ser Waymar insists on seeing the corpses for himself to determine what killed them
The group reaches the site and finds the bodies have disappeared
Will scouts around for clues
Gared finds a bloody piece of clothing buried under the snow
A White Walker arrives and kills Ser Waymar with ease
Gared attempts to escape but is caught and killed
Will sees the dead wildling girl from earlier now walking with blue eyes
Will witnesses Gared's death, with the White Walker tossing Gared's severed head at Will's feet
Viserys and Daenerys live in the hospitality of Magister Illyrio Mopatis in Pentos
Viserys arranges to wed Daenerys to Khal Drogo
Viserys plans to receive an army from Drogo in exchange for Daenerys
Viserys prepares Daenerys for meeting Khal Drogo
Viserys tells Daenerys she has turned into a beautiful young woman
Viserys slips off Daenerys’s dress and fondles her breast
Daenerys is silent and looks away, pretending it isn’t happening
Daenerys steps into a hot bath that has just been drawn
Daenerys’s maid is greatly distressed, believing the water will scald her
Daenerys seems not to notice the heat and appears unharmed
Drogo arrives at Illyrio's estate
Drogo glances at Daenerys
Drogo rides away without dismounting from his horse
Viserys is concerned about Drogo's reaction
Illyrio assures Viserys that they would have known if Drogo did not approve of Daenerys
Illyrio and Viserys discuss the quest to sail back to Westeros
Viserys inquires when the wedding will take place
Daenerys expresses displeasure with the arrangement and says she does not want to marry Drogo
Viserys insists that Daenerys will marry Drogo to gain an army
Viserys says he would let his sister be raped by all forty thousand of Drogo's men and their horses if it meant getting his throne back
Daenerys marries Khal Drogo
a great celebration is held
several people die in impromptu duels at the wedding
Illyrio says a Dothraki wedding without at least three deaths is considered a dull affair
the bride and groom receive many gifts
Ser Jorah Mormont gives Daenerys a selection of books containing stories and songs from the Seven Kingdoms
Ser Jorah swears fealty to Viserys as King and offers his support and advice
Viserys accepts Ser Jorah’s support and advice
Illyrio gives Daenerys a box containing three beautiful, scaly stones (dragon eggs)
Daenerys feels some connection with the dragon eggs but brushes it off
Drogo gives Daenerys a beautiful white mare
Viserys tells Daenerys to make Drogo happy
Drogo and Daenerys ride down to the shore
Drogo and Daenerys consummate their marriage on the beach at sunset
Daenerys is visibly upset and terrified
The bells ring out in King's Landing to commemorate the death of Lord Jon Arryn
The Silent Sisters prepare Jon Arryn's body for burial
Cersei looks on as Jon Arryn's body is prepared
Cersei worries that Jon Arryn discovered something he shouldn't have and may have told someone
Cersei confesses her fears to Jaime
Jaime tells Cersei not to worry, saying if the King knew the truth they'd be dead already
Cersei tells Jaime he should be the new Hand of the King
Jaime refuses the job, considering it too much work
Cersei accuses Jaime of taking nothing seriously
Will flees south of the Wall in terror, rather than return to warn Castle Black of the White Walker threat
Will is apprehended by outriders loyal to House Stark, the Wardens of the North
Will is taken to Lord Eddard Stark
Ned is at Winterfell with his wife and family
Ned and Catelyn watch Bran practice archery
Catelyn watches Bran practice archery
Bran practices archery
Robb helps Bran with archery
Jon helps Bran with archery
Robb and Jon laugh when Bran's arrow goes high over the wall
Jon laughs when Bran's arrow goes high over the wall
Ned chides Robb and Jon, then encourages Bran to keep at it
Rickon watches his brothers
Arya hits Bran's bullseye with an arrow before Bran can
Arya leaves needlework where Sansa is
Sansa is at needlework
Bran chases Arya
Ned, Catelyn, Robb, and Jon laugh
Catelyn laughs
Robb laughs
Jon laughs
Ned receives news that a Night's Watch deserter has been found
The law decrees that Will, as a deserter from the Watch, must be executed
Eddard takes Robb, Jon, and Bran to witness the execution as per Stark custom
Theon Greyjoy, Rodrik Cassel, and Jory Cassel join Eddard
Will accepts his fate, tells Eddard about the Walkers, and asks that word be sent to his mother
Eddard carries out the execution using the ancestral Valyrian blade, Ice
Bran witnesses an execution for the first time without flinching
Jon praises Bran for not flinching
Eddard explains to Bran that the man who passes the sentence should swing the sword
Bran asks Eddard about Will’s talk of White Walkers
Eddard dismisses Will’s talk as a madman’s ramblings, insisting the Walkers are gone
The party sets out for Winterfell
The party finds a stag dead in the road, partially disemboweled
Theon wonders if the stag was killed by a mountain lion
Eddard states that no mountain lions live in the area
The party looks for what killed the stag
The party finds a rare dead female direwolf, killed with a stag antler piece broken off in her throat
The party observes that the female direwolf died after giving birth, and her five pups huddle by the corpse for warmth
Eddard advocates killing the pups out of mercy
Jon convinces Eddard that the direwolf is an omen and should be spared
Bran looks on beseechingly
Eddard agrees to allow the pups to live, but only if the children care for and raise them themselves
The party prepares to leave
Jon finds a sixth pup, an albino runt of the litter
Theon mockingly points out that the sixth pup belongs to Jon
Jon adopts the sixth pup
A raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death to Winterfell
Catelyn informs Eddard of Jon Arryn's death
Eddard is stricken by the news, recalling Arryn as a father-figure from his time at the Eyrie
Catelyn tells Eddard that King Robert Baratheon and much of the royal court are riding for Winterfell
Eddard realizes King Robert intends to name him Hand of the King to replace Jon Arryn
Eddard ponders refusing the position, feeling his place is at Winterfell
Robb names his direwolf Grey Wind
Sansa names her direwolf Lady
Arya names her direwolf Nymeria after a great warrior-queen
Jon names his direwolf Ghost for his white color and uncanny silence
Bran names his direwolf Summer
Rickon names his direwolf Shaggydog
Catelyn catches Bran climbing on the rooftops of the castle as the royal party comes into view
Bran promises to stop climbing
Catelyn knows Bran is lying
Winterfell prepares for guests
Catelyn wants to make sure Tyrion has enough candles
The young men of the house get groomed and gossip about the queen’s beauty
All of Winterfell gathers in the courtyard as the royal party approaches
Arya is late to the gathering
Arya annoys Sansa by not being silent and respectful when the royal party arrives
Sansa is annoyed by Arya's behavior
King Robert rides in with his knights
Queen Cersei arrives in a large wheelhouse
Eddard presents his household to King Robert
Eddard and Robert greet each other warmly
Robert demands to see the crypts and pay respects to Eddard's deceased sister, Lyanna
Cersei is impatient with Robert's request
After Robert and Eddard depart, Cersei sends Jaime to find Tyrion
Jaime goes to find Tyrion
Tyrion enjoys the attentions of Ros at the brothel outside Winterfell
Jaime tells Tyrion he will be needed at the feast that night
Jaime hires three more prostitutes for Tyrion
Robert offers up his prayers at the tomb of Lyanna Stark
Robert tells Eddard that in his dreams he kills Rhaegar every night anew
Eddard reminds Robert that House Targaryen was destroyed
Robert replies that not all of the Targaryens were destroyed
Robert formally offers Eddard the role of Hand
Eddard asks for time to consider
Robert agrees and offers Joffrey’s hand in marriage to Sansa as a sweetener
A great feast is held
Cersei and Catelyn exchange cordial words
Cersei seems impressed by Sansa, thinking she will be a great hit at court
Jon works at his sword practice, angry that Catelyn thought it inappropriate for a bastard to attend the feast
Benjen arrives to join the feast
Jon asks Benjen to take him to the Wall
Benjen agrees to consider Jon’s request
Tyrion arrives and talks to Jon
Tyrion suggests Jon should wear the insult of his illegitimacy like armor
Jon angrily asks Tyrion what he knows about it
Tyrion replies that all dwarfs are bastards in their father’s eyes
Eddard and Catelyn receive a letter in the night, delivered to Maester Luwin by a messenger from the Eyrie
The letter is revealed to be from Catelyn's sister, Lady Lysa Arryn, the widow of Jon Arryn
Lysa claims in the letter that Jon Arryn was murdered by the Lannisters and that they plan to conspire against King Robert
Luwin counsels Eddard to accept Robert's offer to be Hand, so he can investigate and protect the King
Eddard, though he would prefer to stay at Winterfell, agrees with Luwin and accepts the offer
Eddard and Robert go hunting, leaving the castle largely empty
Bran indulges in climbing
Bran climbs a broken tower
Bran hears someone moaning
Bran peers through a window and finds Cersei and Jaime engaged in a sexual act
Cersei spots Bran and warns Jaime
Jaime grabs Bran
Jaime looks out the window to make sure nobody else is watching and only sees Summer
Jaime turns to Bran and asks how old he is
Bran replies "ten"
Jaime remarks, "The things I do for love."
Jaime pushes Bran out of the window

The_Kingsroad.action: The Dothraki khalasar led by Khal Drogo depart Pentos and head east into the Dothraki Sea
Viserys Targaryen accompanies the khalasar, intending to stay with Drogo until he receives an army to invade Westeros
Ser Jorah Mormont suggests Viserys should not rely on Drogo
Ser Jorah tells Viserys he offended Eddard Stark by selling poachers into slavery
Ser Jorah explains he fled to Essos after learning Eddard meant to execute him
Viserys laughs at the notion, dismissing the idea that such trivialities will be punished under his reign
Daenerys struggles to adapt to her new role as khaleesi, feeling fearful of her husband and injured from their first sexual encounter
Ser Jorah Mormont offers Daenerys solace, telling her it will become easier
Drogo spends time drinking with his warriors
Drogo goes into his tent to visit Daenerys
Drogo violently takes Daenerys from behind
Daenerys is visibly uncomfortable
Daenerys finds solace in her dragon eggs
Daenerys asks her handmaidens if they have ever heard of dragons surviving in the east
Handmaidens reply no
Doreah tells Daenerys an old story about two moons and dragons
Irri and Jhiqui dismiss the story, saying the moon is a goddess, wife to the sun
Daenerys seeks the advice of her handmaid Doreah
Doreah instructs Daenerys on how to please Drogo in bed and how to use her skills to gain respect from him
Doreah tells Daenerys that she is no slave and should not have sex like one
Daenerys nervously puts Doreah’s plans into action
Daenerys is surprised to find Drogo responsive, especially when she uses her body and the Dothraki language to speak to him
Bran lies unconscious and badly wounded at Winterfell, with his mother by his side
Catelyn stays by Bran's side
Tyrion insists Joffrey pay his respects to Lord and Lady Stark, since his absence has been noted
Joffrey refuses, saying Bran means nothing to him
Tyrion slaps Joffrey three times until he accepts the suggestion
Tyrion breaks his fast with the rest of his family
Tyrion informs his family of his decision to journey north to see the Wall before returning to King's Landing
Tyrion tells his family that Bran is expected to live
Tyrion notes Cersei and Jaime's guarded reactions
Cersei visits Catelyn Stark, who is sitting by her son's side
Catelyn sits by her son's side
Cersei tells Catelyn that her first child, a beautiful black-haired boy, died of a fever
Cersei says she and Robert were grief-stricken and her prayers and tears were for naught
Cersei offers to pray for Bran's survival, hoping the gods will listen this time
Jon has Winterfell's blacksmith, Mikken, forge a light sword for Arya
Arya is excited by the gift and names it Needle
Jon gives Arya advice on how to use the weapon: "Stick them with the pointy end."
Jon urges Arya to practice every chance she gets
Jon and Arya hug
Jon visits comatose Bran to say his farewells and kisses his forehead
Jon departs at his stepmother's cold request
Eddard says his goodbyes to Jon and to a grieved Catelyn
Catelyn is angered at Eddard's departure while Bran remains ill
Robb and Jon hug each other before they depart, hoping to see each other soon
Jon, Tyrion, and Benjen Stark depart Winterfell and head north to the Wall
Eddard, Arya, Sansa, and the royal party depart Winterfell and head south to King's Landing
Eddard tells Jon, "You are a Stark. You might not have my name, but you have my blood."
Eddard promises Jon to tell him about his mother the next time they see each other
King Robert calls a halt on the Kingsroad south to the capital
Robert discusses dire news with Eddard
Robert's spies learn of Daenerys Targaryen's marriage to Khal Drogo, whose khalasar numbers over one hundred thousand warriors
Eddard points out that the Dothraki cannot cross the Narrow Sea, as they have no ships
Robert expresses concern that the Seven Kingdoms will soon face another war
Robert asks Eddard about Wylla, the mother of his bastard son
Eddard refuses to enlighten Robert about Wylla
Robert reminds Eddard that at that time, they were in the middle of a war and no one knew if they would ever return to their families
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's side, causing problems for the smooth running of the castle
Robb offers to work with Maester Luwin to put the castle to rights
a fire breaks out in the castle, serving as a distraction
an assassin attempts to finish off Bran
Catelyn holds the assassin off long enough for Summer to enter the chamber
Summer (Bran's direwolf) enters the chamber and tears out the assassin's throat
Summer settles down comfortably in the bed next to Bran, assuring Catelyn of his continued protection
Jon and Tyrion talk while traveling north
Tyrion explains that he reads books to sharpen his mind, his greatest asset and weapon
The party is joined by several new recruits: rapers who chose to "take the black" rather than be gelded
Tyrion tells Jon that the Night's Watch is not the noble institution it once was
Tyrion says Jon is smarter than to believe stories about "grumkins and snarks" and other monsters
The party reaches the Wall
Benjen welcomes the party
Tyrion seems impressed by the Wall's size
Catelyn goes to the tower that Bran fell from and finds a strand of blonde hair on the floor
Catelyn summons Robb, Theon, Luwin, and Rodrik Cassel to discuss her suspicions
Catelyn tells them she suspects the Lannisters tried to kill Bran because he might have seen something in the tower
Robb is eager for conflict
Theon is eager for conflict
Luwin schools Robb and Theon to caution
Catelyn decides that Ned must know the truth and resolves to travel south to King's Landing
Ser Rodrik escorts Catelyn to King's Landing
Robb remains in Winterfell to rule the castle in Catelyn's absence
The royal party reaches the Inn at the Crossroads on the Kingsroad
Sandor Clegane introduces Sansa to Ser Ilyn Payne, the king's headsman
Joffrey saves Sansa from the unpleasant introduction
Joffrey takes Sansa for a walk along the riverbank
Joffrey and Sansa find Arya practicing sword work with Mycah
Joffrey pretends to take offense at Mycah using a sword on Sansa
Joffrey starts to cut Mycah's face with his sword Lion's Tooth
Arya hits Joffrey on the head with a stick
Joffrey becomes violent and threatens Arya at sword point
Nymeria, Arya's direwolf, attacks Joffrey, biting his arm
Mycah runs off
Arya throws Lion's Tooth in the river
Arya runs off
Sansa tries to help Joffrey
Joffrey spitefully tells Sansa to leave him alone
Arya forces Nymeria to flee by throwing a rock at her
Arya is taken before the King
The King is angered that a minor quarrel has become a major incident
Cersei and Eddard blame each other's children
Joffrey offers a false account of the incident
Cersei asks Sansa to confirm Joffrey's account
Sansa refuses to contradict Joffrey, putting her in an impossible position
Arya is enraged by Sansa's refusal
The King decides to let Eddard discipline Arya and will discipline Joffrey himself
Cersei demands that the direwolf be put down
When Nymeria cannot be found, Cersei requests that Sansa's direwolf Lady be killed instead
Robert acquiesces to Cersei's request
Sansa is upset by Lady's death
Arya is further angered by the outcome
Eddard attends to the matter himself, furious
The Hound arrives with Mycah's bloodied corpse
Eddard is shocked and disgusted
Eddard asks the Hound if he rode Mycah down
The Hound responds that Mycah ran, but not very fast
Eddard kills Lady with a dagger
Bran awakens hundreds of miles away

Lord_Snow.action: The royal party reaches King's Landing
Eddard is asked to attend a meeting of the king's Small Council and to change his clothes
Eddard refuses to change his clothes
Eddard encounters Jaime in the throne room
Jaime expresses unhappiness that Eddard judged him dishonorable for killing the Mad King and sitting on the Iron Throne
Jaime is unrepentant about his actions
Eddard meets the council members: Littlefinger, Grand Maester Pycelle, Renly, and Varys
Varys tells Eddard that the council members heard about his troubles on the Kingsroad and that they pray for Joffrey's recovery
Eddard grimly responds "It's a shame no one prayed for the butcher's boy"
Varys is visibly upset by Eddard's response
Eddard is stunned to learn that the crown is six million gold dragons in debt, half owed to Lord Tywin Lannister
King Robert orders a lavish tournament to celebrate Eddard's appointment
Eddard resolves to advise Robert to cancel the tournament
The council is doubtful that the king will listen
Cersei talks to Joffrey about the incident on the river
Cersei tells Joffrey that when he is king, the truth will be whatever he decides
Joffrey thinks there will be trouble with the Starks
Joffrey promises to double the Starks' taxes, force them to contribute to a standing army, and, if they object, crush them and install someone loyal as Warden of the North
Cersei points out that the North is too vast and wild to be conquered by outsiders
Cersei advises Joffrey to be kind to Sansa Stark to avoid trouble later
Cersei concludes that "Anyone who isn't us is an enemy."
Arya and Sansa remain at odds over the incident with Joffrey and her direwolf
Eddard explains to Arya that Sansa was put in a difficult position by the king and queen
Eddard says Sansa shouldn't have lied, but that winning Joffrey's approval was important
Arya is unhappy with the situation
Eddard mollifies Arya by allowing her to practice with her sword, Needle
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik arrive at King's Landing and attempt to sneak into the city
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik are spotted by one of Littlefinger's men
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik are taken to meet Littlefinger at a brothel he owns
Lord Varys waits at the brothel
Varys explains that one of his 'little birds' told him Catelyn and Ser Rodrik were coming
Catelyn is angered by the way they've been treated
Catelyn asks Littlefinger for aid in finding the people who tried to have Bran killed
Littlefinger answers that the elaborate dagger used by the assassin used to be his, but he lost it in a gamble with Tyrion Lannister
Littlefinger fetches Eddard to meet his wife
Eddard suspects a trick
Eddard pushes Littlefinger up against a wall outside the brothel, wanting to know what his game is
Catelyn appears
Eddard is pacified
Catelyn tells Eddard her news
Eddard vows to find the truth and expose the Lannisters to Robert
Littlefinger agrees to be Eddard's ally, for the affection he once bore Catelyn as a youngster
Ravens arrive at King's Landing with news of Bran's recovery
Cersei fears Bran will expose their secret
Jaime comforts Cersei
Jaime expresses confidence they can outwit a child if necessary
Robert swaps old war stories with Ser Barristan Selmy
Robert summons Jaime Lannister
Robert jokingly questions Jaime about Aerys Targaryen's death
Jaime coldly replies that the master he betrayed died screaming "Burn them all"
Eddard contracts Syrio Forel, the former First Sword of Braavos, to teach Arya how to use her sword properly
Arya is thrilled to begin training
Syrio begins teaching Arya, and 'the dance' begins
Eddard watches Arya and Syrio practice
Eddard's pleasure at seeing Arya happy sours as he realizes a conflict between the Starks and Lannisters may be inevitable
Eddard realizes Arya may be training herself for war
Bran awakens but has no memory of his accident
Bran is left bedridden and unlikely to walk again due to his injuries
Old Nan tells Bran stories to pass the time
Bran expresses that he doesn't want Old Nan's usual heroic stories
Old Nan tells Bran about the White Walkers and the coming of a vast winter
Robb visits Bran
Bran asks Robb if it is true that he will never walk again
Robb reluctantly confirms that Bran will never walk again
Bran remarks that he would rather be dead
Jon shows his worth to Ser Alliser Thorne by besting several fellow members of the Watch in the practice yard
Ser Alliser Thorne is disgusted at the poor performance of the others
Ser Alliser Thorne has no praise for Jon, dubbing him Lord Snow and calling him "the least useless person here"
Tyrion watches over the new recruits with Lord Commander Jeor Mormont
Tyrion notes Thorne's stern and unusual ways
Jeor Mormont expresses that he only requires Thorne to turn the "thieves and runaways" into men of the Night's Watch
Several other recruits gang up on Jon
Tyrion defuses the situation
Tyrion points out to Jon that none of the other recruits had the benefit of a skilled master-at-arms to train them
Tyrion tells Jon he is alienating the others through his standoffish attitude and superior combat skills
Benjen meets Jon atop the Wall and tells him he is going on a ranging in the Haunted Forest
Benjen refuses to take Jon with him, telling him he isn't ready and that rank and nobility mean nothing
Benjen tells Jon that every man must earn everything he gets
Benjen stops by the eating area to say farewell to Yoren
Benjen scolds Tyrion for his naivety regarding what is north of the Wall
Benjen states that Tyrion has no authority to say what is or isn't beyond the Wall as he hasn't been out there himself
Tyrion discusses north of the Wall with Jeor Mormont and Maester Aemon
Jeor asks Tyrion to intercede at court and get the Watch more men
Aemon warns that winter is coming and it will be harsher and more dangerous than any in living memory
Aemon mentions that one of their number swore the White Walkers had returned, until Ned Stark executed him
Tyrion admits his skepticism about supernatural creatures
Tyrion agrees to bring word to his queen
Tyrion climbs to the top of the Wall
Tyrion urinates off the edge of the Wall
Tyrion and Jon share a moment of fellowship
Tyrion and Jon share a moment of fellowship
Tyrion departs for King's Landing in the company of Yoren
Yoren accompanies Tyrion to King's Landing
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt, exercising her authority
Viserys threatens Daenerys after being commanded
Rakharo lassoes Viserys around the neck with a whip
Daenerys calms the situation
Viserys is forced to walk as a sign of shame among the Dothraki
Irri reveals to Daenerys that she is pregnant
Daenerys relays the news of her pregnancy to Khal Drogo and tells him she thinks it will be a boy
Daenerys and Khal Drogo lie naked in each other's arms
Ser Jorah Mormont and Rakharo compare Westerosi and Dothraki ways of battle
Jorah mentions he has heard of Rakharo's father, a great warrior
Jorah admits he disgraced his own father, a man of great honor
Jorah learns Daenerys is pregnant and announces he must ride to Qohor, but will rejoin them before Vaes Dothrak

Cripples,_Bastards,_and_Broken_Things.action: Bran has a nightmare in which he sees a three-eyed raven sitting on a stone direwolf outside the Stark crypts
Bran wakes up to find his direwolf Summer by his side
Theon Greyjoy summons Hodor to help carry Bran
Hodor helps carry Bran down to the throne room
Robb hosts a meeting with Tyrion and Yoren in the throne room
Tyrion and Yoren travel down from the Wall to King's Landing together
Robb offers hospitality to any man of the Watch, pointedly excluding Tyrion
Tyrion asks Bran if he likes to ride and sympathizes with him for being a cripple
Bran is annoyed by Tyrion's comment
Tyrion says that being a dwarf is the truth, no less so for being a hard truth
Tyrion gives Bran the design of a saddle that will allow him to ride
Tyrion explains to Robb how to train a horse for Bran
Tyrion says he is repaying Jon's friendship and has a weakness for "cripples, bastards and broken things"
Robb says Tyrion can stay at Winterfell
Tyrion says he would prefer the brothel in town
Theon comes to Tyrion and condescendingly sends him off, recommending Ros at the brothel
Tyrion asks Theon why Lady Catelyn was not present to receive him
Tyrion concludes that Catelyn is not in Winterfell
Tyrion taunts Theon about being reduced to playing lackey in Winterfell
Theon is infuriated by Tyrion's taunt
Tyrion gives Theon a coin, assuming he has not already had sex with Ros
Samwell Tarly arrives at Castle Black as a new recruit for the Night's Watch
Samwell performs dismally in his first practice fight
Ser Alliser Thorne expresses no patience for Samwell's weakness
Ser Alliser Thorne directs other recruits to attack Samwell, calling him 'Ser Piggy'
Jon finds this cruel and unfair, and takes pity on Samwell
Jon fends off the attackers
Thorne tries to turn it into a game, ordering three trainees to take on Jon, taunting him about a new Lady love
Jon fends off all three trainees
Samwell claims to be a coward and craven, to Jon's astonishment
Grenn says they shouldn't even be seen talking to Samwell
Sam joins Jon on the Wall for sentry duty, despite his weak eyesight, fear of heights, and inability to take the cold
Jon asks Sam why he joined the Night's Watch since he is afraid of everything
Sam explains that on his eighteenth nameday, his father gave him a choice
Sam reveals he was his father's heir but not skilled at combat and considered unworthy
Sam's father forces him to join the Night's Watch
Sam's father threatens to arrange a "hunting trip" and implies he would kill Sam if he refused
Jon is shocked that a true born son of high birth is treated worse than he was as a bastard
Sam expects to be made to fight again the next day and thinks it will be bad
Jon orders the recruits to go easy on Sam
Rast refuses Jon's order and mocks Sam, saying he will slice himself off a side of bacon if ordered to fight 'Lady Piggy'
Jon uses his direwolf Ghost to intimidate Rast into playing along
No one will fight Sam the next day
Grenn takes a weak blow from Sam and yields
Ser Alliser scolds Jon for his rebellious actions
Jon and Sam clean the dining hall and talk about never being with a woman
Sam tells Jon he has not had success with girls
Jon reveals he would rather not risk siring a child who would be a bastard
Ser Alliser Thorne comes in, furious, and tells Jon that charity won't help Sam survive the winter
Ser Alliser recounts his horrific experience trapped by a blizzard north of the Wall that ended in cannibalism
Khal Drogo's khalasar arrives at Vaes Dothrak, the Dothraki city
There is a large gateway arch made of two statues of rearing horses
Viserys is annoyed that Drogo has been taking 'his' army in the wrong direction
Viserys bathes with Doreah
Viserys tells Doreah about how he was raised with stories about the Targaryen dragons and his own destiny
Viserys recites the names of several dragons
Viserys tells Doreah about the skulls of dragons that once decorated the throne room at King's Landing
Doreah starts to annoy Viserys with talk of dragons, reminding him that they are dead
Viserys tells Doreah to finish pleasuring him in silence
Daenerys sends Doreah with an invite for Viserys to dine with her
Viserys becomes enraged by the invitation
Viserys drags Doreah into Daenerys' tent
Viserys insults Daenerys' dinner and the gifts of clothes she offers
Viserys refuses to dress like a Dothraki and remains in ragged clothes from Pentos
Viserys sneers that Daenerys will try to braid his hair like a Dothraki
Daenerys retorts that Viserys has no right to braid his hair until he is victorious in battle
Viserys becomes further infuriated
Viserys tackles Daenerys to the ground and tries to strike her, calling her a "horselord's slut" and warning her that she's "awoken the Dragon"
Daenerys fights back, hitting Viserys in the face with a metal and leather belt
Daenerys tells Viserys that she is the Khal's wife, is carrying his child, and warns him not to raise a hand against her again
Viserys gawps at Daenerys in astonishment
Daenerys confides in Mormont her realization that Viserys will never become King of Westeros
Jorah agrees with Daenerys' assessment of Viserys
Sansa visits the throne room where the Iron Throne sits
Septa Mordane discusses with Sansa the expectation that she will have to provide Joffrey with an heir
Sansa thinks the realm will hate her if she only has daughters
Sansa believes Joffrey is still angry over the incident by the river
Sansa feels anger at her father over Lady's death
Lord Eddard learns that the forthcoming tournament is causing the city to swell with visitors and has already resulted in a tavern riot, a brothel fire, three stabbings, and a drunken horse race
Eddard tells Littlefinger that money will have to be found to strengthen the city guards
Eddard lends twenty of his own men to Janos Slynt's command until the tournament is over
Eddard talks to Grand Maester Pycelle about Jon Arryn's last few weeks
Pycelle says he did all he could to save Jon Arryn, but the illness was sudden and severe
Pycelle recalls that Jon Arryn borrowed a boring book about the lineages of the Seven Kingdoms shortly before his illness
Pycelle says Jon Arryn's last words were, "The seed is strong."
Eddard takes the book with him
Eddard comes across Arya standing on one leg in the hall as part of her training with Syrio
Arya stands on one leg as part of her training with Syrio
Arya asks Eddard whether Bran would be able to live in King's Landing with them now that he's awoken
Eddard states that Bran would have to regain his strength first
Arya comments that Bran had always wanted to be a knight in the Kingsguard, but will no longer be able to due to his paralysis
Eddard confirms that Bran will not be able to be a knight, but says Bran can do many other things such as become lord of a holdfast, sit on the King's council, or raise castles
Arya asks if she would be able to become any of those things
Eddard tells Arya she will marry a highborn lord and have lords and princes for sons
Arya is not pleased with Eddard's response and tells him, "that's not me."
Arya goes back to balancing on one leg
Eddard looks on
Littlefinger suggests Eddard keep his investigation into Jon Arryn's death lower in profile
Littlefinger gives Eddard intelligence that Jon Arryn's former squire has been knighted and will fight in the forthcoming Hand's tourney
Littlefinger advises Eddard to investigate an armorer in the city
Eddard decides Littlefinger may be more trustworthy than he first thought
Littlefinger discourages Eddard from trusting him
Eddard sends Jory Cassel to question Jon Arryn's former squire, Ser Hugh of the Vale
Ser Hugh rebuffs Jory and refuses to speak with him due to his lower rank
Eddard's investigation leads him to a smithy in the city
Eddard meets master smith Tobho Mott and his apprentice Gendry
Mott has Gendry show Eddard a bull's head helm as a sample of his work
Eddard commends Gendry's work
Gendry adds that the helm is not for sale
Mott scolds Gendry
Gendry states he made the helm for himself
Eddard assures Mott he is not offended
Gendry tells Eddard that Jon would drop by and ask him questions
Jon asks Gendry if he likes being at Mott's smithy and if he is treated well
Jon asks Gendry who his mother was
Eddard studies Gendry's face and realizes Gendry is one of King Robert's bastard sons
Eddard can't work out why Jon Arryn would be interested in this
Eddard notes that Robert has many bastard children and shows little interest in any of them
Eddard tells Mott that if Gendry becomes interested in wielding a sword instead of forging them, to send him to Eddard
Eddard sends Jory with a message for King Robert
Jory arrives at King Robert's quarters and hears the voices of several women coming from inside
Jaime is on duty guarding King Robert's chamber and is angry about the king's behavior
Jaime resents King Robert flaunting his dishonorable treatment of Jaime's sister
Jaime and Jory reminisce about fighting together in the Greyjoy Rebellion
Jaime and Jory reminisce about fighting together in the Greyjoy Rebellion
Jory tries to leave Eddard's message with Jaime
Jaime rebuffs Jory, stating he does not serve Lord Stark
The tournament begins
Sansa watches from the stands
Ser Gregor Clegane takes the field
Ser Gregor jousts with Ser Hugh
Ser Gregor kills Ser Hugh "by accident" with a splinter of the lance impaling his neck
Littlefinger sits next to Sansa
Littlefinger remarks that Sansa and Joffrey have had a lover's quarrel
Littlefinger tells Sansa about Gregor's dark reputation, including burning his brother Sandor's face
Littlefinger recommends Sansa not spread that story around
Cersei visits Eddard at his chambers and remarks on his absence from his own tournament
Cersei attempts to start a conversation to put the Kingsroad incident behind them
Eddard deduces that Cersei has an ulterior motive for her visit
Cersei wonders aloud about Eddard's true purpose for being at King's Landing
Eddard tells Cersei he is there to faithfully serve the King
Cersei remarks that Eddard was born to follow, while his brother Brandon was born to lead
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik Cassel eat a meal at the Crossroads Inn while traveling in secret to Winterfell
Marillion tries to ingratiate himself with Catelyn and Ser Rodrik
Tyrion and Yoren arrive at the inn, surprising Catelyn and Ser Rodrik
Tyrion is told there is no vacancy
Tyrion holds up a gold coin and asks if someone has a room for him
Bronn agrees to give his room to Tyrion
Marillion draws Tyrion’s attention to Catelyn’s table by offering to serenade him and singing of his father’s military victories
Tyrion is surprised to see Catelyn and inadvertently ends her anonymity
Catelyn tells the inn that Tyrion tried to murder her son Bran twice and asks for their help in bringing him to justice
The knights present, who owe fealty to Catelyn’s father, react to her accusation
Tyrion finds himself surrounded by half a dozen men with swords and in Catelyn’s custody

The_Wolf_and_the_Lion.action: Tyrion has his blindfold removed and discovers the party is on the Eastern Road to the Vale of Arryn
Catelyn loudly claims they are riding for Winterfell
Tyrion congratulates Catelyn on her cunning
Tyrion points out his father will give a handsome reward for his safe return, piquing Bronn's interest
Tyrion says Lysa Arryn has gone mad and will try to kill him in the Eyrie
Tyrion demands his innocence be known in the attempt on Bran's life
Tyrion points out he would have to be an idiot to give the assassin his own blade
The party comes under attack from one of the hill tribes
The party takes several losses but drives off the attackers
Tyrion fights instead of escaping, kills his first man, and saves Catelyn's life
The party resumes their trek to the Eyrie
Lady Lysa Arryn holds court on behalf of her young son, Robin
Lysa becomes furious that Cat has endangered them by bringing Tyrion to the Vale
Tyrion points out that harming him will cause war with House Lannister
Lysa is unconcerned about Tyrion’s threats, believing the Vale is strongly-defended and the Eyrie impregnable
Lysa orders Tyrion sent to the sky cells
Tyrion is forced to huddle in a corner and try not to roll out to his death in his sleep
Bran is angry about his mother's absence
Maester Luwin tries to set Bran's mind at rest about the matter
Bran remains unconvinced by Luwin's efforts
Bran gets frustrated at not being able to practice his archery
Luwin suggests Bran could learn to use a shortbow like the Dothraki
Bran is pleased by the notion of using a shortbow
Theon Greyjoy enjoys the attentions of his favorite whore, Ros
Ros compares Theon's prowess unfavorably to that of Tyrion Lannister
Theon becomes annoyed by Ros's comparison
Eddard arrives at the tourney ground to speak to Barristan about the death of Ser Hugh of the Vale
Barristan notes that Ser Hugh had no family or friends, so he stood the knight's vigil himself
Eddard is suspicious of Ser Hugh's death and wants to know how he could afford new armor so soon after being knighted
Barristan reflects on the irony of being on opposite sides during Robert's Rebellion and now working together
Someone tells Eddard that King Robert is planning to joust during the day's activities
Eddard finds King Robert tormenting his squire, Lancel Lannister
King Robert sends Lancel Lannister off on a snipe hunt in amused disgust
King Robert insists on fighting in the joust
Eddard tells Robert he is too fat for his armor and the other knights would let him win
King Robert is frustrated but admits Eddard is right
King Robert and Eddard agree to watch the joust from the stands
Ser Gregor Clegane (the Mountain) and Ser Loras Tyrell (the Knight of the Flowers) prepare for the first tilt of the day
Loras gives a beautiful flower to Sansa Stark
Sansa receives a flower from Loras
Littlefinger and Lord Renly make a wager on who will win
Renly bets on Loras
Loras rides a mare that is in heat
The Mountain rides a stallion
Loras’s mare agitates and excites the Mountain’s stallion
The Mountain’s stallion throws its rider
Gregor decapitates his horse in anger after losing
Gregor attacks Ser Loras
Sandor Clegane comes to Loras's defense, preventing Gregor from killing him
Sandor and Gregor exchange several blows
King Robert orders them to cease
Sandor immediately bows to the king
Gregor storms off the field
Ser Loras declares Sandor the victor of the match, causing discomfort to the Hound
Varys meets with Eddard in the Red Keep
Varys tells Eddard that the king is a fool and will soon be dead unless Eddard can save him
Eddard asks Varys why he is telling him this now
Varys tells Eddard he had to be sure Eddard could be trusted
Varys calls Eddard an honorable and true man, maybe the only one in the city
Varys confirms that Jon Arryn was assassinated with a poison called the Tears of Lys because he started asking questions
Arya chases cats as part of her sword training
Arya chases a cat into the dungeons under the castle
Arya finds the great dragon skulls of the Targaryens in storage
Arya hears two men talking: Varys and Magister Illyrio Mopatis
Varys says that events have been set in motion and the lion and the wolf will soon be at each other's throats
Varys says war is almost upon the Seven Kingdoms, as they planned, but it has come too soon
Varys says Khal Drogo will not invade Westeros until his son is born and pronounced healthy, which is still many months away
Illyrio suggests assassinating Eddard to forestall the war
Varys refuses Illyrio's suggestion to assassinate Eddard
Varys finds Littlefinger in the Great Hall staring at the Iron Throne
Varys reveals he knows about Littlefinger's assistance in Eddard's investigation of Jon Arryn's death
Varys reminds Littlefinger of the consequences if the Lannisters discover his involvement
Littlefinger reveals he knows about Varys meeting with both Eddard and Illyrio earlier that day
Littlefinger implies he knows of Varys's allegiances across the Narrow Sea
Renly interrupts their conversation
Renly informs them that Robert will be joining them at their Small Council meeting
Arya escapes from the dungeon
Arya tries to warn her father about what she overheard but gets the words muddled up
Yoren interrupts Arya and Eddard
Yoren brings Eddard forewarning of Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion Lannister
Eddard keeps the information to himself
Arya asks Jory Cassel about the state of her father's household guard
Jory tells Arya she does not have to worry about her father's safety
King Robert tells Eddard that Daenerys Targaryen is pregnant and her son will have a claim to the Iron Throne
King Robert expresses his desire for Daenerys, the unborn child, and Viserys to be killed as soon as possible
Eddard states that the act would be dishonorable, cowardly, and beneath their dignity
Pycelle, Renly, Littlefinger, and Varys concur that Daenerys must die for the good of the realm
King Robert orders Ned to enact the order
Eddard refuses and resigns as the Hand of the King
Robert is left in utter disbelief and fury
Eddard orders his household to begin packing
Eddard packs his belongings
Littlefinger arrives and offers to take Eddard to the last place Jon Arryn went before he "fell ill"
Eddard agrees to go with Littlefinger
Ser Loras and Lord Renly, who are lovers, meet in a bedchamber
Renly complains about Robert organizing a hunt in the Kingswood, which will involve traipsing around the forest in the cold and rain
Loras urges Renly to claim the crown himself, offering the allegiance of his rich and powerful family
Renly dismisses the notion, saying he is fourth in line to the Iron Throne
Loras points out that Joffrey is a monster and Tommen is only eight
Loras laughs at the idea of Stannis becoming king, saying he has the charisma and personality of a lobster
Loras asserts that the people love Renly and want to serve him because he is kind and does not revel in violence like his brothers
Cersei and Robert share a rare drink
Cersei and Robert discuss the Targaryen threat
Cersei takes Eddard's side in the discussion
Cersei points out that the Dothraki cannot cross the Narrow Sea as they have no ships
Robert agrees that if the knights of Westeros take shelter behind castle walls, the Dothraki could burn and pillage the continent until the smallfolk accept Viserys as king
Cersei suggests that the unified armies of the Seven Kingdoms could defeat the Dothraki
Robert says Cersei underestimates the skill and ability of one army with one leader and one purpose compared to the scattered armies of Westeros
Robert and Cersei discuss Lyanna Stark, surprising Robert since Cersei has never asked about her in seventeen years
Robert tells Cersei that Lyanna's death left a void in him that seven kingdoms could not fill
Robert tells Cersei their marriage never had a chance because of Lyanna's memory
Robert bitterly adds he can barely remember what Lyanna looked like
Cersei says she felt something for Robert once, even after they lost their first boy
Cersei asks Robert if there was ever any chance for their marriage
Robert answers "no"
Littlefinger brings Eddard Stark to the last place Jon Arryn went before falling ill, one of his brothels
Eddard meets another of Robert's bastards, a baby girl and her mother, Mhaegen
Mhaegen remarks that her child has Robert's nose and "black" hair
Eddard asks Littlefinger why Jon Arryn was searching for Robert's bastards
Littlefinger says he doesn't know, offering a half-hearted guess that Robert took paternal interest in them
Eddard looks skeptical of Littlefinger's reason
Eddard, Jory Cassel, and two guards leave the brothel
They are confronted by a large group of Lannister guardsmen led by Ser Jaime Lannister
Jaime demands an explanation for Tyrion's arrest
Eddard says that Tyrion was arrested on his orders
Jaime threatens to kill Eddard
Eddard points out that if Jaime kills him, Tyrion will die as well
Jaime acknowledges the truth and says he will settle for killing Eddard's men instead
Lannister spearmen kill Heward and Wyl, Eddard's guards, in the opening seconds of the fight
Eddard and Jory fight and kill six Lannister soldiers
Jory engages Jaime
Jaime quickly kills Jory by stabbing him through the eye with a dagger
Eddard crosses swords with Jaime, furious
Jaime is shocked and then exhilarated by Eddard's skill
Jaime gradually grows worried as Eddard's high level of skill becomes apparent
A Lannister guardsman steps in and stabs Eddard through the leg with a spear, disabling him
Jaime is annoyed at the dishonorable interruption and knocks out the guardsman
Jaime departs, telling Eddard he wants his brother back

A_Golden_Crown.action: Eddard Stark wakes up after a long period of unconsciousness due to injuries inflicted by Jaime Lannister's men
Eddard finds King Robert Baratheon and Queen Cersei waiting
King Robert Baratheon waits for Eddard to wake up
Queen Cersei waits for Eddard to wake up
Cersei is furious with Eddard for having Tyrion arrested and attacking Jaime in the street
Cersei wants Robert to take action against Eddard
Robert refuses to condemn Eddard without hearing his side
Robert tells Cersei to be silent
Cersei protests further and calls Robert a woman
Robert hits Cersei
Cersei says she will wear the bruise with honor
Robert tells Cersei to be silent or he will honor her again
Cersei leaves in anger
Robert restores Eddard to his position as King's Hand
Robert tells Eddard not to quit again or he will give the position to Jaime
Robert tells Eddard to end the animosity between House Stark and House Lannister
Robert says strife between the two houses could lead to war
Robert announces he is leaving on a hunt and wants the realm at peace by his return
Arya has another water dancing lesson with Syrio Forel
Arya is not in the mood for the lesson due to Jory's death
Syrio tells Arya that she may need to fight at any time, not just when she's in the mood
Syrio reminds Arya that being prepared to fight is more important in times of sadness than in peaceful times of happiness
King Robert goes out hunting, accompanied by Renly, Ser Barristan Selmy, and Lancel Lannister
Lancel supplies King Robert with wine
King Robert becomes drunk and nostalgic, longing for the days of young knights and open enemies
King Robert brags about all the women he has bedded
King Robert questions Renly about his history with women
Renly becomes irate
Renly calls King Robert a fool and remarks that the past had its share of violence
Renly storms off
Ser Barristan looks on, concerned
Lancel keeps up King Robert's wine supply
a group of refugees come to the Small Council to tell of their villages being destroyed
Eddard hears tales of a huge knight ravaging the Riverlands
Eddard realizes the knight is Ser Gregor Clegane, likely under Lannister orders to punish the Riverlands
Eddard remembers the Riverlands are held by House Tully, the former House of his wife Catelyn, who now holds Tyrion captive
King Robert told Eddard to make peace with the Lannisters
Eddard cannot let the injustice stand
Eddard strips Gregor Clegane of his knighthood, land, and titles
Eddard sends Lord Beric Dondarrion with a retinue of one hundred knights to arrest and execute Gregor Clegane in the king's name
Eddard orders Grand Maester Pycelle to send word to Casterly Rock, demanding Lord Tywin Lannister's presence in court
Eddard threatens that Tywin will be branded an enemy of the crown and a traitor to the realm if he does not comply
Petyr Baelish and Grand Maester Pycelle are concerned with the harshness of Eddard's orders and actions against the Lannisters
Eddard insists on seeing justice done
Prince Joffrey comes to apologize to Sansa for the incident on the Kingsroad
Joffrey calls Sansa his lady love
Joffrey gives Sansa a necklace
Sansa forgives Joffrey
Sansa and Joffrey seem reconciled
Sansa is unaware that the Queen put Joffrey up to the apology some time ago
Eddard decides to send his daughters back to Winterfell
Arya worries that she must leave Syrio
Sansa protests that no one cares about Arya's dancing instructor, focusing on her own betrothal to Joffrey
Eddard says he will find someone else for Sansa to marry
Sansa refuses and insists she will marry Joffrey and have lots of golden-haired babies
Sansa's words give Eddard pause
Eddard consults the book that Jon Arryn was looking at before he died
Eddard finds that every time a Baratheon has married, the children have had black hair, except for Cersei and Robert's children, who are all blond
Bran has another dream about the Three-Eyed Raven flying into the castle's crypts
Bran wakes up and finds that Hodor has brought the finished saddle designed by Tyrion
Hodor brings the finished saddle to Bran
Tyrion designs a saddle for Bran
Bran goes on a ride with Robb and Theon
Robb and Theon ride with Bran
Robb and Theon ride with Bran
Robb and Theon receive word from King's Landing about Eddard's injury, Jory's death, and Tyrion's capture
Theon thinks Robb should prepare the North for war
Robb decides to wait and follow his parents' orders to remain in Winterfell
Bran wanders off on his horse
Robb and Theon go looking for Bran
Robb and Theon go looking for Bran
Robb finds a group of wildlings threatening Bran and trying to steal his horse
Robb kills two wildlings
Another wildling holds Bran with a knife to his throat
Theon shoots the wildling holding Bran from behind
Robb is angered by Theon for endangering Bran
Osha, the remaining wildling, yields and begs for her life
Robb agrees to take Osha as a prisoner
Ros departs Winterfell for King's Landing on the back of a turnip cart
Theon stops Ros and unsuccessfully tries to persuade her to stay
Ros explains that war is imminent and her departure is an economically essential decision
Theon throws Ros a coin to get her to raise her skirt one last time
Ros raises her skirt
Ros rides off
Theon says that he will miss Ros
Ros agrees that Theon will miss her
Tyrion almost rolls out of his sky cell in his sleep, to his consternation
Tyrion tries to make a bargain with the gaoler, Mord, to no avail
Tyrion convinces Mord to carry a message to Lady Lysa Arryn in exchange for gold
Mord carries Tyrion’s message to Lady Lysa Arryn
Lysa summons Tyrion, believing the sky cell has broken his spirit
Tyrion confesses only to being a liar, a whoremonger, and a player of disgusting practical jokes
Tyrion maintains his innocence of the crimes of conspiracy to commit murder, specifically that of Bran Stark
Tyrion publicly demands his right to justice and a trial in front of Lysa's court
Lysa is shamed into agreeing to a trial
Tyrion demands a trial by combat
Lysa reluctantly agrees to Tyrion's right to trial by combat
Lysa asks for a volunteer to be her champion, as neither she nor her son can fight
Ser Vardis Egen agrees to be Lysa's champion
Ser Vardis Egen is unwilling to fight Tyrion, as killing an outmatched opponent would be dishonorable
Tyrion names his brother Jaime as his champion
Lysa objects, as Jaime is not present and she does not want to wait
Lysa demands Tyrion pick a champion from those present
Tyrion desperately asks for volunteers
Bronn steps forward and says he will stand for Tyrion
Bronn fights defensively, waiting for Vardis to tire from his full armor
Bronn wounds Vardis
Bronn kills Vardis, sending his body rolling out of the "Moon Door"
Lysa complains that Bronn did not fight with honor
Bronn agrees, but indicates that Vardis did fight with honor
Tyrion retrieves his gold from Ser Rodrik
Tyrion gives his gold to Mord
Robin asks if he can make the little man fly now
Tyrion says, "This little man is going home."
Bronn and Tyrion are allowed to depart
Daenerys studies her dragon eggs
Daenerys ponders if the eggs might be hatched by extreme heat
Daenerys places a dragon egg on a brazier
Nothing happens to the egg
Daenerys is disappointed
Daenerys removes the egg from the brazier
Irri enters and thinks Daenerys will burn herself
Irri takes the egg from Daenerys
Daenerys is completely unharmed
Irri suffers burns on her hands
Daenerys is summoned by the Dosh Khaleen
Daenerys eats a stallion's raw heart
The crones read the omens
Daenerys struggles to keep the organ down
Daenerys composes herself
Drogo is relieved
The crones declare Daenerys and Drogo's son will be "The Stallion That Mounts The World"
Daenerys says her son's name will be "Rhaego"
Viserys is not impressed and is disgusted by the ritual
Jorah Mormont explains the ritual to Viserys
The Dothraki chant Rhaego's name
Drogo lifts Daenerys up and carries her about the room
Viserys realizes the Dothraki love Daenerys
Viserys becomes jealous of the tribe's worship of his sister and storms out of the tent
Viserys is discovered in Daenerys' tent by Ser Jorah Mormont, trying to steal Dany's dragon eggs
Ser Jorah prevents Viserys from stealing the dragon eggs
Viserys tells Ser Jorah that to rule one must be feared or loved, and he has never been loved as the Dothraki love Daenerys
Viserys says he knows Jorah wants Daenerys, but he doesn't care
Viserys states he just wants the eggs to hire an army
Viserys cannot get past Ser Jorah and must leave the eggs
Ser Jorah watches Viserys as he leaves
Viserys, drunk, bitter, and angry, interrupts a feast for Daenerys and Drogo
Viserys angrily demands his payment in return for giving Daenerys to Drogo
Viserys draws his sword, committing an offense punishable by death in the sacred city
Viserys threatens Daenerys and her unborn child
Irri translates Viserys's words to Drogo
Drogo says he will give Viserys a golden crown "that men will tremble to behold"
Viserys is pleased
Qotho seizes Viserys, breaks his arm, makes him drop the sword, and kicks him to the floor
Drogo melts his golden belt of medallions in a pot
Viserys begs Daenerys to help him
Ser Jorah tells Daenerys to look away
Daenerys refuses to look away
Daenerys watches as Drogo pours molten gold over Viserys's head, "crowning" him
Viserys cries out in agony
Viserys falls forward, his head making a loud clang as it connects with the floor
Daenerys coldly states that Viserys was no true dragon, having been killed by fire

You_Win_or_You_Die.action: Lord Tywin Lannister assembles an army of sixty thousand men on the borders of the Riverlands
Tywin plans to oppose the Starks
Tywin plans to punish Catelyn for capturing his son Tyrion Lannister
Tywin stands in his tent skinning a dead stag
Tywin tells Jaime that the conflict will decide the fate of their family
Tywin says they will either forge a dynasty or cease to exist like the Targaryens
Tywin orders Jaime to become the man he was meant to be
Tywin orders Jaime to take thirty thousand men and besiege Riverrun
Jaime ponders why Tywin would risk so much for Tyrion
Tywin considers Tyrion the least worthy member of their family
Tywin believes any affront to their family must be punished or it makes them look weak
Theon Greyjoy attempts to bully Osha, the wildling woman captured in the recent raid
Theon insists that Osha refer to him as "My Lord"
Osha is unimpressed with Theon's antics
Osha points out that Theon is not entitled to be called "My Lord" as his father is still alive
Maester Luwin dismisses Theon
Maester Luwin questions Osha about why her band crossed the Wall and were fleeing south
Osha claims that the White Walkers have awoken after a millennia asleep and are coming south
Maester Luwin is skeptical of Osha's claim
Jon and Samwell stand watch atop the Wall
Benjen's horse is spotted returning
Jon is horrified to see there is no sign of Benjen himself
Jeor Mormont names the trainees as men of the Night's Watch
Jeor Mormont hands out assignments
Jon is assigned to the stewards, not the rangers, and will work as the Lord Commander's personal steward
Jon is disgusted by his assignment
Jon attempts to plead his case to Maester Aemon
Maester Aemon meets Jon's pleas with indifference
Maester Aemon selects Sam as his own steward
Sam suggests to Jon that the assignment may be an opportunity for the Lord Commander to groom Jon for command
Sam suggests the move may have been orchestrated by Ser Alliser Thorne to punish Jon
Pypar admits he was sent to the Watch for refusing to give sexual favors to a lord, not for stealing cheese
Jon and Sam are surprised by Pypar's admission
Jon and Sam are surprised by Pypar's admission
The unfairness of being sent to the Wall is noted
Jon is allowed to say his oath of fealty to the Night's Watch in front of a heart tree
Sam decides to take the oath with Jon
Othell Yarwyck and three more brothers of the Watch accompany Sam and Jon
Sam and Jon take their oaths
Sam and Jon are acknowledged by the others as members of the Watch
Ghost finds a severed hand in the woods
Ghost brings the severed hand back to Jon and a horrified Sam
Daenerys attempts to convince Khal Drogo about the benefits of invading the Seven Kingdoms
Drogo is reluctant to cross the Narrow Sea, despite the availability of thousands of ships in the Free Cities
Drogo says a man doesn't need an "iron chair" but only a horse
Daenerys travels to the marketplace with Ser Jorah Mormont and her handmaidens
Daenerys asks Jorah to help convince her husband to invade, citing her birthright
Jorah reminds Daenerys that Aegon the Conqueror took the kingdoms because he had dragons
A little bird gives Jorah a message from Varys, a royal pardon for his crimes
Jorah realizes the pardon means he is no longer needed as a spy
Jorah sees Daenerys with a wineseller who is eager for her to try a vintage from the Arbor
Jorah realizes the wine has been poisoned and that this is an assassination attempt
Jorah stops Daenerys from drinking the poisoned wine
The wineseller attempts to flee
Rakharo brings down the wineseller with a bolo, tangling his ankles
Ser Jorah tells Daenerys that Robert Baratheon will never stop trying to kill her and her future children
Drogo arrives in the tent
Drogo glares at the wine seller
Drogo goes to Daenerys to ensure she is okay
Drogo offers Jorah the reward of any horse he chooses for foiling the assassination
Drogo yells in Dothraki that his army will cross the "poison water" and take the Iron Throne for his son
Drogo's khalasar departs Vaes Dothrak the next morning
The wine seller is tied naked to the saddle of Daenerys' horse, forced to walk until he falls of exhaustion and is dragged to his death
Eddard meets with Cersei
Eddard tells Cersei he knows the secret that Jon Arryn died for: her three children are not Robert's, but the product of incest between her and Jaime
Cersei does not deny the charge and is proud of it, comparing her love with Jaime to the old Targaryen practice of marrying siblings
Cersei admits to despising Robert since their wedding night, when Robert drunkenly called her "Lyanna"
Eddard angrily tells Cersei to take her children and leave the city immediately
Eddard says he will tell Robert the truth when he returns from his hunt, and warns Cersei to run before Robert's wrath finds her
Cersei icily calls Ned a fool for turning down the throne after the sack of King's Landing
Eddard tells Cersei he has regrets, but that isn't one of them
Cersei departs with a chilling warning: "When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die. There is no middle ground."
Littlefinger instructs Ros and Armeca on how to please their customers and make them happy
Ros and Armeca ask Littlefinger to join them
Littlefinger refuses to join them, saying he only ever had eyes for one woman
Littlefinger tells Ros and Armeca about his past, how he loved and fought a duel for a woman
Littlefinger explains he lost the duel and realized he could never beat those in power by honorable means
Ros asks Littlefinger what he wants
Littlefinger replies, "Oh, everything."
Renly, blood-stained and shaken, finds Eddard and tells him that Robert has been badly mauled by a boar and is not expected to survive
Eddard finds Robert telling Joffrey that he wishes he could have been a better father to him
Eddard chooses not to tell Robert about his discoveries to spare his final hours from pain
Robert instructs Eddard to make a letter naming him as the Lord Protector of the Realm, to rule until Joffrey comes of age
Eddard writes the letter, but does not write Joffrey's name, instead saying "the rightful heir"
Robert asks Eddard to cancel the attempt on Daenerys Targaryen's life
Eddard leaves the chamber
Eddard talks to Varys and Ser Barristan Selmy
Selmy laments that Robert insisted the others stand aside to let him take on the boar alone
Selmy blames himself for failing in his duty
Ned gently reassures Barristan that no one could protect Robert from himself
Varys slyly points out, by asking Selmy about the wine, that Lancel Lannister ensured the King kept "refreshed" with alcohol during the hunt
Varys implies Robert's reactions were slowed and unreliable
Varys says it is far too late to stop the attempt on Daenerys' life
Renly confronts Eddard and offers him a hundred swords to take Joffrey into custody
Renly insists that any delay will only serve Cersei's schemes
Eddard refuses to dishonor Robert's last hours by shedding blood in the Red Keep and taking frightened children hostage
Renly suggests that he could make a good king
Eddard angrily points out that Stannis is Renly's older brother and the true heir
Eddard writes a letter laying out his findings to Lord Stannis
Eddard instructs a courier to take the message to Dragonstone
Littlefinger arrives and talks to Eddard
Eddard tells Littlefinger the truth about Joffrey's parentage
Littlefinger suggests they keep quiet, acknowledge Joffrey as king, and make him their puppet
Littlefinger suggests that if Joffrey proves intractable, they can reveal the truth and crown Renly instead
Eddard considers this treason
Littlefinger realizes Eddard doesn't have the men he needs to seize control from Cersei
Littlefinger understands Eddard needs the loyalty of the City Watch
Littlefinger agrees to do what needs to be done
Eddard is summoned to the throne room by "King Joffrey" after Robert's death
Eddard arrives to find Littlefinger, Varys, Commander Janos Slynt, and a detachment of the City Watch waiting for him
Varys tells Eddard that Renly has fled the city with Ser Loras Tyrell and a number of retainers
The party enters the throne room, where Joffrey sits on the Iron Throne
Joffrey demands oaths of fealty from his councilors and subjects
Eddard gives Ser Barristan Selmy the proclamation naming him as Lord Protector of the Realm
Cersei takes the proclamation and tears it up, to Barristan's shock
Cersei suggests that Eddard bend the knee and swear allegiance
Joffrey offers to allow Eddard to return to Winterfell in return for something
Eddard responds that Joffrey has no claim to the Iron Throne
Eddard commands Slynt to take the Queen and her son into custody
City Watch kill Eddard's bodyguards
Littlefinger holds a knife to Eddard's throat
Littlefinger says, "I did warn you not to trust me."

The_Pointy_End.action: Jon and Sam return to Castle Black with their party and two dead bodies
The two corpses are identified as members of Benjen Stark's missing ranging party, Jafer Flowers and Othor
Sam notes that the bodies do not smell like they have been rotting
Jon and several other Sworn Brothers urge Lord Commander Jeor Mormont to burn the bodies
Jeor Mormont refuses to burn the bodies, wanting Maester Aemon to examine them
Jon is summoned to Mormont's chambers as news arrives of Eddard's arrest and imprisonment
Jon asks Mormont for news of his father
Mormont tells Jon that Eddard has been arrested for treason
Jon is in disbelief
Mormont gives Jon the letter to read for himself
Jon attempts to leave, devastated
Mormont tells Jon not to do anything stupid and reminds him of his Night's Watch oath
Jon expresses concern for his sisters
Mormont replies he is sure Jon's sisters are unharmed
Alliser Thorne taunts Jon, calling him "not just a bastard, a traitor's bastard"
Jon angrily charges at Thorne with a knife
Grenn and Pyp hold Jon back, saving Thorne
Commander Mormont witnesses the incident and confines Jon to quarters
Ghost scratches at Jon's door, rousing Jon at night
Jon senses trouble and heads to the Lord Commander's chambers with Ghost
Jon is confronted by a wight, the reanimated corpse of a dead Night's Watchman
Jon stabs the wight through the chest with his sword, but the wight is unharmed
The wight removes the sword from its chest
Mormont appears, seeking out the commotion
Jon grabs Mormont's oil lamp and throws it at the wight, destroying it
The two wight bodies have been thoroughly burned
Sam says he read in a book that the dead are animated by the touch of the White Walkers and only fire can destroy such creatures
Sam hopes the Wall is big enough to hold the Walkers back when they come in force
Catelyn is furious with Lysa for not immediately notifying her about Eddard's arrest
Catelyn is furious with Lysa for refusing to consider summoning the knights of the Vale to war
Lysa refuses to consider summoning the knights of the Vale to war
Lysa does not believe that war is in the Vale's best interests
Lysa wants the knights to stay in the Vale to protect her son and their Lord
Lysa set these events in motion by implicating the Lannisters in Jon Arryn's death
Tyrion and Bronn reach the western edge of the Vale
Tyrion and Bronn are surrounded by members of the hill tribes led by Shagga
Shagga orders Tyrion and Bronn to be killed
Tyrion fast-talks and convinces the hill tribes that House Lannister is an enemy of House Arryn
Tyrion proposes an alliance to the hill tribes to enact vengeance against House Arryn and take ownership of the Vale
The hill tribes eagerly agree to the alliance
The hill tribes escort Tyrion and Bronn westwards towards the Lannister armies
Robb receives a letter from Sansa in King's Landing, asking him to pledge fealty to King Joffrey
Maester Luwin deduces the letter is actually the work of the Queen
Robb refuses to consider the request
Robb sends out ravens, summoning the lords bannermen of the North and their armies to assemble at Winterfell
Released ravens fill the sky
Robb holds a feast for several of his newly-arrived bannermen at Winterfell
Greatjon Umber assumes he will lead the vanguard
Greatjon is offended by the suggestion he would march behind a Glover and threatens to withdraw from the host
Robb promises to oust Greatjon from his keep and hang him for breaking his oaths after the conflict with the Lannisters
Greatjon goes to draw a weapon in anger
Robb's direwolf Grey Wind sets upon Greatjon, biting off two of his fingers
Robb recites that it is death to bear steel against your liege lord
Robb diffuses the situation by excusing Greatjon's aggression, joking that Greatjon only meant to cut his meat for him
Greatjon roars with laughter and accepts Robb's commands
Bran is shocked as he witnesses the entire incident
Robb says farewell to Bran and Rickon
Rickon is convinced he'll see neither Robb nor his parents again
Bran prays by the heart tree
Osha approaches Bran and tells him the Old Gods are listening to him and that the wildlings also worship the Old Gods
Osha laments that the South has lost touch with the past and that the southern Weirwoods were cut down years ago
Osha says the Southerners have no idea what's awakening in the north
Hodor interrupts, having forgotten to put his clothes back on after bathing
Osha remarks that Hodor might have giant blood in him
Bran sends Hodor away
Bran asks Osha if giants live beyond the wall
Osha affirms that giants and many other beings live beyond the wall, though not all are friendly
Osha asserts that Robb's army should be marching north, not south
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik Cassel intercept Robb's army in the Neck as it presses southwards
Robb exclaims with excitement upon seeing his mother
Robb stops short of embracing Catelyn lest he look weak in front of his bannermen
Catelyn looks at Robb with understanding
Catelyn and Robb embrace once their liege-lords have left their tent
Catelyn tells Robb he has no choice but to go to war
Catelyn tells Robb he cannot lose, for the sake of Ned, Sansa, and Arya
Catelyn warns that if Robb is defeated, Tywin Lannister will show their family no mercy
Robb and his lords debate the route of their march and whether to march against Tywin's army or Jaime's army at Riverrun
Robb and his lords consider the need to cross the Green Fork of the Trident to reach Jaime
Robb and his lords note that the only crossing is at the Twins, held by Lord Walder Frey
Lord Walder Frey is described as notoriously prickly and easily-offended
A Lannister scout is captured
Robb sends the Lannister scout back to warn Lord Tywin that twenty thousand Northern soldiers are marching against him
Tyrion, Bronn, Shagga, and the hill tribesmen arrive at the Lannister encampment
Tywin and Ser Kevan plan to engage the Stark army moving south from the Neck
Tywin shows no sign of relief that Tyrion is alive
Tywin accepts the aid of the hill tribesmen
Shagga insists he will only fight if Tyrion fights alongside them
Tywin welcomes the prospect of Tyrion fighting with the hill tribesmen
Tyrion is terrified by the prospect of fighting alongside the hill tribesmen
Khal Drogo takes an oath to take the Iron Throne and begins his march of conquest to the Narrow Sea
The Dothraki raid a village in Lhazar
The Dothraki kill villagers and rape their women
The Dothraki take people to sell into slavery to finance the war and hire ships
Ser Jorah and others try to explain the necessity of the raids to Daenerys
Daenerys is disturbed by the aftermath of the raid
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop killing and raping
The Dothraki grow angry at not being able to take the spoils of victory
Daenerys starts claiming all the women to protect them
The angry warriors take their complaints to Khal Drogo
Khal Drogo is amused at Daenerys’s fierceness
Khal Drogo tells the warriors that Daenerys may keep the women she has claimed, and they can find others
One man refuses to listen and angrily accuses Drogo of being slave to a foreign whore
Khal Drogo stops the complaints by killing Mago, the offended warrior who challenges him
Drogo takes a deep wound to the chest in the process
Drogo dismisses the wound as a scratch
Daenerys insists the wound be treated
Daenerys allows Mirri Maz Duur, a healer she rescued, to treat Drogo's wound
Drogo's bloodriders express distaste and derisively call Mirri Maz Duur a maegi (witch)
Lannister soldiers complete their purge of the Stark guards and household staff
Sansa and Septa Mordane hear sounds of fighting
Lannister guardsmen led by Sandor Clegane confront Sansa and Septa Mordane
Septa Mordane tells Sansa to run and lock herself in her room
Septa Mordane stays to confront the Lannister guardsmen
Sandor Clegane finds and takes Sansa into custody
Lannister guardsmen led by Ser Meryn Trant interrupt a water dancing lesson between Syrio Forel and Arya
Syrio Forel holds Arya back and questions why Lord Eddard would send Lannister men
Arya refuses to go with the Lannister guardsmen
Syrio disarms and disables all of the lightly-armored guardsmen with his wooden practice sword
Syrio tells Arya to run as he faces down Ser Meryn Trant
Syrio notes that "the First Sword of Braavos does not run"
Syrio's fate after this is unclear
Arya runs to the stables where the men waiting with their baggage were supposed to be
Arya finds the men dead
Arya finds her real sword, Needle, in the bottom of her trunk
Arya starts to leave the stables
Arya remembers how to get out of the Red Keep via the tunnel she found while chasing cats
Stableboy tries to grab Arya
Arya turns around brandishing Needle and accidentally runs the stableboy through, killing him
Arya is horrified and flees
Varys visits Eddard Stark in his cell in the dungeons
Varys expresses incredulity that Eddard warned Cersei
Eddard explains he wanted to extend mercy to Cersei's children
Varys tells Eddard that his mercy is what killed King Robert
Eddard feels shame
Varys informs Eddard that Catelyn no longer holds Tyrion as her prisoner
Varys explains the Starks have nothing to barter for Eddard's life
Eddard suggests they should just kill him
Varys replies "Not today, my lord."
Varys departs
Eddard asks Varys who he truly serves
Varys replies "The realm, my lord. Someone must."
Cersei and the small council (Pycelle, Varys, Littlefinger) summon Sansa to inform her that her father has been arrested for treason
Cersei and the small council manipulate Sansa into writing a letter to Robb, asking him to bend the knee to Joffrey
Cersei and the small council tell Sansa that Eddard's fate may depend on Robb and the Northern lords' actions
Sansa agrees to write the letter
King Joffrey holds court in the Red Keep
Janos Slynt is made Lord of Harrenhal as a reward for his loyal service
Cersei dismisses Ser Barristan Selmy as head of the Kingsguard, citing honorable retirement
Selmy expresses confusion, pointing out Kingsguard serve for life
Joffrey angrily says Selmy is too old and failed to protect King Robert Baratheon
Selmy learns his post as Lord Commander is to be given to Jaime Lannister, and is disgusted
Varys announces Selmy is to be given a castle and land in recognition of his service
Selmy, insulted, takes off his armor, throws his sword at Joffrey's feet, and storms out, stating he could still kill all five other Kingsguard present
Sansa begs Joffrey for her father's life
Sansa claims the medicine Eddard was taking for his injured leg was responsible for his treasonous talk
Joffrey says Sansa's sweet words have moved him
Joffrey says he will spare Eddard if Eddard bends the knee and acknowledges him as king
Sansa says Eddard will do it

Baelor.action: Robb's army reaches the Twins, the heavily fortified crossing over the Green Fork of Trident
Theon Greyjoy shoots down messenger ravens to prevent word of their presence from being relayed
Theon finds nothing more than messages to extended family members
Catelyn enters the castle to negotiate their crossing the river, as entering would be perilous for Robb
House Frey is held by Walder Frey, bannermen to Catelyn Stark's father
Walder Frey does not take up arms immediately
Walder Frey claims he was preparing to march and waiting for his forces to arrive
Walder Frey actually waits to see which side is winning before getting involved
Tully's army is defeated and Riverrun is held by the Lannisters
Walder Frey questions why he should help Robb and take up arms against the king
Catelyn and Walder bargain hard
Catelyn and Walder strike a deal: the Freys will join Robb, allow him to cross, and commit troops, in exchange for Robb and Arya marrying Frey children
Robb agrees to the deal, noting Arya won't like it
Robb must also take Olyvar Frey as a squire
The Lannister armies prepare for battle
Tywin tells Tyrion he will use the hill tribe forces as cannon fodder against the Stark army
Tywin orders Tyrion to lead the hill tribes from the front of the vanguard
Tyrion angrily accuses Tywin of trying to kill him
Tyrion storms back to his tent
Tyrion finds that Bronn has found him a whore named Shae, as instructed
Tyrion takes an immediate liking to Shae
Bronn is told they go into battle in the vanguard in the morning
Bronn goes off to find a whore for himself
Tyrion, Bronn, and Shae play a drinking game the night before the battle
Tyrion makes observations and guesses about Bronn and Shae
Tyrion guesses Bronn has been beyond the Wall and killed someone before he was twelve
Bronn confirms Tyrion's guess, explaining he killed a woman in self-defense
Tyrion guesses Shae's mother was a whore, her father deserted them, and she is low born
Tyrion is wrong about Shae's background
Tyrion cannot read Shae's personality and finds her more intriguing
Shae starts to question Tyrion
Bronn reveals he heard Tyrion was once married
Tyrion reluctantly explains his past marriage
Tyrion and Jaime find a young common girl in distress from bandits near Casterly Rock
Jaime chases the bandits off
Tyrion comforts the girl, Tysha
Tyrion and Tysha get drunk at a local inn
Tysha becomes enamored of Tyrion
Tyrion has sex with Tysha, his first time with a woman
Tyrion falls madly in love with Tysha
Tyrion bribes a drunken septon to marry them in secret
Tywin finds out that his son married a commoner and becomes furious
Tywin forces Jaime to reveal the truth: the girl was a prostitute
Jaime sets up the situation to do something nice for Tyrion
Tywin instructs his guards to have sex with the girl, paying her a silver coin for each man, while Tyrion is forced to watch
Shae points out that Tyrion was foolish to believe a woman would sleep with him just hours after almost being raped
Shae gets romantic with Tyrion
Bronn leaves the tent
The Lannister army discovers that the Starks have stolen a march on them and are about to attack
The Lannister army hastily prepares for battle
Tyrion gives a rousing speech to his hill tribesmen
The hill tribesmen charge for the front
Tyrion is knocked out by an accidental blow to the head from someone's war hammer
Tyrion is nearly trampled
Tyrion regains consciousness on the back of a wagon
Bronn tells Tyrion that the Lannisters won the battle, but it was a feint
Robb sends a small portion of his army to confront and delay Tywin's forces
Robb's main army marches on Riverrun to attack Jaime's army
Catelyn and Ser Rodrik Cassel wait in the woodlands and hear sounds of battle
Robb returns from his victory with his guards and a prisoner: Ser Jaime
Robb’s victory at the Battle of Whispering Wood lifts the siege of Riverrun and destroys Jaime’s army
Jaime suggests ending the war with single combat between himself and Robb
Robb refuses Jaime’s offer and has him imprisoned
Robb laments that his diversion sent 2,000 men to their deaths
Robb gives a speech to his army, telling them they have won a great victory but the war is far from over
Jeor Mormont gives Jon Snow the Valyrian steel sword, Longclaw, in thanks for saving his life
Jeor Mormont reinstates Jon to his former position
Jeor Mormont tells Jon he has dispatched Ser Alliser Thorne to King's Landing
Jeor Mormont instructs Ser Alliser Thorne to lay the hand of the wight at the feet of the King and ask for aid
Mormont explains to Jon that Longclaw was meant for his own son, Ser Jorah Mormont, but Ser Jorah disgraced himself and fled to Essos
Jon goes to the dining hall
The other recruits make a fuss over Jon's sword
Samwell Tarly seems distant from the revelry
Sam takes Jon aside and informs him about Robb leading an army south to war
Jon ponders that he should be with Robb
Maester Aemon tells Jon their duty to the Night's Watch comes first
Aemon reveals his full name is Aemon Targaryen, son of a king, brother to another, uncle of the Mad King
Aemon recounts that he had to stand aside and do nothing during Robert's Rebellion, trembling with rage
Aemon remarks that he cannot make Jon stay or go
Aemon tells Jon he must make the choice himself and live with the consequences
Daenerys is concerned that Khal Drogo's wound has festered and become infected
Khal Drogo falls from his horse, showing weakness among the Dothraki
Daenerys tells the khalasar that they are stopping
Daenerys orders Qotho to get Mirri Maz Duur to help Drogo
Qotho is unhappy with entrusting Drogo's care to Mirri Maz Duur, whom he calls a maegi or witch
Qotho relents and follows Daenerys's order
Ser Jorah armors himself at Daenerys' request
