Jorah advises Daenerys to leave because Drogo is certain to die and his lieutenants will fight to be his successor
Jorah warns that whoever wins will kill Daenerys's son to prevent a rival
Daenerys refuses to abandon her husband
Mirri Maz Duur promises to save Drogo's life in exchange for a sacrifice
Mirri Maz Duur takes Drogo's stallion into his tent and starts the ceremony
Mirri Maz Duur slits the stallion's throat over Drogo's comatose form
loud growls are heard as a supernatural ritual begins
Some of Drogo's warriors become enraged at Daenerys' attempts to interfere with the natural way of things
Qotho tries to attack Daenerys
Ser Jorah kills Qotho
Daenerys starts to go into labor
Dothraki midwives refuse to help Daenerys, thinking she is cursed
Jorah takes Daenerys into the tent as the ceremony continues
Varys visits Eddard Stark in the dungeons under the Red Keep
Varys tells Eddard that Sansa begged for his life before the whole court
Varys tells Eddard that Robb is leading an army from the North
Varys informs Eddard that the Queen is concerned about Stannis's intentions
Varys urges Eddard to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Varys explains that, in return, Cersei will let Eddard take the black and join Benjen and Jon on the Wall
Eddard is angered by the suggestion that he trade his honesty and honor for his life
Varys insinuates that Sansa would pay the price for Eddard's defiance
Eddard appears to have second thoughts
Arya lives rough on the streets of the city
Arya catches a pigeon
Arya tries to trade the pigeon for a slice of bread, but fails
Arya sees crowds moving through the streets
Other tells Arya that the Hand of the King is being brought before the Great Sept of Baelor
Arya, stunned that she will see her father, drops the pigeon and joins the crowd
Arya, unable to see, climbs onto the pedestal of a statue of Baelor
Arya sees her father being brought in chains to the steps of the temple
Eddard sees Arya on the statue
Eddard is dragged through the crowd and passes Yoren of the Night’s Watch
Eddard looks at the statue and says, "Baelor," puzzling the crowd
Yoren understands Eddard’s message and moves toward the statue where Arya is watching
Eddard is given a chance to confess his "crimes"
Eddard sees Arya and Sansa present
Eddard chooses to save Arya and Sansa by making a false confession
Eddard says that Joffrey is the true King on the Iron Throne, pleasing Joffrey
Grand Maester Pycelle extols the virtues of mercy
Joffrey considers being merciful and sending Eddard to the Night's Watch as his mother suggested and as Sansa begged
Joffrey acknowledges that as women, Cersei and Sansa are "soft" and says treason must never go unpunished
Joffrey commands Ser Ilyn Payne to bring him the traitor's head
The crowd goes into an uproar
Arya tries to make her way through the crowd, drawing her sword to save her father
Yoren grabs and stops Arya, shielding her from seeing her father's death
Sansa screams and is restrained by guards as she faints
Cersei frantically tries to persuade her son to reconsider
Varys tries to persuade Cersei's son to reconsider
Eddard is forced to kneel, looks at the statue, and sees Arya has been rescued by Yoren
Ser Ilyn draws Eddard's sword, Ice, and cuts Eddard's head off with a single blow
A flock of pigeons takes flight immediately afterwards
Arya sees the pigeons and knows her father is dead

Fire_and_Blood.action: Eddard Stark is executed by beheading at the Great Sept of Baelor
The great sword Ice is covered in blood
Sandor Clegane holds Eddard's head up to the adulation of the baying crowd
Ser Ilyn Payne stands next to Sandor Clegane
Sansa Stark faints
Yoren drags Arya away in the crowd, calling her "Boy"
Yoren gets Arya out of the courtyard
Yoren cuts Arya's hair short with a knife
Yoren tells Arya he will get her out of King's Landing and to Winterfell as a recruit of the Night's Watch
Yoren warns Arya not to trust the other recruits, explaining the dangers she faces
Joffrey holds court
Marillion is brought before Joffrey, charged with making up an offensive song about Robert and Cersei
The song claims the boar killed King Robert, but the lion in his bed had previously castrated him
Marillion is revealed to be the singer who accompanied Catelyn and Tyrion to the Vale
Joffrey asks Marillion if he wants to keep his hands or tongue
Marillion fearfully responds that everyone needs hands
Joffrey commands Ilyn Payne to cut out Marillion’s tongue
Joffrey takes Sansa up to the walls to see Eddard’s head mounted on a spike and makes her look at it
Joffrey cruelly mocks Sansa, promising to give her Robb’s head next
Sansa quietly retorts that Robb might give her Joffrey’s head instead
Joffrey, infuriated, orders Ser Meryn Trant to beat Sansa, as Cersei has told him a king should not hit his queen
Sansa moves up behind Joffrey, apparently preparing to shove him off the walkway
Sandor Clegane dabs the blood off Sansa’s lip with a handkerchief
After Joffrey leaves, Sandor tells Sansa to give Joffrey what he wants
Cersei takes a new lover, her cousin Lancel
Cersei receives a letter informing her of Jaime's capture
Lancel is naked in Cersei's bedroom
Lancel feels excited about being at war, having just been made a knight
Lancel asks Cersei for news of the war and their next move
Cersei tells Lancel to shut up and get back in bed
Pycelle takes Ros to bed
Pycelle boasts about the several great kings he has served
Pycelle laments how Aerys Targaryen devolved from a charming young man into a paranoid lunatic obsessed with fire
Pycelle comments that Robert Baratheon was a great soldier but perhaps not an ideal king
Pycelle displays sycophantic loyalty to Joffrey
Ros leaves Pycelle's chamber
Pycelle stands and limbers up, revealing he is not as frail as he lets on
Pycelle resumes his act after dressing and leaving his chamber
Littlefinger and Varys speak in front of the Iron Throne
Varys asks Littlefinger how he envisions himself sitting on the Iron Throne
Littlefinger flips the question and asks Varys the same
Varys says he must be one of the few people in the city who doesn't want to be king
Littlefinger quips that Varys is one of the few men in the city who isn't a man, referring to him being a eunuch
Littlefinger asks if they took both the "pillar" and the "stones"
Joffrey arrives and asks if they are ready to begin a small council meeting
Yoren assembles a band of new recruits for the Night's Watch
Yoren has Arya join the group, pretending she is a boy called "Arry"
Hot Pie, Lommy Greenhands, and Gendry join the group
Gendry, King Robert's bastard son, is fired by Tobho Mott after Robert's death and told to join the Night's Watch
Hot Pie and Lommy try to bully Arya into giving up Needle
Arya angrily draws Needle and warns she has already killed one fat boy
Gendry breaks up the argument by taking Arya's side and threatening the boys with violence
Yoren prepares to lead the group to the Wall through the war-torn Riverlands
The group sets out for the Wall
Bran has another dream of the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran wakes up and tells Osha about his dream
Bran tells Osha he thinks his father is in the tombs
Osha takes Bran down to the crypts under the castle
Bran tells Osha about the histories of some ancestors entombed there
Shaggydog leaps out at them from the empty future tomb of Eddard Stark
Rickon calls Shaggydog off
Rickon reveals to Bran that he had the same dream
Osha tries to tell Bran it could be a coincidence as they make their way out
They see Maester Luwin outside, holding a letter and looking at them with sadness
Catelyn stoically walks through the encampment
northern bannermen bow to Catelyn as she passes and offer condolences and respect
Catelyn makes her way into the woods around the camp
Catelyn breaks down and hyperventilates with grief once out of sight
Catelyn hears something from deeper in the woods
Robb hacks his sword at a tree in anger and grief
Catelyn calls to Robb and tells him he is ruining his sword
Robb drops the sword and falls into Catelyn’s arms
Robb vows to kill the Lannisters who murdered his father
Catelyn comforts Robb and promises him revenge, but only after they rescue Sansa and Arya first
Robb and his bannermen debate whether to cooperate with Stannis or Renly Baratheon in the war against King Joffrey
Greatjon Umber questions why the northerners should be told what to do by rulers in the south
Greatjon Umber reminds the group that the North was independent before the Targaryens threatened them with dragons
Greatjon Umber declares there is only one king worthy of his respect and allegiance
Greatjon Umber bows before Robb and calls out, "The King in the North!"
The other northern bannermen take up the cry and bow before Robb, swearing fealty
Catelyn visits the captive Jaime Lannister
Jaime taunts Catelyn about Ned's death
Jaime offers to serve Catelyn as a lover
Catelyn hits Jaime in the head with a rock
Jaime says Catelyn can kill him if she keeps hitting him and claims he doesn't fear death
Jaime realizes the Starks won't kill him as long as Cersei holds Sansa and Arya hostage
Catelyn tells Jaime he has violated the laws of man and gods
Jaime asks where Ned's gods were when his head was cut off and questions the existence of all gods
Catelyn says there is injustice in the world because of men like Jaime
Jaime responds, "There are no men like me, only me"
Jaime admits to pushing Bran from the tower, but doesn't reveal why
Tywin holds a strategy meeting with his generals at the Lannister camp
Tywin is furious that Jaime has been captured by the Starks in the Battle of Whispering Wood
Kevan describes the war as a "catastrophe"
Kevan and other commanders debate whether to attack, fall back and raise reinforcements, or sue for peace
Tyrion points out that any chance of negotiating with Robb Stark died when Eddard was executed
Tywin decides to retreat to Harrenhal and use it as a base of operations
Tywin orders Tyrion to go to King's Landing and serve as Acting Hand of the King
Tywin instructs Tyrion to control Cersei and Joffrey, prevent mistakes, and kill Baelish, Varys, and Pycelle if he sees treachery
Tywin orders Tyrion to leave his whore behind
Tyrion complains about his father to Shae in his tent
Tyrion decides to defy Tywin and take Shae to court anyway
Jon learns of his father's death
Jon saddles a horse and prepares to leave to join Robb's army
Jon ignores Samwell's pleas to stay and fulfill his oaths
Jon leaves Castle Black
Jon is pursued by others
One of Jon's pursuers hits a tree branch and is unhorsed
Jon realizes the unhorsed pursuer is Sam
Jon turns back to help Sam
The other riders, Grenn and Pypar, catch up
Jon tells Grenn and Pypar to go back or they will get in trouble
Grenn and Pypar refuse to go back without Jon
Grenn, Pypar, and Sam surround Jon and recite their oath to the Night's Watch
Jon realizes they are right about his duty
Jon reflects that leaving would make him a deserter and an oathbreaker, and that his father would not approve
Jon considers that Robb might not accept his help or might turn him in
Jon agrees to return to the Wall with them
Jeor Mormont decides to overlook Jon's brief desertion
Jeor Mormont says many have fled the Wall, only to return
Jeor Mormont states that if they executed everyone who deserted, they'd have no men left
Jeor Mormont tells Jon that the war between the Lannisters and Starks is less important than the current threat
Jeor Mormont explains the renewed threat from wildlings, White Walkers, and wights
Jeor Mormont announces his intention to lead the Night's Watch into the Haunted Forest
Jeor Mormont states the goals: to learn more of their foe, prepare for battle, and find Benjen Stark
Jon agrees to commit himself fully to the Night's Watch and all that lies ahead
Daenerys wakes up to terrible news from Ser Jorah Mormont
Ser Jorah tells Daenerys that her son, Rhaego, is dead and was born deformed, covered with scales
The majority of the khalasar move on, leaving Daenerys and her group behind
Drogo remains alive
Daenerys insists on seeing her husband
Daenerys finds Drogo in a catatonic state
Daenerys demands to know why Drogo is in this state
Mirri Maz Duur explains her revenge and the events that happened to her after the Dothraki sacked her village
Mirri Maz Duur reveals she has taken revenge on Drogo and Daenerys' child, Rhaego
Mirri Maz Duur says Daenerys saved her life, but questions what life is worth when all else is gone
It is night
Daenerys tends to Drogo in their tent
Daenerys bathes Drogo
Daenerys talks to Drogo
Daenerys tries to seduce Drogo
Daenerys realizes Drogo is no longer there
Daenerys is heartbroken seeing Drogo in his vegetative state
Daenerys cries
Daenerys kisses Drogo goodbye
Daenerys smothers Drogo with a pillow
Daenerys and her small retinue prepare a funeral pyre for Drogo
Daenerys makes a speech to those who are left, offering them the choice to leave or stay and follow her
Daenerys frees the slaves among her followers and asks them to stay as equals
The maegi smirks as some people begin walking away
At Daenerys' command, Rakharo places the dragon eggs on the pyre
Mirri Maz Duur is lashed to a pillar of the pyre
Mirri Maz Duur tells Daenerys she will not scream while being burned alive
Daenerys replies that she only wants the maegi's life
Ser Jorah pleads with Daenerys to reconsider, believing she intends to kill herself, and suggests they sell the eggs and leave
Daenerys ignores Jorah's pleas, kisses his cheek, and sets the pyre alight
Mirri Maz Duur screams as she burns
Daenerys calmly walks into the flames
Daenerys stands below the pyre as flames appear to consume her
The fire dies down at dawn
Jorah and the rest of the khalasar awake to find Daenerys sitting among the still smoldering embers with three newly-hatched baby dragons crawling over her
Daenerys sits among the embers, unharmed, with her clothes burnt away and her body covered in soot
One dragon nuzzles in Daenerys's arm
Another dragon climbs Daenerys's leg
The third dragon pulls itself up to sit on Daenerys's shoulder
Jorah and the few remaining Dothraki kneel and swear allegiance to Daenerys
Daenerys slowly stands up
The black hatchling on Daenerys's shoulder rises, spreads its wings, and screams, announcing the return of dragons to the world

The_North_Remembers.action: Jeor Mormont's rangers arrive at Craster's Keep after passing several abandoned wildling villages
Jon, Grenn, Eddison Tollett, and Samwell Tarly are repulsed by Craster and his many wives/daughters
Craster tells Jeor he has not seen Benjen Stark and reports wildlings are assembling for Mance Rayder
Jon challenges Craster, who is low and crude but demanding and demeaning to Commander Mormont
Commander Mormont ignores Craster's insulting behavior
Craster refers to the Night's Watch as southerners
Jon argues with Craster
Jeor reprimands Jon, noting that the Night's Watch needs Craster
Jeor reminds Jon that Craster's information and shelter have helped them before
Jeor asks Jon if he wants to lead someday and tells him he must first learn how to follow
King Joffrey holds a tournament to celebrate his nameday on the walls of the Red Keep
Joffrey is irritated by Ser Dontos Hollard, who arrives late and intoxicated for his bout
Joffrey orders his guards to pour an entire barrel of wine down Dontos' throat with a funnel
Sansa intercedes, telling Joffrey that ordering a death on one's name day is bad luck
Joffrey dismisses Sansa's warning as a mindless superstition
Sandor Clegane seconds Sansa's warning
Sansa convinces Joffrey to make Dontos his court fool instead
Dontos is grateful to Sansa
Tyrion arrives back in King's Landing, to Joffrey's disdain
Joffrey is disdainful of Tyrion's arrival
Tommen and Myrcella are delighted by Tyrion's return, having worried he was dead
The Small Council holds a session to discuss the Conclave's announcement that the long summer has ended
Cersei orders the city gates closed to refugees from the war in the Riverlands
Tyrion arrives with a letter from Lord Tywin, appointing him as acting Hand of the King
Cersei becomes livid at Tyrion's arrival and appointment
Cersei is suspicious that Tyrion is taking power for himself
Tyrion convinces Cersei that he is there to act as an advisor
Tyrion criticizes Cersei for failing to rein in Joffrey and for bad decisions, especially the execution of Ned Stark
Tyrion points out that their actions have sparked a war, which their side is currently losing
Tyrion is incredulous when he learns Arya Stark escaped
Tyrion points out their position would be stronger with three Stark hostages
Tyrion hides his lover Shae in his chambers in the Tower of the Hand
Tywin forbids Tyrion from bringing Shae with him
Shae is initially thrilled at being brought to the capital and says big cities make her "want to fuck"
Cersei tasks Petyr Baelish with finding Arya
Cersei needles Petyr about his unrequited love for Catelyn
Petyr counters by referencing Cersei's relationship with Jaime
Petyr tells Cersei that knowledge is power
Cersei threatens Petyr's life by demonstrating her control over her guards
Cersei orders the guards to seize Baelish and cut his throat
Cersei immediately rescinds the order
Cersei warns Petyr that power is power
Joffrey redecorates his throne room, hoping to restore the intimidating grandeur it held under the Targaryens
Joffrey refuses his mother's suggestion to ask Tywin Lannister for aid in the search for Arya
Joffrey does not feel it is necessary to find Arya
Joffrey does not feel it is fitting for a king to ask for help
Joffrey blames Tywin for their losses in the war and the capture of Jaime
Joffrey confronts his mother after hearing rumors about his parentage
Cersei dismisses the rumors as gossip spread by Joffrey's enemies
Joffrey questions Cersei about his father's bastard children
Joffrey insults Cersei by saying Robert had grown tired of her
Cersei slaps Joffrey
Joffrey warns Cersei never to hit him again under threat of death
Lord Janos Slynt leads a city-wide massacre of Robert's bastards
The infant Barra is killed in Petyr's brothel in front of her mother and the prostitute Ros
Tobho Mott is interrogated
Tobho Mott reveals that Gendry has left the city as a recruit of the Night's Watch
Tobho Mott says that Gendry can be identified by his bull's head helm
Gendry and Arya travel north on the Kingsroad with Yoren's recruits
Bran suffers through the petty complaints of his vassals, facing unrest at Robb calling his banners and taking the young men south
Maester Luwin guides Bran in carrying out a lord's duties
Bran dreams that he is his direwolf Summer walking in the Godswood
Bran looks in the pool and sees Summer's reflection rather than his own, which wakes him
Bran has Osha and Hodor take him to the pool
Bran is reassured to find that his waking reflection is his own
Bran, Osha, and Hodor discuss the phenomenon of the red comet
Osha tells Bran she believes the comet signifies the return of dragons to the world
Stannis allows Melisandre to burn statues of the gods of the Faith of the Seven as an offering to the Lord of Light
Melisandre burns statues of the gods of the Faith of the Seven
Maester Cressen is outraged at the affront to his gods and fearful for Stannis
Melisandre proclaims Stannis a champion of the Lord of Light
Stannis draws a burning sword from one of the statues
Cressen asks Davos Seaworth to help him intervene
Davos says it is not the right time to intervene
Stannis receives Ned Stark's letter, informing him he is the true heir of King Robert Baratheon
Stannis sends copies of a letter announcing his claim to the throne to Lords throughout the realm
Stannis details the incestuous relationship between Cersei and Jaime Lannister and challenges Joffrey's legitimacy
Stannis rejects Davos's appeal to consider an alliance with Renly Baratheon
Renly claims the throne for himself, despite having widespread support
Stannis rejects an alliance with Robb Stark, seeing Robb as "stealing the Northern half of his kingdom"
Cressen feigns an apology to trick Melisandre into sharing a cup of poisoned wine
Davos sees Cressen drop poison in the cup and tries to stop him
Cressen drinks first and hands the cup to Melisandre
Melisandre, aware of the poison, smiles and drinks anyway, unaffected
Cressen dies in vain
Robb receives the letter
Robb interrogates captive Jaime about his reasons for crippling Bran
Jaime reveals nothing
Jaime attacks Robb's decision to keep him in his mobile camp, suggesting it shows a lack of trust
Robb asserts his faith in his men and expresses concern about House Lannister's efforts to free Jaime
Grey Wind, Robb's direwolf, menaces Jaime during the questioning
Robb sends his prisoner Alton Lannister to King's Landing with peace terms
Robb demands the release of his sisters, the return of his father's remains and those who served him, and recognition of the North as a free and independent kingdom
Theon Greyjoy appeals to Robb to send him to Pyke to seek an alliance with his father Balon, citing the need for a fleet
Catelyn counsels against trusting Balon because of his history of rebellion
Catelyn asks Robb to consider exchanging Jaime for his sisters
Catelyn asks to return to her younger sons at Winterfell
Robb tasks Catelyn with seeking an alliance with Renly
Robb sends Ser Rodrik Cassel to Winterfell instead
Daenerys leads her diminished khalasar east into the Red Waste to avoid her rivals
Daenerys is surrounded by powerful enemies
Ser Jorah tells Daenerys her enemies will kill her and take her dragons
The deadly emptiness of the Red Waste is Daenerys's only option
Daenerys struggles to make her dragons eat what little meat she has
Daenerys's people face starvation
Daenerys's beloved white mare dies
Daenerys halts the march
Daenerys sends three of her bloodriders in three different directions to scout for sustenance and refuge

The_Night_Lands.action: Arya continues to pose as a boy while traveling north with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Arya urinates privately to conceal that she is a girl
Arya is drawn to the caged prisoners containing Jaqen H'ghar, Rorge, and Biter
Jaqen asks Arya for water and introduces himself
A pair of Gold Cloaks approach the party
Arya hides, believing the Gold Cloaks have come for her
Gold Cloaks deliver a warrant for Gendry
Yoren intimidates the Gold Cloaks into leaving empty-handed
Gold Cloaks promise to return with more men
Arya asks Gendry why he is wanted
Gendry admits that he does not know why he is wanted
Gendry tells Arya that he was questioned by Lord Jon Arryn and Lord Eddard Stark before fleeing King's Landing
Gendry questions why Arya thought they had come for her
Gendry tells Arya that he has seen through her disguise
Arya reveals her identity to Gendry
Arya swears Gendry to secrecy
Varys discovers Shae living with Tyrion in the Tower of the Hand and makes implied threats about revealing her presence
Tyrion warns Varys not to underestimate him
Alton Lannister delivers King Robb Stark's peace terms to the Small Council
Cersei rejects Robb Stark's peace terms
Grand Maester Pycelle relays a message from the Night's Watch requesting manpower and warning of wights
Tyrion is the only one to take the Night's Watch warning seriously
Ros struggles to continue her work as a prostitute after witnessing the murder of Mhaegen's infant daughter Barra
Petyr Baelish threatens Ros with a tale about selling an unhappy girl to a depraved customer to recoup his investment
Petyr Baelish orders Ros to return happy after a night off
Tyrion has dinner with Lord Janos Slynt and questions him about the massacre of the royal bastards
Janos admits his involvement in the massacre but refuses to reveal who ordered it
Tyrion has Janos arrested and exiled to the Night's Watch
Tyrion installs Bronn as Commander of the City Watch in Janos's place
Cersei confronts Tyrion about his actions
Tyrion warns Cersei that she is losing the support of the people
Cersei claims that she does not care about the people's support
Tyrion concludes that Joffrey ordered the massacre, having initially suspected Cersei
Tyrion jokes about Cersei's relationship with Jaime
Cersei says that Tyrion has always been funny
Cersei ends the discussion by saying Tyrion's finest joke was killing their mother when he was born
Davos recruits the Lysene sellsail Salladhor Saan and his fleet of thirty ships to King Stannis Baratheon's cause
Matthos Seaworth encourages Davos to learn to read so he can study the holy texts of the Lord of Light
Davos is happy to put his faith solely in Stannis
Melisandre seduces Stannis with the promise of a son
Stannis hesitates, explaining he has a wife
Melisandre successfully seduces Stannis
Theon Greyjoy travels to the Iron Islands on a merchant ship
Theon has sex with the Captain's daughter
Theon tells the Captain's daughter he is expecting a warm reception
Theon is crushed when no one meets him in Lordsport
A woman offers Theon a ride to Pyke
Theon attempts to seduce the woman during the journey
Theon is disappointed when his father Balon questions his loyalty and memory of his roots
Theon delivers Robb Stark's letter suggesting an alliance against House Lannister
Robb offers to make Balon King of the Iron Islands again in exchange for the alliance
Theon's guide enters and Theon realizes she is his sister Yara when Balon greets her warmly
Balon rejects Robb's proposal and compares Theon unfavorably to Yara
Balon insists he will follow the Ironborn tradition of paying the iron price
Balon says no one will give him his crown
Theon warns Balon he will not defeat the Lannisters
Balon retorts that he has no intention of fighting the Lannisters
Daenerys and her khalasar lie exhausted in the heat, their water dwindling as they wait for the return of her bloodriders
Rakharo's horse returns riderless
Ser Jorah Mormont discovers Rakharo's head and severed braid in the saddle bag
Ser Jorah surmises that Rakharo was caught and killed by a rival khalasar
Irri is devastated by Rakharo's death and believes the mutilation will prevent him from entering the Night Lands
Daenerys promises to hold a funeral for Rakharo
Samwell Tarly rescues Gilly from Ghost when Ghost bothers her for food
Gilly confides her desire to flee from the keep because she fears what Craster will do if her unborn child is a boy
Sam takes Gilly to Jon
Gilly begs Jon to take her with them on the ranging but will not say what she is afraid of
Jon tells Gilly it is not possible for her to come with them because they are going deeper into wildling country
Gilly leaves
Sam and Jon argue about the impracticality of taking Gilly with them
Jon notices Craster taking a baby into the woods at night
Jon follows Craster
Jon sees Craster leave the child to the White Walkers
Jon goes to the child
Craster surprises Jon and knocks him out before he can intervene

What_Is_Dead_May_Never_Die.action: Craster drags his captive Jon Snow back to his keep
Craster beats Jon bloody for spying on him
Craster orders the men of the Night's Watch out of his home, blaming Jon
Jeor Mormont questions Jon about his actions
Jon realizes Jeor has known about Craster sacrificing his sons
Jeor considers Craster too valuable a contact to risk offending
Jeor is disgusted by Craster's human sacrifices and incestuous relationships
Jeor forbids Jon from interfering with Craster
Jon reveals seeing something take the child
Jeor predicts Jon will see whatever it was again
Samwell Tarly gives Gilly a thimble that once belonged to his mother
Samwell Tarly promises to come back for Gilly when they make the return journey to Castle Black
Bran experiences strange dreams where he sees the world from the perspective of his direwolf Summer
Bran, as Summer, follows Hodor into his own bedroom and looks down on himself sleeping
Bran confides in Maester Luwin about his dreams
Maester Luwin is dismissive of the significance of Bran's dreams
Catelyn arrives in the camp of King Renly Baratheon during a tournament
Catelyn watches Brienne of Tarth defeat Ser Loras Tyrell and claim a place in Renly's kingsguard
Brienne of Tarth defeats Ser Loras Tyrell and claims a place in Renly's kingsguard
Renly introduces Catelyn to his new wife, Margaery Tyrell
Renly shows off his 100,000 strong army
Catelyn warns Renly to take the war seriously
Renly tries to initiate sex with his lover Loras but is rebuffed
Loras reminds Renly of the importance of fathering an heir
Loras goes to fetch his sister Queen Margaery
Margaery attempts to seduce Renly but fails
Margaery is pragmatic about Renly's homosexuality
Margaery suggests including her brother Loras in their lovemaking
Margaery asserts the importance of producing an heir
Theon is included in his father Balon's war council
Balon reveals his plan to attack the lightly defended North while their armies are fighting House Lannister
Theon is assigned a single ship to raid the fishing villages of the Stony Shore
Yara is put in command of thirty ships and told to take Deepwood Motte
Theon fumes at his assignment
Theon implores his father to reconsider the plan
Theon claims that attacking the North risks total destruction if they lose
Theon argues that allying with the Starks could make Balon King of the Iron Islands and win Casterly Rock
Balon rebuffs Theon and chastises him for becoming loyal to the Starks
Theon challenges Balon's lack of faith and reminds him he was given up as a ward
Balon hits Theon and storms out
Theon shouts at Balon from behind
Theon considers warning Robb about Balon's actions
Theon reaffirms his loyalty to his family by being baptised by a Drowned Man
Shae complains to Tyrion about being confined to her chambers
Tyrion liaises with Varys to find Shae a job that will give her some freedom
Cersei hosts an awkward dinner for her children and captive Sansa Stark
Sansa maintains her façade of loyalty to her captors
Shae reports as Sansa's new handmaiden
Sansa vents some frustration about Shae's inexperience
Tyrion tests the loyalty of the Small Council by feeding each member a different plan for a marriage alliance involving Princess Myrcella Baratheon
Tyrion uncovers Pycelle as an informant to Cersei when Cersei confronts him about the plan he fed to Pycelle
Cersei confronts Tyrion about the plan involving Myrcella
Tyrion insists on pursuing the exposed plan to marry Myrcella into House Martell of Dorne over Cersei's protests
Cersei protests Tyrion's plan
Tyrion has Bronn arrest Pycelle for his treachery
Bronn arrests Pycelle
Petyr Baelish is furious about being involved in Tyrion's deception
Tyrion placates Petyr Baelish by offering him an opportunity to act as an envoy to Renly and Catelyn
Arya struggles with insomnia
Yoren gives Arya advice about coping with her painful memories
Yoren tells Arya to focus on retribution
Ser Amory Lorch arrives with Lannister men and Gold Cloaks
Yoren marshals the recruits for a battle
Yoren confronts Ser Amory and refuses to surrender Gendry
Yoren dies fighting bravely
The recruits are overcome in battle
Arya rescues Jaqen H'ghar from a fire near the prisoner wagon
Lommy Greenhands picks up Gendry's helmet and is wounded by a crossbow bolt
Arya is incapacitated by Polliver, who takes Needle from her
Gendry and Hot Pie are captured
Lommy begs Polliver to carry him
Polliver stabs Lommy in the throat with Needle
Arya convinces her captors that Lommy was Gendry because he was carrying the helmet
Ser Amory orders the prisoners be taken to Harrenhal

Garden_of_Bones_(episode).action: Robb leads a surprise night-time assault against an encamped Lannister army
The Lannister army is manned by Rennick and another Lannister guardsman
Grey Wind causes considerable damage during the assault
Robb wins the Battle of Oxcross, fulfilling his promise to give Cersei "another Whispering Wood"
Robb surveys the battlefield the next day
Silent Sisters tend to both Stark and Lannister wounded
Roose Bolton argues that they should kill all Lannister prisoners due to supply issues
Robb disagrees, insisting they fight honorably and follow the laws of war
Bolton suggests torturing Lannister officers for information
Robb refuses, not wanting to give the Lannisters an excuse to torture Sansa and Arya
Robb sees a woman battlefield healer treating a wounded Lannister soldier
a woman battlefield healer treats a wounded Lannister soldier
the wounded Lannister soldier protests when told his leg must be amputated
Robb helps hold the soldier down as the healer saws off his leg
the woman introduces herself as Talisa of Volantis
Talisa criticizes Robb for the need for this war
Robb says he doesn't want the Iron Throne, just revenge on Joffrey for his father's death
Talisa is shocked to learn Robb doesn't care which king takes the throne after Joffrey is defeated
Robb says he just wants the north to be left alone afterward
Talisa points out that Robb hasn't hurt the ones who killed his father, Joffrey and Tywin Lannister
Talisa says the men Robb's army slaughtered were unwilling boy soldiers and conscripts, not Tywin's main army
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie are taken by Ser Amory Lorch's soldiers to Harrenhal
Ser Amory Lorch's group transports prisoners to Harrenhal
The group is herded in with many other prisoners, mostly peasant refugees from the Riverlands
Ser Gregor Clegane commands the Lannister force at Harrenhal
Ser Gregor Clegane, previously declared an outlaw by Eddard Stark, now has the support of the Iron Throne
Eddard Stark previously declared Ser Gregor an outlaw for raiding in the Riverlands
Ser Gregor brutalizes the Rivermen with the support of the Iron Throne
Arya notices the stench of corpses as soon as they are in sight of the castle
Lannister soldiers under Ser Gregor, especially the Tickler, torture many prisoners
Lannister soldiers use a method of torture involving a rat in a bucket and a flame
Lannister soldiers torture prisoners to find out where they hid valuables
Lannister soldiers ask some prisoners about the Brotherhood
Gendry is about to be tortured
Lord Tywin Lannister arrives with the main host of his army at Harrenhal
The main Lannister army, led by Lord Tywin, settles in Harrenhal as their new base
Tywin orders an immediate halt to the tortures, saving Gendry
Tywin points out the tortures are disproportionate to the peasants' wealth and suggests putting them to work around the castle
Gendry is set to work as a blacksmith's apprentice
Tywin notices that "Arry" is really a girl dressed as a boy
Tywin criticizes his soldiers for not noticing Arya's disguise
Tywin asks Arya why she is dressed as a boy
Arya explains that it was safer to dress as a boy
Tywin is impressed with Arya's intelligence
Tywin takes Arya on as his new cupbearer while his army is based at the castle
King Joffrey becomes furious upon hearing of Robb Stark's victory against Lannister forces
Joffrey has Sansa brought before the assembled court in the throne room
The throne room is redecorated with iron spikes and roaring fires surrounding the pillars
Lancel Lannister tells Sansa and the nobles that Robb only won with trickery and sorcery
Joffrey menaces Sansa by brandishing a crossbow and toying with the thought of killing her in front of the throne
Sansa, though terrified, refuses to give Joffrey the satisfaction of scaring her and pledges her loyalty
Joffrey, annoyed, concedes to his mother's insistence that they need Sansa alive
Joffrey lowers the crossbow and orders Sansa to stand
Joffrey sits down on the Iron Throne
Joffrey commands Ser Meryn Trant to beat and strip Sansa as punishment, instructing him to leave her face untouched
Ser Meryn punches Sansa violently in the stomach
Ser Meryn strikes Sansa in the back of the legs with the flat of his sword, causing her to fall
Ser Meryn rips open the back of Sansa’s gown, leaving her partially naked from the waist up
Ser Meryn continues beating Sansa
The entire court watches in horror
Sandor Clegane is particularly disgusted
No one in the room intervenes out of fear of Joffrey’s wrath
Tyrion marches in and puts a stop to the spectacle
Tyrion orders someone to cover Sansa up
Sandor steps forward, takes off his cloak, and gently wraps it around Sansa
Tyrion turns his wrath upon Joffrey, berating his actions for shaming Sansa
Joffrey proclaims that he was punishing Sansa
Tyrion denounces Joffrey as a "halfwit" and points out the absurdity of punishing Sansa for her brother's actions
Joffrey bellows that as king, he can do whatever he pleases
Tyrion coldly counters that the Mad King thought the same, which led to his overthrow and death
Ser Meryn tries to support the king
Tyrion silences Ser Meryn by ordering Bronn to kill him if he opens his mouth again
Tyrion comforts the shaken Sansa
Tyrion personally escorts Sansa out of the hall
Tyrion asks Sansa whether she wants to end her engagement to the king
Sansa parrots the lines that she loves Joffrey and will uphold her duty
Tyrion is impressed and remarks that Sansa has learned how to survive around Joffrey and might make it out alive, respectfully calling her "Lady Stark"
Bronn suggests to Tyrion that Joffrey needs to have sex with a woman to release his frustrations
Tyrion agrees with Bronn's suggestion
Tyrion arranges for two prostitutes, Ros and Daisy, from Littlefinger's brothel to entertain Joffrey
Joffrey brings a loaded crossbow into the bedchamber instead of having sex with the prostitutes
Joffrey forces Ros at crossbow-point to beat Daisy unconscious
Joffrey forces Ros to dump Daisy's body in Tyrion's chambers
Joffrey sends a message to Tyrion warning him not to interfere with his handling of Sansa again
Tyrion works in his chamber late at night
Lancel Lannister knocks on Tyrion's door delivering orders from Cersei to release Grand Maester Pycelle
Lancel holds Tyrion in contempt and calls him "Imp"
Tyrion asks Lancel why he is so late delivering the message
Lancel retorts that he always delivers messages immediately and is never late
Tyrion points out that Lancel is coming from Cersei's bedchambers in the middle of the night
Lancel nervously claims Cersei keeps late hours working tirelessly
Tyrion notices Lancel reeks of Cersei's favorite lavender oil perfume
Tyrion enquires whether Lancel was made a knight before or after sharing Cersei's bed
Lancel admits Cersei's father ordered him to obey her every command as a squire
Tyrion sarcastically asks if Cersei forced Lancel to have sex with her
Tyrion wonders aloud what Joffrey would do if he found out about Lancel and Cersei
Lancel gets down on his knees and pleads for his life
Tyrion instructs Lancel to report everything Cersei does to him
Lancel agrees to Tyrion's terms
Tyrion agrees to free Pycelle if Cersei wants him as a "pet," but not back on the Small Council
Petyr "Littlefinger" Baelish arrives at King Renly Baratheon's camp as a political envoy from King Joffrey Baratheon
Littlefinger tries to gain Renly's trust by implying he will help take down the Lannisters from within if Renly marches on the capital
Renly is tempted by Littlefinger's offer, despite disliking him
Littlefinger has a conversation with Queen Margaery Tyrell, trying to intimidate her by hinting that her husband is homosexual
Margaery appears unfazed, indicating she already knows and does not care
Margaery demonstrates her political savvy and belief in Renly as the best candidate for king
Margaery reveals she voluntarily entered a paper marriage to secure the alliance between Renly and House Tyrell
Littlefinger meets with Catelyn Stark, who is furious at his betrayal of her husband
Littlefinger tries to kindle a romance with Catelyn, suggesting they could be together now
Catelyn draws a dagger and demands Littlefinger stop speaking
Littlefinger presents Tyrion's offer of a prisoner exchange: Jaime Lannister for Sansa and Arya Stark (lying about Arya)
Catelyn insists Robb would never allow the exchange
Littlefinger tries to manipulate Catelyn's love for her daughters
Littlefinger brings forward Silent Sisters carrying a box with Ned Stark's remains as a show of good faith from Tyrion
Catelyn grieves deeply and tells Littlefinger to get out
Catelyn looks at Ned's bones with pain, then shuts the box with resolve
Stannis Baratheon arrives at Renly's camp with Melisandre and Ser Davos Seaworth in his retinue
Stannis comes to treat with Renly and offers him a chance to relinquish his claim and serve Stannis
Renly, with Brienne and Ser Loras Tyrell, counters that no one wants Stannis to be king
Renly scoffs at Stannis's conversion to the religion of the Lord of Light
Catelyn criticizes both brothers for acting like sulking children
Stannis points out the oddity of Catelyn standing with Renly, given Ned Stark's support for Stannis
Stannis offers Renly his old seat on the Small Council and to name him heir if he relinquishes his claim
Catelyn reminds Stannis that their common enemy is the Lannisters
Stannis refuses to listen and asserts his right to the Iron Throne
Renly states that his 100,000 soldiers make him king and that Stannis is outnumbered
Stannis says they will see the next morning when their armies clash, then departs
Melisandre warns Renly, "Look to your sins Lord Renly, for the night is dark and full of terrors."
Renly watches Stannis leave and asks his companions if they can believe he once loved Stannis
King Stannis commands Ser Davos to row Melisandre to caves near Renly's camp
Ser Davos rows Melisandre to the caves
Ser Davos recalls knowing these caves from his old smuggling days
Melisandre asks Davos if he desires her and about his views on the conflict between right and wrong, insisting she is on the side of good
Ser Davos and Melisandre find the way through the caves blocked by metal bars
Melisandre takes off her cloak, revealing she is naked and heavily pregnant
Melisandre gives birth to a creature made of shadow
Ser Davos looks on in horror

The_Old_Gods_and_the_New.action: Maester Luwin frantically sends off a messenger raven

Joffrey impetuously orders his soldiers to kill the entire crowd
Riots erupt and spread throughout the city
The royal party's guards are overwhelmed
Joffrey, Cersei, and Tyrion barely make it back to the castle unscathed
Joffrey, Cersei, and Tyrion barely make it back to the castle unscathed
Joffrey, Cersei, and Tyrion barely make it back to the castle unscathed
The High Septon is torn apart by the mob

Brienne reveals her prowess as a fighter to Jaime
Jaime reminds Brienne that she killed Stark men
Brienne responds that she does not serve House Stark, but Catelyn Stark
Theon Greyjoy is besieged by a northern army under command of Roose Bolton's bastard son
Maester Luwin tells Theon his chances of holding the castle with only twenty men are negligible
Luwin suggests that Theon join the Night's Watch to redeem himself
Theon refuses Luwin's suggestion, fearing Jon Snow will kill him for his betrayal
Theon tries to rally his men for battle
Theon's men betray him
Dagmer knocks Theon out
Luwin tries to intervene
Dagmer stabs Luwin with a spear
The ironmen place a bag on Theon and depart Winterfell to deliver him to the Bolton army
Bran, Rickon, Osha, and Hodor emerge from their hiding place in Winterfell's crypts
Bran, Rickon, Osha, and Hodor find the castle burning
The group discovers the dying Luwin in the godswood
Luwin insists that Bran and Rickon must go north to the Wall
Luwin explains there are too many enemies in the south and Jon will keep them safe and tell their mother of their survival
Luwin affirms his pride at having served the Starks
Luwin asks Osha to give him a quick death
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie travel away from Harrenhal
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie cross paths with Jaqen H'ghar, who has been waiting for them
Jaqen offers to take Arya with him to Braavos to learn his skills of killing and find a way to kill those on her list
Arya declines Jaqen's offer, wanting to be reunited with her family
Jaqen gives Arya an iron coin and the words "Valar Morghulis," telling her to speak them to any man from Braavos if she needs to find him again
Jaqen bids Arya farewell, using her real name
Jaqen departs, changing his face to that of a different man as he leaves
Daenerys arrives at House of the Undying and is magically separated from Jorah and Kovarro
Daenerys finds herself in an empty circular room with many doors
Daenerys chooses one door and opens it
Daenerys is presented with tempting visions
Daenerys sees the snowy, ruined throne room of King's Landing and turns away from the Iron Throne
Daenerys walks through the gates of the Wall surrounded by snow
Daenerys enters Drogo's tent and finds Drogo and her infant son Rhaego
Daenerys and Drogo talk about whose dream they are in
Daenerys touches Rhaego's hair and silently leaves
Daenerys returns to the room with many doors
Daenerys finds her dragons chained to a pedestal in front of her
Pyat Pree appears and explains that Daenerys and her dragons are the source of his restored magic
Pyat Pree magically chains Daenerys
Daenerys regards Pyat Pree with a cool, almost lazy gaze
Daenerys utters "Dracarys"
All three dragons unleash their fiery breath after a couple of tries
Pyat Pree is incinerated by the dragons' fire
Daenerys realizes Xaro has betrayed her
Daenerys returns to Xaro's palace to confront him
Daenerys finds Doreah in bed with Xaro
Daenerys takes Xaro's key
Daenerys uses the key to open Xaro's Valyrian stone vault
Daenerys is surprised to find the vault empty
Daenerys observes that something can easily come from nothing
Daenerys orders Doreah and Xaro to be locked in the empty vault to die
Doreah and Xaro plead as the door closes, cutting off their pleas
Daenerys salvages enough from Xaro's household to buy a ship
On Jorah's orders, the Dothraki seize Xaro's gold and jewels
Daenerys triumphantly watches with her three dragons
Jon and Qhorin are marched towards the camp of Mance Rayder as captives of the Lord of Bones
Qhorin realizes he must sacrifice himself to help Jon's ruse of desertion
Qhorin stuns one of his captors and acquires a sword
Qhorin attacks Jon with the seeming intention of killing him for treachery
The Lord of Bones lets Jon and Qhorin fight
Ygritte gives Jon back his sword
Qhorin enrages Jon by insulting his parentage
Jon duels Qhorin fiercely
Jon mortally wounds Qhorin per Qhorin's instructions
Qhorin whispers "We are the watchers on the Wall" to Jon before dying
Qhorin signals to Jon that he has done the right thing and reminds him of his mission
The wildlings believe Jon has betrayed his oath and release him
The Lord of Bones cuts Jon free, impressed
Ygritte shows Jon the wildlings' vast army in a valley below
Ygritte promises to take Jon to their King
Jon follows Ygritte, having no other choice
Sam, Grenn, and Edd forage for fuel at the Fist of the First Men
Three horn blasts are heard, signaling White Walkers
Sam, Grenn, and Edd run back to the Fist
Sam is left behind, caught in a sudden blizzard
Shadowy figures move through the blowing snow
Sam hides behind a rock and watches as dozens of wights walk past him, led by a White Walker riding an undead horse-wight
Sam cowers in fear
The White Walker seemingly ignores Sam
The White Walker raises a spear with a blade of ice and gives a piercing shriek
The army of wights and White Walkers, numbering several hundred, advances on the Fist

Valar_Dohaeris.action: shouts and sword clashes are heard in the darkness as the White Walkers and their army of undead wights attack the men of the Night's Watch off-screen in the fight at the Fist
Samwell Tarly wanders through a blizzard in search of safety
Samwell sees another man of the Night's Watch up ahead
Samwell finds the man has been decapitated and his head placed in his own hands
a wight approaches Sam and is about to attack him with an axe
Ghost knocks the wight down
Ghost tears at the wight's legs as it pulls itself forward towards Sam
the wight bursts into flame, destroyed by a torch wielded by Lord Commander Jeor Mormont
Mormont rallies the few dozen survivors out of his original three hundred men at the Fist of the First Men
Mormont is angry that Sam wasn't able to send off any messenger-ravens during the attack
Mormont announces they must retreat back to the Wall to warn Castle Black and the rest of Westeros of the coming threat
Jon is led by Ygritte and the Lord of Bones into the main wildling camp in the Frostfangs mountains
Wildling society, including women and children, is on the move to escape the White Walkers
Jon enters the camp and is shocked to see a real-life giant walk past
Many wildlings are surprised to see Jon in his Night's Watch clothing and shout "Crow!" at him
Several young boys pelt Jon with ice and small stones
Ygritte shoos the boys away
Jon is led into the tent of King-Beyond-the-Wall Mance Rayder
The Lord of Bones explains that Jon is Eddard Stark's bastard son to a large man with a heavy beard eating chicken by the fire
The large man says he doesn't care, but is interested when he hears Jon killed Qhorin
Jon kneels before the large man, assuming him to be Mance Rayder, and calls him "Your Grace"
The wildlings burst into laughter at Jon's mistake and formality
Mance Rayder, an unassuming man sitting in a corner, introduces himself
Mance tells Jon to stand, explaining that no man kneels before another among the Free Folk
Mance says he is glad that Jon killed Qhorin, as Qhorin had killed many wildlings
Mance notes that Qhorin was his brother once, back when he was a member of the Night's Watch and Qhorin had a whole hand
Mance asks Jon why he wants to join the wildlings
Jon replies that he wants to be free
Mance thinks Jon just wants to be a hero
Jon explains that he saw Craster leave his newborn son in the woods as an offering, and the inhuman creature that took it
Jon says he wants to leave the Night's Watch because Mormont knew what Craster was doing but did nothing to stop it
Jon states that the First Men he is descended from defeated the White Walkers once during the Long Night
Jon says he wants to fight for the side who fights for the living
Mance is satisfied and advises Jon to get a new cloak
King Joffrey Baratheon's forces stand victorious after the Battle of the Blackwater
House Tyrell, new allies of House Lannister, begin to settle into the capital
Tywin bluntly tells Tyrion he intends to disinherit him
Tyrion is shocked by Tywin's intention to disinherit him
Jaime gave up all right to inherit his father's lands by joining the Kingsguard
Tyrion is rightfully the next in line of succession
Tywin refuses to believe Tyrion was helpful in the defense of the city or as acting Hand
Tywin maintains his belief that Tyrion is a whore-mongering, drunken, "ill-made, spiteful little creature"
Tywin claims Tyrion is lying about his accomplishments out of envy
Tywin is insulted that Tyrion brought Shae to the capital against his wishes
Tywin convinces himself Tyrion was in bed with Shae while events spiraled out of control
Tyrion asks Tywin why he is doing this
Tywin is shocked as if Tyrion insulted him
Tywin says Tyrion "killed" his mother Joanna in childbirth
Tywin says the laws of men force him to let Tyrion use the family name
Tywin wishes he could prove Tyrion wasn't his son
Tywin says Tyrion's existence makes a mockery of the name "Lannister"
Tyrion fumes at the situation to his sellsword Bronn
Bronn has recently been knighted as a reward for his service in the Battle of the Blackwater
Bronn now styles himself as "Ser Bronn of the Blackwater"
Bronn's knighthood elevates his social standing and his pay
Joffrey rides back from the Great Sept of Baelor in a heavily guarded palanquin
Margaery Tyrell stops the procession in Flea Bottom unexpectedly
Margaery shocks the guards and bewilders Joffrey by getting out and exploring
Margaery arrives at an orphanage and interacts with the children
Margaery’s servants pass out bread and toys to the children
Margaery earns the love of Flea Bottom’s smallfolk
Joffrey and Cersei visit Margaery and Loras Tyrell for a small dinner party that evening
Cersei admonishes Margaery’s boldness
Cersei reminds Margaery that her charity work took place on the same streets where the royal party was assaulted weeks earlier
Joffrey naively defends Margaery and criticizes his mother
Cersei realizes she is on the verge of being outmaneuvered
Cersei recognizes that she only knows how to rule through fear, while Margaery knows how to win the love of the people
Sansa and Shae sit on the docks watching ships arrive and depart
Sansa wants to play a game making up stories about where the ships are headed
Shae is uninterested in Sansa's game
Petyr Baelish arrives to speak with Sansa
She displays an eagerness to leave as soon as possible
He suggests that when he next leaves the capital by sea, she might be able to stow away
Sansa advises him not to give her too many details since she is a terrible liar
Ros reminisces about the day Sansa was born, when all the bells in Winterfell were rung in celebration
Ros asks Shae to look out for Sansa, particularly in regards to Littlefinger
Davos is rescued from the sea by Salladhor Saan after being thrown overboard by the wildfire blast in the battle
Davos mourns the death of his son Matthos
Davos feels that Melisandre has led Stannis to disaster
Davos is horrified to hear from Salladhor that Melisandre has begun to conduct human sacrifices
The defeated remnant of Stannis' forces return to the island
Melisandre orders any who spoke out against her to be burned alive as offerings to the Lord of Light
Davos convinces Salladhor to bring him to Dragonstone
Stannis's shattered forces regroup on Dragonstone after being almost totally destroyed in the Battle of the Blackwater
Davos returns to Dragonstone and finds Stannis despondent, sitting alone with Melisandre in the Chamber of the Painted Table
Davos openly calls Melisandre his enemy and condemns her for conducting human sacrifices
Davos says Melisandre is leading Stannis astray
Melisandre counters that Davos urged Stannis not to bring her to the battle
Melisandre implies it was Davos's fault that thousands of their men burned to death, including his own son
At the mention of his son, Davos draws a knife on Melisandre in anger
Guards restrain Davos
Stannis orders Davos put in the dungeon
The morale of Robb Stark's army starts to wane in the Riverlands
Robb's army has not had a significant victory since the Battle of Oxcross
News arrives of the Lannisters' victory over Stannis and their new alliance with the Tyrells
Robb wins every battle he fights
The Lannisters shift strategy to fleeing ahead of Robb's army instead of attacking head-on
Robb's forces win many small but insignificant victories
The Lannisters wait Robb out and exhaust his army far from home while rebuilding their numbers and gaining new allies
Robb, in frustration, launches a new offensive to the east and takes Harrenhal
Tywin withdraws his garrison from Harrenhal to King's Landing to counter Stannis
Tywin does not leave a defense at Harrenhal
Robb does not find the decisive victory he hoped for at Harrenhal
Robb and his forces find that the Lannisters massacred two hundred Northern and Riverlands prisoners of war before leaving
The courtyards of Harrenhal are choked with piles of bodies
Robb and his forces find one survivor, a maester named Qyburn
Daenerys's ship arrives at the city of Astapor in Slaver's Bay after leaving Qarth
Daenerys's dragons continue to grow and are now about the size of small dogs
Daenerys's dragons fly about the ship as it approaches the city, plucking fish from the sea and roasting them in mid-air with fire-breath
Daenerys notes that her dragons are still not big enough to use as weapons of war to invade Westeros
Daenerys notes that she needs an army
Ser Jorah Mormont notes that the Unsullied in Astapor are said to be among the finest soldiers in the world
Daenerys stresses that the Unsullied are slave-soldiers, which may make some look on her negatively in Westeros
Jorah says they have no better ideas for how to acquire an army
Daenerys expresses interest in expanding her tiny Dothraki khalasar
Most of the Dothraki are seasick because they have never traveled on ships before
Jorah says other Dothraki will only join Daenerys if they think she is strong, and for that she needs an army
Daenerys arrives at Astapor
Kraznys mo Nakloz gives Daenerys a tour of the Unsullied barracks
Missandei translates Kraznys's Low Valyrian into the Common Tongue for Daenerys
Kraznys and Missandei explain that the Unsullied are trained for battle from age five and only one in four survives
Kraznys demonstrates the Unsullied's lack of fear by slicing off the nipple of one soldier
The Unsullied soldier shows no sign of pain and thanks Kraznys for the opportunity to serve
Daenerys is disgusted by the demonstration
Kraznys explains the Unsullied are trained to show no mercy by killing a newborn slave child in front of its mother
Daenerys is outraged by this explanation
Daenerys asks how many Unsullied are available
Kraznys tells Daenerys there are eight thousand soldiers for sale and she has until the next day to decide
Ser Jorah urges Daenerys to purchase the Unsullied, arguing they will have a better quality of life under her command
Daenerys is distracted by a playing child following her and Jorah
Neither Daenerys nor Jorah notice a man in a hooded black cloak following them, armed with a dagger
The child offers a wooden ball to Daenerys
Daenerys picks up the wooden ball
The hooded stranger knocks the ball out of Daenerys's hands
Ser Jorah grabs the hooded stranger
Ser Jorah and the stranger struggle, knocking Daenerys to the ground
The wooden ball cracks in half and a manticore emerges to attack
The manticore attempts to harm Daenerys with its sting
The hooded stranger impales the manticore with his dagger
The hooded stranger charges at the child
The child hisses in a reptilian manner, baring teeth and features reminiscent of Pyat Pree
The child uses magic to escape
Daenerys realizes the assassin was likely an agent of the surviving Warlocks of Qarth
Daenerys turns her attention to her savior and is surprised when the man addresses her as his Queen and removes his hood
Daenerys asks Jorah if he knows the man
Jorah, stunned, admits he knows the man and identifies him as Ser Barristan Selmy, Lord Commander of Robert Baratheon's Kingsguard
Ser Barristan falls to his knees before Daenerys
Ser Barristan explains that after Robert's death and his dismissal by Joffrey, he has been searching for Daenerys to beg forgiveness and offer his service
Ser Barristan expresses his hope to make amends for failing House Targaryen during and after Robert's Rebellion
Ser Barristan hails Daenerys as the rightful Queen and asks to serve in her Queensguard

Dark_Wings,_Dark_Words.action: Jon marches slowly South with the Free Folk army of Mance Rayder
Mance explains that his army is a diverse force formed of about ninety different groups or clans of wildlings, who speak seven different languages, and have numerous internal rivalries
Mance unites the wildlings by telling them the truth: that they will all die if they remain north of the Wall
Mance brings Jon to one of his scouts, Orell
Orell sits silently with his eyes rolled back as an eagle circles above him
Mance says that Orell is a "warg"
Ygritte is surprised that Jon doesn't know what a warg is
Mance explains that a warg is a person who can enter the mind of an animal, see what it sees, and control its actions
Orell controls his menagerie to scout miles ahead
Mance asks Orell what he has seen
Orell says he saw the Fist of the First Men and many dead "crows"
Jeor Mormont leads the few survivors of the fight at the Fist south in hope of reaching the Wall
Samwell Tarly is exhausted and collapses
Rast is annoyed that Sam hid during the battle and thinks they should leave him because he's slowing them down
Sam is upset that Grenn and Edd abandoned him at the start of the attack
Grenn and Edd refuse to leave Sam now
Mormont tells Sam that he has to keep moving and sternly forbids Sam to die
Mormont orders Rast to keep Sam alive until they reach the Wall on pain of death
Bran dreams that he is walking and hunting in the woods
Bran sees the mysterious Three-Eyed Raven again
Bran aims his bow and relives memories of practicing archery with Jon and Robb
Bran hears the voice of his father Eddard
Bran shoots at the raven but misses
A young stranger appears and tells Bran he cannot shoot the raven because he is the raven
Bran awakens suddenly, worrying Hodor for a moment
Hodor is worried about Bran
Bran says that he is fine
Osha says they have enough problems without dark dreams
Bran, Rickon, Osha, Hodor, Summer, and Shaggydog continue to head north from Winterfell to the Wall
The same boy from the dream approaches the camp in the woods unarmed
Summer snarls at the boy initially
Summer sniffs the boy's hand and turns away
Osha comes up behind the boy with a sharpened wooden spear
The boy's sister surprises Osha and puts a knife to her throat
The boy introduces himself as Jojen Reed, and his sister as Meera Reed
Jojen and Meera explain that House Reed are loyal bannermen of House Stark and have been searching for Bran and Rickon to protect them
Bran and Jojen discuss Bran's wolf-dreams while on the move
Jojen explains to Bran that he is a warg
Jojen describes how warging starts as vivid dreams and can develop into conscious control of the wolf
Bran asks Jojen if prophetic dreams are part of being a warg
Jojen explains that prophetic dreams are a different, related ability called the Sight
Jojen explains the Sight allows seeing past, future, or distant events
Jojen confirms Bran's dream of Eddard the day he died was an example of the Sight
Jojen says he had a similar dream about Eddard's death on the same day
Jojen tells his father, Lord Howland Reed, about his dream
Howland Reed weeps, realizing the dream means Eddard is dead
Bran says Howland was a great friend of Eddard and fought with him during Robert's Rebellion
Jojen says Howland doesn't like to talk about the war
Jojen confirms that Bran contacted him in his earlier dream and that he also saw the Three-Eyed Raven
Theon is chained up in a dungeon after being knocked out before the Sack of Winterfell
Men wearing Ironborn clothing come in and interrogate Theon
Theon asks what they want
One captor says he wants to do "this" and starts prying Theon's fingernails off with a knife
The captors use a foot press to torture Theon, turning a screw into his foot until it bleeds
The captors repeatedly ask Theon why he captured Winterfell, ignoring his answers
Theon tells the truth that he wanted to impress his father by taking Winterfell
Theon starts frantically lying, saying he hates the Starks and is their enemy
The guards leave and put a bag over Theon's head
A servant boy comes to Theon, unwinds the foot press, and tells him Yara sent him to rescue Theon
The servant boy tells Theon to wait until nightfall and puts the bag back on Theon's head
Theon is left alone, crying for help
King Joffrey Baratheon is fitted for new clothes in his chambers in preparation for his upcoming wedding to Margaery Tyrell
Cersei criticizes Margaery, saying she is only charitable to the poor to build support for herself
Cersei says she thinks Margaery dresses like a harlot
Joffrey barely tolerates his mother's chiding
Joffrey dismissively tells Cersei off
Shae dresses Sansa in her chambers
Shae warns Sansa about Littlefinger
Shae points out that Baelish has offered to help Sansa for nothing in return, implying he wants something
Sansa naively says that Baelish is an old friend of her mother's, believing that should be reason enough
Shae suggests that Baelish is actually sexually attracted to Sansa
Sansa is disturbed by this suggestion, but thinks it is impossible because Baelish is much older
Shae continues to warn Sansa that Baelish is manipulative and men usually only want "one thing" from pretty young ladies
Ser Loras Tyrell arrives at the door and escorts Sansa to the gardens to meet with Margaery and Lady Olenna Tyrell
Sansa reminds Loras that they met once before at the Tourney of the Hand
Loras introduces Sansa and takes his leave
Lady Olenna bluntly says her son Mace was a fool for supporting Renly Baratheon's claim to the throne
Margaery speaks well of Renly
Lady Olenna insists that Sansa tell her the truth about Joffrey
Sansa is initially reluctant to speak
Olenna mentions Sansa's father
Sansa breaks down and angrily recounts how Joffrey promised mercy but beheaded her father, then forced her to look at his head on a spike
Sansa nervously attempts to backtrack after realizing what she has said
Olenna continues to prod Sansa for the truth
Sansa fidgets and is visibly afraid of being overheard
Olenna insists Sansa can trust her
Sansa blurts out that Joffrey is "a monster"
Olenna is disappointed but unsurprised by Sansa's statement
Sansa worries the Tyrells will cancel the marriage alliance
Olenna assures Sansa that her son Mace is too intent on Margaery entering a royal marriage to cancel it
Shae sneaks into Tyrion's new chambers
Tyrion warns Shae that his father threatened to kill her if found with him again
Tyrion tells Shae that his father follows through on such threats
Shae starts undressing Tyrion
Shae asks Tyrion to protect Sansa from Baelish
Tyrion says he no longer has enough power or influence to protect Sansa
Shae accuses Tyrion of being attracted to Sansa
Tyrion cheerfully denies being attracted to Sansa
It comes out that Tyrion had sex with Ros back in the North
Tyrion points out it was before he met Shae
Tyrion and Shae playfully bicker about it as they start having sex
Tyrion and Shae playfully bicker about it as they start having sex
Margaery is summoned to Joffrey's chambers to see if she needs anything before he leaves on a hunting trip
Cersei plants a seed of doubt in Joffrey's mind about Margaery's previous marriage to Renly
Joffrey questions Margaery about her marriage to Renly and implies she had sex with him
Margaery explains she was ordered to marry Renly as her family duty
Joffrey angrily questions Margaery about her relationship with Renly
Margaery placates Joffrey by feigning shyness and painting herself as a dutiful but frustrated bride
Margaery uses rumors about Renly's homosexuality to redirect Joffrey's anger
Margaery claims Renly avoided sex and once drunkenly suggested something painful that could not result in children
Joffrey sympathizes with Margaery and says he considered making Renly's "perversion" punishable by death
Margaery diverts Joffrey's attention to his new custom crossbow
Joffrey becomes excited by violence and sadism
Margaery flirts with Joffrey by complimenting the crossbow and musing about killing something herself
Joffrey offers to take Margaery with him to share in the excitement of killing something
Robb settles into the council chamber at Harrenhal with Queen Talisa
Roose Bolton interrupts Robb and Talisa with two messenger-raven missives
Roose delivers news that Hoster Tully has died after a long illness at Riverrun
Roose delivers a letter from Ramsay Snow stating the Ironborn torched Winterfell, killed its inhabitants, and fled before his arrival
Robb hopes Bran and Rickon are still alive, but acknowledges the strong possibility they are dead
Robb notes there has been no word of Theon or any demands from the Greyjoys
Catelyn is reduced to tears by the double-blow of bad news
Catelyn laments not having seen her father in years
Catelyn is horrified that Bran and Rickon were in danger while she was away and could not protect them, and are now likely dead
Robb leaves Roose in command of Harrenhal with a detachment of the Northern army
Robb leads his main host back across the Riverlands to Riverrun
Rickard Karstark angrily says that this is a waste of time
Robb points out that Edmure Tully, now lord of Riverrun, will reinforce their army with his levies
Karstark maintains that Edmure's levies will make no difference because the Lannisters now outnumber them over two to one
Robb asks if Karstark has lost faith in their cause
Karstark says he still believes in revenge
Robb's loss of his home castle makes him seem vulnerable in the eyes of his bannermen
many bannermen no longer believe in Robb as they once did
Lord Karstark asks to speak his mind
Karstark says he thinks Robb lost the war the day he married Talisa
Talisa notices that Catelyn is making a prayer wheel for the Faith of the Seven
Talisa offers to help Catelyn
Catelyn explains that prayer wheels can only be made by mothers praying for their children
Catelyn reveals she has only made prayer wheels twice before
Catelyn recounts making a prayer wheel when Bran was in a coma and survived, though he lost the use of his legs
Talisa asks about the other time Catelyn made a prayer wheel
Catelyn explains that when they were younger, one of the boys had a pox and Maester Luwin said if he survived the night he would be all right
Catelyn reveals the boy was Jon Snow
Catelyn admits she inwardly prayed for Jon to die of natural causes
Catelyn becomes horrified at herself for wishing Jon’s death, realizing Jon was an innocent child and her hatred was for his mother
Catelyn makes a prayer wheel and stays up with Jon all night, praying for his survival and promising to urge Eddard to legitimize Jon if he lived
Jon recovers from his illness
Catelyn does not keep her promise to Jon
Catelyn expresses her grief over the deaths of her father, her two youngest sons, and Eddard, believing the gods are punishing her with the war
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie head north from Harrenhal, planning to reach the Red Fork and then Riverrun
Arya explains to Gendry and Hot Pie how Jaqen H'ghar helped them escape by offering to kill any three people she named
Gendry criticizes Arya for using up two names on minor Lannister guards instead of King Joffrey or Tywin Lannister
Arya gets defensive at Gendry's criticism
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie run into a scouting party of the Brotherhood Without Banners in the forest
Thoros of Myr sings "The Rains of Castamere" as they approach
Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie recognize the Brotherhood as the group the Lannister torturers were asking about
Thoros makes the children come with the Brotherhood
The Brotherhood gives Arya, Gendry, and Hot Pie food and drink at a local inn
Gendry says he worked as a smith in King's Landing for Tobho Mott
Thoros recognizes Tobho Mott and comments on his prices
Gendry counters that the quality was double
Arya insists she knows how to use a sword despite Thoros' disbelief
Arya tries to mock-fence with Thoros, but he quickly disarms her
Other members of the Brotherhood return to the inn with Sandor "The Hound" Clegane as a captive
Thoros trades insults with Sandor
Arya tries to leave unnoticed
The Hound stops Arya and reveals her identity to all by calling her "the Stark bitch"
Jaime and Brienne make their way across the Riverlands toward King's Landing, intending to exchange Jaime for the Stark girls
Jaime mocks Brienne along the way
Brienne keeps Jaime on a leash and watches him closely, even while he urinates
Jaime deduces Brienne isn't from the North because he would have noticed her at Winterfell
Jaime guesses Brienne is a Baratheon supporter
Brienne angrily denies supporting Stannis, confirming she served Renly
Jaime deduces Brienne fancied Renly and points out Renly was attracted to Loras, not women
Brienne says she doesn't want to hear Jaime's "filthy lies"
Jaime claims he knew Renly better than Brienne did, referencing their years in the Red Keep
Jaime insists Renly's homosexuality was the "worst kept secret at court"
Jaime mocks Renly, causing Brienne to grab him
Jaime relents and says he doesn't care that Renly loved Loras, as people can't choose who they love
Jaime and Brienne come across a farmer with a heavily laden mule
The farmer notices they are avoiding major roads and suspects they are avoiding soldiers
Jaime tells Brienne he suspects the farmer is a scout or could spread word about them
Jaime urges Brienne to kill the farmer
Brienne refuses to kill the farmer
Brienne and Jaime need to cross a river, choosing between a dangerous fording or a watched bridge
Brienne chooses the safer route of the bridge
Jaime distracts Brienne and takes away her spare sword
Jaime cuts his bonds
Brienne and Jaime engage in an extended sword fight on the bridge
Jaime starts the fight aggressively, putting Brienne on the defensive
Jaime’s mobility is reduced due to manacled hands and malnourishment
Jaime begins to tire
Brienne wears Jaime down with body-blows, kicks, and punches
Jaime slumps to the ground in exhaustion after a protracted fight
Riders from House Bolton arrive, led by Locke
Jaime asks if they want to negotiate
Locke refuses, saying he must bring the Kingslayer back to the King in the North
Locke takes Brienne and Jaime prisoner

Walk_of_Punishment_(episode).action: Daenerys walks along the Walk of Punishment in Astapor, accompanied by Ser Jorah Mormont and Ser Barristan Selmy
Slaves who showed insubordination are strapped to crosses and left to die in public as a warning
Daenerys offers a condemned man water
The condemned man refuses to drink and says he just wants to die quickly

Gilly is deeply distraught about the fate of her newborn son
Gilly yells at Sam not to draw attention to the fact that it's a boy by calling it a "he" so loud
Gilly explains Craster will want to offer the baby as a sacrifice if he finds out it's a son
Sam asks Gilly if she's decided on a name
Gilly responds that there's no point in naming her baby if he's going to be offered as a human sacrifice
Gilly gives Sam back his mother's thimble
Gilly says she doesn't care about such stupid things and is focused on protecting her baby
Some of the wounded die from their injuries
Jeor Mormont leads a funeral service as they burn Bannen's corpse on a pyre
Mormont delivers a eulogy, praising Bannen's bravery
Mormont closes the eulogy with "And now, his watch is ended"
The assembled men repeat the closing line
Mormont checks a map in his journal
Craster crassly berates the men of the Night's Watch
Mormont says they have to stay until the wounded recover enough to travel
Craster waves off Mormont's concern about the wounded
Craster suggests killing the severely wounded men
Craster offers to finish off the wounded if Mormont won't
Mormont declines Craster's offer
Karl Tanner complains that Craster is feeding them bread cut with sawdust and asks about the hidden larder
Craster gets drunk on the wine gifted to him
Rast joins in the accusations against Craster
Craster admits to having winter stockpiles but refuses to share them
Rast calls Craster a bastard
Craster becomes enraged and threatens Rast with an axe
Mormont restrains Rast
Craster shouts that he is throwing them all out to lie in the cold
Craster threatens to chop off the next man's hands who calls him "bastard"
A tense moment of silence passes
Mormont grabs Rast to lead him out the doorway
Karl challenges Craster and insults him
Craster lunges at Karl in a blind rage
Karl holds off Craster's axe with his left hand
Karl rams a dagger through Craster's throat with his right hand
Karl flings dying Craster to the ground
Karl punches one of Craster's wives and demands to know where the hidden food is kept
Lord Commander Mormont bellows that the gods will curse Karl for killing a host
Karl shouts that there are no laws beyond the Wall
Karl threatens the girl with a knife
Mormont draws his sword, making Karl drop the girl and face off against Mormont
Rast stabs Lord Commander Mormont in the back
Mormont drops his sword
The men stare in shock
Grenn charges and tackles Karl
The entire room explodes into pure bedlam
Desperate Night's Watch recruits like Rast turn on officers loyal to Mormont and loyal common recruits like Grenn
Mutiny spreads throughout Craster's Keep
Mormont turns to fight off Rast
Mormont, enraged and wounded, grabs Rast by the throat and lifts him off his feet
Mormont spins Rast around and hurls him against the opposite wall
Mormont nearly crushes Rast's windpipe with his bare hand
Mormont starts to cough up thick red blood due to his mortal knife wound
Mormont sinks to the ground, continuing to cough up blood
Rast grabs a knife and repeatedly stabs Mormont in the throat until he is dead
Sam rushes to Gilly's hut and orders her to escape
Sam runs out of Craster's Keep with Gilly and her baby
Loyal Night's Watch members fall to the mutineers
Mutineers kill or rape Craster's other wives
Gilly leads Sam and her baby into the night, knowing the woods
Rast, covered in Mormont's blood, shouts into the darkness that he'll soon be cutting Sam's throat too
Daenerys arrives before Kraznys mo Nakloz and the other slave-masters of Astapor
Daenerys's Dothraki guards carry Drogon in a large cage
Daenerys is accompanied by Ser Jorah Mormont and Ser Barristan Selmy
Missandei, now dressed in higher-quality clothing matching Daenerys's style, is gifted to Daenerys as a translator
All eight thousand Unsullied are gathered in the city's main square for the transaction
Upper-class men and women of Astapor crowd around the square, gawking at the dragon
Slaves in heavy chains serve refreshments to the upper-class spectators
Kraznys tells Daenerys, via Missandei's interpretation, that many Unsullied have not yet seen battle and suggests she "blood them early" by attacking minor cities and towns
Kraznys explains that the slavers will purchase any slaves Daenerys captures
Kraznys notes that young boys sold as slaves will be castrated and trained to become new Unsullied
Daenerys lets her dragon out of its cage
Daenerys leads the dragon on a long chain leash as it hovers about twenty feet in the air
Daenerys hands the leash to Kraznys
Kraznys gives Daenerys a cat-o-nine-tails with a hilt shaped like a harpy, signifying ownership of the Unsullied
Daenerys asks if the transaction is done
Kraznys impatiently says it is, as he struggles to rein in the dragon
Daenerys calls the Unsullied to attention in Valyrian
Daenerys tests the Unsullied with basic Valyrian marching commands
Daenerys smiles slightly, knowing the Unsullied are now hers
Missandei is surprised by Daenerys's actions
Kraznys complains for Missandei to tell Daenerys that the dragon won't come when he commands
Daenerys turns to face Kraznys and says "A dragon is not a slave" in Valyrian
Kraznys, dumbfounded, demands an explanation
Daenerys declares her identity and reveals Valyrian is her mother-tongue, indicating she had been feigning ignorance
Daenerys addresses the thousands of Unsullied
Daenerys orders the Unsullied to kill all of the slave masters in Astapor, their household soldiers, and any man who holds a whip
Daenerys cautions the Unsullied to spare any children and to strike the chains off any slave they see
The Unsullied obey Daenerys’s commands without question
The Unsullied begin attacking all of the assembled slave-masters
Kraznys frantically shouts for someone to kill Daenerys
Daenerys calmly orders "dracarys"
Drogon breathes fire onto Kraznys, engulfing him in flames as he dies
Drogon flies across the upper walls, burning the other high-ranking slave masters to death
The Unsullied sack the entire city, slaughtering the slave masters and freeing fellow slaves, supported by Daenerys' dragons
Daenerys walks past Kraznys' charred remains
Daenerys mounts a white horse
Daenerys addresses her Unsullied
Daenerys declares the Unsullied free and offers them the choice to leave without harm
Daenerys asks the Unsullied to stay and fight for her as free men
The Unsullied stare blankly, unaccustomed to freedom
One Unsullied starts tapping his spear against the ground in support
The other Unsullied join in, striking their spears against the ground in praise of Daenerys
