Daenerys gains an army with which to reconquer the Seven Kingdoms, fulfilling her brother's dream
Daenerys rides triumphantly out of the smoking wreck of Astapor
Daenerys drops the slave-master's whip on the ground
The Unsullied trample the whip under their feet as they march past
Daenerys rides out with her eight thousand strong army of Unsullied marching in formation
Daenerys's three dragons fly above the army
A Targaryen begins to conquer again, beginning the Liberation of Slaver's Bay

Kissed_by_Fire.action: Jon is with a group of twenty wildlings under Tormund’s command, sent ahead of Mance Rayder’s army to scale the Wall and attack Castle Black from behind
Jon and Ygritte gather firewood
Ygritte gathers firewood with Jon
Orell asks Jon about the defenses of the Night’s Watch
Orell observes patrols on top of the Wall through his eagle
Orell asks Jon how frequent the patrols are
Jon says patrols are usually in teams of four: two builders and two rangers, but the frequency often changes
Orell says the wildlings know there are nineteen castles along the south side of the Wall and asks how many are manned
Jon reluctantly says only three castles are currently manned: Castle Black, Eastwatch-by-the-Sea, and the Shadow Tower
Orell asks Jon how many men garrison Castle Black
Jon is very reluctant to answer, but after being threatened by Orell, gives an exaggerated number, saying there are one thousand men
Tormund tells Jon that if he’s lying, he’ll rip his guts out through his throat
Jon and Ygritte walk away together
Ygritte steals Jon's sword Longclaw
Jon chases Ygritte to get his sword back
Ygritte leads Jon into a nearby cave heated by natural hotsprings
Environment forms a waterfall and a pool in the cave
Ygritte starts disrobing
Ygritte says she wants Jon to prove he has broken his Night's Watch vows by sleeping with her
Ygritte quickly removes all her clothes and walks up to Jon naked
Jon hesitates to break his vows and is shy
Ygritte questions why Jon is still dressed
Jon and Ygritte start kissing
Jon continues to kiss down Ygritte's body as she insists he has no experience
Ygritte begins to say "You know nothing, Jon Sn--" but stops mid-word as Jon starts performing oral sex on her
Jon and Ygritte lie together in a naked embrace after having sex
Ygritte asks Jon if "that thing you did with your mouth" is what Lords do to their Ladies in the south
Jon says Ygritte seemed to like it when he kissed her there
Jon admits he's never had sex with anyone before
Ygritte says Jon is "a maid"
Ygritte lists off some wildling boys she's had sex with before
Ygritte describes the first boy as having red hair like her, which is rare and considered lucky among wildlings
Ygritte says another was a Thenn "built like a mammoth" who could not speak the Common Tongue
Jon and Ygritte slide into the hot springs pool to take a bath together
Jon and Ygritte romantically cuddle more
Ygritte tells Jon she wishes they could stay in the cave forever instead of facing the outside world
Daenerys's new army of freed Unsullied, having just sacked Astapor, head north to Yunkai
Jorah tells Barristan how he was knighted at the end of the Greyjoy Rebellion
Jorah explains he was the second man through the breach during the Siege of Pyke, right behind Thoros of Myr
Thoros of Myr charges in headlong waving his flaming sword
Barristan finds Jorah's story amusing, as he was commanding the assault on Old Wyk at the time
King Robert Baratheon knights Jorah for his bravery that day
Jorah says the proudest day of his life was being knighted, but he was mostly thinking about needing to piss after being in armor for sixteen hours
Barristan admits that Robert was a good man and a good warrior, but a terrible king
Barristan laments wasting so much of his life defending kings who didn't deserve it
Barristan recalls spending seventeen years defending Robert, and the Mad King before that
Barristan explains that a man of honor must keep his vows no matter what, whether serving a drunk or a lunatic
Barristan expresses his desire to serve with pride and fight for someone he believes in before he dies
Jorah says that Barristan can believe in Daenerys
Barristan says Daenerys will have good men to advise her
Barristan warns it might not be well for Daenerys to be seen with Jorah when they return to Westeros
Jorah admits he may never be rid of the moral taint of selling slaves
Jorah begins to subtly ask about the advisors on the Small Council
Jorah tries to figure out if Barristan knew he was initially a spy for King Robert, reporting to Varys
Jorah reports Daenerys was pregnant, prompting Robert to demand her assassination at a Small Council meeting with Eddard Stark
Jorah points out the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard traditionally holds a seat on the Small Council
Barristan explains he was left out of Small Council meetings because Robert didn't trust his advice
Jorah is relieved Barristan was not present at council meetings and couldn't have learned about Jorah's reports
Jorah and Barristan briefly bicker about Barristan's recent arrival and Jorah's defense of Daenerys
Jorah asserts he takes his orders from Daenerys
Daenerys and Missandei meet with the assembled officers of the Unsullied
Daenerys addresses the Unsullied in Low Valyrian, telling them they are now free men and can make their own choices
Daenerys asks the Unsullied to choose one among them to be their leader
The Unsullied part ranks and one eunuch steps forward
Daenerys asks the eunuch to remove his helmet and tell her his name
The eunuch introduces himself as "Grey Worm"
Daenerys is confused by the name
Missandei explains the Unsullied are given demeaning slave-names by the slavemasters of Astapor
Daenerys, disgusted, commands all Unsullied to choose their own new names or reclaim their birth names
The Unsullied commander says he will continue to be known as "Grey Worm" because it is the name he had when he was set free
Daenerys is deeply touched by Grey Worm's loyalty
Martyn and Willem Lannister awake to hear shouting and fighting outside their cell door
Lord Rickard Karstark fights his way into the room with some of his own men
A guard is killed while attempting to stop them
Willem, confused, asks if this is a rescue
Karstark men promptly kill Willem
Martyn begs that he is just a squire and didn't do anything
Lord Rickard ignores Martyn's pleas and stabs him in the belly with a dagger, killing him
The bloody corpses of the two Lannister prisoners are laid out on the floor in front of Robb Stark in Riverrun's main meeting room
Robb is disgusted and remarks that Karstark needed five men to brutally murder two unarmed squires in their own prison cell
Karstark insists that it was a father's vengeance
Robb points out that the boys had nothing to do with the death of Karstark's sons, who were killed by Jaime Lannister
Rickard explains he was denied vengeance when Catelyn set Jaime free in hopes of a prisoner exchange
Catelyn sets Jaime free in hopes of a prisoner exchange for her daughters
Karstark kills Jaime's kin who they held prisoner
Robb angrily shouts that they were only little boys, and Karstark can't blame Catelyn for his treasonous killing of prisoners
Karstark stands firm and says the only treason is letting their enemies go, and insults Robb by asking if his father ever taught him that
Brynden Tully punches Karstark over this remark
Robb tells Brynden to leave Karstark alone
Karstark says Robb will just give him a scolding and mocks him as "the King Who Lost the North"
Robb orders all of Karstark's men hanged, and to hang the watcher last
Rickard Karstark is sent to the dungeons
Edmure Tully insists that if word of the deaths leaves Riverrun, Tywin Lannister will exact heavy reprisals
Edmure suggests quietly burying the boys and keeping silent about their deaths until the war is over
Robb refuses to be a liar and says he cannot fight a war in the name of justice if he will not serve justice to murderers within his own ranks
All of Robb's advisors tell him this is a bad idea
Catelyn and Talisa warn Robb that the Karstark soldiers will abandon his cause and return home if he executes their lord
Catelyn and Talisa warn Robb that they are already badly outnumbered
Catelyn says they should keep Lord Rickard hostage
Edmure agrees and says they can keep Lord Rickard hostage and tell the Karstarks that no harm will come to him if they remain loyal
Robb ignores the pleas of others
Robb has Lord Karstark brought out to the courtyard of Riverrun to be executed during a driving rainstorm
Rain pours down during the execution
Karstark points out that both Houses are descended from the First Men and are kin
Robb says their blood relationship did not stop Rickard from betraying him and won't stop the execution
Rickard says he wants the execution to haunt Robb until the day he dies
Lord Rickard says Robb will be cursed as a kinslayer and is no king of his
Robb pronounces the sentence of death
Robb personally beheads Lord Rickard
Robb strictly adheres to justice
Karstarks withdraw their soldiers from Robb's army and march for home
Robb loses almost half of his forces stationed at Riverrun
Robb admits to Talisa that she was right and he made a mistake
Robb says Tywin Lannister realizes he is in a strong position and doesn't need to attack the Northerners anymore
Tywin waits and lets the Northerners' demoralized forces unravel
Robb's army loses momentum and his generals bicker
Robb shows Talisa a war map of the Seven Kingdoms

The map depicts Robb's armies around Riverrun and Harrenhal, Lannister and Tyrell armies overrunning the Stormlands, Lannister/Tyrell armies in King's Landing, and Greyjoy forces occupying the western coasts of the North
Talisa suggests Robb take the fight to the Lannisters
Robb explains attacking King's Landing is hopeless
Robb says Tywin, the main Lannister army, and a large Tyrell army are defending King's Landing
Robb says attacking the capital head-on would be suicide
Talisa suggests Robb lead his army back to the North to repulse the Greyjoys and rebuild his powerbase
Robb points out that his tired soldiers won't want to leave the North again, especially with a long winter coming
Robb notes the Northerners haven't collected harvests for winter stockpiles
Robb says returning to the North will make it hard to rally men to defend the Riverlords
Robb and Talisa eye the map together
Robb decides to strike where the enemy is weakest
Robb decides to make an all-or-nothing assault against Casterly Rock
Robb notes the loss of the Karstark forces means they don't have enough men to attack Casterly Rock
Robb realizes he must win back the allegiance of House Frey to gain enough soldiers
Robb must try to repair his alliance with House Frey
Locke presents the captive Jaime Lannister and Brienne of Tarth to Lord Roose Bolton at Harrenhal
Locke boasts about torturing Jaime and cutting off his hand
Roose Bolton is surprised that Jaime has lost his hand
Locke points out that he cut off Jaime's hand and hung it around Jaime's neck
Bolton becomes angry that Locke maimed such a valuable hostage
Bolton demands Locke dispose of Jaime's rotting hand
Bolton orders accommodations for Jaime and Brienne
Jaime asks Bolton if there is news from the capital
Bolton informs Jaime that Stannis Baratheon laid siege to King's Landing in the Battle of the Blackwater, and drags out revealing the result
Bolton toys with Jaime but remains calm
Bolton finally reveals that Cersei is alive and Tywin's forces repulsed Stannis
Jaime is overcome with relief and collapses
Bolton calls for Qyburn to attend to Jaime's injuries
Qyburn peels away the crude tourniquet from Jaime's arm and finds the stump badly infected
Jaime asks Qyburn if he will die
Qyburn says Jaime won't die, but suggests amputating the rest of the arm at the shoulder
Jaime, half-delirious, refuses and threatens to kill Qyburn if he tries
Jaime notices Qyburn is not a maester because he lacks a chain
Qyburn explains he was stripped of his chain by the Citadel for conducting "too bold" experiments
Qyburn prepares to cut Jaime, saying he'll try to save the elbow
Jaime grabs Qyburn by the throat with his left hand and threatens him
Qyburn relents and suggests cutting out the rotten flesh and burning the wounds with boiling wine
Jaime agrees to try this
Qyburn warns it will be very painful and offers Milk of the Poppy
Jaime refuses Milk of the Poppy and says he'll just scream
Qyburn begins to cut out the gangrenous tissue from Jaime's stump
Jaime howls in agony
Brienne scrubs off weeks' worth of grime and filth in Harrenhal's bathhouse
Jaime appears in the doorway assisted by a servant, barely able to stand
Jaime asks the servant to help him take off his muddy rags and leave
Jaime walks naked into the same communal bath as Brienne, on the far side
Brienne, also naked, angrily points out there are other baths
Jaime says he wants this bath and asks Brienne to save him from drowning if he passes out
Brienne asks why she should care how Jaime dies
Jaime points out Brienne swore a vow to get him to King's Landing in one piece
Jaime indicates the stump of his hand
Jaime quips that it's no wonder Renly died with Brienne guarding him
Brienne defiantly stands up out of the bath, facing Jaime fully nude
Jaime gravely says that was "unworthy" and asks for forgiveness
Brienne angrily orders Jaime not to mock her
Jaime insists he is seriously apologizing
Jaime, exhausted, says he is tired of fighting and wants a truce
Brienne says you need trust to have a truce
Jaime says he trusts Brienne
Brienne sits back down
Jaime notes that Brienne has the same look of contempt he has seen for seventeen years
People despise Jaime, calling him "kingslayer," "oathbreaker," and "a man without honor"
Jaime recalls the Mad King's obsession with wildfire and enjoyment of burning people alive
Aerys burns lords, Hands, and anyone he thinks is against him
Half of Westeros rebels against Aerys
Aerys orders pyromancers to place wildfire caches all over King's Landing
Robert wins at the Trident and marches on King's Landing
Tywin arrives at King's Landing first with the Lannister army, promising to defend the city
Jaime warns Aerys that Tywin cannot be trusted and urges him to surrender
Aerys refuses to listen to Jaime
Varys gives the same warning about Tywin, but Aerys ignores him
Pycelle convinces Aerys that the Lannisters are there to help
Aerys opens the city gates to the Lannisters
The Lannisters sack King's Landing
Jaime begs Aerys to surrender again
Aerys orders Jaime to bring him Tywin's head and commands the pyromancer to burn the city
Jaime tells Brienne this was his breaking point and asks what she would do in his place
Brienne sits in shocked silence
Jaime kills the pyromancer first
Jaime kills Aerys as the Mad King tries to flee
Jaime speculates that Aerys believed he would be reborn as a dragon in the fire
Jaime slits Aerys's throat to make sure he dies
Eddard Stark finds Jaime at that point
Brienne, stunned, asks why Jaime never defended himself with this story
Jaime angrily rants that Eddard Stark would never have believed him
Eddard had already judged Jaime guilty and didn't want to hear his side
Jaime rages, yelling what right Stark had to judge him and by what right the wolf dares to judge the lion
Jaime is overcome by the strain of recounting his tale and begins to faint, nearly falling into the water
Brienne catches Jaime and calls for help for the Kingslayer
Jaime whimpers to himself "Jaime. My name is Jaime..." as he slips out of consciousness
Sandor Clegane prepares to fight Beric Dondarrion in a trial by combat at the Brotherhood Without Banners' hideout
Thoros of Myr prays to the Lord of Light to judge Sandor or give strength to his sword
Beric calls upon the Lord of Light to ignite his sword with flame using his blood
Beric gains an initial advantage against Sandor due to Sandor's fear of fire
Beric sets Sandor's wooden shield on fire, which is strapped tightly to Sandor's arm
Sandor is nearly burned by the flaming shield
Sandor overpowers Beric with his raw physical strength and anger
Sandor cuts through Beric's flaming sword, armor, and deep into his shoulder, killing him
Beric falls to the ground
Thoros quickly rushes to Beric's side and begins praying to the Lord of Light to revive him
Sandor falls to the ground and desperately tries to extinguish his flaming shield
Arya grabs a dagger and attempts to kill the Hound while he is down
Gendry stops Arya from killing the Hound
Arya curses Gendry
Sandor taunts Arya about the god preferring him over her dead friend
Beric is revived by the Lord of Light's power and interrupts Arya and Sandor
Sandor is allowed to leave, having won the trial and proven his innocence in the eyes of god
The Brotherhood takes all the gold Sandor had on him to fund their cause
Beric gives Sandor a promissory note for payment at the end of the war instead of returning his gold
Sandor scoffs at the note and throws it to the ground
Anguy tells Sandor he should be thankful they're letting him leave with his life
Clegane is hooded so he won't know the way back to their hideout
Clegane is led away to be released some distance away
Gendry works on repairing Beric's armor
Arya questions Gendry about repairing the armor since they will be leaving soon
Gendry says he has decided to stay and work as a smith for the Brotherhood Without Banners
Gendry explains he wants to aid the Brotherhood in protecting the smallfolk during the war
Arya says their plan was to find her brother Robb, who also needs soldiers
Gendry points out he has no attachments to Robb Stark
Gendry explains Arya would be going to her noble-born family, while he would just be serving another lord
Gendry says he has been serving other men his whole life and has nothing to show for it
Gendry recounts working for Tobho Mott, who sold him to the Night's Watch
Gendry recalls serving Tywin Lannister as a prisoner at Harrenhal, living in fear
Gendry says he would be serving in the Brotherhood too, but they chose Beric as their leader and are more like a family
Gendry reflects on never having a family as a common-born orphan
Arya sadly chokes out that she could be Gendry's family
Gendry politely but firmly points out that class differences mean they could never truly be family
Arya looks into the campfire and recites her nightly prayer-list for the death of her enemies
Thoros says they plan to bring Arya to Riverrun to reunite her with her family, in exchange for a reward from Robb
Arya points out that they are ransoming her
Thoros admits they technically are ransoming her, but says this is different
Thoros explains they intend to escort Arya safely to her family, but need the gold to support themselves
Beric says he would like to return Arya for free out of memory of her father, but they need the gold
Beric says he knows Arya must be angry with him, but releasing Sandor was the right thing to do
Beric explains they promised to let Sandor go free if he won the trial by combat
Arya is quietly upset, having seen honorable men keep promises and be killed for it
Arya wonders why Beric would want to let Sandor go when he nearly killed him
Beric insists that he did actually kill Sandor
Arya asks how Beric killed Sandor
Beric points Arya to Thoros to explain
Thoros says he isn't the one who brings Beric back to life, it's the Lord of Light, and Thoros just recites the prayers
Beric opens his shirt to reveal several grievous scars on his torso
Beric and Thoros explain that Beric has died and been brought back to life six times
They describe Beric's deaths: impaled on a lance, shot with an arrow, hanged and stabbed in the eye by the Lannisters
Arya earnestly asks Thoros if he could bring back a man without a head
Thoros and Beric understand Arya is asking about her father
Thoros says he doesn't think it works that way, because Eddard was decapitated and has been dead too long
Beric consoles Arya, saying her father was a good man, but he wouldn't wish his resurrected life on him
Beric explains that being brought back from the dead carries a heavy toll, as pieces of his memory and identity chip away each time
Arya says she would wish Beric's life on her father, because at least Beric is still alive
Stannis is deeply depressed after his defeat at the Battle of the Blackwater
Melisandre leaves Dragonstone on an important mission
Most of Stannis's army is destroyed
The Lannisters do not attack Dragonstone because their fleet was destroyed at Blackwater
Stannis visits his wife, Queen Selyse, for the first time since returning from battle
Stannis finds Selyse praying over a fire to the Lord of Light in her chamber
Selyse is revealed to be a fanatical believer in the Lord of Light religion
Selyse first invited Melisandre to Dragonstone
Selyse reveres and is in awe of Stannis as her king and the Prince That Was Promised
Selyse tells Stannis not to despair and assures him he will be victorious
Stannis laments that he used to believe that
Stannis confesses to Selyse that he has sinned and shamed her by sleeping with Melisandre
Selyse interrupts Stannis and says Melisandre already told her everything
Selyse says no act done in service of the Lord of Light can be a sin
Selyse wept for joy when Melisandre told her of the service with Stannis
Selyse is glad Melisandre gave Stannis a "son" which she never could
Stannis's face is filled with shock, disgust, and relief
Selyse walks to a corner of her chamber where she keeps the corpses of her three stillborn sons in glass jars
Selyse weeps and laments that she has given Stannis nothing
Stannis tells Selyse that is not true, with pity
Selyse understands he is referring to their daughter, Princess Shireen Baratheon
Selyse grows annoyed when she realizes Stannis has come to see Shireen too
Selyse says Stannis shouldn't waste time on such distractions
Stannis insists that Shireen is his daughter
Selyse relents because it is not her place to question her king
Shireen draws and sings a song about life under the sea to herself while alone in her room
Shireen is excited to see her father
Stannis wants to see his daughter but is awkward in expressing warm emotions
Shireen asks Stannis if he won a battle she heard about
Stannis good-naturedly says no
Shireen is slightly let-down
Shireen asks if the Onion Knight (Ser Davos) came back as well
Stannis grows tense over their falling out
Stannis briefly explains that Ser Davos is a traitor and is rotting in the dungeon
Shireen is confused
Stannis flatly says she should best put Davos out of her mind
Shireen sneaks down to the dungeon of Dragonstone and finds the cell of "Ser Onion Knight"
Davos wakes up and tells Shireen she shouldn't be there
Shireen explains that Bert, the fat guard, is on duty and usually drinks wine and sleeps through his watch
Shireen asks Davos if it is true that he is a traitor
Davos says that it is true, as he disobeyed her father
Shireen says she doesn't care and calls Davos her friend
Shireen brings Davos a book to read about Aegon the Conqueror and his dragons
Shireen points out that the Targaryens built Dragonstone and Aegon lived in the castle
Davos explains that he can't read
Shireen offers to teach Davos how to read by coming down to his cell and reading to him when Bert is on duty
Davos chides Shireen that they can't do that
Shireen asks what’s the worst the guards could do if they find out, lock them in cells?
Shireen and Davos have a quiet laugh
Shireen starts to read to Davos An History of Aegon the Conqueror and His Conquest of Westeros
Cersei encounters Littlefinger in a courtyard in King's Landing
Cersei tells Littlefinger she fears the Tyrells do not have the Lannisters' best interests at heart
Cersei says her father won't listen to her concerns
Cersei asks Littlefinger to use his spies to find out what the Tyrells are up to
Cersei notes that Littlefinger has a good working relationship with the Tyrells after brokering the Lannister-Tyrell alliance
Littlefinger agrees to Cersei's request
Cersei warns Littlefinger that this had better produce more results than when she previously asked him to find Arya Stark
Cersei reminds Littlefinger that Arya could not be found
Sansa and Margaery watch Ser Loras spar with his squire in the castle gardens
Sansa remarks confidently on Loras's fighting ability
Sansa inquires when she and Loras will wed, referencing the Tyrell plot
Margaery replies she will "plant the seed" of the idea after marrying Joffrey
Sansa expresses skepticism that Joffrey will let her go
Margaery expresses confidence Joffrey will do it to please her
Ser Loras's squire Olyvar flirts with him after the match
Olyvar and Loras have sex in Loras's chambers
Loras mentions he is engaged to be married
Olyvar passes this information to his real master, Littlefinger
Littlefinger prepares to pass the information on to Cersei
Littlefinger meets with Sansa and offers her a place on his ship to the Vale of Arryn
Sansa practices lying and tells Littlefinger they should wait until after Joffrey's wedding
Sansa fears for Littlefinger's safety if the plan fails
Littlefinger is suspicious of Sansa's motives but doesn't press the matter
Littlefinger says he is touched by Sansa's concern for his safety
Littlefinger insists that Sansa call him "Petyr"
Tyrion meets with Lady Olenna Tyrell to discuss the cost of the upcoming royal wedding
Tyrion expresses concern about the expense of the wedding
Olenna points out the Tyrells have supplied the city with soldiers and provisions for the war and winter
Olenna expresses boredom with financial matters
Olenna lists specific details of the Tyrells’ contributions to the crown
Olenna insists the royal wedding must proceed as planned to improve city morale and keep the people happy
Olenna warns that the people will blame the Lannisters for the war if the wedding is canceled
Tyrion tries to outmaneuver Olenna but fails
Olenna decides House Tyrell will pay for half the cost of the festivities
Tyrion is grateful for Olenna’s agreement
Tyrion heads to the Small Council chamber to report success to Tywin
Tyrion finds Cersei present as well, much to his annoyance
Tywin explains that the Tyrells are plotting to wed Sansa to Ser Loras
Tywin states he will not allow the Tyrells to steal "the key to the north"
Tyrion questions how Sansa is the key to the North, given Robb is still alive
Tywin explains that the Karstarks' desertion has cost Robb half his army
Tywin explains that Robb is badly outnumbered by the Lannister-Tyrell alliance and his days are numbered
Tywin explains that Theon Greyjoy murdered Robb's younger brothers at Winterfell, making Sansa Robb's heir
Tywin states that if Robb dies, rule of Winterfell and the North goes to Sansa and any man she marries
Tywin states he has no intention of letting the Tyrells get their hands on Sansa
Tyrion questions if it is wise to offend their allies by refusing the match
Tywin points out that the Tyrells' plan is a secret, so the Lannisters can't be said to be refusing them
Tywin notes the Tyrells won't go through with their plan until after Joffrey's wedding
Tywin states they need to act first by finding Sansa another husband
Tyrion realizes from his father and sister's comments that they are talking about him
Tyrion tries to protest, pointing out that Joffrey has made Sansa's life a living hell since her father's execution and that forcing Tyrion on her is cruel
Tywin questions if Tyrion plans to mistreat Sansa and asserts he is not concerned with the girl's happiness and neither should Tyrion
Tyrion protests that Sansa is a child
Cersei counters that Sansa has flowered and is now a woman who can bear children
Tyrion flatly refuses

Tywin points out Tyrion recently complained about not being rewarded for his actions in the Battle of the Blackwater, and says Sansa is a far better reward as heiress to the North, and that it is long past time Tyrion was wed
Tyrion, angered, snarls that he was wed before, referring to his past marriage to Tysha, and sarcastically asks if his father has forgotten
Tywin returns Tyrion's hateful glare and growls that he remembers all too well
Cersei gloats over the matter
Tywin commands that Cersei will wed Ser Loras
Cersei flatly refuses to marry Loras
Tywin insists on the marriages of Cersei and Tyrion to secure the North and the Reach for the Lannisters
Tywin points out that Cersei is still fertile and needs to marry and "breed"
Cersei protests that she is Queen Regent and not a brood mare
Tywin angrily roars that Cersei is his daughter and will do as she is told
Tywin commands Cersei to marry Loras to end the rumors about her and Jaime
Cersei earnestly begs Tywin not to force her into another arranged marriage
Tywin refuses to listen and storms out in disgust, raging about the disgrace to the Lannister name
Cersei shifts her gaze toward Tyrion to gauge his reaction
Cersei sees Tyrion is too glum about his own predicament to react

The_Climb.action: Tywin meets with Olenna Tyrell to discuss having Loras marry Cersei
Tywin and Olenna trade barbs about Cersei's age, her ability to have children, and accusations of incest with Jaime, as well as Loras's homosexuality
Tywin insists that Cersei should marry Loras
Olenna refuses to consent to the marriage, hinting that Joffrey is the result of incest
Tywin replies that if Joffrey is illegitimate, the Tyrells have thrown a flower into the dirt
Olenna says she will not risk another flower if Cersei cannot bear heirs
Tywin threatens to make Loras a knight of the Kingsguard, forcing him to give up marriage and inheritance
Olenna relents and consents to the marriage of Loras and Cersei
Sansa tries to get closer to Loras by discussing their hopefully impending wedding
Loras is uncomfortable being close to Sansa
Loras brightens when discussing the lavish wedding he has planned
Sansa becomes suspicious when she realizes Loras is more excited by the food and joust than by her
Loras placates Sansa with a compliment
Loras describes the wedding gown he’s envisioned for Sansa
Sansa says she’s looking forward to seeing Highgarden and leaving King’s Landing
Loras finds common ground with Sansa and agrees the Red Keep is "the most terrible place there is"
Cersei and Tyrion observe the couple from Cersei's chambers overlooking the garden
Tyrion sarcastically asks which of the four of them has it the worst
Tyrion accuses Cersei of trying to have him killed during the Battle of the Blackwater
Cersei remains quiet, tired of fighting Tyrion
Tyrion deduces that only two people can command the Kingsguard
Tyrion realizes Cersei has the authority but wouldn't command Ser Mandon Moore to kill him in public
Tyrion realizes Joffrey ordered Ser Mandon Moore to kill him
Cersei tells Tyrion his life is not in danger from Joffrey now that Tywin is the Hand
Cersei and Tyrion discuss Jaime's possible return to King's Landing
Cersei wonders where Jaime could possibly be
Cersei asks Tyrion which one of them should break the news to Sansa
Tyrion decides to dash Sansa's hopes sooner rather than later
Tyrion goes to Sansa's chamber
Tyrion asks Sansa for a private word
Sansa declines to dismiss Shae
Tyrion gives Shae a carefully coded apology for not telling her in private before breaking the news to Sansa
Lord Varys finds Lord Petyr Baelish in the throne room
Varys and Baelish discuss the Iron Throne and the pageantry and propaganda that creates nations and cements dynasties
Varys asserts that he serves the realm
Baelish scoffs that "the realm" is an invention of Aegon the Conqueror
Baelish tells Varys that he is aware of Varys' arrangement with Ros
Baelish says he has given Ros to "a friend" who is eager for a new experience
Baelish revels in the chaos he has caused, likening chaos to a ladder rather than a pit
Sansa weeps as Baelish's ship departs
Sansa's plans to leave King's Landing are in tatters
Joffrey coldly contemplates Ros's quarrel-ridden corpse in his bedchamber
Anguy trains Arya with a bow outside the hollow hill
Arya spots someone behind her target
Melisandre and a small group of Stannis’s men are revealed behind the target
Melisandre speaks to Thoros in High Valyrian, demanding to know what became of his mission to convert Robert Baratheon
Thoros bluntly tells Melisandre he failed
Melisandre is brought to the Brotherhood’s hideout
Melisandre is shocked to learn of Beric Dondarrion’s six deaths
Melisandre exclaims that Thoros should not have that kind of power
Thoros counters that he has no power, only asks for favors from the Lord of Light
Melisandre seems troubled by Thoros’s response
Melisandre says the Brotherhood has someone the Lord of Light needs
Melisandre’s men take Gendry into her custody
Arya protests when she sees Melisandre has given the Brotherhood two heavy sacks of gold in exchange
Arya confronts Melisandre, calling her a witch
Melisandre ignores Arya’s insult and looks into Arya’s eyes
Melisandre sees many other eyes that Arya will shut forever
Melisandre tells Arya they will meet again
Robb and his advisors meet with "Black Walder" and Lothar Frey to discuss an alliance for his planned attack on Casterly Rock
The Freys present Walder Frey's demands for an alliance
The demands include a formal apology from Robb, the castle Harrenhal and all its lands and incomes, and for Edmure Tully to marry Roslin Frey
Edmure is reluctant to marry a woman he has never met
The group convinces Edmure to go through with the arrangement
Roose Bolton has dinner with Jaime and Brienne of Tarth at Harrenhal
Bolton tells Jaime he will send him to King's Landing as restitution for his lost hand
Bolton sets the condition that Jaime must swear to tell his father that Bolton was not responsible for maiming him
Jaime assumes that Brienne will accompany him
Bolton intends to keep Brienne under arrest for abetting treason
tensions rise at camp between Osha and Meera Reed
Bran diffuses the situation between Osha and Meera
Jojen experiences a seizure while sleeping
Meera explains Jojen's seizures are caused by his visions
Jojen tells Bran that in his vision he saw Jon "on the wrong side of the wall, surrounded by enemies"
The cleaning boy awakens Theon Greyjoy to continue torturing him
The cleaning boy threatens to remove Theon's pinky finger if Theon cannot guess his true identity and their current location
Theon guesses the boy is the younger brother of Torrhen Karstark, Rickard Karstark's son, and that they are in Karhold
The cleaning boy gives the impression that Theon guessed right
The cleaning boy tells Theon he was lying
The cleaning boy begins flaying Theon's finger
The cleaning boy tells Theon he is torturing him only for his own amusement
Samwell Tarly and Gilly stop to camp during their journey to the Wall after fleeing Craster's Keep
Sam shows Gilly the Dragonglass dagger he found at the Fist of the First Men
Sam tells Gilly about Castle Black
Sam sings The Song of the Seven
Tormund leads the wildling party as they prepare to climb at the base of the Wall
Jon and Ygritte talk about their impending climb and their relationship
Ygritte reveals she knows Jon is still loyal to the Night's Watch, but tells him they must be loyal to each other instead
Ygritte strikes the Wall, causing a massive crack and dislodging a large sheet of ice
A large sheet of ice falls, sending several wildlings to their deaths
Ygritte and Jon hang precariously by their safety rope
Orell decides Tormund cannot continue climbing with Jon and Ygritte's weight and begins cutting the rope
Jon sees Orell cutting the rope and swings to his left, planting a hook in the ice to save himself and Ygritte
Jon and Ygritte reach the top of the Wall
Ygritte is awed by the view beyond the Wall and to the south
Jon and Ygritte share a passionate kiss

The_Bear_and_the_Maiden_Fair_(episode).action: Jon, Ygritte, and the wildlings led by Tormund survive the perilous climb of the Wall and make their way towards Castle Black to await Mance Rayder's signal
Jon confronts Orell about cutting his and Ygritte's rope loose on The Wall, nearly killing them
Orell tells Jon that Ygritte isn't complaining because she is a wildling and understood what needed to be done
Orell tells Jon that he will never be able to keep Ygritte
Orell, clearly jealous, confronts Ygritte and says he would be a better man for her than Jon, while expressing his distrust in Jon
Jon tries to persuade Ygritte that the wildlings' cause is doomed to failure, reminding her of the six Kings-Beyond-the-Wall who have failed in the past thousand years
Ygritte refuses to listen and points out that Jon is technically one of them; if it fails, so does he
Ygritte renews her passion for Jon and tells him that if they die, they die, but before they die they will live in each other
Two beautiful girls release Theon from the cross to which he is chained
The women lay Theon down on a bed
The women dramatically seduce Theon
The women say they have heard the heir to Pyke is well endowed and want first hand experience
Theon is distrustful, fearing another trick
One of the women, now naked, straddles Theon and begins a rocking motion
Theon becomes aroused
The familiar horn sounds
The boy enters
Theon is terrified and tries to escape
Theon is struck hard on the head and flung to the ground
The boy claims he has heard about Theon's famous body part and announces it must be his favorite
The boy brandishes an odd knife
Theon realizes the boy means to remove his penis
Theon begs for mercy
The boy quips that this is mercy, as he is not killing Theon, merely altering him
Two large men hold a screaming Theon down
The boy moves in to emasculate Theon
Bran Stark's party makes camp on their way to the Wall
Osha remains distrustful of Meera and Jojen Reed
Osha reacts with horror when Meera and Jojen mention they intend to go beyond the Wall
Osha bluntly refuses to go beyond the Wall
Osha explains she lost her husband, who then rose from the dead and tried to kill her
Osha rages that none of them understand what lies beyond the Wall and that there is nothing left for men there
Daenerys's army marches toward Yunkai, the next great city of Slaver's Bay
Daenerys and Barristan Selmy express confidence in taking the city, noting Yunkai's reputation for training bed slaves rather than soldiers
Ser Jorah warns that Yunkai's defenders will avoid open battle and use guerrilla tactics from behind their walls
Ser Jorah advises that conquering Yunkai does not help with the main goal of retaking Westeros
Daenerys insists on freeing the slaves of Yunkai, who number in the hundreds of thousands
Daenerys orders Grey Worm to send a messenger to Yunkai's rulers, demanding their surrender or threatening them with the same fate as Astapor
The Yunkish herald is carried into Daenerys's camp on a litter by slaves, along a road flanked by Unsullied
Missandei announces the newcomer as Razdal mo Eraz, one of the ruling "Wise Masters" of Yunkai
Daenerys meets Razdal in her pavilion, flanked by her dragons
Daenerys tosses pieces of meat to her dragons, who vie for her attention and fight over the meat
Daenerys's dragons snarl at Razdal when he gets too close
Razdal declares that Yunkai was an ancient civilization and boasts of its history of resisting conquerors
Daenerys comments that a hard-fought battle would give her Unsullied practice, showing she is not threatened
Razdal proposes that Yunkai will overlook Daenerys's attack on Astapor and provide her with gold and ships if she leaves Yunkai in peace
Daenerys makes a counter-offer to spare Razdal and the slave-masters if every slave in Yunkai is set free and given food, clothing, and property
Daenerys warns that Yunkai will be shown no mercy if her offer is rejected
Razdal takes offense and promises Yunkai will be more of a challenge than Astapor or Qarth
Razdal threatens Daenerys with the retribution of Yunkai's "powerful friends"
Drogon and his siblings roar in anger at Razdal's threat
Razdal protests that he was promised safe conduct
Daenerys counters that her dragons made no such promise and take offense to threats against their mother
The dragons prevent Razdal's slaves from reclaiming the chests of gold
Razdal storms out of the pavilion, muttering curses
Daenerys tells Jorah to find out more about Yunkai's "powerful friends" before attacking the city
A ship carrying Melisandre and Gendry leaves King's Landing and passes through Blackwater Bay
Melisandre stares at the broken wrecks of Stannis's fleet destroyed by wildfire
Melisandre asserts that her presence might have turned the tide of battle
Melisandre questions Gendry
Melisandre is surprised to learn Gendry is unaware of his father's real identity
Melisandre reveals to Gendry that he is King Robert Baratheon's bastard son as they pass by the Red Keep
Arya becomes disillusioned with the Brotherhood for selling Gendry
Arya takes an opportunity to escape and runs off into the night
Sandor Clegane, lying in wait, snatches up Arya before she gets too far
Robb Stark's army is forced to make camp due to heavy rain
Heavy rain delays the army's progress
Catelyn warns that Lord Walder Frey will take the delay as a deliberate insult
Edmure Tully points out that Frey is getting the wedding he wanted
Catelyn counters that Frey is getting a wedding, but not the one he wanted, and glares at Robb and his wife
Catelyn points out that Frey wanted one of his daughters wed to a king
Robb retorts that Edmure is the best match House Frey has been offered
Robb and Talisa prepare for bed
Talisa tells Robb she is pregnant with his child
Robb is gladdened by the news
Jaime speaks one last time to Brienne before he leaves Harrenhal
Jaime remarks that he owes Brienne a debt for keeping him alive and giving him a reason to live
Brienne tells Jaime to repay his debt by keeping his pledge
Jaime promises to keep his word to return Catelyn Stark's daughters to her
Jaime leaves Harrenhal in the company of Qyburn and a number of Bolton soldiers under Steelshanks' command
Qyburn accompanies Jaime to continue treating his arm, hoping to regain his maester's chain through Tywin's influence
Jaime jokes that Tywin will make Qyburn Grand Maester if he grows Jaime a new hand
Roose Bolton prepares to depart Harrenhal for the Twins under Robb Stark's orders
Roose Bolton requests that Jaime pass on his regards to Tywin
Jaime asks Roose Bolton to do the same for him with Robb
Locke, left in charge of Harrenhal, pokes fun at Jaime and sneers that he will 'take good care' of Brienne
Jaime ignores Locke's taunt and does not rise to the bait
Qyburn checks Jaime's stump as their party makes camp
Jaime questions Qyburn about how he lost his Maester's chain
Qyburn explains he lost his chain for performing vivisections on the sick and dying to better understand illness
Jaime is disgusted by Qyburn's revelation
Qyburn counters that what he learned has saved numerous lives and questions Jaime about how many he has killed compared to how many he has saved
Jaime responds that he has probably killed thousands, but saved half a million lives by killing the Mad King to save King's Landing
Qyburn is surprised by Jaime's answer
Jaime asks about Brienne
Qyburn confirms they sent a raven to Lord Selwyn Tarth
Lord Selwyn Tarth offers a ransom of 300 gold dragons for Brienne's safe return
Locke refuses the ransom offer
Locke believes Lord Selwyn is trying to cheat him because he thinks Tarth owns every sapphire mine in Westeros
Qyburn mentions most of the men would rather kill Brienne for entertainment before they die
Jaime feels guilty because his lie has placed Brienne in greater danger
Jaime urges Steelshanks to return to Harrenhal
Steelshanks initially refuses
Jaime convinces Steelshanks to go back by promising a reward and threatening Tywin Lannister's retribution
Jaime arrives at Harrenhal and is shocked and furious to find Brienne in a bear pit
Locke and his cronies throw Brienne into a bear pit, giving her only a wooden sword to defend herself
Locke and his cronies mockingly sing "The Bear and the Maiden Fair"
Jaime orders Locke to pull Brienne out and offers to pay ransom
Locke dismisses Jaime’s offer, preferring the spectacle
Jaime leaps into the pit to help Brienne
Steelshanks shoots the bear with a crossbow, distracting it
Jaime and Brienne are pulled out of the pit
Locke tries to stop Jaime and Brienne from leaving
Jaime tells Locke that Lord Bolton cares more about getting him back to King's Landing alive
Steelshanks and his men back up Jaime, preventing Locke from stopping them
Jaime departs Harrenhal with his escort and Brienne
Sansa and Tyrion are both unhappy about their impending marriage
Margaery consoles Sansa, reminding her that Tyrion is far from the worst Lannister and might make her happy
Bronn points out to Tyrion that he can wed Sansa, get a son and heir, and keep seeing Shae in private
Tyrion is concerned that Shae will not go along with the plan and is unenthusiastic about the situation
Bronn tells Tyrion he is wasting his time trying to be universally loved
Shae bluntly tells Tyrion she will no longer sleep with him after he weds Sansa
Tywin Lannister enters the throne room to answer a summons from King Joffrey
Tywin and Joffrey exchange false courtesy
Tywin asks Joffrey why he wished to see him
Joffrey asks for an update on what the Small Council has been doing
Tywin informs Joffrey that the king can attend any Small Council meeting
Joffrey claims he is too busy to attend the meetings and demands to know why the location has changed
Tywin says he is holding the meeting in the Tower of the Hand to save time
Joffrey complains about having to climb too many steps to attend
Tywin silently ascends the steps to stand next to the throne, glares down at Joffrey, and sardonically says he could arrange to have Joffrey carried
Joffrey, disconcerted, changes the subject to Daenerys Targaryen
Tywin is surprised that Joffrey knows about Daenerys
Tywin admits the reports are true that Daenerys is alive and has hatched dragons
Joffrey is puzzled why Tywin doesn't want to act against Daenerys
Tywin explains that Daenerys' dragons are probably just curiosities and not a threat
Tywin says even if the dragons are strong, they are far away and not a threat to Joffrey's rule
Tywin warns Joffrey to heed his advisor's counsel on matters he does not comprehend
Joffrey protests that he hasn't been counseled on anything
Tywin says he is advising Joffrey at this very moment and will inform him if necessary
Tywin smiles slightly, bows, and leaves Joffrey squirming on the Iron Throne
Their exchange makes it clear who is really ruling the kingdom

Second_Sons_(episode).action: Arya picks up a rock and stands over Sandor Clegane, planning to strike
Arya thinks Sandor is sound asleep
Sandor opens his eyes and tells Arya she has one chance to hit him and kill him, but if she fails he will break her hands
Arya sits sullenly on the horse with Sandor, refusing food he offers her
Sandor points out Arya could have been taken captive by someone far worse
Sandor tells Arya about Sansa and how he rescued her from would-be rapists
Arya disbelieves Sandor’s story
Sandor tells Arya to ask her sister if she ever sees her again
Arya and Sandor arrive at a river; Arya thinks it is the Blackwater
Sandor points out the river is the Red Fork of the Trident
Arya believes Sandor is taking her back to King’s Landing
Sandor reveals he is taking Arya to the Twins to ransom her to her mother and brother
Sandor tells Arya if she wasn’t so busy trying to kill him, they might make it in time for the wedding
Arya has a small, hidden smile as Sandor spurs the horse to a gallop
Daenerys, Jorah, and Barristan hide behind a ruined building to spy on the encampment of the Second Sons
Barristan explains that the Second Sons, though only 2,000 strong, are armored and mounted and could cause trouble for the Unsullied
Daenerys tells Barristan to organize a meeting with the Second Sons' captains
Daenerys says that men who fight for gold "can't afford to lose to a girl"
Daenerys hosts the captains Mero and Prendahl na Ghezn, along with Daario Naharis
Mero makes himself at home, states that Daenerys reminds him of a whore he knows in Lys, and feels up Missandei
Daenerys shrugs off Mero's insults
Prendahl and Mero refuse Daenerys's offer of an alliance
Daenerys replies that a fortnight ago she had no army, and a year before that she had no dragons
Daenerys gives the captains two days to make a decision and sends them off with a barrel of wine
Daario smiles over his back at Daenerys as they depart
Daenerys drops her pretence of cordiality and glowers at Mero's back
Daenerys instructs Barristan Selmy to kill Mero first if they must fight the Second Sons
Barristan Selmy replies that he would be glad to do so
Mero fondles a Yunkish pleasure-slave while discussing the situation with Prendahl and Daario
Prendahl admits that the Second Sons cannot defeat Daenerys's Unsullied
Mero suggests that they assassinate Daenerys
Mero gives the slave three coins: one from Volantis, one from Meereen, and one from Braavos
Mero tells the slave to randomly give the coins to the three mercenaries
The slave gives the coins to the three mercenaries
Mero says the one who gets the Braavosi coin will kill the queen
Daenerys bathes
Daenerys is surprised to learn Missandei speaks no fewer than 19 languages
Missandei explains that it only took Daenerys a year to gain a reasonable grasp of Dothraki
Daenerys bristles at the idea she speaks only reasonable Dothraki and switches to the language to teach Missandei a lesson
Missandei corrects Daenerys’s pronunciation
An Unsullied enters and holds a knife to Missandei’s throat, advising the women not to scream
The Unsullied removes his helmet, revealing himself as Daario
Daario explains he was ordered to kill Daenerys by his captains, but disagreed due to her beauty
Daario produces the severed heads of his captains
Daenerys, intrigued, rises from her bath and asks if Daario will swear fealty to her
Daario bends the knee and swears his sword, his men, and his heart to Daenerys
Melisandre arrives back at Dragonstone with Gendry
Stannis is less than impressed by the sight of Gendry
Stannis is bemused when Melisandre orders Gendry to be fed, bathed, and clothed
Melisandre orders Gendry to be fed, bathed, and clothed
Stannis believes it is pointless as they intend to sacrifice Gendry
Melisandre reveals the kindness is a sham to keep Gendry feeling secure
Melisandre compares the situation to keeping a sacrificial lamb from seeing the blade
Davos languishes in his cell
Stannis visits Davos in his cell
Stannis tells Davos about the impending sacrifice of Gendry
Davos protests, saying Gendry is innocent and has never wronged Stannis
Stannis argues that sacrificing Gendry will bring victory and save Westeros
Stannis asks Davos how he can doubt Melisandre's god, referencing visions and the shadow creature
Davos speculates that Stannis came because he knows what he's about to do is wrong and needs to hear the truth
Stannis extracts a promise from Davos not to act against Melisandre again
Stannis releases Davos from his cell
Melisandre seduces Gendry to distract him
Melisandre ties Gendry to the bed
Melisandre places leeches on Gendry's body
Stannis and Davos enter the room
Melisandre explains that Davos wanted a demonstration of the power in king's blood
Melisandre removes the leeches from Gendry
Melisandre lights a fire in a nearby brazier
Stannis throws the leeches into the flames at Melisandre's direction
Stannis recites the names of three people he wants dead as the leeches burn: "The usurper Robb Stark, the usurper Balon Greyjoy, the usurper Joffrey Baratheon."
Tyrion speaks with Sansa before their wedding at the Great Sept of Baelor
Tyrion acknowledges Sansa is not thrilled about marrying him
Tyrion promises Sansa he will not mistreat her
Sansa agrees there are worse Lannisters she could be wed to
Margaery Tyrell tries to ingratiate herself to Cersei before the wedding
Margaery comments that she and Cersei will technically be sisters soon
Cersei tells Margaery the story behind "The Rains of Castamere" and the destruction of House Reyne
Cersei threatens to have Margaery strangled in her sleep if she calls her sister again
Joffrey smugly escorts Sansa to the altar in place of her father
Joffrey removes the stool Tyrion was to stand on to cloak Sansa in Lannister colors
The congregation snickers at Joffrey's action
Lord Tywin scowls, quickly silencing the congregation
Tyrion, humiliated, asks Sansa to kneel
Sansa kneels
Tyrion places the cloak around Sansa's shoulders
The new High Septon begins the ceremony
Tyrion and Sansa have their reception dinner, which is grim and miserable
Tyrion spends the wedding feast getting deeply drunk
Tyrion makes light of his father's insistence that his inebriation will render him unfit to impregnate his wife
Tyrion drunkenly proclaims himself "the god of tits and wine"
Tyrion reminds his father that Tywin has long called him "a drunken little lust-filled beast"
Tyrion asserts that doing his duty in the marriage bed will not pose a problem
Joffrey drinks too much wine
Joffrey loses his inhibitions and becomes increasingly offensive to Sansa and Tyrion
Cersei ineffectually protests Joffrey's behavior
Joffrey gets up, flanked by his Kingsguard, and taunts Sansa about marrying a Lannister
Joffrey reflects that it doesn't matter which Lannister gets Sansa pregnant
Joffrey openly says he might want to rape Sansa after Tyrion is done with her, while the Kingsguard hold her down
Joffrey declares it time for the bedding ceremony
Tyrion angrily insists there will be no bedding ceremony
Joffrey keeps pushing the matter
Tyrion slams his dagger into the table and threatens Joffrey
The court stands in shocked silence
Joffrey seethes with outrage
Tyrion glares at Joffrey with pure hatred
Tywin defuses the situation by agreeing there will be no bedding ceremony and placates Joffrey
Tyrion swallows his anger and begins to act far more drunk than he actually is, intentionally humiliating himself
Tyrion says he only made a bad joke
Everyone in the room plays along to help the tension pass
Tyrion, pretending to be drunk, escorts Sansa to their bedchamber
Sansa nervously and slowly undresses
Tyrion tells Sansa to stop undressing
Tyrion decides not to consummate the marriage if Sansa does not want to
Tyrion says he will only consummate if Sansa changes her mind
Sansa questions what Tyrion will do if she never changes her mind
Tyrion sarcastically jokes "And so my watch begins"
Tyrion abruptly passes out on a nearby couch
Shae notices Sansa's bed sheets are not stained with blood and Sansa is still a virgin, and smiles
Samwell Tarly and Gilly continue on their journey to Castle Black
Samwell Tarly and Gilly take refuge in a destroyed cabin next to a heart tree
Two crows land on a branch on the heart tree
The crows begin to squawk loudly
Sam tries to light a fire, but is frustrated in his attempts
Gilly tries to light a fire and does it easily
Sam suggests that Gilly name her son
Gilly says she does not know any boy names
Sam gives Gilly some examples of boy names
Sam explains the difference between first and last names
Gilly says she likes the name Randyll, Sam's father's first name
Sam asks Gilly not to give the child that name
Sam shares the truth about his father and his upbringing marked by cruelty
Sam and Gilly realize they both have upbringings marked by cruelty in common
thunderous squawking of the crows interrupts them
Sam and Gilly go outside to investigate
Sam and Gilly discover hundreds of crows filling the nearby tree and on the structure, screeching violently
Gilly is horrified to see a White Walker approaching
Gilly realizes the White Walker is after her child
White Walker emerges from the trees; it is shown to be the same one that spared Sam before
Sam draws his sword and orders the Walker to halt
White Walker grabs Sam’s sword blade, shattering it
White Walker swats Sam aside with a backhand
White Walker bears down on Gilly, who refuses to give up her baby
White Walker reaches out to seize the infant
Sam staggers to his feet, pulls out his dragonglass dagger, and runs at the Walker
Sam plunges the dragonglass blade into the Walker’s left scapula
Walker screeches in agony, whirls to face Sam, recognizes him, and screeches again in disbelief
Walker falls to its knees and shatters, leaving only white dust and the dagger
Sam and Gilly race away in a panic
crows fly out of the trees and chase Sam and Gilly, screaming their hatred

The_Rains_of_Castamere_(episode).action: Daenerys and her commanders plan an attack on Yunkai
Daario Naharis suggests a plan to infiltrate the city using a lightly defended back gate
Jorah Mormont is skeptical of the plan and Daario's loyalty
Daenerys asks Grey Worm if he trusts Daario
Grey Worm says he trusts Daario
Daario touches Daenerys's hand, flirting for her favor
The captains depart
Ser Barristan asks to go along on the mission
Jorah reminds Ser Barristan that a Queensguard's place is at the Queen's side
Ser Jorah, Daario, and Grey Worm sneak into the city at night
Ser Jorah, Daario, and Grey Worm fight their way through the slave soldiers guarding Yunkai
Daario underestimates the number of soldiers, to Jorah's irritation
Ser Jorah, Daario, and Grey Worm are evenly matched with the slave soldiers, but only barely
Daenerys paces in her tent, impatiently
Daenerys asks Barristan how long a sacking of a city usually takes
Jorah and Grey Worm enter, covered in dust and gore but grinning widely
Jorah and Grey Worm announce that Yunkai is defeated
Daenerys is elated
Daenerys asks after Daario, who is still absent
Jorah pauses suspensefully
Daario strides into the room and kneels before Daenerys
Daario presents Yunkai's torn flag and says the city is hers
Samwell Tarly and Gilly continue marching south north of the Wall
Samwell and Gilly barely escape an encounter with a White Walker
Samwell kills the White Walker using a dragonglass dagger
Sam tells Gilly they are a considerable distance west of Castle Black
Sam explains that being west of Castle Black means they are close to the Nightfort
Sam explains his plan to get them across the Wall using a secret sally-port at the Nightfort
Gilly is amazed that Sam knows so much history just from reading books
Gilly insists that Sam is a wizard, much to Sam's delight
Gilly is awestruck when she catches a glimpse of the Wall
Bran and his group travel far enough north to leave the Seven Kingdoms and enter the Gift
The Reeds are puzzled by the emptiness of the countryside despite the Gift's good arable land
Bran explains that wildling attacks caused most of the smallfolk to flee the Gift over the centuries
Osha is discomforted by Bran's explanation
Bran explains that the Watch's diminishing numbers mean they don't need much support from the Gift anymore
Bran's group has not encountered any brothers of the Watch working the lands
Bran's group takes shelter in an abandoned mill to get out of a thunderstorm
a thunderstorm occurs
Jon and the wildling party encounter an old man who lives alone at a small farm breeding horses for the Night's Watch
The wildling party plans to attack the old man to steal his horses and gold
Jon insists the old man is no threat to them
Orell and Tormund intend to kill the old man to stop him from alerting the Night's Watch
The wildling party attacks the farmstead
Jon surreptitiously hits a rock with his sword, alerting the old man
The old man flees
Jon distracts Ygritte when she tries to shoot the fleeing man with an arrow
Bran and Jojen Reed discuss how to cross the Wall while in the mill
Meera spots the old horse breeder riding nearby
The old man is captured by the wildlings
Hodor, scared by thunder, begins yelling and threatens to give away their location
Bran uses his warg abilities to enter Hodor's mind and cause him to pass out
Tormund moves to kill the old man
Orell tells Tormund to have Jon kill the old man to prove his loyalty
Jon is unable to kill the innocent man
Ygritte kills the old man with an arrow
Tormund realizes Jon is still loyal to the Night's Watch and orders his men to kill Jon
Tormund restrains Ygritte to stop her from helping Jon
Jon battles with Orell
Jojen urges Bran to help Jon
Bran enters the mind of Summer, his direwolf, to aid Jon
Summer and Shaggydog kill two wildlings threatening Jon
Jon battles Orell to the death and kills him
Orell wargs into his pet eagle with his last strength
Orell's eagle swoops down and attacks Jon, clawing his face
Jon beats the eagle off
Jon mounts a nearby horse and escapes, leaving Ygritte and heading back to the Wall
Bran asks Osha to take Rickon to Last Hearth, the home of House Umber
Bran, Meera, and Jojen plan to head through the Wall in search of the Three-Eyed Raven
Osha promises to look after Rickon
Osha orders the Reed siblings to look after Bran
Osha, Rickon, and Shaggydog depart in the dead of night
Robb consults with his mother about attacking Casterly Rock
Robb claims it is a dangerous move, but says if Tywin's castle is taken, the lords of Westeros will realize Tywin is not invincible
Catelyn points out the plan requires Lord Frey's cooperation and warns that if reinforcements arrive from King's Landing before the castle is taken, the Stark host will be destroyed
Catelyn silently examines the map
Catelyn says in a harsh voice, "Show them how it feels to lose what they love"
Robb's army arrives at the Twins for Edmure's wedding
Lord Walder Frey insults Robb and his wife
Robb endures Lord Frey's insults
Robb makes a public apology to Lord Frey's daughters and granddaughters for breaking his promise to marry one of them
Lord Frey accepts the apology
Lord Frey offers the Starks and their men his hospitality
Edmure is introduced to his bride Roslin Frey
Edmure discovers that Roslin is a beauty, much to his relief
The wedding and the feast that follows are celebratory and lively affairs
All the participants are in high spirits
Edmure focuses his attentions solely on his bride, clearly in love
Lord Walder calls for the bedding ceremony
Robb agrees to the bedding ceremony
The bride and groom are carried off to their wedding bed
Roslin is carried off by the male guests
Edmure is collected by the Frey women and follows closely
Brynden Tully excuses himself from the celebration to "find a tree to piss on" after drinking too much wine
Catelyn becomes suspicious when she notices Black Walder Rivers close the banquet hall doors
Black Walder Rivers closes the banquet hall doors
Musicians in the gallery begin playing "The Rains of Castamere"
Catelyn notices the musicians playing "The Rains of Castamere"
Walder rises to make a toast to Robb
Catelyn, seated beside Roose Bolton, notices Roose is wearing mail under his clothing
Catelyn realizes they are in a trap
Catelyn slaps Roose across the face and screams a warning to Robb
Walder signals his men to attack
Lothar draws a knife and repeatedly stabs the pregnant Talisa in the stomach, killing her unborn child
Talisa collapses to the ground as chaos surrounds
Musicians shoot Robb with crossbows several times
Robb falls to the floor
Numerous other Stark men are killed by crossbow bolts or set upon by Frey soldiers
Musician shoots Catelyn in the back
Catelyn falls to the floor
Arya and the Hound arrive at the Twins in disguise
Frey guards turn Arya and the Hound away
Arya gives Sandor the slip and sneaks past the gate guards
Arya witnesses Frey men kill Stark soldiers and Grey Wind
Frey men kill Stark soldiers and Grey Wind
Arya makes a desperate run for the castle
The Hound stops Arya and tells her it is too late
Arya refuses to stop
The Hound knocks Arya unconscious and carries her out of the castle to safety
Catelyn crawls to safety beneath a table
Walder motions the soldiers to cease their slaughter
Walder mocks Robb as he limps to his dying wife
Robb limps to his dying wife, Talisa
Robb holds Talisa in his arms as she dies
Catelyn sees an opportunity and limps out from her hiding spot
Catelyn seizes Walder’s young wife, Joyeuse, from under his table
Catelyn holds a knife to Joyeuse’s throat and threatens to kill her if Walder does not negotiate
Walder refuses Catelyn, recalling Robb’s previous betrayed oath
Catelyn begs that they take her hostage and allow Robb to leave
Robb lingers despondent and heartbroken beside his wife's corpse
Catelyn swears on her honor as both a Tully and a Stark that if Walder does not let Robb go, she will kill his wife
Walder chuckles and nonchalantly responds, "I'll find another."
Robb stands and utters, "Mother"
Roose Bolton seizes Robb and whispers, "The Lannisters send their regards,"
Roose Bolton stabs Robb in the heart
Robb maintains eye contact with Catelyn as he collapses to the floor
Catelyn cries out in anguish and kills Walder's wife, fulfilling her promise
Catelyn stands catatonic with grief, accepting her fate
Black Walder slips up behind Catelyn and viciously cuts her throat
Catelyn falls to the floor with a thud

Mhysa.action: Northern lords are slaughtered in the main hall during the Red Wedding
House Frey soldiers ambush the Stark army in its own camp outside the castle walls
Northern army is taken by surprise and massacred due to betrayal
Betrayers torch all of the camp's tents, burning many men alive inside
Frey men mutilate the dead and dying
Roose Bolton watches the massacre from one of the twin towers of the Frey stronghold
Sandor Clegane attempts to slip away amidst the carnage on a horse, along with the unconscious Arya Stark, by casually riding away while carrying a Frey banner
Arya regains consciousness before they can leave
Sandor and Arya turn to see a group of Frey men shouting as they march through the main gate
The Freys desecrate Robb Stark's corpse by decapitating it and attaching the severed head of his direwolf Grey Wind in its place
The Freys parade the mutilated body around the castle on a horse while mockingly chanting "King in the North! King in the North! Here he comes, the King in the North!"
The crowds of Frey soldiers cheer and laugh at the spectacle
Sandor is horrified by the gruesome spectacle
Sandor holds Arya
Arya looks on with a dead stare
The Hound rides away with Arya, undisturbed thanks to the Frey banner in his hand
servants clean up the pools of blood left by former wedding guests in the great hall of the castle
Walder Frey and Roose Bolton gloat over their victory and the rewards their treachery has won them
Walder Frey becomes Lord of Riverrun
The Freys replace the Tullys as the Lannisters' vassals in the Riverlands
Roose Bolton is appointed as the new Warden of the North, replacing the Starks
Tywin Lannister intends to eventually give the position of Warden of the North to a future Lannister heir produced by Tyrion and Sansa
Bolton expresses concern that Brynden Tully escaped the massacre
Walder dismisses Bolton's worries, pointing out that the Blackfish and the Tully garrison at Riverrun are alone and outnumbered
Bolton remains visibly concerned
Bolton expresses contempt for Robb Stark, calling him arrogant, politically incompetent, and unwilling to take Roose's advice
Walder questions Roose if he intends to rule the North from Winterfell
Roose says he might move his ruling seat to Winterfell after it is rebuilt
Walder asks how Winterfell ended up a ruin
Roose explains that Ramsay Snow offered Robb Stark's terms to the Ironborn, promising mercy if they handed over Theon Greyjoy
Roose reveals that Ramsay had his own way of doing things after the Ironborn surrendered
Ramsay Snow eats his breakfast of a thick sausage
Ramsay taunts Theon about his emasculation by pointing to his sewn up and blood-stained trousers
Ramsay laughs as Theon begs for death as a release from the nightmare
Theon begs for death
Ramsay tells Theon he is more valuable alive than dead and refuses his request
Ramsay notes that Theon reeks and decides his new name shall be Reek
Theon refuses to acknowledge the name and insists he is Theon Greyjoy
Ramsay beats Theon
Theon breaks down and says his name is Reek
Balon Greyjoy reads a message from Lord Bolton's bastard
Yara Greyjoy cautiously opens a box crudely carved with the Greyjoy kraken
Ramsay's letter explains the box contains "Theon's favorite toy"
Ramsay's message threatens the Ironborn to withdraw from the North by the full moon or receive more pieces of Theon
Ramsay's message threatens that any remaining Ironborn will be flayed alive
Ramsay flays alive Theon's twenty Ironborn followers at Winterfell after they surrendered
Ramsay had promised the Ironborn safe passage
Ramsay and his soldiers from House Bolton burn Winterfell to the ground
Ramsay sends false reports for his father Roose to deliver to Robb Stark, claiming the Ironborn were responsible for burning Winterfell
The Boltons prepare to betray the Starks months before the Red Wedding
Balon acts indifferent to Theon's situation because Theon defied his orders and Yara's common sense by staying at Winterfell
Balon decides Theon cannot further the Greyjoy line as the contents of the box prove
Yara does not share her father's attitude toward her last living brother
Yara announces her intention to take her fastest ship with her fifty best men, sail around Westeros, besiege the Dreadfort, and liberate Theon
Arya and Sandor Clegane ride away from the outskirts of the Twins in the Riverlands
Arya and Sandor Clegane pass close to a small group of Frey men
A Frey man brags about cutting off Grey Wind's head and attaching it to Robb Stark's body
Arya approaches the Frey men and sweetly asks to warm herself by the fire
Arya offers the coin given to her by Jaqen H'ghar as payment
A Frey man investigates the coin
Arya stabs the Frey man in the neck
Sandor Clegane deals with the other Frey men
Sandor Clegane demands to know where Arya got the knife
Arya reveals the knife is his
Sandor Clegane admonishes Arya to let him know next time she plans something like that
Sandor Clegane grumbles and cleans up the bodies
Arya recovers the coin and whispers, "valar morghulis"
Tyrion and Sansa walk through the gardens in King's Landing
a pair of passing gentlemen snigger at Tyrion
Sansa tells Tyrion to pay them no mind
Tyrion reminds Sansa that he is used to it, as he has always been a dwarf
Tyrion states that he is not Joffrey and would rather bide his time and humiliate the gentlemen
Sansa playfully follows Tyrion's line of thinking and suggests "sheep-shifting" as a prank
Tyrion is amused but confused by the term, realizing Sansa has misheard the word "shit"
Podrick Payne calls Tyrion to a meeting of the Small Council, rescuing him from having to explain Sansa's mistake
Pycelle dithers over giving Tyrion a coded message from Lord Frey
Joffrey jubilantly explains the message’s meaning: Robb and Catelyn Stark are both dead
Tyrion is visibly startled by the news, foreseeing long-term problems
Joffrey brushes off Tyrion’s concerns and gleefully states he intends to serve Robb’s severed head to Sansa at his wedding feast
None of the Small Council are able to mask their distaste for Joffrey’s idea
Varys points out that Sansa is Joffrey’s aunt by marriage and should be given basic respect
Cersei forces a smile and insists to the rest of the council that Joffrey was only joking and did not mean it
Joffrey is puzzled and states to his mother that he did mean it, and he will have Robb's head served at his wedding feast
Tyrion flatly forbids it and tells Joffrey that Sansa isn't his to torment anymore
Joffrey angrily proclaims that he is the king, and everyone is his to torment
Tywin counters that any man who has to remind his subjects that he is king, is no true king
Joffrey goes apoplectic and tries to fight his grandfather, deriding Tywin as a coward who hid under Casterly Rock while King Robert won the real war
The entire Small Council tenses, waiting for Tywin to respond to the insult
Tywin coldly and calmly declares that the king is tired and advises the Queen Regent to see him to bed
Joffrey weakly protests that he is not tired but leaves with his mother
Tywin asks Pycelle to give Joffrey a sedative to make sure he goes to sleep
Tywin dismisses the rest of the council, save Tyrion
Tyrion drily notes that the Lord of the Seven Kingdoms has been sent to bed without his supper
Tyrion confronts Tywin about his part in the Red Wedding
Tyrion deduces that Tywin must have orchestrated the Red Wedding, as Walder Frey is not brave enough to have carried it out on his own
Tywin confirms his involvement in the Red Wedding
Tyrion expresses concern that the Northerners will never forget nor forgive such an outrage
Tywin argues he wants the Northerners to remember so they will be wary of rebelling again
Tyrion shows his distaste for Tywin's choice of a wedding as the place to inflict the slaughter
Tywin responds by asking why killing ten thousand soldiers in battle is nobler than killing a dozen guests at a wedding
Tywin waxes poetic about family, noting Tyrion's failure to consummate his marriage to Sansa
Tyrion refuses to force himself on Sansa to do his duty
Tyrion notes that Sansa is unlikely to warm to him after the Lannisters murdered her mother and brother
Tyrion demands to know when Tywin ever put the family's interests above his own
Tywin retorts that he put the family's interests first on the day Tyrion was born
Tywin says he wanted to throw Tyrion into the sea as a newborn, but spared and raised him as a Lannister
Tyrion returns to his chambers
Tyrion calls for Sansa
Sansa regards Tyrion with a forlorn, tear-streaked face, confirming she was already informed of her family's death
Tyrion backs out, knowing his sympathy would be cold comfort
Varys meets with Shae in the Red Keep
Varys tells Shae he knows she is genuinely in love with Tyrion
Varys offers Shae diamonds and urges her to leave immediately for Essos to build a new life
Varys explains that Tyrion is the last hope for the Seven Kingdoms' regime and that Shae is a weakness
Shae confirms she is in love with Tyrion and cares deeply for Sansa
Shae states she will not leave until or unless Tyrion asks her to
Jaime, Brienne, and Qyburn arrive in King's Landing
City folk do not recognize Jaime after his long absence
Jaime, Brienne, and Qyburn make their way to the Red Keep
Jaime immediately calls on Cersei
Cersei regards Jaime with a confused look as she takes in his disheveled appearance and missing hand
Davos tests his new-found literacy on Stannis's correspondence
Davos declines Shireen's invitation to read more tales of Aegon I Targaryen
Davos expresses dissatisfaction about the odd spelling of the word "night"
horns signal Melisandre's intent to sacrifice Gendry
Davos hears the horns
Davos argues with Stannis about sacrificing Gendry
Stannis is convinced by Melisandre that using Gendry in bloodmagic will give him power
Davos fails to convince Stannis
Davos frees Gendry, giving him a rowboat and directions to King's Landing
Stannis sentences Davos to death
Davos produces a letter from the Night's Watch
Davos insists Stannis must assist the black brothers and will need Davos's help
Melisandre burns the letter
Melisandre acknowledges the true war is in the North and Davos has a role to play
Stannis sarcastically laughs at the Lord of Light's sense of humor
Stannis notes the god Davos mocks has chosen him for a higher purpose
Meera tells Bran, Hodor, and Jojen that the Nightfort is safe to enter
Bran tells the story of the Rat Cook around the fire
Bran is awoken by a sound during the night
Bran wakes his companions
The group sees something large climbing out of the castle well
Meera attacks and overpowers the intruder
The intruder is revealed to be Sam, accompanied by Gilly
Sam deduces Bran's identity by recognizing his direwolf and Hodor
Bran asks Sam to take him and his group north of the Wall
Sam protests, citing the threat of the White Walkers and the undead horde
Sam relents and takes them through the passage
Sam gives the group the rest of his dragonglass supply and explains its power
The groups head in different directions: Sam and Gilly to Castle Black, Bran and his companions beyond the Wall
Sam and Gilly meet with Maester Aemon at Castle Black
Maester Aemon is furious at the prospect of Sam having violated his vows
Sam defends himself by reciting the Night's Watch oath and repeating Jeor Mormont's assertion about the Wall
Maester Aemon's demeanor softens upon learning Gilly was one of Craster's wives
Maester Aemon acknowledges Gilly is now a refugee and should be sheltered
Maester Aemon orders Sam to begin writing letters immediately
Maester Aemon orders Sam to make sure all forty-four of Castle Black's ravens are well-fed
Maester Aemon declares every raven is to fly that night to warn Westeros of the White Walkers' return
Jon stops to rest and tend to his injuries after fleeing the wildlings
Ygritte confronts Jon with an arrow nocked to shoot him
Jon tries to talk Ygritte out of shooting him, insisting that he still loves her and that his loyalty to the Watch was never gone
Ygritte’s feelings for Jon are clearly reciprocated, but she is still angered by his betrayal
Ygritte shoots Jon three times with her bow as he flees
Ygritte watches Jon go with tears in her eyes
Jon is badly injured and hovers in and out of consciousness
Jon's horse carries him the rest of the way to Castle Black
Jon reaches safety at Castle Black
guards bring Jon inside the castle
Jon encounters Sam and Pypar
Sam and Pypar are overjoyed to see Jon and insist his injuries be taken care of
Daenerys and her advisors wait outside the walls of Yunkai for the city's slaves to appear
Daenerys frets that the Yunkish slaves might have grown to like their chains and will not welcome freedom
The gates of Yunkai open and the freedmen pour out
Missandei begins to tell the freedmen how Daenerys the Unburnt freed them
Daenerys interrupts Missandei and says it is the slaves' own choice to reach for their freedom
One of the freedmen stretches his hand towards Daenerys and calls out "mhysa"
Other freedmen follow suit, until the entire crowd is chanting "mhysa"
Daenerys turns to Missandei, bewildered
Missandei reveals that "mhysa" means "mother" in the Old Ghiscari language
The freedmen advance on Daenerys
The Unsullied step into formation to protect Daenerys
Daenerys tells the Unsullied to stand down
Daenerys tells her dragons to fly
Daenerys steps out of the protection of the Unsullied and into the crowd
The crowd continues chanting and embraces Daenerys
Daenerys is embraced by the former slaves, offering a glimmer of hope

Two_Swords.action: Tywin has the Stark ancestral Valyrian steel greatsword, Ice, melted down and re-forged into two smaller swords
Tywin watches the smiths re-forge the sword
Tywin tosses the sword's wolf pelt scabbard on the smith's fire and smiles as he watches it burn
Tywin presents one of the new swords to Jaime as a gift
Jaime is impressed by the Valyrian steel sword
Tywin tells Jaime he acquired the original sword from "someone who no longer needed it"
Tywin explains he commissioned a Volantene blacksmith to re-forge the sword
Tywin asks Jaime to be his heir and return to Casterly Rock
Jaime insists on remaining in the Kingsguard
Tywin becomes furious at Jaime's disobedience and lack of ambition
Tywin tells Jaime to keep the sword anyway
Tyrion, Bronn, and Podrick wait on a road outside the city for Prince Doran Martell's arrival
Tyrion quizzes Bronn on the sigils of the Dornish houses
Bronn fails to answer the sigil questions
Podrick answers the sigil questions perfectly
Lord Blackmont approaches on horseback and informs them that Prince Doran is in poor health and remained at Sunspear
Lord Blackmont says Prince Oberyn will attend the wedding instead and arrived before dawn
Tyrion assigns the reception of the Dornish Lords to the City Watch
