Tyrion says they should find Oberyn as soon as possible "before he kills somebody or several somebodies"
Oberyn and Ellaria examine prostitutes in a brothel owned by Petyr Baelish, managed by Olyvar
Oberyn and Ellaria choose the contortionist Marei
Oberyn orders Olyvar to get undressed
Oberyn hears men singing "The Rains of Castamere" and becomes infuriated
Oberyn confronts two Lannister soldiers who insult him and Ellaria
Oberyn moves closer and chastises the soldiers for being "pink little men who are far too slow on the draw"
One of the soldiers reaches for his sword
Oberyn quickly stabs the soldier through the wrist with a dagger
Oberyn tells the other soldier his friend will only live if he immediately gets help
Tyrion and Bronn enter the brothel
Oberyn and Tyrion step outside to speak privately
Oberyn says he wants revenge for the murder of his niece Rhaenys, nephew Aegon, and the rape and murder of his sister Elia during the sack of King's Landing
Oberyn states he wants Gregor Clegane and Tywin, whom he believes gave the order for Elia and her children to be killed
Tyrion, Sansa, Shae, and another handmaiden sit in a garden
Sansa is distraught over the deaths of her brother and mother
Tyrion attempts to console Sansa but fails
Sansa recalls the gruesome details of the Red Wedding and the desecration of her mother and brother's corpses
Sansa leaves for the godswood, not to pray but to be alone
Tyrion returns and finds Shae in his bed
Tyrion scolds Shae because no one can find out about their relationship
Shae attempts to seduce Tyrion
Tyrion resists Shae's advances
Shae accuses Tyrion of trying to bribe her with diamonds and send her to the Free Cities
Tyrion is confused, not knowing what Shae is talking about
Shae angrily leaves the room
Handmaiden Bernadette, hiding behind a pillar, overhears Tyrion and Shae's conversation and spots Shae leaving
Cersei has a golden prosthetic hand made for Jaime
Qyburn attaches the golden prosthetic hand to Jaime's stump
Qyburn asks Cersei about her symptoms
Cersei says her symptoms are gone
Qyburn departs
Jaime tells Cersei he will stay in the Kingsguard with one hand to stay close to her
Jaime tries to kiss Cersei
Cersei turns away from Jaime
Jaime is confused and angry
Cersei implies she has taken other lovers and that their relationship has changed because of his absence
Jaime is wounded by Cersei's statement
Bernadette enters and says she has important information for Cersei
Preparations for the royal wedding continue
Margaery Tyrell and Olenna are interrupted by Brienne of Tarth
Olenna is delighted to see the truth of the rumors about Brienne
Brienne requests a private word with Margaery
Brienne tells Margaery the truth of Renly's death
Brienne vows to one day exact revenge on Stannis Baratheon
Brienne and Margaery walk past a statue of King Joffrey standing over a dead direwolf
Statue commemorates the victory over the northern rebellion
Jaime, Ser Meryn Trant, and King Joffrey discuss security at the upcoming wedding
Jaime is irked by Joffrey's arrogance and Meryn's sycophantic agreement with the king
Joffrey brags that he is responsible for keeping the people fed
Jaime notes that feeding the people is more the doing of the Tyrells
Joffrey claims he singlehandedly won the war
Jaime points out that the war is far from won and that Stannis is still alive and may raise fresh armies
Joffrey dismisses the threat posed by Stannis after the Battle of the Blackwater
Joffrey leafs through the Book of Brothers, which details the history of the Kingsguard
Joffrey reads Jaime's brief entry and sneers that someone must have forgotten to record all of Jaime's great deeds
Joffrey questions Jaime's ability to serve in the Kingsguard with one hand
Jaime defensively quips that using his left hand makes for more of a challenge
Sansa prays alone in the godswood
Jaime and Brienne watch Sansa
Brienne expects Jaime to uphold his oath to return Catelyn Stark's daughters in return for his freedom
Jaime counters by pointing out the situation is complicated by Catelyn's death and Sansa's marriage to Tyrion
Jaime assumes Arya is dead, because no one has seen her since her father's death
Sansa leaves the godswood
Dontos Hollard approaches Sansa
Dontos thanks Sansa for convincing Joffrey to spare his life
Dontos gives Sansa his mother's necklace as a token of gratitude
Ygritte and Tormund stand outside a stone ravine
Tormund chides Ygritte for failing to finish off Jon Snow
Scouts signal that someone is approaching
Styr and a band of Thenns approach through the ravine
Tormund comments out of earshot about how much he hates Thenns
Styr says his party raided a village south of the ravine and gathered meat
Styr says the meat tastes better south of the Wall
Styr chides Tormund for losing their warg and Jon
Styr reminds Tormund that the Night's Watch are feasting and getting fat at Castle Black
Styr suggests Tormund taste crow meat before he dies, as a human arm roasts over a fire
Jon survives the injuries inflicted by Ygritte and largely recovers, though he grimaces while getting dressed and walks with a slight limp
Jon is told of Robb's death
Jon shares with Samwell that he was always jealous of Robb for having more of their father's affection and for being better at riding, fighting, and with girls
Jon says he could never bring himself to hate Robb
Sam responds that he has felt the same way about Jon, that Jon is better than him at everything apart from reading
Sam is sent to retrieve Jon
Jon is summoned to testify before a panel of five sworn brothers, including Maester Aemon, Alliser Thorne, Othell Yarwyck, and Janos Slynt
Thorne and Slynt are openly hostile to Jon, disbelieving much of what he says
Jon admits to killing Qhorin at the hearing
Jon explains that Qhorin wanted Jon to kill him to give Jon credibility with the wildlings
Slynt dismisses Jon as "the bastard son of a traitor"
Thorne reacts with incredulity that Jon would presume to act like he knew Qhorin
Jon responds by saying Thorne should know Qhorin would have done anything to protect the Night's Watch
Slynt accuses Jon of going native and talking like the wildlings
Jon snaps back, acknowledging he talks like a wildling
Jon admits to living amongst the wildlings, eating with them, and climbing the Wall with them
Jon catches himself before admitting he lay with a wildling, but says it anyway
Slynt calls for Jon's execution for breaking one of his vows
Aemon points out the impracticality of executing every ranger who lays with a girl, saying the Wall would be manned by headless men
Thorne says there is a difference between sneaking off to the Mole's Town brothel and sleeping with the enemy
Jon does not defend himself
Jon says that Mance Rayder intends to attack the Night's Watch with his army of 100,000 wildlings and gives intelligence on Mance's plan of attack
Thorne disbelieves what Jon has to say
Slynt disbelieves what Jon has to say
Aemon realizes that Jon is speaking the truth
The hearing ends with Jon being allowed to keep his head
Thorne expresses his distrust for Jon after Jon leaves
Arya and Sandor Clegane travel through the Riverlands on a single horse
Arya and Sandor Clegane pass by overturned carts and dead bodies, including those of children
Arya asks when she is going to get her own horse and complains about the Hound's stench
Sandor scoffs at the idea of giving Arya her own horse, noting horses are hard to come by and Arya is the only thing of value he has
Sandor explains he did not take anything of value when he left King's Landing
Arya assures Sandor she has no plans to run off, saying she would die by nightfall without him
Sandor reminds Arya she still has an aunt in the Vale, Lysa Arryn, to whom he plans to ransom her
Arya and the Hound come upon a tavern
Arya suggests attacking the men in the tavern
The Hound expresses reluctance because there are five men
Two men exit the tavern to relieve themselves
Arya recognizes one of the men as Polliver, who took her sword Needle and killed her friend Lommy
Arya notices Polliver still has Needle
Arya runs off to get Needle back
The Hound chases after Arya, stopping her at the tavern's door
Arya and the Hound are seen and choose to go into the tavern anyway
Polliver and the other men make trouble and intend to rape the Innkeeper's daughter
Arya and the Hound go to a table far away from everyone else
Polliver recognizes the Hound and walks over to their table
Polliver strikes up a conversation with the Hound
Polliver does not recognize Arya and assumes she is the Hound's latest sexual conquest
Polliver implies that he and his men intend to rape and pillage on their way back to King's Landing and invites the Hound to come along
Polliver says that he and his men wear the King's colors and can do whatever they like
The Hound leans forward and states emphatically, "fuck the king."
The conversation becomes tense and turns into a standoff
They draw their swords
The Hound fights the five men alone
Arya stands in the corner
The Hound dispatches two of the men easily
The Hound fights off the rest, stabbing one in the groin
Another man gets the Hound on the ground, holding a dagger to his throat
The Hound fights him off, stabbing him repeatedly in the head with his own dagger
Arya smashes a pot over the head of one of the men
Arya stabs the man with his own long sword
Arya slashes Polliver's leg from behind, causing him to fall to the ground
Arya takes Needle from Polliver and stands over his prone form
Arya echoes what Polliver had said to Lommy before killing him
Polliver does not recognize Arya and has no idea what she is talking about
Arya repeats what Polliver had said to her when he took Needle
Polliver recognizes Arya and becomes frightened
Arya kills Polliver by driving Needle slowly into his throat
Arya and the Hound leave the tavern, taking a horse for Arya and a chicken for the Hound
The Hound leaves the tavern with Arya, taking a chicken
Daenerys sits near the sea with her three dragons
Daenerys strokes Drogon's head
Rhaegal and Viserion fight over a dead lamb
Drogon joins the fight over the dead lamb
Daenerys tries to calm Drogon down
Drogon snaps at Daenerys with a hiss as a warning
Drogon roars and goes to contest the kill
Daenerys is left visibly shaken by the act of her own children threatening her and outgrowing her influence
Jorah Mormont approaches Daenerys
Jorah Mormont tells Daenerys that dragons can never be tamed, not even by their mother
Daenerys returns to her Unsullied army to resume the march to Meereen
Daenerys notices Grey Worm and Daario Naharis are absent
Daenerys sets out to find Grey Worm and Daario after being told they are "gambling"
Daenerys finds Grey Worm and Daario sitting face-to-face holding their weapons in front of them
Grey Worm and Daario contest their endurance since midnight
Daario explains they are deciding who will ride up front with Daenerys in the vanguard
Daenerys, frustrated, states the honor goes to Ser Jorah and Ser Barristan for not keeping her waiting
Daenerys orders Grey Worm and Daario to ride at the back of the army with the livestock
Daenerys adds that the last man holding his sword shall find a new queen to fight for
Grey Worm and Daario immediately drop their weapons
Daenerys speaks with Missandei on the road to Meereen
Missandei tells Daenerys that Meereen would be wise to fear her approach
Daario meets Daenerys and Missandei on the cliff side
Daenerys expresses annoyance when Daario shows her flowers he has picked
Daario reveals the flowers represent a portrait of the landscape and serve various purposes
Daario explains that knowing her surroundings is important to Daenerys' strategy
The marching army halts
Daenerys goes to the forefront
Daenerys discovers a crucified slave child, dead and her hand pointing the way to Meereen
Ser Jorah tells Daenerys there is one crucified child for each mile to Meereen, 163 in total
Ser Barristan offers to have outriders go ahead to bury the children
Daenerys refuses Ser Barristan's offer
Daenerys orders that each child be buried and their collars removed, but not before she has looked upon each face

The_Lion_and_the_Rose.action: Melisandre presides over a public burning ceremony, setting three people chained to stakes ablaze as an offering to the Lord of Light
Axell Florent, Queen Selyse Baratheon's brother, is executed for his lack of faith
Ser Davos Seaworth watches the proceedings, holding his tongue but showing clear disgust for the ritual and Melisandre
Melisandre remains silent throughout the proceedings
Selyse takes the greatest pleasure in the ritual
Stannis and Selyse eat dinner with Melisandre as a guest
Stannis and Selyse argue over their daughter, Princess Shireen Baratheon
Selyse claims Shireen's disfigurement is a punishment from the Lord of Light
Stannis forbids Selyse from physically chastising Shireen
Selyse suggests Melisandre speak to Shireen
Stannis does not protest Selyse's suggestion
Melisandre appears uninterested in speaking to Shireen
Melisandre speaks with Shireen, who heard the death of her uncle from her bedroom window
Melisandre explains in gentle yet matter-of-fact terms that the Faith of the Seven and its holy books are lies
Melisandre insists there are only two gods, the Lord of Light and the Great Other, constantly at war
Shireen asks if there is any truth to the Faith's belief in seven heavens and seven hells
Melisandre replies "There is only one hell... the one we live in now"
Ramsay hunts a peasant girl for sport in the forests surrounding the Dreadfort
Theon Greyjoy (Reek) and Myranda accompany Ramsay, along with a pack of savage dogs
The dogs bring down and tear the peasant girl to pieces
Reek is horrified by the girl's fate
Roose Bolton arrives at the Dreadfort with a detachment of his army, including Locke and his new wife, Walda Bolton
Ramsay greets his father and new step-mother
Ramsay privately congratulates Locke for maiming the Kingslayer
Roose wants to see Ramsay's captive, Theon Greyjoy
Theon is brought to Roose's chambers
Roose is disgusted and angered to learn Ramsay has tortured and flayed Theon
Roose notes Theon was a valuable hostage as Balon Greyjoy's sole surviving male heir
Roose notes he has been named Warden of the North
Roose notes Tywin Lannister will not help him reclaim the north from the Ironborn
Roose had intended on trading Theon for Moat Cailin, a strategically placed fortress held by Greyjoy forces
Ramsay retorts that he already sent terms and Balon refused him
Roose is furious that Ramsay acted without his consent
Ramsay demonstrates that his actions have made Reek docile and unable to betray them
Ramsay orders Reek to shave him
Ramsay cajoles Reek into admitting that Bran and Rickon Stark are still alive
Ramsay reminds his father that Bran and Rickon are now heirs to House Stark's rule of the North after Robb's death
Ramsay notes that nearly all the Northerners, furious at House Bolton and House Frey's treachery, will rally behind Bran and Rickon if they learn the boys are alive
Ramsay gleefully taunts Theon by revealing details of the Red Wedding, especially that Roose murdered Robb
Theon is clearly shaken to know Robb is dead at the hands of his new masters
Theon does not openly react
Roose dispatches Locke with orders to find and kill Bran and Rickon
Reek suggests that Jon Snow might be sheltering Bran and Rickon at Castle Black or may know where they have gone
Ramsay advocates killing Jon as well, arguing that Jon's Stark blood could make him a threat
Roose gives Ramsay orders to take Theon and an army to Moat Cailin and reclaim the fortress from the Greyjoys
Roose promises to consider legitimizing Ramsay as a member of House Bolton if he succeeds
a panting figure travels through a dark, snow-covered forest
the figure kills a deer
it is revealed the figure is Summer and Bran had been warging into Summer
Bran’s warging is interrupted by Meera Reed
Meera tells Bran he had been gone for hours
Bran expresses unhappiness about being snapped out of his warging, saying he was eating
Jojen clarifies that Summer was eating and Bran cannot gain sustenance from what Summer eats
Jojen and Meera caution Bran that warging so long is dangerous for other reasons
Jojen and Meera warn Bran he could become trapped and forget his friends, family, home, and himself
Jojen and Meera explain that if Bran forgets himself, they all lose everything
The group breaks camp and continues traveling through the forest
The group comes upon a weirwood tree
Bran has Hodor take him to the tree
Bran touches the tree just under the face carved into it
Bran sees a vision
Several images rush to Bran's mind, including swarms of crows, wights, and the fall that paralyzed him
Bran sees the Three-Eyed Raven taking flight in a darkened corridor and later land on a tree
Bran sees Eddard sharpening Ice, in the Black Cells, and being led to his execution
Bran sees the Red Keep abandoned and covered in snow
Bran sees the shadow of a dragon flying over King's Landing
The face of the Night King flashes by as Bran hears the voice of Cersei crying out "He saw us!"
Bran sees several images of a different weirwood tree located on top of a barren mountain
A gravely voice tells Bran to "look for me beneath the tree... NORTH."
Bran snaps out of his vision out of breath
Bran states, "I know where we have to go."
Jaime and Tyrion discuss Jaime's gilded steel hand over a meal
Jaime discloses to Tyrion that he can no longer fight, as all of his instincts are wrong using his left hand
Jaime is perturbed that people will find out that he cannot fight
Tyrion tells Jaime that he needs to train with his left hand with someone trustworthy
Tyrion advises Jaime to train in order to better protect the king as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard
Jaime waits in a courtyard by the sea for his training partner arranged by Tyrion
Bronn emerges with two training swords
Jaime remarks that he hasn't wielded a sparring sword since he was nine
Jaime and Bronn commence training
Bronn uses dirty tactics and gets the better of Jaime
Bronn teaches Jaime to use his offhand and encourages him to fight dirtier
Tyrion finds Varys on his way to a breakfast celebration on the day of Joffrey's wedding
Varys divulges that Shae has been spotted and that Cersei has been notified
Varys says he can no longer protect Shae for fear of Tywin or Cersei discovering his deception
Tyrion says that Shae will not leave King's Landing
Tyrion is warned that Tywin has threatened to hang the next whore Tyrion is seen with
Mace Tyrell gifts Joffrey a magnificent golden chalice with seven facets and wishes him and Margaery a long and happy marriage
Joffrey graciously accepts the chalice
Cersei spots Shae and tells Tywin that she is Tyrion's whore
Tywin commands for Shae to be sent to the Tower of the Hand before the wedding
Tyrion overhears Cersei and Tywin and looks vexed
Tyrion approaches Joffrey
Podrick Payne presents Joffrey with The Lives of Four Kings, a book by Grand Maester Kaeth
Joffrey accepts the book, but his sincerity seems questionable
A member of the Kingsguard presents a Valyrian steel sword to Joffrey
Tywin tells Joffrey that it is one of two swords of its kind in the capital
Joffrey rushes to wield the sword
Joffrey slashes at Tyrion's gift, the rare book
Joffrey asks for suggestions on the sword's name from the guests
Joffrey settles on the name Widow's Wail
Tyrion is visited in his chambers by Shae
Tyrion informs Shae that there is a ship waiting for her bound for Pentos and that their "friendship" is at an end
Tyrion realizes Shae will refuse to flee in the face of danger
Tyrion concludes the only way to convince Shae to leave is to brutally reject her
Tyrion tells Shae that she is only a whore and he cannot love her, causing pain to both
Shae is hurt by Tyrion's words
Bronn enters and tries to escort Shae away
Shae slaps Bronn and runs out, furious and broken-hearted
Tyrion later asks Bronn if Shae is gone
Bronn confirms that Shae boarded the ship
Tyrion asks if Bronn saw the ship sail away
Bronn assures Tyrion that no one has been following him while escorting Shae, and that only the two of them and Varys know about it
Bronn advises Tyrion to go and drink until it feels like he did the right thing
Joffrey and Margaery's wedding ceremony takes place in the Great Sept of Baelor, conducted by the High Septon
Joffrey and Margaery are wed in front of gods and men
At the wedding feast, Joffrey speaks to the crowd about contemplating history
Five dwarves ride out of a large golden lion, representing the five kings in the War of the Five Kings
The dwarves perform, jousting, fighting, and making crude sexual acts
Joffrey, Tommen, and Cersei laugh at the performance
Margaery, Olenna, Mace, Loras, Varys, Oberyn, Tywin, Tyrion, and Sansa make little effort to hide their disgust
Loras leaves in anger after watching the crude depiction of Renly
Sansa looks on in obvious grief when the dwarf playing Robb is killed
Joffrey expresses his delight with the play and prepares to give the champion dwarf a purse
Joffrey mockingly suggests that Tyrion fight the champion dwarf
Tyrion counters by suggesting Joffrey should fight instead and mocks him about the Battle of the Blackwater
Some guests stifle chuckles at Tyrion's remark
Joffrey, enraged and humiliated, pours wine over Tyrion's head
Joffrey orders Tyrion to serve him wine as his cupbearer
Tyrion approaches Joffrey
Joffrey purposely drops the goblet and kicks it under the table
Sansa hands the goblet to Tyrion
Tyrion fills the goblet with wine and hands it back to Joffrey
Joffrey demands that Tyrion kneel before him
Tyrion refuses to kneel
Margaery interrupts and says it is time to cut the pigeon pie
Joffrey hands the goblet to Margaery
Margaery places the goblet at the edge of the table near Olenna
Joffrey draws his new sword and cuts the pie
several white doves fly out of the pie
Sansa and Tyrion attempt to leave the feast
Sansa and Tyrion attempt to leave the feast
Margaery serves Joffrey a piece of pie
Joffrey commands Tyrion to serve him wine
Tyrion hands the goblet to Joffrey
Joffrey quickly drinks all of the wine
Joffrey tries to speak but begins coughing
Joffrey grabs his throat
Margaery and Olenna exclaim that Joffrey is choking and needs help
Joffrey collapses face down and begins vomiting and convulsing in excruciating pain
Jaime rushes in and turns Joffrey over on his back
Cersei rushes in and helps turn Joffrey over
Dontos Hollard approaches Sansa from behind and quietly tells her to come with him quickly
Sansa goes with Dontos Hollard
Joffrey points an accusatory finger in Tyrion's direction while examining the wine goblet
Joffrey's eyes turn red, blood runs from his nose, he stops convulsing, and dies
Cersei sobs with grief
Cersei orders the Kingsguard to seize Tyrion for the poisoning of her son

Breaker_of_Chains.action: Cersei mourns the death of her eldest son, Joffrey, while holding him in her arms
Cersei demands that Sansa Stark be found
Cersei's father seals the capital so no one can leave
Prince Tommen pays his respects to Joffrey in the Great Sept of Baelor
Tywin instructs Tommen on the qualities of a good king
Tywin and Tommen leave the sept
Jaime enters to see Cersei calling for the death of Tyrion and Sansa Stark
Jaime sends the septon and septas away to be alone with Cersei
Jaime tries to comfort Cersei through his affections
Cersei refuses Jaime's advances initially
Jaime is aggressive toward Cersei
Cersei insists the sept is an inappropriate place for sex and asks Jaime to stop
Jaime refuses to stop
Jaime and Cersei have sex next to their dead son's corpse
Sansa escapes with Ser Dontos Hollard from King's Landing to a ship off the coast
Sansa is taken aboard the ship and greeted by Lord Petyr "Littlefinger" Baelish
Baelish greets Sansa
Baelish remembers he owes Dontos his fee
Baelish has two men kill Dontos with crossbows
Baelish explains to Sansa that Dontos was a drunken fool and unreliable ally
Baelish reveals Dontos only helped Sansa for money and would turn her in for money
Baelish proves his point by showing the necklace Dontos gave Sansa was made on his orders
Sansa is confused and horrified
Baelish promises to keep Sansa safe
Tywin interrupts Oberyn Martell, Ellaria Sand, and Olyvar during a group sex act
Tywin discusses Tyrion's upcoming trial for the murder of Joffrey
Tywin brings up Oberyn's knowledge of poisons and the rumors about Joffrey's death
Tywin wonders whether Oberyn helped Tyrion
The topic changes to the murder of Oberyn's sister Elia by the Mountain during the Sack of King's Landing
Tywin denies ordering Elia's death
Tywin promises to arrange a meeting between Oberyn and the Mountain so Oberyn can have his justice
Tywin offers Oberyn a position on the Small Council if Oberyn agrees to be the third judge at Tyrion's trial
Tywin explains his eagerness to bring Dorne back into the fold to unite the Seven Kingdoms against threats
Tywin lists the Greyjoy rebellion, the wildling army, and Daenerys with her dragons as threats to Westeros
Tywin observes that only the Dornish successfully resisted Aegon Targaryen's dragons
Oberyn notes it must be hard for Tywin to admit he needs the Dornish for help
Tyrion is in a cell in King's Landing
Podrick Payne visits Tyrion in his cell
Podrick smuggles food and other items to Tyrion
Podrick is asked to compile a list of Tyrion's witnesses
Tyrion names Sansa as a witness
Podrick informs Tyrion that Sansa disappeared after Joffrey's death
Tyrion asks about Bronn, but Podrick says Bronn is under investigation
Podrick confirms there has been no word of Shae
Tyrion takes the lack of news about Shae as good news
Tyrion asks to speak with Jaime
Tyrion warns Podrick to be aware of his surroundings and watch out for spies
Tyrion opines that many people had a motive for killing Joffrey
Tyrion rules out Cersei as a suspect
Tyrion urges Podrick to leave King's Landing for his own safety
Podrick refuses to testify against Tyrion despite being offered a knighthood
Tyrion calls Podrick back as he is about to leave
Tyrion tells Podrick that there has never lived a more loyal squire
Arya and the Hound are spotted by a farmer and his young daughter while crossing the Riverlands
Arya and the Hound make their way to the Vale to ransom Arya to Lysa Arryn
Arya fabricates a story that she and the Hound are father and daughter, with Clegane as a knight in service of House Tully
The farmer believes Arya's story and allows them to stay the night and share a meal
Arya and the Hound scarf the food down hastily
Arya and the Hound scarf the food down hastily
Arya wakes the next morning to the farmer's daughter screaming
Clegane assaults the farmer and takes his silver
Arya demands to know why Clegane did that
Clegane states "a dead man doesn't need his silver," claiming the farmer and his daughter will not survive the winter
Stannis learns of Joffrey's death
Stannis warns Ser Davos that their time to claim the Iron Throne is running out if they cannot secure an army
Ser Davos begins reading lessons with Princess Shireen Baratheon
Ser Davos realizes the Iron Bank of Braavos could be a possible ally
Wildlings under Tormund and the cannibalistic Thenns attack a village south of the wall
The village is massacred
The Styr sends a lone boy as a messenger to Castle Black
The boy is sent to relay an account of the massacre and a warning that more would follow
Samwell Tarly fears for the safety of Gilly
Samwell Tarly moves Gilly to a vassal town that services the Night's Watch brothers
Samwell Tarly pledges to visit Gilly as much as possible
Samwell Tarly instructs the inn that Gilly will not be touched
Samwell Tarly is resolute in keeping Gilly safe from all harm
The Lord Commander states that they do not have the manpower to afford venturing away from the Wall
Edd and Grenn return to Castle Black after escaping Craster's Keep
Jon reveals he told Mance Rayder that a thousand men armed Castle Black
Jon points out that when Mance reaches Craster's Keep, Rast and Karl Tanner will reveal the truth
Jon insists the Night's Watch send a party to Craster's Keep to kill their traitor brothers before Mance gets to them first
Daenerys marches on the city of Meereen and begins her siege
A riding knight of Meereen challenges Daenerys to choose a champion for a duel
Grey Worm, Jorah, and Barristan volunteer to stand as Daenerys's champion
Daenerys refuses Grey Worm, Jorah, and Barristan as too valuable
Daario Naharis volunteers to be Daenerys's champion
Daenerys accepts Daario as her champion
Daario quickly dispatches the Meereen champion by killing the champion's horse
Daenerys begins her siege by speaking of freedom to the gathered slaves
Daenerys catapults the broken chains of freed slaves across the city walls
The slaves examine the broken chains
The Great Masters look on, perhaps in fear

Oathkeeper_(episode).action: Missandei teaches Grey Worm how to speak the Common Tongue
Grey Worm reveals he remembers only being born in the Summer Isles since his castration
Missandei reveals she remembers seeing Naath burning as she was taken as a slave
Daenerys commands Grey Worm and several Unsullied to infiltrate Meereen disguised as slaves and convince the slaves to rise
Grey Worm and Unsullied visit a slave pen where slaves discuss rising against the Great Masters
A young slave tries to convince others to fight
Older slaves argue they have no means to oppose their Masters
Grey Worm arrives and tells the slaves only they can free themselves, then presents them with weapons
The slaves rise up and the uprising is a resounding success
Daenerys orders the crucifixion of 163 Great Masters in retaliation for the 163 slave children crucified on the road to Meereen
Ser Barristan advises Daenerys against crucifixion, suggesting mercy
Daenerys declares she will answer injustice with justice
The cries of the crucified Great Masters reach Daenerys atop the Great Pyramid of Meereen
The golden harpy of Ghis is draped in the banner of House Targaryen
Daenerys conquers all three cities of Slaver’s Bay
Jon and Grenn teach several recruits, including Locke and Olly, how to fight against wildlings
Alliser Thorne admonishes Jon and reminds him he is a steward, not a ranger, trying to provoke Jon
Jon leaves the yard and talks with Locke
Locke pretends he is from the Stormlands and claims he wants to protect the realm
Janos Slynt points out to Thorne that Jon is well liked, while Thorne is not
Maester Aemon insists upon an election for a new Lord Commander
Slynt convinces Thorne to allow Jon to deal with the mutineers, hoping they will remove Jon
Samwell Tarly frets about Gilly's safety in Mole's Town with wildlings loose
Jon sympathizes with Sam and confides he wants to find Bran
Sam explains he tried to bring Bran back, but Bran was determined to go beyond the Wall
Sam and Jon speculate that Bran must have found shelter at Craster's Keep
Locke interrupts Sam and Jon
Locke brings Jon to Thorne
Thorne sanctions Jon's mission to Craster's Keep but tells him to secure volunteers
Jon gives an impassioned speech about dealing with the mutineers and justice for Mormont
Grenn, Eddison Tollett, and other men volunteer to join Jon's mission
Locke asks to say his vows if required before going north of the Wall
Bronn implores Jaime to visit Tyrion in the dungeon, reminding him that Tyrion named Jaime his champion in the Eyrie
Jaime visits Tyrion in the dungeon
Jaime confesses to Tyrion that Cersei asked him to murder him
Tyrion convinces Jaime that he didn't kill Joffrey
Tyrion notes that not even irrefutable evidence and a confession by the real perpetrator would satisfy Cersei
Tyrion says Cersei is out for his blood and that the trial is worse because at least one judge has always wanted him dead
Jaime tells Tyrion that Cersei offers a knighthood to whoever captures Sansa Stark
Tyrion insists that Sansa had nothing to do with Joffrey's murder, despite having the best motive
Jaime visits Cersei
Cersei is disgusted, believing Jaime is taking Tyrion's side because of his affection for their brother
Lady Olenna tells Margaery she is about to leave for Highgarden
Lady Olenna mentions the prospective marriage between Margaery and Tommen
Lady Olenna insists that Cersei will turn Tommen against Margaery if Margaery doesn't act fast
Cersei is distracted by accusing her brother of the murder of her son
Margaery insists Olenna cannot be certain of Tyrion's innocence
Lady Olenna states that Tyrion is innocent and confesses she would never have let Margaery marry Joffrey
Margaery is shocked
Lady Olenna ends the conversation
Margaery enters Tommen's chambers at night, slipping past the Kingsguard
Margaery charms Tommen
Margaery suggests to Tommen that one day she will be his and they should learn to like each other
Margaery compliments Tommen on his cat and kind demeanor
Margaery promises to visit again
Tommen falls asleep smiling
Jaime tasks Brienne with finding Sansa
Jaime urges Brienne to keep Sansa safe from those who would hand her over to Cersei
Jaime provides Brienne with a horse, supplies, and a freshly forged suit of armor
Jaime gifts Brienne the sword his father gave him, forged from Ned Stark's sword
Jaime says the sword should be used to defend Ned Stark's daughters
Jaime insists Brienne take Podrick Payne as a squire
Jaime explains Podrick should be rewarded for his service to Tyrion and kept safe
Brienne names the sword "Oathkeeper"
Brienne vows to find Sansa for the sake of Jaime and Lady Catelyn
Sansa accuses Petyr Baelish of having Joffrey Baratheon murdered
Baelish evades the accusation, pointing out he has been away for weeks and suggesting Tyrion could be the perpetrator
Sansa defends Tyrion, expressing certainty of his innocence
Sansa concludes Baelish is too smart to trust Ser Dontos as an accomplice
Baelish is impressed with Sansa's instincts
Baelish admits Tyrion is innocent
Baelish reveals Sansa's part in Joffrey's death: Ser Dontos's necklace was the murder weapon, and a poisonous stone was slipped into Joffrey's wine
Sansa is bewildered by the revelation
Sansa notes the Lannisters have done much for Baelish, including granting him Harrenhal
Baelish confesses his friendship with the Lannisters was fruitful, but says it is best to keep enemies confused and his absence from the wedding keeps him above suspicion
Sansa declares she doesn't entirely believe Baelish, saying he's too clever to poison a king just to cause confusion
Baelish is again impressed by Sansa
Baelish confirms Joffrey was too volatile and more a hindrance than a benefit as an ally
Baelish reveals Joffrey's death was desired by his "new friends," and a gift is helpful to grow a new friendship
The mutinous deserters indulge themselves on food and rape Craster's wives at Craster's Keep
Karl Tanner drinks wine from the skull of Jeor Mormont
Karl Tanner drunkenly brags about himself and his skill as a hired killer in King's Landing
Karl Tanner orders Rast to "feed the beast"
One of Craster's wives enters with Craster's last son, insisting the infant must be given as a 'gift to the gods'
At Karl's orders, Rast leaves the infant on a patch of open ground
Rast deals with the beast, Ghost, who the mutineers have caged
Rast taunts Ghost by withholding food and water
A snowstorm suddenly whips up and the temperature drops
The water Rast was taunting Ghost with freezes
Rast recognizes the approach of White Walkers and flees
Bran and his companions hear the child's cries
Bran wargs into Summer to investigate
Summer falls into a trap outside Craster's Keep
Meera senses danger and urges the group to move on
Bran insists on trying to free Ghost and Summer
Bran and his companions are taken prisoner by the mutineers
Some deserters torment Hodor
The others are taken to Karl
Karl asks who they are and threatens to kill Meera and Jojen when they remain silent
Jojen is overcome by a seizure and falls to the floor
Meera begs to be let go so she can help Jojen
Karl allows Meera to help Jojen when Bran reveals his true identity
A lone White Walker rides its undead horse towards a shattered mountain, cradling Craster's last son in its arm
The White Walker reaches the mountain and strides up a large ring of icicle columns
The White Walker dismounts and places the child on an ice altar in the center
A line of dark-garbed White Walkers watch the proceedings
One White Walker strolls forth and carefully picks up the wailing child, who swiftly calms
The White Walker presses the nail of its index finger into the baby's cheek
The sound of cracking ice can be heard as the child's eyes turn a depthless, icy blue

First_of_His_Name.action: Daenerys learns of Joffrey's death and Tommen's coronation
Daenerys is irritated to learn that Daario Naharis has captured Meereen's navy without her explicit orders
Daenerys considers setting sail for Westeros and taking King's Landing with her 8,000 Unsullied and 2,000 Second Sons
Barristan Selmy expresses optimism about their chances, believing old Westerosi families will flock to their cause
Jorah Mormont expresses less enthusiasm, warning that the rest of the continent will still be a problem
Daenerys's advisors reveal that the Wise Masters have re-enslaved every freed man in Yunkai
Daenerys's advisors reveal that Astapor remains free, but the council she installed has been deposed by Cleon
Daenerys dismisses everyone but Jorah
Daenerys muses that her plans are in shambles
Jorah reminds Daenerys that she is the last Targaryen and the Mother of Dragons
Daenerys says she needs to be more than that
Daenerys laments that if she cannot keep order in three cities, she cannot control seven kingdoms
Daenerys resolves to remain in Meereen and rule
Tommen is crowned king of the Seven Kingdoms
Cersei makes a seemingly peaceful gesture towards Margaery
Margaery remains wary of Cersei
Cersei talks to Prince Oberyn and asks him a favor
Cersei mentions she hasn't seen her daughter Myrcella in over a year
Cersei asks Oberyn to take her daughter a gift, a ship, since she missed her last nameday
Tywin reveals to Cersei their problem with the Iron Bank of Braavos and that the gold mines of the Westerlands ran dry three years ago
Tywin admits the Lannisters can trust nobody except themselves, but they need the Tyrells' wealth and resources
Cersei suggests coming to some arrangement with one of the Bank’s representatives
Tywin dismisses the idea, stating the Bank cannot be avoided, lied to, or swayed
Podrick proves to be a hindrance to Brienne while on the road
Podrick has problems with his horse
Podrick accidentally sets fire to a rabbit they were cooking
Brienne tries to get rid of Podrick by releasing him from his vow as her squire
Podrick refuses to leave Brienne
Brienne and Podrick travel toward Castle Black, where Brienne believes Sansa may have taken refuge with Jon Snow
Brienne questions Podrick about his duties as Tyrion’s squire
Podrick tells Brienne his job was mostly pouring wine and other menial tasks
Brienne asks if Podrick did anything related to combat
Podrick reveals he killed Ser Mandon Moore by shoving a spear through his head during the Battle of the Blackwater
Brienne grants Podrick a bit of respect and allows him to help her remove her armor
Petyr Baelish and Sansa Stark arrive safely at the Bloody Gate
Petyr has Sansa cover her hair, as auburn is a Tully trait
Petyr introduces Sansa as his niece, Alayne
Sansa tries to slip into her alias upon arriving at the Eyrie
Lysa Arryn tells Sansa not to bother with her alias around family
Lysa laments Sansa's treatment in King's Landing
Sansa attempts to defend Tyrion's character
Lysa ignores Sansa's defense of Tyrion
Robin Arryn is delighted to see Petyr and to show Sansa the Moon Door
Lysa reminds Robin never to reveal Sansa's identity
Lysa tells Robin to show Sansa to her chambers
Robin shows Sansa to her chambers
Lysa insists she wants to marry Petyr immediately
Lysa claims they already had a wedding night years ago
Lysa references poisoning her former husband's wine and framing the Lannisters by writing to Catelyn
Lysa uses these actions as proof of her love for Petyr
Petyr is unable to deter Lysa from marrying him
Lysa reveals a Septon and witnesses have been waiting outside the main door
Lysa declares she will scream so loudly that night they will hear her across the Narrow Sea
Sansa attempts to sleep as Lysa tries to fulfill her promise
Lysa has a meal with Sansa and gives her some lemon cakes
Sansa indulges herself in the lemon cakes, relieved to be safe
Sansa becomes self-conscious when Lysa tells stories of Catelyn's overeating in her youth
Lysa encourages Sansa to keep eating and explains Petyr brought lemons from the Crownlands for her cakes
Lysa suddenly becomes jealous and demands to know why Petyr cares about Sansa and if he has slept with her
Lysa rants about Catelyn's love for Brandon and how he nearly killed Petyr
Sansa weeps, horrified, and insists she is still a virgin and that Petyr thinks she is just a stupid girl
Sansa claims she cannot lie and always tells the truth
Lysa immediately calms down and comforts Sansa, assuring her that she believes her
Lysa tells Sansa she can marry Robin since neither Tyrion nor Petyr had slept with her
Sansa seems unsure what to make of this information
It becomes clear that King's Landing has made Sansa a consummate actress
Sandor Clegane and Arya Stark continue to spar verbally and physically on the road to the Eyrie
Sandor Clegane disparages Arya's water dancing
Sandor Clegane insults Syrio Forel for being defeated by Meryn Trant
Locke scouts Craster's Keep for the Night's Watch party
Locke finds the hut where Bran, Jojen, Meera, and Hodor are being held captive
Locke reports back to Jon Snow and the others
Locke tells them that only eleven traitors are present and most are drunk
Locke lies about the hut, claiming only hounds are inside and they should keep away
Jon believes Locke
Jon tells the party they attack at nightfall
Karl enters the prisoners' hut and attempts to rape Meera
Jojen distracts Karl by revealing his possession of the Sight and claiming to have seen Karl dead before the night is out
Rast rushes in to warn Karl that the keep is under attack
Jon's party rushes through the defenses and starts killing their traitorous former brethren
Locke slips into the hut and attempts to abduct Bran
Bran wargs into Hodor and uses him to kill Locke
Bran spots Jon in the fighting and wants to go to him
Jojen prevents Bran from going to Jon, reminding him they must continue onward to reach the Three-Eyed Raven and that Jon would stop them
Bran reluctantly leaves despite being so close to Jon
The Night's Watch swiftly overcome their enemy
Jon confronts Karl in single combat
Karl uses dirty tactics learned in his criminal past to gain the upper hand
Sissy, one of Craster's wives, stabs Karl from behind
Karl rounds on Sissy
Jon gets back to his feet and drives Longclaw through the back of Karl's skull, killing him
Four brothers of the Night's Watch, including Locke, are found dead
All the traitors but one are accounted for
Locke's true intentions are never discovered
Rast, the sole surviving mutineer, runs in terror through the forest
Rast reaches the cage where Ghost was held
Rast notices someone has opened the cage
Ghost lunges out of nowhere and kills Rast
Ghost finds his way back to Jon
Jon is overjoyed to see Ghost again
Jon warns Craster's wives about Mance Rayder's army and offers to take them to safety south of the Wall
Craster's wives refuse Jon's offer, preferring to set out on their own
Jon has Craster's Keep burned to the ground, along with the bodies of all those who died there

The_Laws_of_Gods_and_Men.action: Yara prepares her troops for their assault on the Dreadfort to rescue Theon
Yara reads the letter sent by Ramsay Snow, threatening to flay all Ironborn who do not leave the North
Ramsay takes Myranda's virginity and engages in violent sex
Yara and her troops land and scale the walls of the castle
Yara and her troops quietly take out the guards as they search for Theon
Yara interrogates a guard and learns Theon's location is the dog kennels
Yara kills the guard, despite promising not to
Yara and the Ironborn see Theon sitting in a cage
Yara opens the cage
Theon refuses to go with Yara, believing it is another cruel trick by Ramsay
Ramsay and several Bolton troops enter and a fight ensues, with several Bolton and Greyjoy soldiers killed
Theon tells Yara his name has always been Reek, bites her, and cowers in his cage
Ramsay releases the hounds
Yara is forced to retreat to her ship
One of the ironmen asks about Theon
Yara says Theon is "dead"
Ramsay allows Reek to take a bath as a reward for his loyalty
Reek fears the bath is another trick
Ramsay assures Reek it is not a trick
Reek bathes
Ramsay plans to assault Moat Cailin
Ramsay tells Reek he needs him to pretend to be Theon Greyjoy
Stannis and Davos visit the Iron Bank of Braavos to secure funds for Stannis's war effort
Tycho Nestoris rejects Stannis and Davos's request for funds
Davos entreats Tycho Nestoris and argues that funding Stannis is the best way for the Bank to get their gold back from Tywin Lannister
The Iron Bank agrees to lend gold to Stannis and Davos
Davos uses the gold to recruit his old pirate friend Salladhor Saan to Stannis's cause
The goatherd and his son tend goats on a hill while the son throws rocks
Drogon appears and burns the field, kills a flock of goats, and takes one with him
The goatherd travels to Meereen to visit the queen
The goatherd lays the charred remains of his flock before Daenerys
The goatherd hopes to not anger the queen and believes it to be punishment
Daenerys apologizes for Drogon's actions
Daenerys promises to pay back three times the value of the goatherd's flock
Hizdahr zo Loraq presents his petition to bury his father's remains in the Temple of the Graces
Daenerys counters that Loraq's father was one of the masters who crucified the 163 children
Loraq reveals his father spoke out against the crucifixion of the slave children but was overruled
Loraq explains his father was killed due to Daenerys's judgment
Daenerys, somewhat mollified, allows Loraq to bury his father
Daenerys asks how many more supplicants are waiting
Missandei answers that there are over 200 more supplicants
Tywin informs the Small Council that Tyrion's trial will begin that afternoon
Varys informs Tywin that the Hound has been spotted in the Riverlands
Tywin orders a bounty on the Hound's head
Varys informs the council of Daenerys's conquest of Meereen
Tywin decides to take measures to prevent Daenerys from launching an invasion
Jaime has Tyrion shackled and brought into the throne room to begin his trial
King Tommen recuses himself from the trial
King Tommen appoints Tywin, Oberyn Martell, and Mace Tyrell as judges
Several witnesses are called for the prosecution, including Ser Meryn Trant, Grand Maester Pycelle, Queen Regent Cersei, and Varys
Cersei gives testimony backing the accusations against Tyrion
Tyrion is accused in the trial
Jaime asks Tywin to spare Tyrion, offering to break his oath to the Kingsguard and return to Casterly Rock as his heir
Tywin accepts Jaime's offer and tells Jaime that Tyrion will be allowed to join the Night's Watch after being found guilty, provided he pleads for mercy
Jaime speaks with Tyrion before the recess ends and urges him to plead for mercy
Tyrion expresses deep distrust of the offer, referencing Eddard Stark's fate
Jaime pleads with Tyrion to trust him, insisting Tywin will keep his word, and advises Tyrion to keep quiet and avoid outbursts
Tywin orders the next witness to testify: Shae
Shae appears, astounding Tyrion and Jaime
Shae testifies that Tyrion and Sansa planned Joffrey's murder together
Shae claims Sansa wanted vengeance for the murder of her family
Shae tells the judges of Tyrion's sexual desires, embarrassing him
Tyrion, enraged, demands to confess his crimes
Tyrion roars about saving King's Landing from Stannis and says he should have let Stannis kill everyone
The people in the court react with horror and disgust
Tyrion claims to be guilty
Tywin assumes Tyrion is guilty of murdering Joffrey
Tyrion denies guilt in Joffrey's murder, says he is guilty of being a dwarf
Tywin scoffs and says Tyrion is not on trial for being a dwarf
Tyrion says he has been on trial for his entire life as a dwarf
Tywin asks if Tyrion has anything to say in his defense
Tyrion says he has nothing to say in his defense but only this
Tyrion reveals his true feelings about Joffrey, denies guilt, but says he wished to have killed Joffrey
Tyrion turns his fury towards Cersei and says watching her son die gave him relief
Tyrion tells the court he wishes he had become the monster they thought he was and would have watched them die
The court is enraged
Tywin orders Ser Meryn to escort Tyrion back to the dungeons
Tyrion bellows that he will not lose his life for a crime he did not commit
Tyrion demands a trial by combat

Mockingbird.action: Tyrion and Jaime discuss Tyrion's fate and the irony of Lord Tywin striking down both his heirs if Jaime were to die being Tyrion's champion
Jaime declines to be Tyrion's champion as he cannot fight well enough with his left hand
Tyrion requests Jaime to bring him Bronn, hoping the sellsword will stand for him as he did at the Eyrie
Gregor Clegane mercilessly kills prisoners with a greatsword
Cersei approaches Gregor and thanks him for his haste in returning to King's Landing as her champion for the trial by combat
Gregor asks Cersei who he will be fighting
Cersei asks Gregor if it matters
Gregor shakes his head
Bronn visits Tyrion dressed in the clothes of a lord
Bronn is betrothed to Lollys Stokeworth by Cersei's doing
Bronn refuses to fight the Mountain, citing lack of reward and danger
Bronn bids farewell to Tyrion
Tyrion feels heartbroken by Bronn's decision but understands
Tyrion is approached by Oberyn Martell
Oberyn tells Tyrion of his disappointment in their first encounter as children
Oberyn and his sister Elia are told stories of the "Lannister Monster" and his deformities
Oberyn and Elia visit Casterly Rock as children
Cersei shows Tyrion to Oberyn and Elia with great ceremony
Oberyn tells Tyrion he saw no monster, just a baby
Cersei tells Oberyn and Elia that Tyrion killed her mother
Cersei cruelly assaults Tyrion until Jaime stops her
Jaime stops Cersei from assaulting Tyrion
Oberyn says he and Elia were disturbed by Cersei's hatred toward Tyrion
Tyrion is disturbed by Cersei's hatred and tears up
Oberyn tells Tyrion he seeks justice for his sister's death
Tyrion replies, "You are in the wrong place."
Oberyn counters that he is in the perfect place to seek justice
Oberyn offers to be Tyrion's champion in the trial by combat, intending to start his revenge with Gregor Clegane
Daario surprises Daenerys in her private chambers
Daenerys feigns outrage at the intrusion
Daenerys agrees to hear Daario's complaints
Daario laments that he is only good at killing men and sleeping with women, and has no opportunities to pursue either
Daenerys counters that the Second Sons are acting as Meereen's police force and that many women in the city would throw themselves at Daario
Daario dismisses the Second Sons' role as unchallenging and says the only woman he wants isn't interested
Daario begs Daenerys to send him and the Second Sons to make real war
Daenerys focuses on Daario's other proclaimed talent and orders him to remove his clothes
Daario bumps into Ser Jorah
Daario says it’s a good time to talk to the queen, implying he and Daenerys were just intimate
Jorah is not amused
Daenerys dismisses concerns about her intimate life
Daenerys tells Jorah she sent Daario to take the Second Sons to Yunkai to regain control and kill the resurfaced masters
Jorah relates to the mercy shown by Eddard Stark for his dealings in slavery
Jorah convinces Daenerys to bring the masters to justice without execution
Daenerys considers for a moment
Daenerys tells Jorah to assign Hizdahr zo Loraq as her ambassador to offer the masters a choice: live free in the new world or die clinging to the old one
Daenerys says the Second Sons will be stationed in Yunkai to enforce the masters’ choice
Daenerys tells Jorah to let Daario know it was Jorah who changed her mind
Queen Selyse speaks to Melisandre, who is enjoying a bath
Melisandre jokes that the Lord of Light told her to enjoy the bath as it would be her last good one
The joke goes over Selyse's head
Melisandre tells Selyse about using potions and illusions in serving the Lord of Light, and that some pageantry and deception will be forgiven
Selyse asks Melisandre if she used potions when she slept with Stannis
Melisandre replies that she did not use potions with Stannis
Selyse remarks on her desire to leave Shireen behind when they go to the North
Melisandre dismisses Shireen's "heresy" and assures Selyse that Shireen will be needed where they are going
Jon and his fellow brothers return from Craster's Keep
Alliser Thorne sees Jon's direwolf, Ghost
Alliser Thorne sends Ghost away, outside Castle Black
Jon tries to convince the Night's Watch to barricade the Wall by blocking off Castle Black's entry tunnel with ice and rock
Jon is denied after being reminded of his place as Steward
The First Builder reluctantly agrees with Thorne's assessment that the Watch can defend the tunnel
Jon and Sam are sent to serve as sentries on top of the Wall until Mance arrives
Arya and Sandor encounter a dying man in a ransacked village
Sandor gives the dying man a gift of mercy by stabbing him in the heart
Sandor is ambushed by Biter, who bites him on the neck
Sandor snaps Biter's neck and drops him dead in the dirt
Rorge encounters Arya and Sandor and reveals that Joffrey is dead
Arya kills Rorge with a quick thrust of Needle to his heart
Sandor shows approval for Arya's action
Sandor clumsily addresses his wounds from Biter
Arya suggests burning to cauterize Sandor's wound
Sandor rebuffs Arya's suggestion due to his fear of fire
Sandor confirms the story of his facial scars from burns inflicted by Gregor and how his father covered up the truth
Sandor expresses feeling alone
Arya offers to clean and stitch Sandor up
Sandor allows Arya to clean and stitch his wounds
Brienne and Podrick stop at an inn
Hot Pie serves Brienne and Podrick kidney pie
Hot Pie tells Brienne and Podrick about finding the right ingredients for the pie
Hot Pie asks Brienne about her business
Brienne replies that she is in Lady Stark's service to find and protect Sansa Stark
Hot Pie leaves after calling the Starks traitors
Brienne and Podrick prepare to leave the inn
Podrick reminds Brienne that telling people of their quest could be dangerous
Hot Pie approaches Brienne and Podrick and gives them a loaf of bread shaped like a wolf
Hot Pie tells them he knows nothing of Sansa, but has seen Arya alive with the Hound
Brienne is surprised because Arya was presumed dead since Ned's execution
Brienne and Podrick deduce that Arya's captor will try to ransom her
Podrick surmises that Arya's aunt Lysa Arryn is the Hound's only remaining chance of a reward
Brienne and Podrick decide to head for the Eyrie
Sansa builds a snow replica of Winterfell while wandering the Eyrie's snow-covered courtyards
Robin chances upon Sansa and seems impressed with her efforts
Sansa and Robin get along until Robin asks where the Moon Door is
Sansa tells Robin Winterfell doesn't need a Moon Door
Sansa agrees to add a Moon Door
Robin accidentally knocks over a tower while Sansa is adding the Moon Door
Robin insists Sansa replicate the tower
Sansa becomes irritated
Robin throws a tantrum and destroys the replica
Sansa, unable to control her anger, slaps Robin
Sansa immediately softens and tries to apologize
Robin threatens to tell his mother and runs away before Sansa can apologize
Petyr Baelish witnesses the action
Petyr assures Sansa he will deal with Lysa
Petyr says Lysa should have disciplined Robin long ago
Petyr muses on how he should have been Sansa's father
Petyr tells Sansa she is more beautiful than her mother Catelyn
Petyr kisses Sansa, who is so shocked she doesn't resist
Lysa sees the kiss, unknown to Petyr and Sansa
Lysa summons Sansa
Lysa delights in telling Sansa the gory details about Moon Door executions
Lysa grabs Sansa and threatens to throw her through the Moon Door for kissing Petyr
Petyr arrives and swears by everything he can think of that he will send Sansa away
Lysa releases Sansa
Lysa embraces Petyr
Petyr assures Lysa that he only ever loved one woman—her sister
Petyr shoves Lysa out of the Moon Door
Petyr looks down as Lysa plummets to her death hundreds of feet below

The_Mountain_and_the_Viper.action: Ramsay Snow sends Reek, forced to reassume his former identity of Theon Greyjoy, to treat with the Ironborn garrison at Moat Cailin
Theon rides into the fortress and finds corpses lying everywhere from constant attacks
Disease runs rampant among the Ironborn
The Ironborn remaining are sick, under-equipped, and outnumbered
Ralf Kenning, the garrison commander, rebuffs Ramsay's offer of safe passage in exchange for surrender
Ralf Kenning thinks Theon is an imposter
One of Ralf Kenning's men cuts him down and accepts the offer on behalf of the soldiers
The Ironborn surrender to Ramsay
Ramsay has the surrendered Ironborn killed and flayed
Ramsay meets with his father
Ramsay presents his father with the Greyjoy banner that was flying over the fortress
Roose presents Ramsay with a decree of legitimization, making him officially a member of House Bolton
Ramsay is delighted at the knowledge he will succeed his father as Warden of the North
Ramsay and Reek accompany Roose's army as it approaches the ruins of Winterfell
A whore argues with Gilly about her baby waking her up during the night
Wildlings, including Tormund, Styr, and Ygritte, attack Mole's Town, killing everyone in their path
Ygritte discovers Gilly hiding with her baby
Ygritte urges Gilly and her baby to keep quiet, sparing their lives
News from Mole's Town arrives at Castle Black
Sam worries about Gilly and blames himself for bringing her to Mole's Town
Grenn expresses anger at cowering in the castle instead of helping brothers in neighboring towns
Jon Snow says they can't go out to fight against the wildlings, as that is what the wildlings want
Pypar and Edd try to comfort Sam by saying Gilly might be alive and hiding
Pypar and Edd remind Sam that Gilly has survived worse situations
Sam gains hope that Gilly and her baby may have survived
Jon notes that if Mole's Town has been destroyed, Castle Black is next and they are outnumbered by Mance Rayder's army
Edd notes that if the wildlings don't kill them, there is something worse behind them that will finish the job
Edd asks whichever last man is alive to burn the others so he doesn't come back after death
Grey Worm swims with other Unsullied outside Meereen
Grey Worm notices Missandei and other women bathing naked downstream
Missandei spots Grey Worm looking at her and returns his gaze
Missandei stands up slowly for Grey Worm to see her before covering herself
Daenerys asks Missandei if she thinks Grey Worm was spying on her
Missandei says no
Daenerys mentions that Dothraki have no taboos against nudity or public love-making
Missandei says it doesn't matter, as Grey Worm isn't interested in her
Missandei says Grey Worm was interested, surprising both herself and Daenerys
Daenerys inquires if, when a slave is castrated, the masters take "all of it"
Missandei says she doesn't know
Daenerys asks if Missandei has ever wondered
Missandei thoughtfully confirms that she has
Grey Worm comes to apologize to Missandei
Missandei tells Grey Worm he doesn't need to apologize
Grey Worm hopes he didn't frighten Missandei
Missandei says Grey Worm did not frighten her
Grey Worm addresses Missandei in the Common Tongue and tells her the lessons she gives are precious to him
Missandei gently corrects Grey Worm's vocabulary and notes she doesn't remember teaching him the word "precious"
Grey Worm tells Missandei that Jorah taught him the word
Missandei asks Grey Worm if he remembers his birth name
Grey Worm says he remembers nothing
Missandei asks if Grey Worm remembers when they cut him
Grey Worm shakes his head "no"
Missandei tells Grey Worm she's sorry they did that to him
Grey Worm asks Missandei why she is sorry
Missandei says it's a terrible thing to do to a boy
Grey Worm counters that if the masters had not cut him, he never would have been Unsullied, freed, chosen to lead, or met Missandei
Missandei is visibly moved by Grey Worm's words
Grey Worm apologizes once again
Grey Worm turns to leave
Missandei stops Grey Worm and tells him she is glad he saw her
Grey Worm tells Missandei he is glad as well
Ser Barristan Selmy watches the Unsullied taking down the bodies of the crucified masters outside Meereen
a small boy approaches Barristan and gives him a scroll bearing the seal of the Hand
Barristan reads the scroll in consternation
Barristan confronts Ser Jorah with the royal pardon signed by King Robert Baratheon
Barristan surmises that Jorah spied on Daenerys in exchange for the pardon
Jorah begs to be allowed to speak with Daenerys privately
Barristan tells Jorah he will never be alone with Daenerys again
Daenerys holds an audience with Jorah, seething and demanding an explanation
Jorah claims the pardon is a ploy by Tywin Lannister to divide them
Daenerys counters that the pardon was signed the year they met
Daenerys asks Jorah if he claims the pardon was forged
Jorah admits the pardon was not forged
Jorah confesses to giving Varys information on Daenerys' activities in Essos
Daenerys angrily says Jorah's betrayal led to her near-poisoning by a wine merchant
Jorah protests that his actions stopped her from being poisoned
Daenerys retorts that Jorah only stopped it because he knew it might be coming
Jorah begs for Daenerys' forgiveness
Daenerys rebuffs Jorah, saying he betrayed her and sold her secrets
Daenerys spares Jorah's life and gives him a day to leave Meereen
Daenerys warns Jorah that if he is seen in the city after that, his head will be thrown into Slaver's Bay
Jorah is last seen riding from Meereen on a horse
Petyr Baelish testifies before a tribunal consisting of Lord Yohn Royce, Lady Anya Waynwood, and Ser Vance Corbray
The lords are suspicious of Lysa Arryn's death, which occurred soon after her marriage
Baelish attempts to spin Lysa's death as a suicide
The lords don't believe Baelish, given Lysa's devotion to her son
The lords rebuff Littlefinger's explanations and decide to speak with his "niece," Alayne
The lords confirm that Baelish can stay for Alayne's testimony
Sansa reveals her true identity and relates the tale of her captivity and escape from King's Landing
Sansa truthfully tells of Lysa's mental instability, jealousy, and death, changing a few details
Sansa says Littlefinger only kissed her on the cheek and Lysa threw herself through the Moon Door
Sansa breaks down in tears
The lords are convinced by Sansa's testimony
Sansa gives Littlefinger a stone-faced look while none of the lords are watching
Baelish visits Sansa and asks why she lied on his behalf
Sansa explains she does not know what Royce, Corbray, and Waynwood would want from her if Baelish were eliminated, but she does know what Baelish wants
Littlefinger seems impressed by Sansa's calculating decision
Littlefinger attempts to cast doubt on Sansa's certainty by asking if she really knows what he wants
Littlefinger and Robin prepare to depart on a tour of the Vale
Sansa, now raven-haired and wearing a feathered black dress with a plunging neckline, accompanies them
Sandor Clegane and Arya Stark arrive at the Bloody Gate of the Eyrie
Ser Donnel Waynwood informs them of Lysa's death
The Hound stares in shock
Arya begins laughing due to the irony
Tyrion and Jaime share a final drink in Tyrion's cell
Tyrion and Jaime reminisce about Orson Lannister, their simple-minded cousin who smashed beetles with a rock
Tyrion tells Jaime how he observed Orson day after day to understand his motives
Tyrion admits he was never able to understand Orson
Tyrion asks Jaime if he thinks Oberyn stands a chance
Jaime expresses pessimism about Oberyn's chances
a bell rings out to announce daybreak in the city
Jaime takes his leave, wishing Tyrion luck
Lannister guardsmen escort Tyrion to the arena
a large crowd assembles in the arena
Tyrion grows apprehensive upon seeing Oberyn drinking and wearing very light armor
Oberyn drinks and wears very light armor, lacking a helm
Oberyn dismisses Tyrion’s concerns, explaining he always drinks before a fight and favors speed over protection
Ser Gregor Clegane and his squire make their way to the arena, Gregor clad in heavy armor and wielding a greatsword
Ellaria becomes concerned, noting The Mountain is the biggest man she’s ever seen
Oberyn remains confident he will emerge triumphant
Grand Maester Pycelle steps into the arena and begins a long-winded speech asking the gods to favor the innocent
Tywin Lannister cuts Pycelle short and begins the trial by combat
Oberyn impresses the crowd with a display of acrobatic spear maneuvers
Oberyn attacks Gregor, stabbing out and constantly moving out of Gregor's reach
Oberyn forces Gregor to tire himself out chasing his opponent
Oberyn repeatedly taunts Gregor by bringing up his part in the murder of Elia Martell and her children, demanding a confession
Oberyn enrages Gregor further
Oberyn seriously injures Gregor by stabbing him in the chest, severing his Achilles tendon, and burying his spear in Gregor's stomach, pinning him to the floor
Jaime and Tyrion exchange a relieved grin across the arena
Jaime and Tyrion exchange a relieved grin across the arena
Oberyn circles the prone Gregor, furiously demanding a confession for the murder and rape of Elia and her children, and that Tywin gave the order
Oberyn stands too close to his seemingly defeated opponent
Gregor trips and seizes Oberyn
Gregor grabs Oberyn by the throat and lifts him off the ground
Gregor smashes out most of Oberyn’s teeth with a single devastating punch
Gregor climbs on top of Oberyn
Gregor admits for all to hear that he raped and killed Elia
Gregor gouges out Oberyn’s eyes with his thumbs
Gregor crushes Oberyn’s skull between his hands
Gregor proclaims he did the same to Elia’s children
Ellaria screams in horror
a stunned silence sweeps over the crowd
Tyrion and Jaime’s short joyful moments are shattered
Tywin stands and proclaims the will of the gods is clear: Tyrion is guilty and sentenced to death
Tyrion stares in catatonic astonishment at Oberyn’s corpse
Jaime stares in catatonic astonishment at Oberyn’s corpse
Cersei stares at Oberyn’s slaughtered body, listening to Tywin proclaim Tyrion’s death sentence while smirking in vindication

The_Watchers_on_the_Wall.action: Jon and Sam stand watch atop the Wall, staring out into the darkness and awaiting the wildling invasion
Sam attempts to interrogate Jon about what it was like to be with a woman
Sam states that their deaths are looming and it may be his last chance to find out
Sam brings up that the vows of the Night's Watch only forbid taking wives or producing children, and other activities are open to interpretation
Jon replies glumly that Alliser Thorne most likely does not care about their interpretations
Sam presses Jon to describe lying down with Ygritte
Jon attempts to explain but is unable to properly express it
Jon proclaims exasperatedly that he's "not a bleeding poet."
Jon offers to take the watch up alone so Sam can go below
Sam steps into the wooden lift and begins to descend the Wall
An owl lands on top of the wall and begins hooting loudly
The owl is revealed to be controlled by a Thenn warg, a member of the wildling platoon under Tormund and Styr
Tormund boasts about a bear he supposedly had sex with
Ygritte cuts Tormund short and casts doubt on his claim
Ygritte says she only cares about getting as many arrows through the hearts of "crows" as possible
Ygritte angrily talks about how the people south of the Wall have used it to shut the wildlings out and hunt them as enemies
Ygritte says now the wildlings are the hunters and the southerners their prey
Styr mockingly replies that Ygritte has more words about killing than she has arrows
Ygritte defends herself by saying she killed just as many people at Mole's Town as Styr
Styr replies that none of them was Ygritte's former lover
Styr mocks Ygritte by saying that when she sees Jon, she'll likely just serve him a slice of "ginger minge"
Ygritte jumps to her feet in anger
Ygritte stares Styr down, claiming she wants to kill Jon Snow herself
Ygritte warns the others that she has an arrow ready for anyone else who attempts to kill Jon
A shaded person cradling something passes by the wildlings unnoticed just outside the camp
Sam is caught reading a book about wildlings by Maester Aemon
Aemon correctly guesses Sam is reading the book to learn what fate might have befallen Gilly if she fell into wildling hands
Aemon surmises Sam is interested in Gilly's fate because he is in love with her, despite Sam's protests
Aemon muses about the girl he once loved, stating he can still picture her as clear as day and she is more real than Sam
Aemon dismisses Sam from the maester's chambers
Sam leaves the maester's chambers
Sam hears Gilly at the gate begging to be allowed inside, revealing her survival and identity as the cloaked figure
Pypar attempts to deny Gilly passage into the castle, saying he is not allowed to open the gate
Sam roars at Pypar to allow Gilly in
Pypar relents and lets Gilly in
Sam promises Gilly that wherever she goes next, he will go too
Their reunion is cut short by two blows from the horn, indicating Mance's wildling army has begun their assault
Sam rushes to get Gilly safely inside the castle
Other brothers of the Night's Watch prepare for the impending attack by wildlings
Jon hears the horn blowing and looks north, witnessing a tremendous conflagration to the North
Mance's promise of a fire is fulfilled
The Thenn warg's owl spots the fire south of the Wall
The owl informs Styr and Tormund that the time has come to attack Castle Black
Jon approaches Ser Alliser Thorne
Ser Alliser Thorne relents and admits they should have heeded Jon's advice and blocked the gates
Ser Alliser Thorne muses about the burdens of leadership
Ser Alliser Thorne steels himself for the impending siege
Ser Alliser Thorne begins barking orders to the archers, preparing them to fire upon the attackers
Sam brings Gilly and her baby to a storage room to hide
Gilly wants Sam to stay with her as he promised
Sam refuses to stay, explaining he swore to defend the Wall as a brother of the Night's Watch
Gilly begs Sam to promise he won't die
Sam promises Gilly he won't die
Sam and Gilly share a kiss
Sam and Pypar prepare arrows at the southern gate
Pypar laments he has never seen a real battle or held an edged blade
Pypar asks Sam how he is not afraid
Sam replies that he is afraid, as thousands of wildlings are about to overrun them
Pypar asks how Sam managed to kill a White Walker while being afraid
Sam explains he acted to save Gilly and her baby
Sam says that at that moment, he wasn't himself; he was nothing, and when you are nothing there is nothing to be afraid of
Sam explains he is afraid now because "I'm not nothing anymore."
Ygritte spies on the Castle to scout numbers of defenders
Ygritte tells the other wildlings that most guards are at the top of the Wall and few are left in the Castle
The wildling group immediately take off towards Castle Black's southern gate
Ygritte seems to have second thoughts
The massive wildling army emerges from the woods north of the Wall, including giants riding atop mammoths
Alliser orders the archers on the Wall to nock their arrows
The archers draw their arrows instead
Grenn accidentally drops a barrel down the Wall
Alliser angrily drills his men on properly following orders
A horn blows from down below at Castle Black, signaling the appearance of Tormund's band of wildlings at the southern entrance
Alliser realizes the need for experienced men to defend the keep from the surprise attack
Alliser orders Janos Slynt to assume command of the Wall's defenses
Alliser heads below to join the melee
Tormund and Styr's forces charge the gate
Ygritte picks off several men defending the gate with her bow
Pyp fires a crossbow
Sam assists Pyp by reloading another crossbow
Pyp repeatedly misses his shots due to inexperience and nerves
The band of wildlings reach Castle Black's walls and begin scaling them using grappling hooks and ropes
Sam and Pyp retreat
Alliser Thorne arrives with reinforcements from the top of the Wall via the wooden lift system
Olly operates the wooden lift system
Alliser gives an impassioned speech, promising the Night's Watch and Castle Black will survive
Alliser's speech rallies the black brothers
Alliser personally leads the charge against the wildlings who have breached Castle Black's defenses
An intense battle erupts in the courtyard
Wildling strike force led by two giants (one riding a mammoth) charges towards Castle Black's outer gate
Janos Slynt breaks down at the sight of the massive wildling army and begins fumbling his orders
Grenn falsely claims that Alliser needs Janos back down below
Janos believes Grenn's lie and leaves the top of the Wall
Jon takes command of the Wall's defenses
The black brothers rain down arrows on the advancing wildlings, killing some who attempt to scale the Wall
Dongo fires a huge arrow at one of the bunkers atop the Wall, obliterating it and killing the black brother manning it
The force of the giant's arrow carries the black brother off the Wall and shoves him into the ground with tremendous force
The battle rages on between the wildlings and the Night's Watch at Castle Black
Styr slays numerous black brothers
Ygritte slays numerous black brothers
A group of wildlings breach Castle Black's dining hall
A black brother throws boiling stew in a wildling's face and beats him to death with the pot
Hobb enters the dining hall and kills a wildling with his massive butcher's knife
Janos Slynt cowardly retreats to a storage room
Janos Slynt discovers Gilly and her baby in the storage room
Pyp kills a wildling with his crossbow
Ygritte shoots Pyp through the neck
Sam comforts Pyp and stays with him until he dies
Sam is forced to leave Pyp's body
Sam heads to Castle Black's lift system
Sam is spotted by the Thenn warg
The warg charges at Sam
Sam desperately attempts to load his crossbow
Sam fires a bolt into the warg's head just before the warg reaches him
Alliser Thorne duels Tormund on the catwalks of Castle Black
Thorne and Tormund seem evenly matched in skill
Thorne suffers a grievous wound
Thorne is forced to retreat, being dragged to safety while continuing to shout orders to his men
The black brothers defending the Wall repel most of the wildlings attacking the outer gate
One giant makes it through and repeatedly smashes the outer gate with his fists, greatly weakening it
The giant begins lifting the gate singlehandedly
Jon sends a group of black brothers led by Grenn to hold the inner gate at any cost
Grenn leads the group of black brothers to the inner gate
Donnel Hill cowers when faced by the charging giant
Grenn bolsters Donnel Hill's and the others' courage by reciting the Night's Watch oath
The black brothers ready their weapons as the giant slams himself into the inner gate
Sam arrives atop the Wall to ask Jon for more men to defend the castle
Jon tasks Sam with releasing Ghost from his pen to assist in the fighting
Sam releases Ghost from his pen
Jon descends the Wall and enters the fray
Jon quickly dispatches several wildlings
Jon catches the attention of Styr
Jon and Styr meet in single combat
Styr knocks Longclaw away and brutally assaults Jon
Styr smashes Jon’s face into an anvil and tosses him into the blacksmith’s forge
Styr picks Jon up and begins strangling him
Jon spits blood in Styr’s face, distracting him
Jon grabs the blacksmith’s hammer lying next to him
Jon buries the hammer in Styr’s skull
Jon kills Styr
Jon turns around to find Ygritte pointing her bow at him with an arrow drawn
Ygritte points her bow at Jon with an arrow drawn
Jon smiles at Ygritte
Ygritte hesitates
Olly shoots Ygritte near the heart with an arrow
Olly avenges his father's death at Ygritte's hands during the wildling attack on his village
Jon holds Ygritte in his arms
Ygritte tells Jon they should have never left the cave
Jon and Ygritte lament circumstances that prevented them from being together
Ygritte succumbs to her wound
Eddison Tollett, left in command by Jon, orders the men to drop the 'scythe'
A huge section of ice falls away from the Wall, revealing a massive anchor-like blade attached to a chain
The anchor-like blade swings along the Wall, mowing through wildlings attempting to climb its face
Eddison notices the wildling forces beginning to withdraw into the Haunted Forest
Eddison proclaims that they have stopped the wildlings for now, but that the wildlings still outnumber the black brothers 1,000 to 1 and that Mance was simply testing their defenses
Tormund, heavily wounded, is surrounded in the courtyard but continues to fight despite being the only wildling alive in Castle Black
Jon subdues Tormund with a crossbow and Tormund is taken prisoner by the Night's Watch for interrogation
Sam returns to Gilly as he promised
Sam discovers Janos Slynt, who hid in fear during the entire battle
The Night's Watch repels Mance Rayder's forces for the time being
Castle Black's remaining garrison begin to regroup and prepare for the next attack by Mance Rayder
Jon discusses with Sam his plan to assassinate Mance Rayder to end the wildling threat
Sam tries to talk sense into Jon
Jon refuses to change his mind
Jon prepares to leave via Castle Black's tunnel
Jon and Sam discover the bodies of the black brothers who held the inner gate against the giant
Grenn is revealed to be among the casualties
Jon tells Sam that all of the bodies must be burned
Jon remembers his promise to Jeor Mormont and decides to leave Longclaw with Sam
Sam tells Jon to come back
Jon looks at Sam and smiles dolefully before stepping out into the wilderness on the other side of the gate

The_Children.action: Jon emerges from the outer gate of the Wall and trudges north through the aftermath of the previous night's battle towards Mance Rayder's war camp
Jon reaches the tree line and is quickly surrounded by wildlings
wildlings bring Jon before Mance
Mance notes that Jon is wearing the black of the Night's Watch again
Mance brings Jon inside his tent and questions his status as a traitor
Jon confesses that his time with the wildlings was part of Qhorin's plan to infiltrate Mance's army
Jon explains that Qhorin tricked Mance into trusting Jon by allowing Jon to kill Qhorin in a fight
Mance asks Jon about Ygritte
Jon reveals that Ygritte died at the battle for the Wall, though not by his hands
Mance proposes a toast to Ygritte's memory
Jon agrees to the toast, but hesitates when given an unfamiliar drink
Mance assures Jon that poison would be the last of his methods if he wanted to kill Jon
Jon accepts the drink
Jon and Mance drink to Ygritte
Mance asks Jon about the giant who got through the gate but never came out
Jon informs Mance that the giant perished attempting to breach the inner gate at the hands of his friend Grenn
Mance notes that the giant was Mag the Mighty, king of the giants and last of his bloodline
Jon glibly notes that Grenn was from a farm
Jon and Mance have another toast to Mag and Grenn
Jon proposes his terms to Mance: turn his army around and return home
Mance counters that the Night's Watch is low on oil, arrows, and men
