Mance explains that the Night's Watch threw everything they had to repel his attack
Mance guesses there are no more than 50 men left guarding Castle Black
Jon lies and insists there are still over 1,000 men defending the garrison
Mance calls Jon's bluff and reveals he sent 400 men to climb an unmanned section of the Wall
Mance states the 400 men will attack Castle Black's weak southern side as soon as he gives the order
Mance changes his demeanor and reveals his people have suffered enough bloodshed
Mance states he has no intention of conquering the lands south of the Wall
Mance explains the wildlings are moving south to seek refuge from the White Walkers
Mance makes his counteroffer: if the Night's Watch opens the gates, no more harm will come to the black brothers
Mance threatens that if his terms are refused, his wildling army will slaughter every last member of the Night's Watch and move south by force
Mance notices Jon eyeing a cooking knife
Mance deduces Jon came to parley in order to assassinate him
Mance notes his personal guards are far enough away that Jon might be able to kill him before being cut down
Mance realizes Jon must have known he would die assassinating Mance
Mance is shocked and questions Jon about the Night's Watch's intentions
Jon hesitates
war horns are heard from outside
Mance's guards grab Jon during the distraction
Mance demands to know if the Night's Watch is attacking
Jon admits the Night's Watch doesn't have enough numbers to attack

Columns of heavy horse carrying hundreds of mounted cavalry charge into the wildling camp carrying the flaming stag banners of House Baratheon of Dragonstone, led by Stannis Baratheon and Davos Seaworth
The wildlings are caught in their own camp, resting from the battle the night before and only expecting attacks from the Wall, so their eastern flank is completely undefended
The wildlings, undisciplined and inexperienced against heavily armored cavalry, are quickly dispatched by Stannis' riders
Stannis' riders attack the wildling camp from both sides in two separate columns, catching the wildlings in a double envelopment
Stannis and Davos ride up to Mance
Mance throws down his weapons in surrender
The wildlings' siege of Castle Black officially ends
Davos introduces Stannis as the true king of the Seven Kingdoms
Mance points out that they are not currently in the Seven Kingdoms
Stannis demands that it is customary to kneel when surrendering to a king
Mance says that the Free Folk do not kneel, knowing Stannis will kill him if he does not
Davos asks what a man of the Night's Watch is doing in the camp
Jon explains that he came to treaty with the King-Beyond-the-Wall
Jon says he knows Stannis is the true king, as his own father died supporting his claim, revealing himself as the bastard son of Eddard Stark
Stannis regards Jon more seriously
Stannis proclaims that Eddard was an honorable man, and asks Jon what Ned would have done with Mance
Jon responds that Mance could have killed or tortured him, but instead spared his life
Jon tells Stannis that his father would have taken Mance prisoner and listened to what he had to say
Jon urges Stannis to burn the dead before nightfall
a funeral is held for the slain members of the Night's Watch, arranged in a huge funeral pyre in the castle courtyard
Maester Aemon delivers a eulogy, honoring the fallen and reminding the survivors of their duty
Aemon closes his speech with "and now their watch has ended," which is repeated by the gathered men
Samwell aids Aemon in setting the pyre alight
Jon and other men continue lighting the pyre with torches
Stannis, Davos, Queen Selyse, and Princess Shireen look on
Jon looks across the flames and catches Melisandre's gaze
Melisandre stands on the opposite side of the pyre, staring at Jon and making him uneasy
Jon enters the chamber of the castle where Tormund is being held
Tormund asks Jon why Maester Aemon patched up his arrow wounds
Jon says Aemon is sworn to heal all wounded men, friend or foe
Tormund asks Jon if he really loved Ygritte
Tormund tells Jon that Ygritte truly loved him
Jon asks if Ygritte told Tormund this
Tormund says no, and explains he knew because Ygritte always talked about killing Jon
Tormund implores Jon to lay Ygritte to rest in "the real north"
Jon takes Ygritte's corpse to the north side of the Wall
Jon builds a funeral pyre for Ygritte near a sacred Heart tree
Jon looks at Ygritte one last time
Jon lights Ygritte's pyre
Jon weeps silently, turns his back, and heads home
Bran and the Reed children continue their journey to find the Three-Eyed Raven, on the brink of exhaustion
Jojen grows sick and is barely able to stand, eventually collapsing into the snow
Meera begs Jojen to gather strength to continue forward
Bran calls out to Meera and Jojen, revealing they have found the massive heart tree from his visions
Bran, Meera, and Jojen approach the heart tree
wights burst up from the snow, grabbing Jojen's ankles and attempting to drag him under the ice
wights appear as ghastly skeletal warriors who move with surprising agility
Meera attempts to defend Jojen from the wights
wights begin to swarm over Hodor, who is too scared to fight back
Bran wargs into Hodor's mind and begins fighting off the wights with the aid of his direwolf Summer
more wights keep bursting through the ice and the group is quickly overwhelmed
The wights around Bran suddenly burst into fireballs
Bran hears a young girl's voice calling to him from a cave entrance at the base of the heart tree
Jojen is repeatedly stabbed by a wight before Meera can fight it off
The mysterious girl calls to Meera and says that Jojen is lost and she must leave him to live
Meera hesitates
Jojen urges Meera to save herself
Meera, crying, slits her brother's throat to grant him a quick death
Meera runs to the cave entrance along with Bran, Hodor, and Summer
The mysterious child covers their retreat by magically throwing fireballs from her hands at the wights
Just as the life drains from Jojen's eyes, the mysterious child launches a fireball at his body, immolating his corpse and preventing his return as a wight
They run into the cave, pursued by the wights
Wights instantly shatter upon crossing the threshold of the cave
The little girl explains that the power animating the dead is powerless inside the cave
Bran asks the little girl who she is
The little girl replies that the First Men called them 'the Children,' but they were born long before the First Men
Bran realizes the little girl is one of the last surviving Children of the Forest, the original non-human inhabitants of Westeros
The child leads the group deeper into the cave overgrown with white weirwood roots
Other Children of the Forest peek out around corners as the group advances
The group arrives at a large central chamber with a floor littered with bones
Hodor places Bran on the ground
Bran crawls towards Brynden Rivers
Bran asks Brynden if he is the Three-Eyed Raven from his visions
Brynden admits he is the Three-Eyed Raven, having taken several forms in the past
Meera expresses her upset that her brother died to reach Brynden
Brynden says Jojen always knew he would die helping Bran, but came nonetheless
Brynden informs them he has been watching all of them throughout their lives "with a thousand eyes, and one."
Brynden states Jojen died so Bran could come and regain what he has lost
Bran asks if he will regain the use of his legs
Brynden replies Bran will never walk again, "but you will fly."
Daenerys sits in her throne room receiving the day's supplicants
Fennesz approaches the throne, first speaking in High Valyrian
Fennesz reveals that he also knows the Common Tongue
Fennesz explains he was a well-educated teacher and servant to Master Mighdal, not a manual labor slave
Fennesz tells Daenerys that Master Mighdal's daughter Calla admires Daenerys, having learned of the Targaryen dynasty through his teachings
Fennesz is well-treated and respected as a servant in Master Mighdal's household
Daenerys forcibly frees all of the slaves in the city
Daenerys does not understand the full-scale complications of caring for so many freed people
Fennesz stays in Master Mighdal's house after Daenerys takes the city
Mighdal's children beg Fennesz to stay
Mighdal and Fennesz agree that Fennesz must leave to avoid reprisals
Fennesz becomes homeless
Daenerys insists she has established mess halls and barracks for the freed slaves
Fennesz visits the refugee centers and finds them unsafe
Fennesz observes the young preying on the old, assaulting and robbing them
Daenerys insists her Unsullied will restore order
Fennesz points out that even with safety, he has lost his livelihood and purpose and is too old to start anew
Fennesz comes to Daenerys to beg permission to sell himself back to Mighdal
Daenerys is shocked that Fennesz wants to be a slave again
Fennesz implores Daenerys, explaining the old fear change and live in squalor
Fennesz says many supplicants are waiting outside to make similar requests
Daenerys is crestfallen and says she did not liberate the slaves of Meereen only to preside over the very injustice she sought to destroy
Daenerys relents and admits that freedom means making one's own choices
Daenerys allows Fennesz to enter into a labor contract with Mighdal, but lasting no more than one year
Fennesz earnestly thanks Daenerys and leaves
Ser Barristan Selmy warns Daenerys that the masters will take advantage of this, making "contracts" with all the former slaves until they are slaves again in all but name
The goatherd enters carrying a bundle in his arms
Daenerys greets the goatherd cordially and bids him approach
The goatherd timidly shuffles forward and states he cannot speak the common tongue, requiring Missandei to translate
Missandei translates for the goatherd
The goatherd places the bundle on the ground and opens it to reveal charred bones of a human child
The goatherd tells Daenerys that her dragon Drogon killed his three-year-old daughter Zalla
Daenerys is horrified
Daenerys meets with Missandei and Grey Worm in private to discuss the threat of the dragons
Grey Worm reports that Drogon was last seen flying over the Black Cliffs three days ago but cannot be found
Daenerys realizes she can no longer control her dragons and tells them to head to the catacombs
Daenerys leads Rhaegal and Viserion into the catacombs, distracting them with sheep carcasses
Rhaegal and Viserion feed on the sheep carcasses
Daenerys locks iron collars around Rhaegal and Viserion’s necks, securing them with heavy chains
Daenerys weeps as she chains her dragons
Daenerys leaves and closes the stone door to the catacombs behind her
Rhaegal and Viserion grow distressed and call after Daenerys when they realize they are chained
Cersei, Pycelle, and Qyburn stand over Ser Gregor Clegane, who lies immobilized on a table with a massive wound
Pycelle informs Cersei that Oberyn Martell coated his spear with manticore venom, poisoning Ser Gregor
Gregor's wounds putrefy and create a terrible stench
Pycelle says Gregor is beyond any hope of recovery
Qyburn insists to Cersei that he can save Gregor using unorthodox methods
Pycelle chafes at Qyburn's suggestion, stating he is not a maester and referencing Qyburn's expulsion from the Citadel
Cersei dismisses Pycelle and orders Qyburn to do whatever is necessary
Pycelle angrily shuffles out of the laboratory
Cersei asks Qyburn if his treatment will work
Qyburn claims he stands a chance at saving Gregor based on his past work
Qyburn states the process will "change" Gregor
Cersei asks if the process will weaken Gregor
Qyburn replies "Oh no."
Cersei leaves the laboratory
Qyburn begins siphoning out Gregor's tainted blood
Cersei approaches Tywin and insists she will not submit to a forced marriage to Ser Loras Tyrell
Tywin attempts to dismiss the subject by stating that the matter is closed
Cersei persists and explains her reasons for refusing the marriage
Cersei accuses Tywin of wanting to remove her from court and keep her away from Tommen
Cersei claims that with her gone, Margaery and Tywin will both try to manipulate Tommen
Tywin refuses to change his mind
Cersei threatens to destroy House Lannister by telling everyone the truth
Tywin seems confused by Cersei's threat
Cersei scoffs, accusing Tywin of feigning ignorance
Cersei realizes Tywin never noticed what was going on between his own children
Tywin remains confused
Cersei reveals that all the rumors about her and Jaime (and their children) are true
Cersei threatens to destroy Tywin's family legacy, even at the cost of her and Tommen's lives
Tywin shakes with barely constrained rage but denies the truth, accusing Cersei of lying
Cersei says she doubts Tywin truly believes she is lying and leaves him standing alone
Cersei finds Jaime in the White Sword Tower
Jaime expresses disgust at Cersei's attempts to have Tyrion killed
Cersei asserts that Tyrion killed their mother when she gave birth to him
Jaime is surprised that Cersei still blames Tyrion, pointing out he was an infant
Jaime insists that Tyrion is Cersei's family, whether she accepts it or not
Cersei rebukes Jaime's claim, saying Tyrion is not her family and that she chooses Jaime
Cersei reveals she has told Tywin their secret
Jaime is stunned and concerned, asking how Tywin reacted
Cersei states she does not want to discuss their father
Cersei begins to seduce Jaime, saying she chooses him over everyone else
Jaime yields to Cersei's advances, kissing her passionately and throwing her on the table
Jaime and Cersei begin to have sex
Tyrion is awoken in his cell by the sound of his cell door being opened by Jaime
Jaime opens Tyrion's cell door
Jaime reveals he has orchestrated an escape plan for Tyrion
Varys secures a galley in the harbor for Tyrion to escape the city
Jaime leads Tyrion to a stairway leading up to a locked door
Jaime instructs Tyrion to knock on the door twice and then twice more to summon Varys
Jaime and Tyrion share a tender embrace and say their goodbyes
Jaime and Tyrion share a tender embrace and say their goodbyes
Tyrion stares at the door at the top of the staircase, deciding to use the secret passages of the Red Keep
Tyrion infiltrates the Tower of the Hand using the secret passages
Tyrion enters the Hand of the King's quarters
Tyrion sees a woman lying in Tywin's bed, with Tywin's clothes folded on a chair
Tyrion draws closer and sees the woman is Shae
Shae calls out for Tywin, calling him "my lion"
Shae turns over and sees Tyrion, looking utterly betrayed
Shae grabs a knife to defend herself
Tyrion rushes forward onto the bed to wrestle the knife from Shae's grasp
Tyrion knocks the knife from Shae's hands
Tyrion and Shae struggle violently
Tyrion seizes the necklace around Shae's throat and strangles her with it
Tyrion continues to strangle Shae long after she has stopped resisting, until she is dead
Tyrion sinks to the floor and sits next to Shae's lifeless body, weeping, and tells her he's sorry
Tyrion stares at the wall across the room and sees several weapons on display, including Joffrey's old crossbow
Tyrion's expression quickly darkens
Tyrion takes the crossbow down from the wall
Tyrion loads the crossbow
Tyrion heads down the hall to the privy
Tyrion opens the door to the privy and finds Tywin seated on the toilet
Tywin seems surprised to see Tyrion, but quickly regains composure
Tywin asks Tyrion to lower the weapon
Tywin deduces that Jaime helped Tyrion escape his cell
Tywin tells Tyrion they can discuss the matter back in Tywin's chambers
Tywin attempts to stand
Tyrion steadies the crossbow, stopping Tywin
Tyrion states that Tywin has wanted him dead all his life
Tywin acknowledges Tyrion's claim
Tywin tries to defuse the situation by saying he admires Tyrion's will to survive and insists he was never going to have Tyrion killed
Tyrion admits he just murdered Shae with his bare hands
Tywin scoffs that Shae was nothing more than a whore
Tyrion is angered by Tywin's indifference and raises the crossbow, threatening to kill Tywin if he says "whore" again
Tywin balks that Tyrion won't kill his own father in the privy
Tywin tries to convince Tyrion to continue the discussion in his chambers
Tyrion says he cannot go back to Tywin's chambers because Shae's body is still there
Tywin mocks Tyrion by saying "because you're afraid of a dead whore?"
Tyrion loosens his crossbow bolt, hitting Tywin in the belly and knocking him backward
Tywin is incredulous that Tyrion fought back and that he will die at Tyrion's hands
Tyrion reloads the crossbow
Tywin angrily declares that Tyrion is no son of his
Tyrion replies, "I am your son. I have always been your son," and fires a second bolt into Tywin's heart, killing him
Tyrion discards the crossbow and leaves to find Varys
Varys realizes Tyrion has done something terrible and exclaims, "What have you done?"
Varys leads Tyrion out of the castle to the docks
Tyrion is stowed away in a crate aboard the ship
Varys begins walking back towards the Red Keep
The bells begin to toll, signaling the discovery of Tywin's body
Varys realizes returning is unsafe, turns around, and heads back for the ship
Varys sits next to Tyrion's crate in silence as they cast off for Essos
Brienne and Podrick head towards the Bloody Gate in the Vale
Arya practices her water dancing while Sandor Clegane is off relieving himself
Brienne and Podrick happen upon Arya and Sandor
Brienne and Arya converse, showing mutual respect
Sandor returns and is recognized by Podrick
Brienne realizes the girl is Arya
Brienne tells Arya of her vow to protect Catelyn Stark, but admits she failed
Sandor accuses Brienne of working for the Lannisters, noticing her Valyrian steel sword
Brienne admits the sword was given to her by Jaime
Arya is frightened, thinking Brienne works for the Lannisters
Brienne implores Arya to come with her to safety
Sandor mocks Brienne, saying there is no safety for Arya anymore
Brienne and Sandor draw swords and begin fighting
Brienne and Sandor disarm each other and fight hand-to-hand
Brienne gains the upper hand, biting off a chunk of Sandor’s ear and battering him with a rock, spilling him over a cliffside
Arya slips away and hides among the rocks during the fight
Brienne and Podrick search for Arya in vain
Arya makes her way down the cliffside to find Sandor badly wounded
Sandor tells Arya he is finished unless a maester appears
Sandor tells Arya to kill him and scratch his name off her list
Arya sits and stares at Sandor silently
Sandor tries to goad Arya into killing him by recounting his crimes
Arya remains silent
Sandor begs Arya to kill him and put him out of his misery
Arya refuses, takes his money purse, and leaves him screaming as she walks away
Arya rides on horseback to Saltpans on the coast of the Vale
Arya sees a wharf with ships docked
Arya approaches a captain and asks if she can pay for travel to the North
The captain refuses, saying the North is full of war, cold, and pirates
The captain says the ship is going to Braavos instead
Arya remembers the special coin Jaqen H'ghar gave her
Arya presents the coin to the captain
The captain is astonished and asks how Arya obtained the coin
Arya replies "Valar morghulis"
The captain responds "Valar dohaeris" and warmly welcomes Arya aboard
The captain insists Arya will have her own cabin
The ship casts off from the shore
Arya stands aboard the ship and watches the shore of Westeros shrink away
Arya moves to the prow of the ship, looking forward across the Narrow Sea toward Braavos

The_Wars_To_Come.action: Cersei and Melara creep through the woods of the Westerlands
Melara expresses reluctance to continue
Cersei encourages Melara to keep going
Cersei and Melara reach a hut belonging to a woods witch named Maggy
Cersei walks into the hut first, unafraid
Cersei demands Maggy read her future
Maggy hesitates to read Cersei’s future
Cersei threatens to have Maggy’s eyes gouged out
Maggy relents and agrees to read Cersei’s future
Maggy requests a drop of Cersei’s blood
Cersei pricks her finger on a dagger
Maggy tastes Cersei’s blood
Maggy offers Cersei three questions
Cersei asks if she will marry the prince
Maggy says Cersei will not marry the prince, but will marry the king and become queen
Cersei asks if she and the king will have children
Maggy tells Cersei the king will have twenty children, but she will have three, and all three will die before her
Maggy tells Cersei her reign will not last and a younger, more beautiful queen will take her place
Cersei arrives at the Great Sept of Baelor to pay respect to her deceased father Tywin
Cersei enters the Sept
Cersei chastises Jaime for freeing Tyrion and indirectly causing Tywin's death
Lancel Lannister, now a devout member of the Sparrows, approaches Cersei at Tywin's wake
Lancel asks Cersei's forgiveness for their adulterous relationship and for getting Robert Baratheon drunk on the day he died
Cersei mocks Lancel, denies knowledge of Robert's murder, and shrugs him off
Loras and Olyvar contemplate a birthmark on Loras's thigh that looks like Dorne
Margaery walks in on Loras and Olyvar
Margaery complains about Loras's lack of discretion
Margaery sends Olyvar away
Loras retorts that his sexuality is an open secret and there isn't much point in discretion
Loras opines that with Tywin dead, no one will force Cersei to marry him anymore
Loras points out that Cersei will remain in King's Landing, free to harass Margaery
Margaery replies with "perhaps."
Tyrion arrives in Pentos with the aid of Varys
Varys lets Tyrion out of the crate he’d been hiding in for the entire journey
Varys tells Tyrion that he and Illyrio Mopatis had worked together in secret to restore House Targaryen to the rule of Westeros
Varys explains that their errors have left them in Pentos, unable to return to King's Landing
Tyrion descends into bitterness and self-loathing, determined to drink himself to death
Varys angrily snaps Tyrion out of his self pity
Varys tells Tyrion that he still has talents that may yet be of use
Varys states that Westeros is mired in chaos and on the brink of ruin
Varys describes the kind of ruler the Seven Kingdoms require
Tyrion retorts that such a man does not exist
Varys points out that he never said this ruler would be a man
Varys offers Tyrion the choice of drinking himself to death in Pentos or traveling to Meereen to meet Daenerys Targaryen
Tyrion agrees to travel with Varys to Meereen
The Unsullied topple the large golden harpy from the top of the Great Pyramid in Meereen
The Unsullied show the Meereenese people they are under Targaryen rule and old traditions are gone
White Rat walks into a brothel
White Rat pays a prostitute to lie with him and comfort him
White Rat lies in bed
A member of the Sons of the Harpy slashes White Rat’s throat
The murderer leaves a mask at the scene
Daenerys is presented with the murderer's mask
Daenerys orders Grey Worm to find those responsible
Daenerys orders the murdered soldier to be buried in the Temple of the Graces as a statement
Hizdahr zo Loraq and Daario Naharis return to Meereen and declare their mission to Yunkai has been successful
Hizdahr and Daario announce that the Wise Masters will turn over power to a council of former slaves and former slave owners
The Wise Masters ask Daenerys to consent to the reopening of the fighting pits
Daenerys denies the request to reopen the fighting pits
Daario convinces Daenerys to reconsider reopening the fighting pits, sharing his own experience
Daario explains that fighting in the pits gave him the skills to join the Second Sons and meet Daenerys
Daario muses on the irony of a dragon-queen with no dragons after hearing Drogon hasn't been seen in weeks
Daenerys attempts to visit Viserion and Rhaegal, whom she locked underground in the Meereenese catacombs
Daenerys locked Viserion and Rhaegal underground to prevent them from killing innocents or fleeing
Drogon killed innocents and fled
Daenerys approaches Viserion and Rhaegal
Viserion and Rhaegal attempt to attack Daenerys
Daenerys briskly escapes the room
Littlefinger and Sansa watch over Lord Robin Arryn's struggles while sparring with another young boy
Robin Arryn spars with another young boy
Sansa witnesses Baelish receive a message
Baelish reads the message and hides it in his sleeve
Lord Yohn Royce agrees to take Robin as his ward and train him to fight
Lord Yohn Royce expresses pessimism about Robin's abilities
Littlefinger tells Lord Royce that he and Sansa will be traveling to the Fingers
Littlefinger and Sansa instead head west
Sansa asks if they have anything to fear from Lord Yohn
Littlefinger explains he doesn't trust the retainers and soldiers who saw them
Littlefinger says he is taking Sansa somewhere the Lannisters will never find her
Podrick Payne attempts to plan the next move for himself and Brienne of Tarth
Brienne tells Podrick that she doesn't want anyone following her
Podrick reminds Brienne of the oath she swore to Jaime to find the Stark girls
Brienne states that Arya did not want her protection
Brienne points out that Podrick was not safe in King's Landing and now he is safe far from the capital
Brienne suggests they can go their separate ways
Littlefinger and Sansa's carriage passes by Brienne and Podrick
Brienne is unaware of how close her goal is
Jon attempts to train Olly
Melisandre interrupts Jon's training and fetches him for an audience with Stannis
Jon rides to the top of the Wall with Melisandre, feeling discomfited by her presence and questions
Stannis explains his plan to retake the North from Roose Bolton and his need for an army
Stannis asks Jon to convince Mance Rayder to bend the knee and have the wildlings fight for him, offering freedom and land in exchange
Jon relays Stannis's message to Mance
Mance refuses to bend the knee, citing it would show unforgivable weakness to his people
Mance is sentenced to death by burning at the stake for refusing to fight for Stannis
On the night of his execution, Mance stands before Stannis and offers him luck in the wars to come
Mance is led to the stake
Melisandre lights the pyre
Mance begins to feel the pain of the fire
Jon turns and storms off, unable to watch Mance suffer
Others, including Gilly and Shireen, turn away from the scene
As Mance begins to scream, Jon shoots an arrow into his chest, giving him a merciful death
Mance dies before feeling the full agony of the flames

The_House_of_Black_and_White.action: Ternesio Terys and Arya arrive at Braavos
Arya is scared by the horn blown by the man inside the Titan of Braavos
Ternesio and Arya arrive at the House of Black and White on a boat
Arya lingers outside the House waiting for admittance
An elder man rejects Arya despite her showing the coin and mentioning Jaqen H'ghar
Arya begs the man to let her in, saying she has nowhere else to go
The old man coldly replies "You have everywhere else to go."
Arya waits outside the House for days, reciting the names of people she wants to kill
Arya tosses away her coin and wanders off into the streets of Braavos
Arya hunts for pigeons
Several young thugs threaten to take Arya's food and Needle
Arya prepares to defend herself
The boys scatter when the man from the House of Black and White appears
Arya follows the man back to the House
Arya demands the man divulge his identity
The man returns Arya's coin to her
The man peels off his face and reveals himself as the man Arya knew as Jaqen
The man states "a man is not Jaqen H'ghar" and answers Arya's question with "No one. And that is what a girl must become"
The man indicates Arya may now enter the House
Tyrion and Varys depart Pentos in a large wheelhouse pulled by many horses
Tyrion remains despondent and continues to drink himself into a stupor
Varys explains they are traveling to Meereen by way of Volantis
Varys points out Tyrion was an effective ruler as acting Hand of the King
Varys notes that both he and Tyrion are outsiders due to their deformities
Varys says they enjoyed accumulating power but walled themselves off from others
Tyrion remains unmoved from his depression
Tyrion insists he is tired of being confined in a box
Varys warns Tyrion he must remain hidden because Cersei has promised a lordship for his head
Tyrion scoffs and rhetorically asks if Cersei will kill every dwarf in the world to catch him
Cersei is presented with the severed head of another dwarf
Cersei's offer of a lordship for Tyrion's head motivates people to kill any dwarf they can find and try to pass it off as Tyrion
Cersei becomes irate upon seeing the head
Cersei decides not to punish the men who brought her the head, fearing it will deter other bounty hunters
Cersei wants the head disposed of
Qyburn interjects and politely asks if he can keep the head for his experiments
Cersei summons Jaime to their late father's office
Cersei reveals a "gift" from Dorne: an ornately carved viper with Myrcella's Lannister pendant
Cersei insists the gift is a threat against Myrcella
Jaime is unable to refute Cersei's conclusion
Cersei lashes out at Jaime for not being a father to their children
Jaime points out that if he had been a father, their children would be stoned in the streets
Jaime declares he will fix the situation with Myrcella
Jaime ignores Cersei's mocking of his one-handed status
Cersei convenes the Small Council
Cersei sits in the Hand's seat
Cersei announces that the King has declared Qyburn the new Master of Whisperers
Cersei announces that the position of Hand of the King will remain vacant until Tommen is old enough to decide
Cersei announces Tommen has made Mace Tyrell the new Master of Coin in addition to Master of Ships
Cersei announces Tommen has extended the position of "Master of War" to Kevan Lannister
Mace preens at his new responsibilities
Pycelle is outraged at Qyburn's promotion
Kevan is disgusted at Cersei's power play
Kevan refuses the new position
Kevan denounces Cersei as nothing more than the Queen Mother
Kevan berates Cersei for abusing her authority to fill the Small Council with her sycophants
Kevan walks out, saying he will await King Tommen's instructions at Casterly Rock
Bronn spends time with his betrothed Lollys Stokeworth at Castle Stokeworth
Lollys reminds Bronn that her older sister will inherit the castle when their mother dies
Bronn assures Lollys that mean people like her sister usually get what's coming to them, setting his sights on the castle
Jaime finds Bronn and Lollys together
Jaime reveals to Bronn that Cersei has reneged on her deal and offered Lollys's hand to Ser Wyllis Bracken instead
Bronn becomes furious about the change of arrangements
Jaime tells Bronn that if he helps rescue Myrcella from Dorne, Bronn will receive a much better girl and castle
Ellaria Sand rudely demands an audience with Doran Martell
Areo Hotah blocks Ellaria's path
Doran tells Hotah to let Ellaria pass
Ellaria demands to take Princess Myrcella as revenge for Oberyn's death
Princess Myrcella enjoys Trystane Martell's company at that moment
Doran refuses Ellaria's demand and reminds her Oberyn died in a legal trial by combat
Doran states Dorne will not mutilate children while he rules, even if the Sand Snakes side with Ellaria
Ellaria sneers, asks how long Doran expects to rule, and storms out
Hotah and Doran exchange a look as Ellaria departs
Hotah meaningfully indicates his axe
Doran shakes his head and returns his attention to the Water Gardens, contemplating his next move
Brienne and Podrick arrive at an inn and dine
Podrick spots Sansa Stark with Petyr Baelish
Brienne tells Podrick to ready the horses
Podrick readies the horses
Brienne confronts Sansa and Petyr Baelish
Brienne mentions her sworn vow to Sansa's mother Catelyn
Littlefinger points out Brienne was accused of murdering Renly Baratheon and that both he and Catelyn are now dead
Sansa denies Brienne's request
Littlefinger urges Sansa to stay at the inn as the road is not safe
Brienne sees this as a trap and escapes with Podrick on horses they take
Brienne and Podrick are chased through a forest
Brienne fights the men
Podrick is thrown off his horse
Brienne and Podrick reunite
Podrick mentions both Stark girls denied Brienne's request for protection
Brienne doubts Sansa is safe with Littlefinger
Brienne tells Podrick to get his horse
Brienne and Podrick attempt to further pursue Sansa and Littlefinger
Daario and Grey Worm find a house belonging to a Son of the Harpy allegedly responsible for White Rat's death
Daario explains he has been using the Second Sons as spies throughout Meereen
Daario notes the Second Sons are less conspicuous than Unsullied and can indulge in the same vices as their informants
Daario demonstrates his men's effectiveness by stabbing his dagger through a false wall
Daario seriously injures the man hiding behind the wall
Daenerys and her council debate executing the Harpy's Son
The debate devolves into a shouting match between Mossador and Hizdahr zo Loraq
The rest of the council leave
Ser Barristan tells Daenerys about her father, the Mad King
Ser Barristan confirms the Mad King was mad and cites his crimes against House Stark
Daenerys is mollified by the revelation about her father
Daenerys promises Ser Barristan there will be a trial for the accused
Mossador kills the Harpy's Son before the trial begins
Daenerys has Mossador publicly executed to preserve the integrity of law
The freed slaves hiss at Daenerys and throw rocks at her as she leaves
A riot breaks out between the former slaves and masters
Daenerys dismisses her council
Daenerys goes outside
Drogon, now the size of a small house, alights just above Daenerys's balcony
Daenerys extends her hand toward Drogon
Drogon sniffs Daenerys's outstretched hand
Drogon flies away, leaving Daenerys alone
Shireen teaches Gilly how to read in Castle Black's library
Gilly asks about Shireen's Greyscale scars
Gilly reveals that wildlings don't have a word for Greyscale and that two of her sisters contracted the disease and were driven mad before Craster put them out of their misery
Sam appears chagrined by the lesson
Queen Selyse interrupts and dismisses Sam and Gilly
Queen Selyse lectures Shireen about the dangers of spending time with wildlings
Queen Selyse warns that wildlings could use Gilly to harm Stannis through Shireen
Shireen doubts that things will go as her mother fears
Stannis discusses Jon Snow’s recent defiance
Stannis shows Jon a message from Lady Lyanna Mormont refusing to pledge House Mormont to Stannis
Jon tells Stannis that Northerners will only follow one of their own, like the Wildlings
Stannis realizes Jon is right and offers to legitimize him as "Jon Stark" and make him Lord of Winterfell if he leaves the Night’s Watch
Jon is visibly moved by the offer
Jon later admits to Sam that he plans to turn Stannis down because he is a man of the Night’s Watch
Maester Aemon oversees the choosing for the new Lord Commander of the Night's Watch
Janos Slynt nominates Alliser Thorne
Another brother names Denys Mallister, Commander of the Shadow Tower
Sam interrupts to put forward a third nomination: Jon Snow
The votes are cast
A tie is called: Jon Snow and Alliser Thorne have an equal number of votes
Maester Aemon casts his own vote for Jon Snow with a wry smile
The black brothers hail Jon as the 998th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch

High_Sparrow_(episode).action: Cersei passes a large crowd on her way to the Great Sept of Baelor and becomes increasingly annoyed as people cheer on Margaery, ignoring her
The crowd cheers on Margaery and ignores Cersei
Tommen and Margaery are wed, much to Cersei’s dismay
Cersei is dismayed by Tommen and Margaery’s wedding
Tommen and Margaery consummate their marriage that night
Tommen and Margaery are elated and discuss their future together
Margaery manipulates Tommen into thinking Cersei will treat him like a child as long as she stays in King’s Landing
Tommen and Cersei go for a walk the next morning
Tommen tells Cersei she should return to Casterly Rock if it will make her happy
Cersei declines Tommen’s suggestion
Cersei deduces that Margaery is behind Tommen’s suggestion
Cersei visits Margaery, who is busy gloating about her “exhausting” night with the King
Margaery greets Cersei, now more confident, and subtly rubs her queenship in Cersei’s face
Cersei, seeing herself losing, walks away
The Sparrows attack the High Septon as he engages the services of prostitutes dressed as the Seven
The Sparrows force the High Septon to walk naked through the streets while proclaiming him a sinner
The High Septon is humiliated and demands the Small Council arrest the Sparrows and execute the High Sparrow
Cersei, although unsympathetic, decides to meet with the High Sparrow
Cersei meets the High Sparrow, who is serving food to the poor, barefoot
The High Sparrow makes no effort to hide the actions of the Sparrows
Cersei tells the High Sparrow he will not be arrested or executed, and that the High Septon has been imprisoned instead
The High Sparrow is surprised by Cersei’s decision
Cersei returns to the Red Keep and has Qyburn send a message to Petyr Baelish
Qyburn writes the message
Gregor Clegane’s body begins shaking under the sheet behind Qyburn
Qyburn shushes Gregor Clegane calmly
Reek serves lunch to Roose Bolton and Ramsay
Roose Bolton scolds Ramsay for flaying Lord Cerwyn and his family during a tax collection
Ramsay points out that he eventually got the taxes
Roose reminds Ramsay that terror cannot sustain their hold on the North and warns they are outnumbered if the other houses rebel
Roose notes that with Tywin Lannister's death, the pact between the Lannisters and the Boltons is dead
Roose declares that the best way to cement Bolton control of the North is through marriage
Sansa is horrified to see Moat Cailin
Sansa realizes Baelish means to return her to Winterfell, into the arms of the Boltons
Sansa recalls their earlier discussion of a marriage proposal
Sansa realizes the proposal is for her, not Baelish
Sansa furiously declares she would rather die than marry Roose Bolton
Littlefinger corrects Sansa and clarifies she will be marrying Ramsay Bolton
Littlefinger calms Sansa down by convincing her that nobody will look for her there
Littlefinger suggests this is Sansa’s best opportunity for revenge
Sansa reluctantly agrees
Brienne and Podrick witness Petyr and Sansa approach Moat Cailin from afar
Brienne realizes that following them through the castle would be futile
Brienne decides to bypass Moat Cailin by going around
Podrick worries about losing sight of Petyr and Sansa
Brienne tells Podrick they don't need to follow so closely because she knows where they are going
Brienne makes camp
Brienne learns how Podrick became Tyrion's squire
Podrick praises Brienne's martial prowess
Podrick expresses pride in being Brienne's squire despite her behavior
Brienne apologizes to Podrick for her rude behavior
Brienne offers to teach Podrick how to wield a sword and ride a horse properly
Podrick questions Brienne about her past with Renly
Brienne reveals her father held a ball to find her a good match as a young girl
Brienne is captivated by the attention at the ball
Brienne's happiness turns to embarrassment when she realizes the young lords are toying with her
Renly takes Brienne into his arms and comforts her, telling her the young lords aren't worth crying over, and dances with her
Brienne acknowledges that Renly was only interested in men
Brienne continues to love Renly despite his lack of interest in women
Brienne says there is nothing more hateful than failing to protect the ones you love
Brienne intends to avenge Renly by killing Stannis
Brienne believes Stannis played a role in Renly's murder due to Melisandre's shadow resembling Stannis
The Boltons and their households greet Sansa and Littlefinger at Winterfell
Sansa graciously accepts Roose's welcome, putting on an air of politeness
Myranda observes Sansa and Littlefinger from afar with jealousy and anger
Sansa is shown to her old room, which now seems foreign to her
The servant attending Sansa welcomes her back and quietly assures her that "the North remembers"
Sansa explores the castle
Reek hides his face from Sansa and struggles not to call out to her
Baelish subtly warns Ramsay against inflicting any harm on Sansa
Baelish discusses the situation with Roose
Roose is suspicious of Littlefinger's motives in betraying the Lannisters
Baelish points out that Tywin is dead, Jaime is one-handed and politically useless, and that Cersei is about to be forced into the background by Margaery, who is fond of Sansa
Bolton demands to read any messages Littlefinger might send to the Capital
Stannis tries unsuccessfully to convince Jon to accept his offer of legitimization
Jon declines Stannis's offer
Jon reiterates that his place is with the Night's Watch
Jon reiterates the Night's Watch's neutrality in the affairs of the Seven Kingdoms
Jon asks Stannis when he plans to leave
Jon explains that Castle Black's stores are insufficient to feed everyone indefinitely
Stannis confirms his army will leave soon to confront the Boltons at Winterfell
Stannis leaves the fate of the wildling prisoners in Jon's hands
Stannis allows Jon to decide whether to execute the wildlings or negotiate with Tormund
Stannis suggests appointing Alliser Thorne as the new commander of Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
Jon retorts that it is better to have your enemies close by than far away
Davos prepares to leave with Stannis
Davos invites Jon to ponder the second-to-last stanza of the Night's Watch oath
Davos assures Jon that Stannis's advice is offered because the king likes him
Jon asks Sam about Maester Aemon
Sam explains that Maester Aemon's health is waning and is entrusted with his care
Jon begins assigning roles to members of the Watch
Jon assigns a black brother to oversee digging a new latrine pit for the wildling prisoners, joking it is a good job for a ginger
The black brother grins and laughs with the others
Jon appoints Ser Alliser Thorne as the new First Ranger, commending his experience
Jon assigns Janos Slynt the task of restoring and manning Greyguard
Janos Slynt refuses Jon's order three times
Jon clarifies it is an order, not an offer, and warns Slynt he is disobeying a direct order
Janos Slynt flippantly tells Jon to shove his order up his bastard arse
Jon orders Janos Slynt taken outside and calls for Olly to fetch his sword
Ser Alliser briefly hesitates, then moves aside to let Dolorous Edd and others grab Slynt
Janos Slynt angrily protests, claiming he has important friends at court and dismisses the matter as a scare tactic
Jon has Janos dragged out into the courtyard
Jon prepares to publicly behead Janos for treason, following Eddard Stark's law
Janos loses his bluster, starts crying, and begs for mercy
Janos says he will do whatever Jon asks and that he has always been afraid
Jon hesitates for a moment
Jon fixes his nerve and executes Janos with a single swing of Longclaw
Jon exacts justice on one of the men who betrayed his father and murdered Robert Baratheon's children
Stannis Baratheon sees Jon behead Janos for refusing orders and nods in approval
Arya cleans the main sanctuary of the House of Black and White
Arya watches as Jaqen assists a supplicant in drinking from the temple's well
Jaqen assists a supplicant in drinking from the temple's well
Arya protests to Jaqen that she wants to learn
Jaqen brushes off Arya's discontent
The Waif accosts Arya in her room, repeatedly asking who she is and hitting her when she says "no one"
Jaqen arrives and demands to know what is going on, noting the Waif has overstepped her bounds
Jaqen points out Arya cannot be 'no one' because she is wearing Arya Stark's clothes, has silver stolen by Arya Stark, and was about to attack the Waif with Arya Stark's sword
Arya binds her old clothing to a rock and tosses it into the lagoon along with the stolen silver
Arya hides Needle within a seawall instead of discarding it
Jaqen allows Arya to progress to an inner chamber to assist the Waif in stripping and washing corpses
Arya assists the Waif in stripping and washing corpses
The Waif is silent when Arya asks what happens to the bodies after they are cleaned
Tyrion and Varys arrive at Volantis
Tyrion and Varys arrive at Volantis
Tyrion grows sick of the carriage and insists they leave to explore, dismissing the dangers
Tyrion and Varys move through the Long Bridge's markets
Varys explains the meaning of Volantene slave tattoos
Varys nearly loses Tyrion in the crowd
Varys finds Tyrion observing the sermon of a red priestess
Tyrion observes the sermon of a red priestess
Tyrion says he is curious, mentioning Thoros of Myr was the only red priest in King's Landing
The red priestess evangelizes in High Valyrian, telling her story of being a former slave
The red priestess preaches of a prophesied savior, a Dragon Queen
Tyrion's interest wanes when the priestess halts and stares directly into his eyes
The red priestess halts her sermon and stares directly into Tyrion's eyes
Tyrion leads Varys to a brothel
Varys catches sight of a prostitute dressed as Daenerys
Varys tells Tyrion that a woman who inspires both sermons and whores is probably worth meeting
Tyrion woos a dark-haired prostitute
The prostitute agrees to service Tyrion
Tyrion finds he can't bring himself to patronize her
Tyrion goes to urinate off the side of the bridge
Varys loses sight of Tyrion
Tyrion finishes urinating
Jorah Mormont abducts Tyrion
Jorah declares he is taking Tyrion to "the queen"

Sons_of_the_Harpy_(episode).action: Mace Tyrell brings news that the Iron Bank of Braavos is calling in its debts from Westeros
The Iron Bank calls in a tenth of the Iron Throne's current debts
The Iron Throne can only afford half of the payment due to the expense of rebuilding the royal fleet
Mace offers that House Tyrell could front the money for the time being
Cersei refuses to give the Tyrells more power over the crown
Cersei says they will send Mace to Braavos to negotiate for more time
Cersei claims sending a high-ranking official will show the bank how seriously they take their debts
Mace takes the assignment as a high honor and is oblivious to Cersei's true intentions
Cersei sends Ser Meryn Trant of the Kingsguard to "guard" Mace
Mace leaves the Small Council meeting
Pycelle bitterly points out that the Small Council is growing smaller
Cersei says the council hasn't grown small enough yet
Cersei meets with the High Sparrow, who has been elected as the new High Septon by her influence
Cersei tries to build a new alliance between the Crown and the Faith to counter the Tyrells' political influence
Cersei points out to the High Sparrow that the War of the Five Kings has devastated the countryside, resulting in septs being burned, clergymen attacked, and Silent Sisters raped
Bodies of holy men pile up in the streets
The High Sparrow admits that wars make men obey the sword more than the gods
Cersei points out that before the Targaryens, the Faith Militant dispensed the justice of the Seven
The High Septon is confused, as the Targaryens disbanded the Faith Militant over two centuries ago
Cersei explains she will tell her son to repeal the law and allow the Faith Militant to be reestablished and re-armed
Cersei advises the High Sparrow that there is a great sinner in their midst who has been shielded by gold and power
The High Sparrow replies with a wish for the Father to judge the sinner justly
The new Faith Militant storm the streets of King's Landing, attacking taverns, brothels, and street vendors selling idols from other religions
The City Watch look on helplessly and do not intervene
The Faith Militant ransack Littlefinger's brothel, dragging prostitutes like Marei out into the street by their hair
Olyvar is struck and ignored as the Faith Militant barge in
Olyvar hears screams coming from another room and approaches to see the Sparrows have singled out a male client with a male prostitute
The Sparrows assault the customer and prostitute with knives and hurl homophobic slurs at them
One Sparrow approaches one of the men with a knife
Olyvar flees as one of the men screams off-screen
The Faith Militant carve the Seven-Pointed Star symbol of the Faith into their foreheads as a sign of zealotry
Lancel leads a squad of the Faith Militant to apprehend Loras Tyrell while he is sparring in the practice yard
Lancel proclaims that Loras's sexual perversions are sins against the laws of gods and men
Loras is dragged off by the Faith Militant
Margaery is furious at Tommen for her brother's arrest
Margaery tries to manipulate Tommen by weeping about Loras
Tommen goes to Cersei and demands that Loras be released at once
Cersei calmly points out that she didn't arrest Loras and that the Faith Militant imprisoned him, suggesting Tommen ask the High Sparrow
Tommen naively thinks the High Sparrow will do as he requests and heads to the Great Sept of Baelor with Kingsguard and Lannister guards
The path to the Great Sept is blocked by the Faith Militant, who say the High Sparrow is praying and does not wish to be disturbed
The Kingsguard request that Tommen let them kill the Faith Militant to pass
Tommen is surprised and does not want to risk bloodshed, especially on holy ground
The mob of commoners behind Tommen starts shouting that he is a bastard and an abomination of incest
Tommen meekly withdraws back to the Red Keep
Margaery is even more upset at Tommen's ineffectiveness and leaves, saying she will bring her grandmother Olenna back to the capital
Jaime and Bronn travel on a Pentoshi merchant ship headed to Oldtown in the Reach
The ship sails past Tarth, off the coast of the Stormlands
Jaime mistakenly identifies Tarth as Estermont
The captain corrects Jaime, stating they are sailing past the Sapphire Isle
The captain’s mention of the Sapphire Isle reminds Jaime of Brienne
Jaime explains to Bronn that he bribed the captain to let them take a small boat to shore as they pass Dorne
Jaime and Bronn plan to land just outside of Sunspear and the adjacent Water Gardens
Beneath decks, Bronn remarks on why Jaime wants to protect Princess Myrcella for himself
Bronn suggests Jaime wants to make up for letting Tyrion escape
Jaime curtly says that Varys let Tyrion escape
Bronn says if Jaime ever sees Tyrion again, he should give him Bronn’s regards
Jaime angrily says if he sees Tyrion again, he’ll split him in two for murdering Tywin
Bronn rows the pair ashore at dusk
Jaime can't help row with his one hand
Bronn kills a snake near Jaime's head the next morning
Bronn roasts the snake for breakfast
Bronn worries the ship's captain will spread word that a Lannister is in Dorne
Jaime says he bribed the captain with a large bag of gold
Bronn still worries about being discovered
A mounted patrol comes by and sees their tracks in the sand
Jaime and Bronn present themselves and try to pass off as simple travelers whose boat capsized
Jaime and Bronn's ruse doesn't work; sentries demand they drop their weapons to be taken prisoner
Bronn plants his sword in the sand, then throws his dagger into the lead sentry's throat
Jaime and Bronn draw their swords and fight the rest of the sentries
Jaime can't fight well left-handed
Bronn kills one sentry's horse so Jaime can fight on foot
Jaime is overpowered but catches the sentry's sword with his metal hand
Jaime uses the distraction to kill the sentry with his left hand
Bronn finishes off the other sentries
Bronn notes they now have the sentries' horses
Jaime says the bodies need to be buried
Bronn complains it will take a lot of time to dig holes for all the bodies
Jaime points out he can't dig with one hand
Bronn, annoyed, drags off the bodies to bury them
Ellaria Sand meets with three of the eight Sand Snakes outside of Sunspear
Ellaria informs the Sand Snakes that Prince Doran Martell will not raise Dorne's armies to avenge Oberyn's death
The Sand Snakes worry that they have no army to march against the Lannisters
Ellaria tells the Sand Snakes that they don't need an army to start a war
Ellaria suggests that killing Cersei's daughter Myrcella, currently in Doran's possession, will start a war
Obara points out that they may have a problem
The Pentoshi ship captain finds Obara in Planky Town
The ship captain tries to sell information about Jaime Lannister's arrival in Dorne
The Sand Snakes are insulted that the captain wants to be paid for the information
The Sand Snakes take the captain prisoner and torture the information out of him
Nymeria uses her whip to knock a bucket over
The turned-over bucket reveals the captain buried neck-deep in sand with his head under a bucket of venomous scorpions
They realize they are in a race against time to take Myrcella from Doran before Jaime Lannister
Ellaria asks if the three girls are with her, knowing this will bring Dorne into war
Nymeria and Tyene say yes
Obara recounts when she first met Oberyn, who told her to choose between her mother's tears and his spear
Obara chooses the spear, choosing the path of war
Obara throws her spear to impale the ship captain's head
Jorah knocks out a fisherman and steals his boat, leaving with his captive Tyrion
Tyrion urges Jorah to remove his gag while at sea
Jorah refuses to volunteer any information about himself
Tyrion observes the bear sigil on Jorah's armor and deduces his identity
Jorah admits to Tyrion that the "Queen" is Daenerys, not Cersei
Tyrion recalls from Small Council meetings that Jorah was spying on Daenerys for Varys
Tyrion deduces Daenerys found out about Jorah's spying and exiled him
Tyrion deduces Jorah kidnapped him to present as a gift to Daenerys
Tyrion laughs at his situation and tells Jorah he was already heading to meet Daenerys
Tyrion points out there is no guarantee Daenerys will forgive Jorah and may welcome Tyrion instead
Jorah smacks Tyrion across the face, quieting him
Jon trains recruits in the practice yard of Castle Black
Stannis, Selyse, and Shireen watch Jon training
Selyse worries that Stannis wishes Jon was his son because she gave him only stillbirths and weakness
Stannis tells Selyse that wasn't her fault
Melisandre joins Stannis, Selyse, and Shireen
Melisandre takes Stannis's side, declaring the Lord of Light doesn't care about Shireen's Greyscale and that she has her father's blood
Selyse departs
Melisandre discusses the coming battle to take Winterfell from the Boltons with Stannis
Melisandre asks if Stannis intends to leave her behind as he did at the Blackwater
Stannis assures Melisandre he won't leave her behind this time
Jon goes over paperwork with Samwell Tarly, reviewing letters from minor Houses offering new recruits
Samwell hands Jon a request letter for Roose Bolton
Jon balks at the letter, saying Roose killed his brother Robb
Sam points out that Roose Bolton is now Warden of the North and has the most recruits to send
Sam reminds Jon that the Night's Watch has sworn an oath of political neutrality
Jon reluctantly signs the letter to the Boltons with disgust
Sam departs
Melisandre enters
Melisandre entreats Jon to assist Stannis in retaking Winterfell
Melisandre points out Jon’s familiarity with the castle could win the battle
Melisandre acknowledges a greater war is at stake
Melisandre asks Jon if he is on the side of life or death, undoing her dress and straddling him
Melisandre encourages Jon to take her, citing the Lord of Light’s intentions
Jon refuses, citing his vows and Stannis’s disapproval
Melisandre suggests they not tell Stannis
Melisandre points out Jon broke his vows once before
Jon acknowledges he still loves his now-deceased lover Ygritte
Melisandre adjusts her dress and leaves
Melisandre utters the words "You know nothing, Jon Snow"
Stannis works on letters in his chambers at Castle Black
Shireen enters Stannis's chambers
Stannis begins to apologize for bringing Shireen on campaign
Shireen cuts Stannis off
Shireen tells Stannis she is happy to have come and expected to be left at Dragonstone
Shireen asks Stannis if he is ashamed of her because of her deformity
Stannis gets up from his table
Stannis explains about the Dornish trader and the contaminated doll
Stannis recounts that everyone urged him to send Shireen to Valyria
Stannis says he told them all to go to hell and summoned every healer, apothecary, and Maester to save Shireen
Stannis insists Shireen is his daughter and she belongs with her family
Shireen, overjoyed, runs over and hugs Stannis with tears of joy
Stannis, after an awkward pause, returns Shireen's hug
Sansa visits her family's underground crypts at Winterfell
Sansa comes to the tomb and statue of her aunt Lyanna Stark and finds the feather token left by Robert Baratheon
Petyr Baelish arrives and says he thought he'd find Sansa there
Sansa remarks that her father, Eddard Stark, never talked about Lyanna
Sansa recalls finding Eddard down in the crypts lighting candles at Lyanna's tomb
Sansa notes she never knew Lyanna because Lyanna died before she was born
Sansa notes that people say Lyanna was beautiful
Littlefinger says he saw Lyanna once years ago and she was as beautiful as everyone says
Littlefinger recalls living with Sansa's mother's family as a boy
Lord Whent of Harrenhal holds a massive tournament attended by notable figures from across Westeros
Lyanna is publicly betrothed to Robert at the time
Littlefinger remembers being awed to see all the living legends gathered
In the final tilt, Prince Rhaegar rides against Ser Barristan Selmy
Rhaegar wins the tilt
The crowd erupts in cheers
The crowd suddenly falls silent when Rhaegar rides past his wife, Elia Martell, to lay the victor's crown in Lyanna's lap
Rhaegar lays the victor's crown of winter roses in Lyanna's lap
Not long afterward, Rhaegar makes off with Lyanna, sparking Robert's Rebellion
Robert's Rebellion overthrows the Targaryen dynasty
Littlefinger wonders how many thousands died because Rhaegar chose Lyanna
Sansa says Rhaegar chose Lyanna, then kidnapped and raped her
Littlefinger gives Sansa a wry look and moves on to another part of the crypts
Sansa notices that Littlefinger is wearing riding clothes
Littlefinger explains he is heading back to King's Landing because Cersei sent a letter requesting his presence
Littlefinger says it would be best not to make Cersei suspicious by refusing to come
Littlefinger explains his plan to Sansa: Stannis will have to attack the Boltons soon if he is to beat the winter snows
Littlefinger bets that Stannis will probably win, because he is one of the best military commanders in Westeros
Littlefinger says the Boltons will be outnumbered by Stannis, as the other northern houses would be quick to betray their liege lord
Littlefinger says Stannis will make Sansa the new ruler of the North, as the last surviving Stark heir and in memory of Eddard's support
Sansa asks what will happen if Stannis gets killed
Littlefinger explains that marrying Ramsay Bolton will at least leave Sansa in a position to insinuate herself among the Boltons and help destroy them from within
Littlefinger says Sansa will be his powerful "ally" in the North
Littlefinger kisses Sansa on the lips
Littlefinger departs
Daenerys looks down on the streets below from her royal apartments in the Great Pyramid of Meereen
Barristan brings up Rhaegar Targaryen in conversation
Barristan says the city's inhabitants always seem happy from a distance
Barristan recounts that Rhaegar liked to leave the Red Keep and mingle with the common people
Barristan says he often accompanied Rhaegar to guard him
Barristan says Rhaegar liked to sing to people on the streets
Barristan describes Rhaegar singing as a public minstrel and seeing how much money he could make
Barristan says everyone enjoyed Rhaegar's singing and he often made quite a lot of money
Daenerys is surprised because Viserys only told her Rhaegar was a great killer
Barristan scoffs that Rhaegar was a great warrior but never enjoyed killing, and was a gentle man who enjoyed singing
Daenerys asks what they did with all the money
Barristan says they did all sorts of things with the money: gave it to minstrels, donated to an orphanage, or spent it getting drunk
Daario Naharis announces that the day's supplicants are waiting in the throne room, including Hizdahr zo Loraq
Daenerys asks Barristan if he wants to attend her in the throne room, aware of his dislike of politics
Daenerys gives Barristan the day off after being assured that Daario has her back
Daenerys cheerfully tells Barristan to make some music in the city below
Hizdahr beseeches Daenerys to reopen the fighting pits of Meereen
Daenerys refuses to reopen the fighting pits
Hizdahr argues that the fighting pits provide a great spectacle enjoyed by both slave-masters and slaves, and can bring the city together
Hizdahr concedes that bringing back the tradition will not instantly solve all problems, but would be a start at mending her subjects
The Sons of the Harpy mount a series of large-scale attacks in the streets
A Meereenese prostitute helps the Sons of the Harpy distract and kill some of the Second Sons
A Meereenese prostitute feigns fear and directs a patrol of Unsullied into a trap by pointing to a narrow alleyway
The Unsullied give chase into the alleyway
The Unsullied are surrounded by a much larger number of the Sons of the Harpy
Brutal fighting rages throughout the streets of Meereen
Grey Worm is among the ambushed Unsullied and kills many of the attackers
Grey Worm loses his helmet, is slashed in the leg, and is stabbed in his side
Grey Worm pulls the dagger out of his own chest, stabs a foe in the throat, and keeps fighting despite his wounds
Most of the Sons of the Harpy are dead, but they outnumber the Unsullied so much that only Grey Worm is left
Ser Barristan arrives and saves Grey Worm from certain death by drawing the attention of the remaining rebels
Ser Barristan takes on eight Sons of the Harpy, killing seven of them
The eighth Son of the Harpy stabs Ser Barristan in the belly
Ser Barristan falls to his knees
Before the attacker can cut Ser Barristan's throat, Grey Worm thrusts his spear into the attacker's back, killing him
Grey Worm staggers over to the unconscious Barristan before collapsing from his wounds

Unbowed,_Unbent,_Unbroken.action: Arya continues her training with the Faceless Men
Arya cleans a corpse methodically
Two men take the corpse away
Arya becomes curious about what is on the other side of the door
Arya is about to walk through the door
The Waif obstructs Arya’s path and latches the door
Arya wants to know what happens to the bodies she scrubs clean
The Waif tells Arya she will know when the time is right
Arya, frustrated, demands to play the game of faces
The Waif tells Arya she has already tried and failed the game
The Waif asks Arya who she is
Arya replies that she is no one
The Waif tells Arya to get back to work
The Waif is about to walk away
Arya asks the Waif who she is
The Waif tells Arya a story about her past
The Waif asks Arya whether she believed the story
Arya does not respond, embarrassed
The Waif tells Arya to get back to work, hinting about the game of faces
Later, Jaqen H'ghar comes to test Arya while she is asleep
Jaqen asks Arya who she is
Arya tells Jaqen how she came to join the Faceless Men, slipping in lies
Jaqen is able to tell when Arya is lying and hits her with a switch
Jaqen strikes Arya repeatedly when she insists she hated the Hound
Jaqen tells Arya she is lying not only to him, but to herself
A grieving father brings his sickly daughter, Ghita, to the House of Black and White
The father explains to Arya that he has been to every healer in Braavos and spent every penny he had
The father tells Arya that his daughter is suffering and needs to find peace
Arya sits beside the girl and tells her a short false story about being healed at the temple
Arya persuades the sickly girl to drink the poisoned water from the well
The sickly girl drinks the poisoned water and dies peacefully
Arya is brought to the Hall of Faces with Jaqen H'ghar after proving she can lie
The Faceless Man explains to Arya that all the faces in the Hall were taken from corpses washed in the temple
The Faceless Man asks Arya if she is ready to give up who she is to become "no one"
Arya remains silent for a moment
The Faceless Man states Arya is not ready to become "no one" but is ready to become "someone else"
Tyrion and Jorah travel on foot toward Meereen after losing their boat
Tyrion is annoyed they didn't find any villages to steal a boat or supplies from
Jorah had hoped to find villages for a boat or supplies
Tyrion and Jorah walk slowly and only have berries and roots to eat
Jorah brings up the topic of why Tyrion was in Volantis
Tyrion is surprised Jorah did not ask earlier
Tyrion explains he fled Westeros because he killed his father Tywin
Tyrion says he killed Tywin because Tywin tried to have him executed and was sleeping with the woman Tyrion loved
Jorah nods, accepting Tyrion's motivation for kinslaying
Tyrion tells Jorah that despite his misery, at least he had a good father
Jorah asks how Tyrion knew his father Jeor Mormont
Tyrion explains he visited the Wall and met Jeor, describing him as a great leader
Tyrion quotes the Night's Watch eulogy, implying Jeor is dead
Jorah is shocked to realize his father is dead
Tyrion apologizes, thinking Jorah already knew
Jorah asks how Jeor died
Tyrion explains Jeor was murdered by his own men during a mutiny beyond the Wall
Jorah processes the news in silent grief
Jorah changes the subject, saying they have to keep moving
Tyrion asks Jorah why he would support Daenerys and how she would be better than other rulers, or why Westeros would support her given her father's reputation
Jorah explains he was once cynical and didn't believe in destiny
Jorah says after seeing Daenerys emerge from the flames with baby dragons, he believes in her and her right to the Iron Throne
Tyrion remains skeptical, pointing out that this doesn't mean Daenerys will be a good queen, since the Targaryens were known for going insane
Jorah spots a slaver ship in the distance and pulls Tyrion to the ground
Jorah and Tyrion are already spotted before they start hiding
Slavers emerge from the trees behind Jorah and Tyrion and take them captive
Malko, the lead slaver, intends to return them to Volantis
Tyrion persuades Malko that Jorah is a skilled knight who would fetch a better price in the fighting pits of Meereen
Jorah and Tyrion are taken towards Meereen and Queen Daenerys
Bronn and Jaime approach the Water Gardens, wearing Martell disguises from the soldiers they killed
Bronn wonders what Jaime will do once they get inside and find Myrcella
Jaime replies that he likes to improvise
Bronn sarcastically remarks "That explains the golden hand."
Ellaria Sand and the Sand Snakes are already inside the Gardens
Ellaria sends the Sand Snakes out to abduct Myrcella, telling them to do it "for Oberyn."
Myrcella enjoys a stroll with her betrothed, Trystane Martell
Doran Martell and Areo Hotah watch Myrcella and Trystane from above
Doran comments that Myrcella and Trystane look lovely together, but don't realize the danger of their betrothal
Doran says that he and Areo must protect Myrcella and Trystane
Doran asks Areo if he remembers how to use his longaxe
Areo assures Doran that he remembers how to use his longaxe
Jaime and Bronn infiltrate the Water Gardens
Jaime and Bronn come upon Myrcella kissing Trystane
Jaime tries to convince Myrcella to come with him
Trystane interferes, suspicious of the bloodstains on Bronn and Jaime's Dornish robes
Trystane tries to draw his sword
Bronn quickly knocks Trystane down, to Myrcella's horror
Jaime tries to lead Myrcella away
The Sand Snakes attack Jaime and Bronn
Bronn fights against Tyene and Nymeria
Obara attacks Jaime with her spear, driving him away from Myrcella
Obara orders Nym to break away from Bronn and take Myrcella prisoner
Nym tries to pull Myrcella away
Areo and Prince Doran's guards cut off Nym and Myrcella, surrounding everyone
The guards force everyone to drop their weapons
Jaime, Bronn, Ellaria, and the Sand Snakes are taken into custody
Petyr Baelish arrives in King's Landing
Petyr Baelish is on his way to meet Cersei
Brother Lancel and some Sparrows confront Petyr Baelish
Lancel warns Baelish that they have purged King's Landing of its corrupt ways and that the new King's Landing will not tolerate his prostitution business
Baelish dismisses these threats
Cersei meets with Baelish and denies her involvement in Loras Tyrell’s arrest by the Faith Militant
Baelish is not fooled and warns that House Tyrell will not tolerate this insult
Cersei claims she is the insulted one since Ser Loras, who was promised to her, prefers the company of men
Cersei explains why she summoned Baelish; she is suspicious of his loyalties and asks if she can rely on the Vale to fight for the Throne
Baelish assures her that young Robin heeds his advice and he will always counsel loyalty to the Throne
Baelish reveals that Sansa is back in Winterfell, where Roose Bolton has arranged for her to marry Ramsay, omitting his own involvement
Cersei is infuriated by the Boltons’ betrayal
Baelish adds that marrying the last remaining Stark gives the Boltons a stronger hold over the North than an alliance with a southern house
Baelish counsels patience and suggests letting the Boltons and Stannis fight each other, then stepping in to defeat the victor
Baelish suggests that Cersei’s uncle Kevan Lannister muster a force or have Jaime Lannister lead an army to the North
Cersei claims her uncle doesn’t have the courage to lead an army, while Jaime is away on a diplomatic mission
Baelish proposes that the soldiers of the Vale could fight instead
Cersei is unsure of Baelish’s ability to lead an army
Baelish convinces her, stating that he "lives to serve"
Baelish asks to be named Warden of the North in return
Cersei agrees to talk to the King about this
Olenna Tyrell returns to King's Landing after learning of her grandson’s arrest
Olenna tries to talk Cersei into releasing Loras
Cersei claims that the Faith Militant arrested Loras and she had nothing to do with it
Olenna warns Cersei that her actions have endangered the Lannister-Tyrell alliance
Cersei informs Olenna that the High Septon has called for a preliminary hearing to determine whether the charges against Loras have merit
Cersei expresses confidence that Loras will be acquitted
The High Sparrow interrogates Loras
Loras denies the accusations against him
The High Sparrow questions Margaery
Margaery denies any knowledge of the accusations
The High Sparrow calls in Olyvar, in character as Loras’s "squire"
Olyvar claims the accusations against Loras are true
Olyvar testifies that Margaery walked in on them once but didn’t seem surprised
Olyvar tells the High Sparrow of a birthmark Loras has shaped like Dorne
Loras is shocked and furious
The High Sparrow decides there is enough evidence for a holy inquest
The High Sparrow has Loras arrested
The High Sparrow has Margaery arrested for bearing false witness
Margaery is forcefully dragged away and calls out for Tommen
Tommen is paralyzed with indecision
Olenna regards Cersei’s barely contained smug expression with contempt
Sansa is joined by Myranda in her room at Winterfell
Myranda offers to help Sansa take a bath to be presentable for Ramsay during the wedding
Myranda washes Sansa’s back
Myranda mentions three other girls whom Ramsay had been with and how he victimized them
Sansa sees through Myranda’s motives and startles her by asking how long she has loved Ramsay
Sansa informs Myranda that she is a Stark of Winterfell and will not be intimidated
Sansa dismisses Myranda to finish her bath herself
Sansa is visibly shaken by Myranda’s story
Reek comes to fetch Sansa for the wedding
Sansa refuses to hold Reek's arm
Reek pleads with Sansa to hold his arm, saying Ramsay will punish him if she doesn’t
Sansa coldly asks if Reek thinks she cares what Ramsay does to him
Sansa strides past Reek
Reek gives Sansa away to Ramsay during the wedding ceremony
Ramsay allows Reek to call himself Theon Greyjoy
Roose Bolton officiates the wedding ceremony
Northern Lords attend the wedding
Ramsay and Sansa retreat to the bedroom
Ramsay interrogates Sansa about her virginity
Ramsay orders Sansa to take her clothes off
Reek turns to leave
Ramsay tells Reek he must stay and taunts him
Ramsay rips open the back of Sansa's dress and pushes her face-down over the bed
Ramsay unbuckles his clothes
Sansa remains still but begins crying softly
Reek is visibly distraught and begins silently crying
Ramsay rapes Sansa and forcibly consummates their marriage

The_Gift.action: Jon departs for Hardhome with Tormund and a group of rangers
Jon speaks with Ser Alliser Thorne and Sam before leaving
Sam gives Jon some dragonglass weapons and reminds him of their use against the White Walkers
Sam and Gilly visit Maester Aemon, who is bedridden and dying
Gilly brings her baby Little Sam for Maester Aemon to hold and cheer him up
Aemon notes that Little Sam has a laugh like his brother Egg had as a baby
Aemon warns Gilly to leave the Wall with her son before winter comes
Sam and Gilly stay as Aemon's condition deteriorates during the night
Gilly urges Sam to get some sleep while she watches over Aemon
Sam insists on being there for Maester Aemon
Aemon wakes up delirious, calls out to his brother Egg, and says he dreamed he was an old man
Aemon passes away soon afterwards
Sam eulogizes Aemon in the courtyard of Castle Black
Sam lights Aemon's funeral pyre
Sam declares that Aemon was the blood of the dragon and that his watch is ended
Ser Alliser quietly warns Sam that he is losing all his friends
Gilly is attacked in the dining hall by two brothers
Sam and Jon's direwolf, Ghost, come to Gilly's rescue
Sam is badly beaten
Gilly tends to Sam's injuries
Gilly, grieving Aemon's death and impressed by Sam, climbs on top of him and they have sex
Reek brings food to Sansa
Sansa begs Reek for help, telling him how she has been locked in her bedchamber since her wedding and is visited by Ramsay every night
Reek tells Sansa to obey Ramsay or else he would do worse things to her
Sansa asks Reek what Ramsay did to break him
Reek can't bring himself to tell Sansa
Reek tries to leave
Sansa grabs Reek and reminds him that he betrayed her family
Reek apologizes profusely
Sansa informs Reek that her family still has allies in the North and tells him to light a candle at the top of the Broken Tower to signal them
Reek refuses to light the candle
Sansa reminds Reek of his true name, Theon Greyjoy, and makes him promise to help her
Reek hesitantly agrees to help Sansa
Reek trudges through the snow with the candle, up a flight of stairs and into Ramsay’s room
Sansa is taken to see Ramsay on the ramparts, which are still under construction
Ramsay is distracted
Sansa grabs a corkscrew lying on a nearby barrel and pockets it
Ramsay speaks about how they will easily defeat Stannis's army and his future as Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North
Sansa reminds Ramsay of the unborn baby, who will have a stronger claim on the North if a boy
Ramsay tells Sansa he is Roose Bolton's eldest son, legitimized by the king
Sansa reminds Ramsay that the king is another bastard
Ramsay is angered by Sansa's remarks but does not act on it
Ramsay states that bastards can rise in the world too, informing Sansa of Jon's appointment as Lord Commander of the Night's Watch
Ramsay brings up the reason he summoned Sansa
Ramsay shows Sansa the crucified and partly flayed corpse of the old woman who promised to help her
Ramsay reveals to Sansa that Reek told him everything
Reek appears miserable for squashing Sansa's hope of escape
Sansa chokes back tears
Ramsay orders his men to take Sansa back to her chamber
Brienne stares intently at Winterfell from her room in the inn, waiting for the sign to rescue Sansa
Stannis's troops make camp during a violent snowstorm
Davos Seaworth meets with Stannis
Davos informs Stannis that forty horses died the previous night and more will die by sunset
The Stormcrows flee in the night
Davos suggests returning to Castle Black to wait out the weather due to lack of food
Stannis refuses to retreat to Castle Black, not wanting to be known as "the King who Ran"
Stannis explains the risk of being stuck at Castle Black for years and the Boltons regaining strength
Davos, seeing no way to convince Stannis, leaves
Stannis asks Melisandre if she is sure of their victory at Winterfell
Melisandre assures Stannis of their victory, reminding him of the vision in the flames
Stannis expresses doubt about what he saw in the vision
Melisandre reassures Stannis that her visions will come true
Melisandre states that a sacrifice with King's blood must be made, implying Shireen
Stannis becomes infuriated by Melisandre's suggestion
Stannis orders Melisandre to leave
Prince Doran has Areo Hotah bring Myrcella to Jaime in his quarters
Jaime is concerned for Myrcella’s safety
Myrcella is suspicious about Jaime’s "diplomatic mission" to bring her back to King's Landing
Jaime replies that there are "complicated matters" involved
Myrcella refuses to return with Jaime, stating her intent to marry Trystane
Bronn sings "The Dornishman's Wife" in his jail cell
Obara and Nymeria listen with annoyance
Tyene listens with amusement
Bronn finishes singing
Bronn and the Sand Snakes trade barbs
Tyene inquires about Bronn's arm, which she cut earlier
Bronn brushes off the injury as "a new scar"
Bronn and Tyene talk about Tyene's beauty
Bronn dismisses Tyene's beauty
Tyene begins to disrobe
Bronn stares intently at Tyene and begins to change his mind about her
Tyene inquires again about Bronn's arm, then his head
Bronn's nose starts bleeding and his vision blurs
Bronn collapses and tries to play off his sudden illness
Tyene reveals her daggers were coated with a slow-acting poison called the long farewell
Tyene explains her attempts to seduce Bronn were to get his heart pumping faster, allowing the poison to act quicker
Tyene points to an ampoule on her necklace containing the antidote
Tyene offers to give Bronn the antidote if he agrees she is the most beautiful woman in the world
Bronn, growing weaker, barely manages to reply affirmatively
Tyene tosses the ampoule to Bronn
Bronn is relieved
Tyene smiles smugly at Bronn and remarks that she finds him handsome as well
Tyene re-robes and sits down next to her mildly amused half-sisters
Lady Olenna meets with the High Sparrow to discuss the imprisonment of her grandchildren, Ser Loras and Queen Margaery
Olenna and the High Sparrow briefly bond over the infirmities of age
Olenna demands that the High Sparrow release her grandchildren
The High Sparrow declines, stating they will be punished for their crimes
The High Sparrow tells Olenna that the Gods' Laws must be applied to everyone equally
Olenna tries to bribe the High Sparrow
Olenna learns that the High Sparrow cannot be bought
Olenna threatens to end Tyrell support for the capital by ending shipments of food
The High Sparrow is unfazed and remarks that the nobility of Westeros have forgotten they are outnumbered by the common folk
The High Sparrow tells Olenna to ponder what happens when the many stop fearing the few
Olenna returns to her palanquin
Olenna receives a letter bearing Petyr Baelish's seal
King Tommen rails over his inability to help Margaery
King Tommen declares his love for Margaery to a dumbstruck Cersei
Cersei offers to speak with the High Sparrow and advocate for Margaery and Loras's release
King Tommen, although suspicious and overwhelmed, agrees to Cersei's offer
Littlefinger meets with Olenna in his ransacked brothel
Littlefinger and Olenna share a tense conversation
Olenna reminds Littlefinger of their roles in Joffrey's murder
Olenna threatens Littlefinger, saying her men will kill him if he tries to silence her
