Littlefinger explains he returned to King's Landing because he dared not refuse Cersei's summons
Littlefinger says he was obliged to give Cersei information
Littlefinger tells Olenna he will give her the same thing he gave Cersei: a handsome young man, implied to be Lancel
Cersei meets with Margaery in the Sept's cells and brings her food
Margaery refuses the food and reveals she knows Cersei is responsible for her and Loras's imprisonment
Cersei, fearful of being overheard, denies responsibility
Margaery mocks Cersei for her unconvincing lies and lack of understanding of innocence and decency
Margaery claims Tommen loves her and was willing to send Cersei back to Casterly Rock
Cersei, concealing her fury, tells Margaery her trial will soon begin and that Tommen needs his mother
Margaery throws the stew at Cersei and yells at her to get out
Cersei leaves the cell, smiling smugly at Margaery
Cersei meets with the High Sparrow in a chapel that predates the Great Sept
Cersei and the High Sparrow briefly discuss the legal process that Margaery and Loras will face
The High Sparrow discusses the legal process with Cersei
The High Sparrow prepares to turn the tables on Cersei as the conversation continues
Brother Lancel appears
Cersei realizes what is about to happen and her triumphant smile begins to crack
The High Sparrow has Cersei taken into custody by a group of Septas
The Septas ignore Cersei's threats and lock her in a cell
Cersei is locked in a cell
Jorah is sold by Malko to Yezzan zo Qaggaz
Tyrion convinces Yezzan that he and Jorah are a team
Yezzan buys Tyrion as well
Daenerys and Daario discuss her impending marriage to Hizdahr zo Loraq
Daario suggests Daenerys marry him instead
Daenerys rejects Daario's suggestion
Daario advises Daenerys to gather all the Great, Wise, and Good Masters and kill them when the fighting pits are reopened
Daenerys is reluctant to follow Daario's advice, fearing she will gain a reputation as a butcher
Daario replies that all rulers are either butchers or meat
Yezzan takes his fighters to Meereen to test them in the lower pits and seek out worthy fighters for the Great Pit of Daznak
The fighters fight in front of a reluctant Daenerys
Hizdahr convinces Daenerys that Meereen's ruler should make rounds to the lower pits ahead of the main tournament
Jorah enters the Pits early upon realizing Daenerys is watching
Jorah elbows down Yezzan who tried to berate him
Jorah easily defeats the other fighters without killing them
Jorah impresses Daenerys enough to prevent her from leaving
Jorah reveals his identity to Daenerys
Daenerys's face twists into a scowl and she orders Jorah taken away
Before the Unsullied can comply, Jorah declares he brought Daenerys a gift
Tyrion enters the arena and reveals his identity to the astonished Queen

Hardhome_(episode).action: Daenerys ponders what to do with Tyrion and Jorah
Daenerys asks Tyrion why she shouldn't kill him, since the Lannisters are enemies to her House
Tyrion claims to have killed both of his parents and says he is an enemy to the Lannisters as well
Daenerys expresses doubt that killing one's own family is a good reputation for someone in her service
Tyrion wonders aloud if Daenerys is worth his service
Daenerys orders Tyrion to tell her why he has come to her from the other end of the world
Tyrion explains how he learned about Daenerys from rumors and from Varys
Tyrion recounts Daenerys's story: a lost girl born in a storm, who acquired a barbarian horde, an effective army, conquered three cities, broke the slave trade, and hatched three dragons
Tyrion says Daenerys might be worth living for after he lost everything he had lived for
Tyrion adds that Daenerys needs his advice despite her powerful army and dragons, as she lacks political experience and practical knowledge of Westeros
Daenerys agrees to spare Ser Jorah’s life
Daenerys asks what to do about Ser Jorah
Daenerys seems poised to fulfill her promise of killing Jorah if she saw him again
Tyrion defends Jorah, commenting on his devotion and possible love for Daenerys
Tyrion points out Jorah did not trust Daenerys enough to inform her of his mission to spy for King Robert
Tyrion argues that Jorah cannot be trusted to stand by Daenerys’s side
Tyrion argues that a queen should not kill those devoted to her
Daenerys exiles Jorah from the city
Jorah contemplates his greyscale
Jorah returns to Yezzan zo Qaggaz and offers to fight for him
Jorah promises to lead Yezzan's team to the Great Pit and win the tournament for the Queen
Jorah promises that after the tournament, Yezzan can make a fortune selling him on the black market
Tyrion and Daenerys sit in her solar and have a talk about ambitions
Tyrion mentions what a horrible father Tywin was
Daenerys admits she no longer has any illusions about her own father
Daenerys is curious about the circumstances of Tyrion's murder of Tywin
Tyrion wryly promises to tell Daenerys the story one day if she decides not to kill him
Tyrion says they will need more wine than the large decanter they are drinking from
They discuss the role of Varys in their stories
Tyrion assures Daenerys he trusts him
Tyrion tells Daenerys that Varys is probably the reason she wasn't killed years ago
Tyrion is convinced by Varys that Daenerys could be a ruler who deserves his support
Daenerys decides Tyrion will remain as her adviser but confiscates his wine
Tyrion asks Daenerys what she wants to be advised on
Tyrion nearly rolls his eyes at Daenerys's desire for the Iron Throne
Tyrion suggests Daenerys might want to stay in Slaver's Bay and build a new kingdom in Meereen
Tyrion compliments Daenerys on reopening the fighting pits and her upcoming political marriage
Daenerys assures Tyrion she will stay to help stabilize things and keep the slave trade broken, but says Essos is not her home
Tyrion asks Daenerys who will support her in Westeros
Daenerys answers that the common people will support her
Tyrion assumes the common people will support her, though it generally doesn't happen
Tyrion asks Daenerys what ruling Meereen with only the support of the common folk was like
Tyrion notes Meereen has been in chaos without the support of both the Great Masters and the smallfolk
Tyrion extends this observation to Westeros and its Great Houses
Tyrion notes that Houses Targaryen and Stark are effectively dead due to their fathers' actions
Tyrion says House Lannister and House Baratheon of Dragonstone will never support Daenerys
Tyrion says House Tyrell might be willing to switch sides due to Cersei's schemes, but it is not enough
Daenerys likens the Great Houses to spokes on a wheel crushing the commoners beneath them
Tyrion sarcastically warns that others have dreamed of stopping the wheel
Daenerys clarifies that she plans to break the wheel
Arya assumes the identity of Lanna, a clam-seller
Arya shows Jaqen H'ghar she can convincingly become a different person with an elaborate backstory
Jaqen sends Arya, as Lanna, to the harbor
Arya observes the thin man refuse a contract to insure a man's boat, leaving the man desperate
Jaqen explains the thin man's business is a gamble and that he does not honor his agreements
Jaqen instructs Arya to kill the thin man and gives her a vial of poison
The Waif is skeptical of Arya/Lanna's success
Septa Unella demands Cersei to "confess" in return for her freedom
Cersei remains defiant
Cersei is obliged to drink water from the floor out of sheer thirst
Qyburn visits Cersei in her cell
Qyburn informs Cersei that her trial will begin soon for the crimes of fornication, treason, incest, and Robert's murder
Cersei furiously dismisses the charges as "lies"
Qyburn tells Cersei that Grand Maester Pycelle has summoned Kevan Lannister to serve as Hand of the King
Kevan Lannister arrives and refuses to visit Cersei in prison
Qyburn tells Cersei that Tommen has become reclusive, refusing to see anyone and refusing to eat
Qyburn tells Cersei "the work continues" on their secret project before leaving
Sansa confronts Reek about his latest betrayal
Reek apologetically affirms he was helping Sansa and says there is no escaping Ramsay
Reek tells Sansa that Theon Greyjoy tried to escape but Ramsay caught and tortured him until there was no Theon left
Sansa coldly expresses approval and reminds Reek of his role in her family's downfall, saying she would do to him what Ramsay did if she could
Reek shamefully admits he deserved his torture for doing terrible things, specifically killing "those boys"
Sansa, enraged by his dismissive reference to her brothers, demands to know why they should be dead while he still breathes
Reek finally admits that the boys he killed were not Bran and Rickon, but two farm boys
Sansa is stunned by this admission and her anger cools
Sansa presses Reek as to where Bran and Rickon might have gone, calling him Theon
Reek breaks down and runs out of the room, crying "Not Theon! Reek!"
Roose Bolton maintains that his army must wait inside Winterfell to starve out Stannis' forces
Roose Bolton notes that Winterfell has enough supplies to last 6 months under siege
Roose Bolton predicts that such conditions would cause a mutiny in the Baratheon ranks and break them down
Ramsay presents a plan to defeat Stannis decisively
Ramsay asks his father to lend him just "20 good men"
Gilly tends Sam's wounds
Olly enters with some food, having heard what happened
Gilly leaves
Olly expresses concern at joining forces with the wildlings, particularly Tormund, who led the raid in which his parents were killed
Sam reassures Olly that Jon knows what he is doing
Sam explains that unless Jon is successful, neither the Night's Watch nor Westeros stands a chance against the White Walkers
Sam explains this is a hard choice Jon had to make, however unpopular
Sam tells Olly not to worry, as Jon always comes back
Olly appears unconvinced
Jon Snow and Tormund, along with other members of the Night's Watch and the Free Folk, arrive at the wildling town Hardhome
Jon and Tormund attempt to convince the rest of the wildlings to join them in the upcoming war against the White Walkers and their armies of wights
The rest of the wildlings are present at Hardhome
The Lord of Bones refuses to ally himself with a Crow
The Lord of Bones insults Tormund for being at the side of an enemy like Jon Snow
Tormund seizes the Lord of Bones' staff
Tormund beats the Lord of Bones to death with his own staff
The wildling elders decide to gather in the Townshall to hear out Jon's proposal
Jon proposes an alliance between the Free Folk and the Night's Watch against the undead
Wildlings question the whereabouts of Mance Rayder
Jon informs the Wildlings that Mance Rayder is dead and that he shot him with an arrow
The Wildlings become infuriated and move in to kill Jon
Tormund vouches for Jon, explaining how Rayder was about to be burned at the stake by Stannis Baratheon and how Jon ended his life quickly
Some leaders, such as Karsi and Wun Wun, are convinced by Tormund
Other leaders, like the Thenn warrior Loboda, begin to leave
Jon offers the Wildlings dragonglass daggers, telling them his friend killed a White Walker with one
The Wildlings still seem unconvinced
Free Folk gather to set sail to the Wall
Night's Watch gathers dragonglass daggers in the tent
Edd approaches Wun Wun, who is holding a dragonglass dagger and examining it
Wun Wun appears unwilling to hand over the dagger and asks Edd "The fuck you looking at?" in Old Tongue
Edd walks away quietly without collecting the arrowhead
Dogs begin barking and the rising winds of a storm are heard
Loboda recognizes the sign of approaching White Walkers and orders the gates of Hardhome to be shut, abandoning many Free Folk outside
The storm reaches the village; the screams of people outside the walls quickly fall silent
A large host of undead wights begin breaking through the gates and climbing over the walls
Jon, Tormund, Loboda, and Karsi fight alongside others to help people reach their ships
Eddison Tollett, Wun Wun, and others are trapped inside the townhall, which is set upon by wights and catches fire
The bag of dragonglass weapons is lost in the confusion
White Walkers mounted on undead horses observe the battle from a hill, including the Night King
Jon notices the White Walkers and realizes the need for dragonglass
Jon and Loboda go to the burning townhall
Jon and Loboda are confronted by a White Walker warrior
Loboda faces the White Walker threat
Jon goes for the dragonglass
Loboda is quickly killed after his axe shatters upon contacting the White Walker's crystal sword
Jon grabs a dragonglass dagger
Jon is tossed aside by the White Walker several times
Jon and Longclaw end up outside the townhall
Jon retrieves Longclaw and raises it in defense against the White Walker
Jon and the White Walker are astonished to see the Valyrian steel remain intact after colliding with the White Walker's blade
Jon strikes the White Walker, shattering his body into fragments of ice
Jon confirms that Valyrian steel is just as lethal to White Walkers as dragonglass
The Night King watches the entire battle from a distance, appearing mildly intrigued
Karsi fights off multiple wights
Karsi is confronted by a pack of undead children
Karsi is frozen by horror and unable to resist as the undead children pounce on her and maul her to death
The White Walkers deploy another large host of wights, letting them hurl themselves over the cliffs above Hardhome
Jon, Edd, Tormund, and the remaining defenders flee for their lives
Wun Wun throws several wights off himself
Wun Wun wields a burning log as a club
Wun Wun walks into the sea with the wights
Jon and the others get to the last remaining boat and quickly row out to a safe distance
Jon and the others look on in horror
The Night King walks onto the dock, stops, and raises his arms triumphantly
The slain wildlings rise up as undead wights, including Karsi
The Night King and his army continue to stare in silence as Jon and the others retreat

The_Dance_of_Dragons_(episode).action: Jon, Tormund, and the surviving wildlings from Hardhome arrive at the Wall before the gates of Castle Black
Ser Alliser Thorne hesitates to let the wildlings through
Ser Alliser Thorne gives the order to open the gate
Thousands of wildlings come through the Wall to Castle Black, including women, children, and Wun Wun the giant
Many men of the Night's Watch glare at the wildlings
Ser Alliser Thorne tells Jon he has a good heart, but it may get them all killed
Jon reunites with Sam
Jon expresses despondence, saying the mission was a disaster and only a small fraction of wildlings were saved at Hardhome
Sam points to different wildlings passing by
Sam notes that each wildling would have died if Jon had done nothing, so it wasn't a complete failure
Melisandre gazes into the flames of her tent's brazier, hoping to receive visions from the Lord of Light
Several tents burst into flames in the distance
Confused men shout as the tents burn
A horse screams and runs away on fire
Ramsay and twenty men raid the camp, burning much of the food supplies and heavy siege weapons
Hundreds of horses die in the fire
Davos notes that their situation is now dire due to lack of food and siege weapons
Stannis asks how the raid could happen
Davos insists the Boltons know the North's terrain better, making the raid easy
Stannis suggests the watchmen were either collaborating with the enemy or inattentive
Stannis orders the watchmen to be executed
Stannis says to slaughter the dead horses for meat to buy time
Stannis's demeanor becomes grave and half-stunned as he contemplates his next action
Stannis orders Davos to return to Castle Black to ask Jon Snow for more supplies
Davos is confused, questioning the effectiveness and reasoning behind sending him instead of a messenger
Stannis insists he needs his Hand of the King to engage in diplomacy to win Jon over
Stannis secretly sends Davos away so Davos won't be present to stop Stannis's next actions
Davos visits the tent of Stannis's daughter Shireen to say goodbye
Shireen enjoys reading a book by Grand Maester Munkun about the Dance of the Dragons
Davos and Shireen share a laugh about a knight trying to kill the dragon Vhagar with a mirrored shield, but the dragon roasts the knight
Davos gives Shireen a wooden stag figurine he has been carving as a present
Shireen asks Davos why he has been so nice to her
Davos explains he felt bad that his son Matthos always insisted he learn to read and he never did
Davos reveals Matthos died in the war
Davos tells Shireen that by teaching him to read, she helped him fulfill his son's wish
Stannis visits Shireen, visibly shaken but trying to speak kindly
Stannis asks Shireen about her reading
Shireen explains "The Dance of the Dragons" was a civil war between Rhaenyra Targaryen and her half-brother Aegon
Shireen describes how the great lords of the Seven Kingdoms chose sides, dividing the realm and tearing Westeros apart
Shireen recounts that brother fought brother and dragon fought dragon in the devastating war
Shireen explains it was a disaster for House Targaryen, who never truly recovered
Stannis asks Shireen who she would have chosen, Rhaenyra or Aegon
Shireen says she wouldn't have chosen either, blaming the choosing of sides for the civil war
Stannis gravely responds that sometimes the world forces a man to choose, but if he stays true to himself, he knows what he must do
Stannis says that then it isn't really a choice at all, even if he hates doing it
Shireen says it's all right and she wants to help Stannis
Stannis responds that she doesn't even know what he wants
Shireen insists she doesn't care and asks if there is any way she can help
Stannis says that there is a way she can help
Shireen insists that she wants to help because she is Princess Shireen of House Baratheon, his daughter
Stannis and Shireen hug and he embraces her tightly
Stannis whispers "forgive me"
Shireen exits her tent and sees all of the soldiers gathered around
Shireen asks where her father is
Shireen comes to the front of the assembly and sees Melisandre in front of a wooden pyre with a large stake
Shireen starts shouting to see her father
soldiers grab Shireen, drag her to the pyre, and tie her to the stake
Melisandre soothingly tries to calm Shireen by saying it will all be over soon
Shireen's cries ring out through the camp, but no one intervenes
Stannis and Selyse appear in the crowd
Shireen sees Stannis and Selyse and begs them to help
Stannis and Selyse do nothing
Selyse insists aloud to herself that the sacrifice is what the Lord of Light wants
Melisandre begins praying and announces that they offer up the life of Shireen to the Lord of Light
Melisandre sets the pyre on fire
Melisandre explains to Stannis her belief that sacrificing Shireen will gain the Lord of Light's favor
Stannis believes that if they do nothing, they will remain snowbound and all starve to death
Shireen repeatedly cries "Mother, please!" and "Father, please!"
Selyse breaks down, rushes forward, and begs that they can't go through with it
Soldiers restrain Selyse
Selyse sinks to the ground crying in despair
Shireen's cries become more frightened
Stannis continues to watch as Selyse sobs on the ground
The flames begin to consume Shireen
Shireen screams in pain and fear
Stannis cannot bear to look anymore as Shireen burns to death
Areo Hotah escorts Jaime Lannister into the main apartments of the Water Gardens
Jaime is received by Prince Doran Martell in a luxurious solar, along with Ellaria Sand, Trystane Martell, and Myrcella Baratheon
They lounge on a circle of couches
Myrcella cuddles with Trystane
Jaime notes that Myrcella is wearing a revealing Dornish-style dress
Myrcella says that the warm climate of Dorne agrees with her
Doran asks Jaime why he has snuck into Dorne to abduct Myrcella back to King's Landing
Jaime says he feared for Myrcella's safety
Doran insists why Jaime didn't just send a letter by messenger-raven
Jaime explains they received a threatening message: Myrcella's Lannister lion pendant jammed in the mouth of a dead viper
Doran glares at Ellaria
Myrcella, confused, explains that her pendant was simply stolen from her room
Jaime asks Doran if he intends to behead him
Doran responds that he will not behead Jaime, wishing to avoid war
Doran reflects on the horrors of war and refuses to lead his people into it
Ellaria bluntly asks what Doran will do, break bread with the Lannisters
Doran says he will break bread with the Lannisters as wine is poured for Jaime
Doran raises his goblet and offers a toast to King Tommen, First of His Name
Jaime returns the toast
Ellaria deliberately pours out her wine onto the floor in disgust
Doran asks if Tommen commands that his sister be returned to the capital
Jaime politely says Tommen does
Doran says he cannot disobey his king and agrees Myrcella will return to King's Landing
Myrcella is upset for a moment
Doran explains his solution: Trystane will go with them to take Oberyn's seat on the Small Council
Doran insists the engagement of Trystane and Myrcella must stand for the alliance to continue
Jaime finds Doran's request reasonable and agrees
Myrcella is ecstatic that she will not be separated from Trystane
Ellaria gets up to leave and snaps at Doran, insulting him
Doran grabs Ellaria by the arm and warns her to never speak to him that way again
Jaime asks what fate will befall Bronn, who is still imprisoned
Bronn punched out Trystane when they tried to take Myrcella
Jaime is asked what the normal punishment is for striking a prince where he is from
Jaime insists that Bronn was only following orders and shouldn't be punished for following his commands
Trystane agrees and says he has learned the value of mercy from his father, but has one "condition"
Nymeria and Tyene Sand spar out of boredom in the prison cells
Obara tries to ignore Nymeria and Tyene and get some sleep
guards arrive and release Bronn
Tyene mocks Bronn as he leaves
Areo leads Bronn back to the solar where Doran, Jaime, and the rest remain
Bronn apologizes to Trystane and says he didn't mean anything by hitting him before
Jaime informs Bronn that the Martells agreed to let Bronn go with him, but on one condition
Areo strikes Bronn in the face with his elbow, knocking Bronn to the floor as payback for hitting Trystane
Areo brings Ellaria and the Sand Snakes before Prince Doran in his wheelchair
Doran gives Ellaria an ultimatum: swear allegiance or choose death
Ellaria, restraining tears, kneels and kisses Doran's hand
Doran says he believes in second chances and forgives Ellaria
Doran sternly warns Ellaria that he does not believe in third chances
Ellaria finds Jaime in a study writing a letter back to King's Landing
Jaime writes a letter with his non-dominant left hand
Ellaria remarks that Jaime writes like a seven year old
Ellaria notes that she and Oberyn were looked down on for their sexuality in King's Landing
Ellaria notes that Jaime is scorned for his incestuous love for Cersei
Jaime remains silent and gives no acknowledgement
Ellaria says that if Jaime were a Targaryen, no one would care about such a relationship
Ellaria says she knows Myrcella had no part in Oberyn's death, and maybe even Jaime is innocent
Ellaria exits the room
Jaime is left to ponder what seems to be her apology
Arya, as "Lanna", pushes her cart through the canal streets of Braavos selling shellfish
Arya passes the Thin Man, who sells insurance to sailors
Arya prepares to assassinate the Thin Man with poisoned oysters as instructed by the Faceless Men
Arya stops and stares fixedly at a boat at the dock
Mace Tyrell steps out of the boat
Ser Meryn Trant, commander of Mace's guards, steps out of the boat
Arya recognizes Ser Meryn Trant as one of the names on her kill list
It is revealed that Meryn helped betray Arya's father and killed Syrio Forel
Mace Tyrell was sent by Cersei Lannister to treat with the Iron Bank of Braavos
Mace Tyrell is greeted at the dock by Tycho Nestoris, the bank's representative
Mace addresses Tycho politely and amiably, not realizing his jokes aren't funny
Arya remains frozen, staring at Ser Meryn, ignoring the Thin Man's requests for oysters
Arya abandons her mission to assassinate the Thin Man
Arya follows Ser Meryn as he accompanies Mace
Ser Meryn accompanies Mace to the Iron Bank building
Meryn and a few Lannister guards depart from Mace at night to visit a local brothel
Arya sneaks into the brothel under the guise of selling oysters and clams from a hand basket
Brusco, the bouncer, refuses to let Arya in at first
Lhara, a prostitute and Arya’s regular customer, tells Brusco to let Arya stay
Arya sells oysters and clams in the brothel
Arya makes her way to the back chambers and spies on Meryn from behind some shutters
Meryn catches a brief look at Arya and finds her vaguely familiar, but shrugs it off
The brothel's madam presents several girls in succession to Meryn
Meryn passes on each girl as too old
The madam brings out her best and most expensive prostitute, Anara
Meryn dismisses Anara as too old
The madam realizes Meryn is interested in very young girls
The madam brings out a very young girl
Meryn accepts the very young girl and demands another "fresh" one tomorrow
Meryn leaves
The madam runs into Arya and shoos her out
Arya returns to the House of Black and White empty-handed, having abandoned her first mission for the Faceless Men
Jaqen H'ghar asks Arya what happened
Arya lies to Jaqen, saying the Thin Man wasn't hungry and didn't order any oysters
Jaqen quips that perhaps this is why he is a "thin man"
Arya promises she will follow through on the assassination tomorrow
Arya departs
Jaqen seems to suspect Arya was lying, but makes no outward reaction
Daenerys sits in the royal box at the Great Pit of Daznak, attended by Tyrion, Missandei, and Daario
Tyrion attends Daenerys in the royal box
Daario attends Daenerys in the royal box
Missandei attends Daenerys in the royal box
Hizdahr zo Loraq arrives late, claiming to have been putting the final touches on the arrangements
The first two combatants take the field
Hizdahr indicates that Daenerys is to start the fight with a clap of her hands
Daenerys starts the fight with a clap of her hands
Daario verbally spars with Hizdahr for much of the first match
Daario draws Daenerys’s attention from the spectacle
Daenerys’s attention returns to the arena when Jorah gives the traditional dedication to her
Jorah gives the traditional dedication to Daenerys
Jorah participates in the grand melee with six fighters
Jorah pairs up against a Norvosi long-axe fighter
The Norvosi lands several blows against Jorah, eventually knocking him to the ground
Jorah brings out his dagger and buries it in the Norvosi’s chest after a brief melee
A Braavosi water dancer easily dispatches his Dothraki opponent
The water dancer focuses his attention on Jorah
Jorah finds himself hopelessly outmatched by the water dancer
Jorah suffers many cuts from the water dancer's rapier
A Meerenese Pit Fighter lances his opponent in the chest
The water dancer knocks Jorah to the ground and is about to deliver a killing blow
The water dancer fails to notice the pit fighter
The pit fighter kills the water dancer from behind
Jorah battles the pit fighter
Jorah kills the pit fighter
Jorah is the last fighter standing
Jorah stares at Daenerys for a few moments
Jorah suddenly grabs and hurls a spear at the royal box, embedding it in a Son of the Harpy sneaking up behind Daario
Sons of the Harpy reveal themselves on every level of the arena and begin slaughtering collaborating Masters and freedmen, including Hizdahr
Jorah and Daario evacuate Daenerys from the royal box
Tyrion rescues Missandei
The group finds the exits blocked
The group makes a stand in the center of the Pit with the remainder of the Unsullied defenders
Daenerys takes Missandei's hand and closes her eyes, ready to face her death
A draconic screech pierces the air
Drogon descends upon the arena
Drogon flies out of a giant flame burst
Many of the Sons scatter in terror as Drogon bites, crushes, and burns the nearest ones to death
The Sons rally and attack Drogon with spears, embedding them in his hide
Daenerys hastily tries to remove the spears from Drogon
Drogon turns on Daenerys with a huge roar, but stops short of attacking and becomes calm
Daenerys climbs atop Drogon's back and bids him to fly ("Valahd"), becoming the first Targaryen dragonrider in over a century
Drogon soars away with Daenerys
Daario, Jorah, Missandei, and Tyrion look on in astonishment as Drogon soars away

Mother%27s_Mercy.action: The snow begins to melt
Melisandre is elated
Stannis, still grieving, prepares to march on Winterfell
One of Stannis's generals informs him that nearly half of his men have deserted, mainly the sellswords who have also run away with all their horses
Another soldier brings news that Selyse has hanged herself out of grief for her daughter
Stannis views Selyse's body
Stannis's general reports that Melisandre has fled
Stannis marches across the fields with less than half the men he once had
Stannis has his men start preparing for the siege
Stannis is met in combat early by the Bolton army
The mounted Bolton army easily defeats Stannis's tired and mountless army
Stannis, wounded and exhausted, fights off two remaining soldiers in the aftermath of the carnage
Stannis collapses against a tree
Podrick Payne warns Brienne of Stannis's arrival
Brienne leaves her watch for Sansa's signal
Brienne waits after the battle and finds a wounded Stannis
Brienne declares her identity and explains her king was killed by his brother's shadow
Brienne draws her sword to slay Stannis
Stannis accepts his fate and tells Brienne to do her "duty"
Brienne hesitates, then swings her sword at Stannis
Ramsay finishes off the remaining Baratheon soldiers
Ramsay heads back to Winterfell
Sansa uses a corkscrew she pocketed earlier to escape her room
Sansa seeks out the tower to light her signal
Sansa lights the candle in the tower
Sansa sees the Boltons clash with Stannis's army
Sansa decides to slip out of Winterfell
Sansa desperately runs across the ramparts
Sansa is stopped by Myranda and Reek
Myranda threatens Sansa with a bow
Sansa tells Myranda to kill her while there is still a part of the real her left
Myranda tells Sansa that Ramsay needs her to have an heir and does not need all of Sansa in one piece
Sansa braces herself for the worst as Myranda tenses her bow
Theon breaks the Boltons' hold on him and causes Myranda to misfire
Theon grabs and hurls Myranda over the ramparts
Myranda screams as she falls and is killed instantly upon impact with the stone floor
Sansa and Theon run
Sansa and Theon find themselves with nowhere to go as Ramsay returns from battle
Sansa and Theon grasp hands and jump off the rampart, hoping to land in deep snow and survive
Ser Meryn Trant looks upon three young girls in the Braavosi brothel
Two of the girls begin to cry and scream when whipped by Meryn
The last girl has no reaction when whipped
Meryn sends the two weak girls away
Meryn punches the last girl in the stomach
The last girl is revealed to be Arya Stark, wearing the face mask of Ghita
Arya removes her face mask
Arya pulls out a knife and stabs Meryn in both eyes and chest repeatedly
Arya puts a gag in Meryn's mouth to silence his cries
Arya reveals her identity and reminds Meryn of how he killed her dance master, Syrio Forel
Arya calls Meryn 'no one' and slits his throat
Arya returns to the House of Black and White and returns the face she used to hide her identity
Jaqen H'ghar and the Waif appear and say that Meryn's life was not Arya's to take, and that a debt must be paid
The Waif grabs Arya
Jaqen pulls out a vial, presumably containing poison
Jaqen drinks the poison himself and collapses
Arya screams for Jaqen to stay alive
Arya states that Jaqen was her friend
Arya hears Jaqen's voice behind her say 'he was no one'
Arya turns around to see the Waif now has Jaqen's face
Arya asks who the person on the floor with Jaqen's face is
Jaqen, in the Waif's body, says that he is no one
Arya removes multiple faces from the person on the floor
Arya sees her own face on the person
Arya begins to lose her sight as her eyes turn opaque white and screams for help
Daenerys is gone, leaving her council unsure how to continue
Tyrion addresses Jorah and Daario's feelings for Daenerys
Tyrion expresses understanding of loving the wrong woman
Tyrion, Jorah, and Daario argue about Jorah's betrayal
The council decides not to make any judgments without Daenerys
Drogon is seen taking Daenerys north
The council decides they need to search north for Daenerys
Grey Worm, still recovering from injuries, offers to go with Jorah and Daario
Tyrion offers to go with Jorah and Daario
Daario firmly refuses both Grey Worm and Tyrion's offers
Daario says Grey Worm is needed to command the Unsullied and keep the city under control
Daario says Tyrion is the only one with political experience
Daario expresses confidence that he and Missandei can keep Meereen under control until they return with Daenerys
Tyrion watches Daario and Jorah depart the city
Varys calmly walks up to Tyrion
Varys teases Tyrion about abandoning him in Volantis
Varys is pleased to see Tyrion settling into a suitable role
Varys reminds Tyrion of his experience running a city full of people trying to kill each other
Varys implies that he and his little birds are there to help
Daenerys finds herself far away from Meereen, atop a hill in a sea of green grass
Drogon recovers from wounds received in the arena in Meereen and is uninterested in flying back
Drogon is uninterested in finding food for them
Daenerys wanders away to find something for herself and Drogon
Daenerys is startled to see a trio of Dothraki bloodriders emerge
An entire khalasar surrounds Daenerys within minutes
Daenerys quickly removes a ring and drops it in the grass, determined to leave a trail
Jaime, Bronn, Myrcella, and Trystane say their farewells to the Martells to return to King's Landing
Ellaria Sand kisses Myrcella on the lips
Tyene Sand flirts with Bronn
Myrcella and Jaime talk about Cersei aboard the ship
Jaime begins to come clean to Myrcella, telling her we cannot choose who we love
Myrcella reveals she knows Jaime is her real father and says she is glad he is
Jaime and Myrcella embrace
Myrcella's expression changes, her smile fades, and her nose starts to bleed
Myrcella collapses in Jaime's arms
Ellaria wipes blood from her nose and takes the antidote for the poison coated on her lips
Septa Unella enters Cersei's cell and orders her to confess
Cersei gives in and is brought before the High Sparrow to confess her sins
Cersei confesses her adultery with Ser Lancel
Cersei denies her incestuous relationship with Jaime
Cersei begs to be allowed to go to the Red Keep to see her son Tommen
The High Sparrow grants Cersei's request but says there will still be a trial for the other charges
Cersei is brought to a cell, stripped naked, roughly washed, and her hair is cut short
Cersei is brought outside and forced to walk naked through the streets of King's Landing as punishment
Septa Unella accompanies Cersei, repeating "shame" and ringing a bell every few steps, along with six Faith Militant
Crowds leer at Cersei and pelt her with filth and insults
Cersei walks with bloody feet and eventually makes it to the Red Keep, bursting into tears
Cersei finds Kevan Lannister, Grand Maester Pycelle, and Qyburn waiting for her
Qyburn attempts to comfort Cersei by covering her with a cloak
Heavy footsteps are heard
Qyburn introduces Cersei to the newest member of the Kingsguard, an 8 foot tall man in golden armor
Qyburn suggests Cersei go inside so he can attend to her injured feet
The huge knight lifts Cersei up and carries her away
Cersei's expression begins to harden
Jon tells Sam what he witnessed at Hardhome
Jon expresses hope that the White Walkers can't climb the Wall, noting the lack of dragonglass or Valyrian steel in Westeros
Sam asks Jon if he can travel to Oldtown to study and replace Aemon as the new maester of the Watch
Sam explains he cannot help by fighting and wants to help in another way
Sam expresses fear for Gilly and her child's safety and wishes to take them along
Jon reminds Sam that the Citadel will not allow him to be with women
Jon is humored to hear it is already too late for that
Sam promises he will return
Jon and Sam toast to Sam's promise
Jon sees his only friend off
Davos urges Jon to convince the Wildlings for aid
Jon refuses Davos, saying the Wildlings wouldn't side with Stannis and this is not their fight
Melisandre returns to the Wall, speechless and despondent
Davos and Jon are shocked to see Melisandre return
Davos pressures Melisandre for news, specifically asking after Shireen
Melisandre remains silent
Davos sees the empty look in Melisandre's eyes and lets her leave
Jon reads messages at night
Olly tells Jon that a wildling knows the whereabouts of his long-missing uncle
Jon rushes with Thorne to a gathered group of Watchmen
Jon sees a placard with the word "traitor" written on it
Jon turns around to find his Brothers staring him down
Thorne makes the first move and stabs Jon
Thorne says "For the Watch"
Other Brothers close in and continue stabbing Jon, each saying "For the Watch"
Jon is betrayed one after another
Olly approaches Jon tearfully
Jon gasps "Olly"
Olly delivers the final blow, repeating the pledge
The brothers leave Jon to die alone in the snow

The_Red_Woman.action: Ramsay and Maester Wolkan attend to the dead body of Myranda
Ramsay remembers the first time he met Myranda and notes that she was not afraid of him
Wolkan asks if they should bury Myranda or construct a funeral pyre
Ramsay orders Wolkan to feed Myranda to the hounds
Roose Bolton meets with Ramsay
Roose asks Ramsay if preemptively assaulting Stannis Baratheon's army makes him feel like a "winner" after losing Sansa
Roose notes that a Lannister army marching north could overwhelm the Bolton troops at Winterfell and the Dreadfort
Ramsay assures Roose they will recapture Sansa, saying he has sent his best men to find her
Roose states they need Sansa to rally the north against the Lannisters if necessary
Roose warns Ramsay that if he fails to recapture Sansa, his unborn brother will become his heir instead
Theon and Sansa survive the fall from Winterfell's wall and run through the Wolfswood
Theon and Sansa realize there are men with hounds chasing them
Theon tries to hide their scent by crossing a river
Theon guides a frightened Sansa across the river
Theon and Sansa hide by a fallen tree, with Theon hugging Sansa to keep her warm
Theon hears the dogs still after them and tries to lead them away
Theon tells Sansa to keep heading north for Castle Black and Jon
A group of Bolton men with hounds find Theon and Sansa
Theon claims Sansa was hurt and he left her to die
The dogs find Sansa
The leader of the Bolton men mocks Theon and prepares to bring them back to Winterfell
Brienne and Podrick arrive and a fight begins
Brienne kills most of the Bolton men
Podrick uses techniques Brienne taught him and kills one Bolton man
Podrick is attacked by another Bolton man, briefly holds his own, but is knocked down
Theon grabs a sword and kills the Bolton man attacking Podrick
Sansa accepts Brienne into her service
Arya begs for money in the streets while blind
The Waif approaches Arya with two fighting sticks and gives her one
Arya explains she can't fight because she's blind
The Waif replies "that's not my problem" and starts beating Arya
Arya poorly tries to defend herself but fails miserably
The Waif stops and says they'll meet again tomorrow
Varys and Tyrion walk through the city of Meereen, dressed as merchants
Tyrion and Varys come upon a starving woman and her baby
Tyrion offers a coin to the woman, saying the money is for the baby to eat
Tyrion's poor Valyrian frightens the woman, who thinks he wants to buy her baby to eat it
Varys intercedes and explains to the woman that the money is so the baby can eat
The woman is relieved and accepts Tyrion's generosity
Tyrion states they need to find the leader of the Sons of the Harpy to bring peace to Meereen
Varys explains his spies have been sent all over the city and will soon bring information
a crowd of screaming people runs from a huge cloud of smoke, interrupting their conversation
Tyrion and Varys make their way towards the origin of the smoke
Tyrion and Varys see that all the ships in the harbor have been set aflame
Tyrion comments they won't be sailing to Westeros in the near future
Daario and Jorah look for Daenerys
Daario and Jorah come upon the remains of a ram charred by Drogon's fire
Daario and Jorah note numerous hoof prints in the proximity
Jorah realizes that Dothraki have been here
Jorah finds the ring that Daenerys intentionally dropped on the ground
Daenerys is shown walking with her hands bound
Two Dothraki take Daenerys to Khal Moro as a gift
Khal Moro wants to rape Daenerys
Daenerys explains she is Khal Drogo's widow
Khal Moro unbinds Daenerys and promises she will not be harmed
Daenerys asks to be escorted back to Meereen in exchange for one thousand horses
Moro's wife tells Daenerys she must remain in the temple of Vaes Dothrak with the other Khals' widows and join the Dosh Khaleen
Ellaria helps Doran get to his wheelchair
Doran and Ellaria reminisce about Oberyn
Doran states he envied Oberyn for his adventurous life
Ellaria states Doran would have made a bad adventurer, while Oberyn would have been a bad ruler
Doran settles into his chair
Maester Caleotte brings Doran a message that Myrcella has died
Tyene stabs Areo Hotah in the back, killing him
Ellaria stabs Doran in the chest
Doran lies on the floor as his other guards do nothing, indicating they have sided with Ellaria
Ellaria twists her knife into Doran and angrily says he remained passive despite the deaths of his sister and brother, and that the people of Dorne are with her
Doran begs Ellaria to spare Trystane as he is gasping
Ellaria coldly scoffs at Doran
Doran dies in a pool of his own blood
Cersei sits alone in her room
a servant enters and announces that a ship from Dorne has arrived
Cersei realizes that Myrcella has come home
Cersei joyfully runs down to the harbor
Cersei sees a grim-faced Jaime alongside a shrouded body
Cersei tearfully understands that her daughter has died
Cersei asks Jaime when was the first time he saw a dead body
Jaime replies it was their mother's body
Cersei tells of her memory of the horror she felt after first seeing her mother's corpse
Cersei expresses her wish not to see it happen to Myrcella
Cersei reveals Maggy's prophecy to Jaime
Jaime dismisses the prophecy as nonsense
Margaery is imprisoned by the Faith
High Sparrow visits Margaery
Margaery asks about Loras
High Sparrow says Margaery has to confess first
Margaery admits that nobody is without sin
High Sparrow tells Margaery she is on the right path but still has a long way to go
Obara and Nymeria arrive on Prince Trystane's ship
Obara and Nymeria announce their intention to kill Prince Trystane
Nymeria offers Trystane the choice of which one of the Sands he'd like to fight
Trystane chooses to fight Nymeria
Obara impales Trystane through the back of his head when he faces Nymeria, killing him
The mutineers leave Jon's body where he fell
Ghost howls and scratches at the door in grief
Ghost's howling gets the attention of several people, including Davos
Davos leaves his room to investigate
Davos finds Jon's dead body
Edd and some other brothers arrive
Edd and the brothers take Jon's body inside
Edd clears a table and angrily curses Thorne after closing Jon's eyes
Davos asks Edd if he trusts any of his brothers
Edd replies, "the ones in this room."
Davos convinces Edd to go bring Ghost in, deciding they need all the help they can get
Melisandre arrives, stating she saw Jon in the flames, fighting in Winterfell
Davos and the black brothers doubt the now dead Lord Commander could do such a thing
Melisandre returns to her room
Alliser Thorne, Othell Yarwyck, and Bowen Marsh hold a meeting with the remaining black brothers at Castle Black and explain their reasoning for killing the Lord Commander
Most of the brothers come to understand and side with the mutineers
Davos notes that Thorne is sure to have made his move by now
Edd states he doesn't care because Jon was his friend and they butchered him
Edd rallies the loyal brothers to try and take out Thorne in revenge
Davos responds that they are hopelessly outnumbered
Edd brings up Ghost
Davos states Ghost isn't enough
Davos reminds them there are others nearby who owe Jon their lives
Edd realizes Davos means the wildlings
Edd instructs the others to bar the door and not let anyone in
Edd sneaks out to get help
Thorne and other senior members of the Night's Watch approach the room with additional armed members
Thorne speaks to Davos through the locked door and promises full amnesty for the members of the Watch
Thorne offers Davos a fresh horse and safe passage to ride south or in any direction
Thorne adds that Davos can take Melisandre with him
Davos mocks Thorne's insincere offer by requesting mutton
Davos tells Thorne he is a poor hunter and needs mutton for his trip
Thorne promises food will be provided
Thorne notes the offer is available as long as everyone in the room surrenders by sundown
Melisandre appears crestfallen, feeling betrayed and led astray by the Lord of Light
Various containers, potions, and a mirror are present on a table next to Melisandre's bed
Melisandre unbuttons the front of her dress, revealing a young, beautiful body
Melisandre removes her dress completely and stands nude before the mirror
Melisandre removes her large necklace, causing the gem to lose its glow
Melisandre's reflection in the mirror shows her true form as a 400-year-old frail woman
Melisandre, fully nude and despondent, climbs into bed and pulls the fur blanket over herself

Home.action: Bran continues his training under Brynden Rivers at the tree
Brynden Rivers shows Bran a scene from Winterfell
Bran watches Eddard and Benjen training in the yard as young boys
Bran sees Lyanna, Old Nan, and Ser Rodrik Cassel
Bran sees a stableboy who is a young Hodor, realizes his name is Wylis, and that he was intellectually normal and could talk
Bran is surprised by Hodor’s past
Bran wants to stay in the vision
Brynden drags Bran out of the vision and warns him it is dangerous to stay too long
Bran finds Meera Reed outside the tree
Meera appears to be mourning her brother’s death
Meera questions the point of her presence during Bran’s training
Leaf states that Bran will need Meera when he leaves the tree
a man drunkenly boasts of having taunted Cersei during her walk of atonement to the amusement of other patrons
the man finishes his story
the man leaves the tavern to urinate against a wall outside
the man is confronted by Ser Gregor Clegane while urinating
Ser Gregor Clegane kills the man by smashing his head against the wall
Ser Gregor returns to Cersei's side
Myrcella's funeral is held at the Great Sept of Baelor
Cersei cannot attend the funeral
Tommen orders that Cersei be confined to the Red Keep
Tommen is concerned that the Faith Militant will re-arrest Cersei if she enters the sept
Jaime and Tommen talk about Myrcella and Prince Trystane in the sept
Tommen believes Cersei is to blame for Prince Trystane's death
Tommen tells Jaime he isn't upset with Cersei
Jaime convinces Tommen to go see his mother and apologize
The High Sparrow enters the sept
Tommen leaves the sept
Jaime threatens the High Sparrow
Jaime asks the High Sparrow why Cersei is being punished for her sins but he isn't
Jaime brings up that he murdered the king he vowed to protect
Jaime is about to kill the High Sparrow
The High Sparrow seems unafraid to die, confusing Jaime
The High Sparrow explains that if Jaime kills him, many sparrows will die fighting, but it wouldn't matter
The High Sparrow says the sparrows mean nothing individually, but together they can overthrow an empire
Tommen visits Cersei in the Red Keep
Tommen apologizes for not being more assertive during Cersei's imprisonment
Tommen expresses regret for letting the High Sparrow lock Cersei away
Tommen says he should have torn the sept down with the High Sparrow inside
Tommen tells Cersei he needs her help to be a strong ruler
Cersei remains uncharacteristically silent throughout the exchange
Cersei embraces Tommen when he is done speaking
Missandei and Grey Worm tell Tyrion that Rhaegal and Viserion have not been eating since Daenerys left
Tyrion explains that dragons do not do well in captivity and will probably eat if unchained
Tyrion suggests dragons are intelligent enough to remember their friends and will not harm Missandei
Tyrion and Varys go down below the pyramid
Tyrion advances into the darkness alone
One of the dragons emerges from the shadows and Tyrion maintains eye contact
The other dragon emerges
Tyrion puts down his torch and gently advances
Tyrion speaks gently to calm the dragons, telling a story from his childhood
Tyrion releases the locking pin on the first dragon’s collar
The other dragon comes close and turns his head so Tyrion can free him as well
Tyrion frees the second dragon
The dragons retreat
Tyrion hastily returns to Varys
Tyrion instructs Varys to punch him in the face if he ever suggests doing anything like that again
Arya begs on the streets
The Waif approaches Arya and asks her name
Arya says she is "no one"
The Waif hits Arya
The Waif says she doesn't believe Arya is "no one" and neither does Arya
Arya tries to fight back
The Waif vanishes, replaced by Jaqen H'ghar
Jaqen promises Arya food, shelter, and restored sight if she says her name
Arya repeatedly says she has no name
Jaqen, satisfied, tells Arya to follow him and that she is a beggar no more
Ramsay and Roose discuss with Harald Karstark what to do about Sansa and Jon
Ramsay suggests storming Castle Black
Roose warns Ramsay that attacking the Night's Watch would turn the entire North against House Bolton
Roose adds that if Ramsay does not act more sensibly he will be discarded
Maester Wolkan enters and announces that Roose's wife Walda has given birth to a baby boy
Roose tells Ramsay he will always be remembered and treated as his firstborn child
Ramsay, believing the new son is a threat to his inheritance, thanks Roose for saying so
Ramsay stabs Roose to death in front of Wolkan
Ramsay orders Maester Wolkan to announce that Roose was poisoned by their enemies
Ramsay personally feeds Walda and his half-brother to the hounds
Brienne fills Sansa in on her encounter with Arya
Sansa is reluctant to talk about her experiences under Ramsay Bolton's tyranny at Winterfell
Sansa admits she ought to have accepted Brienne's help sooner
Theon, wracked with guilt over his betrayal of the Stark family, decides to leave the group and go home instead of joining the Night's Watch
Theon tells Sansa that Brienne and Pod will do a better job of looking after her
Theon tearfully states he would have gone with Sansa all the way to Castle Black if necessary
Theon asks to take one of the horses with him
Sansa and Theon embrace
It is a dark and stormy night at Pyke
Yara Greyjoy reads a letter to King Balon Greyjoy about the Glovers retaking Deepwood Motte and killing all of the Ironborn who held the castle
Yara notes that their invasion is now a definite failure since Deepwood Motte was the last stronghold the Ironborn held in the North
Balon tells Yara that they will continue fighting
Yara argues that invading the North is pointless since they are unable to hold the strongholds they conquer and the strongholds aren't valuable to the Ironborn
Balon says the invasion wasn't successful because Yara wasted men attempting to rescue Theon
Yara says she will not apologize for attempting to rescue Theon
Yara continues to try to convince Balon to end the rebellion, saying they only took strongholds because the Northerners were away at war, and now the war is over
Yara reminds Balon that the last time they provoked the Mainlanders, they were crushed and her two oldest brothers were killed
Balon tells Yara that when she rules she can be content with their current standing, but for now she must obey or he will make another heir
Balon starts walking on the swinging rope bridge that separates two of the towers of Pyke
Balon sees a man standing on the bridge, blocking his way
Balon tells the man to get out of the way
The man reveals himself to be Balon's younger brother, Euron Greyjoy
Balon tells Euron he’s surprised he’s still alive
Euron mocks the Drowned God, telling Balon that he is the Drowned God
Balon inquires about a rumor that Euron lost his senses during a storm and his crew had to tie him to the mast to prevent him from jumping overboard
Euron does not deny the rumor and explains he cut the tongues out of all his crew members because he wanted silence
Euron says Balon is too old to rule and it’s someone else’s turn
Balon tells Euron he isn’t a true Ironborn because he lost his wits during a thunderstorm and tries to stab him
Euron throws Balon off the rope bridge to his death
Yara tells Aeron she will find the person who killed Balon and feed them to sharks while they're still alive
Yara swears to do this upon the Salt Throne
Aeron explains to Yara that she isn't the ruler yet because the kingsmoot chooses the next ruler
Yara argues that her father wanted her to rule
Aeron says that Balon doesn't make the law
Nightfall arrives at Castle Black
Band of black brothers loyal to Jon have not yet surrendered
Davos refuses Ser Alliser Thorne’s ultimatum by drawing Longclaw
Other black brothers draw their swords
Ser Alliser Thorne orders the door to be battered down with a sledgehammer
Edd returns with Tormund and an army of wildlings
Thorne demands the watchmen stand and fight
One watchman tries to fight and is cut down by Tormund
Wildlings take over Castle Black almost bloodlessly
One archer shoots a crossbow bolt into Wun Wun’s back
Wun Wun seizes the archer, dashes him against a stone wall, and throws the body at Thorne’s feet
Watchmen throw down their arms and surrender
Thorne tries to attack
Olly rushes Tormund with a sword
Thorne is seized
Tormund disarms Olly and pushes him into waiting arms
No wildlings are hurt
Thorne, Olly, and the mutinous officers are led away to the Ice Cells
Edd brings Tormund to see Jon's corpse
Tormund notes Jon's many wounds and quietly says he'll have his men gather wood for pyres
Davos visits Melisandre, who is wearing her normal glamor but is gripped by a crisis of faith
Davos asks Melisandre if reviving Jon is possible
Melisandre admits to having seen resurrection done, but insists she cannot do it due to her lost faith in the Lord of Light
Davos implores Melisandre to try reviving Jon
Melisandre agrees to try
Melisandre washes Jon's wounds and burns his hair
Melisandre attempts to revive Jon using High Valyrian incantations, but it seems to have no effect
Tormund leaves the room
Melisandre offers a final "please" in the Common Tongue and leaves the room despondent
Edd leaves the room
Davos leaves the room
Ghost, sleeping under the table, perks up
Jon suddenly awakens, gasping for air

Oathbreaker.action: Jon awakens and gasps for air
Jon slowly sits up and scans the room
Jon looks down and is horrified by his stab wounds
Jon confirms it is not a bad dream
Davos returns to the room after hearing the noise
Davos is shocked but relieved
Davos helps Jon as he struggles to walk
Davos asks Jon if he remembers anything
Jon says he remembers getting stabbed by Olly and Alliser Thorne for bringing the Wildlings to Castle Black
Melisandre enters, shocked that the resurrection has worked
Melisandre goes to Jon and asks where he was after his death
Jon replies he saw nothing
Melisandre is disappointed
Melisandre says the Lord of Light brought Jon back for a reason, that Stannis was not the Prince That Was Promised, but someone must be
Davos asks Melisandre to leave them alone so Jon can adjust to his revival
Davos grabs a stool
Davos notes that Jon was dead and now he isn't, calling it "completely fucking mad"
Davos imagines what Jon thinks of his resurrection
Jon laments that he tried to do what he thought was right, but got murdered for it
Jon asks why he returned from the dead
Davos answers that they may never know why Jon returned, but it doesn't matter
Davos says the important thing is to fight and go on living for as long as possible
Jon admits he thought he knew how to live, but isn't sure anymore because he failed
Davos tells Jon, "Good, and now go fail again."
Jon dons his Night's Watch armor and the Lord Commander's cape
Jon walks into the courtyard
Jon sees many of the Free Folk and his fellow Brothers arrive before him
Jon, still struggling to walk properly, comes down and greets the dumbfounded Tormund
Tormund jokes with the resurrected Lord Commander
Tormund hugs Jon tightly, slightly paining him due to his stab wounds
Jon greets the relieved Eddison Tollett
Jon and Eddison Tollett embrace
Jon thinks it's still himself in his body, not a wight
Jon jokes to hold off on burning his body for the time being
Jon oversees the public hanging of the traitors responsible for the Mutiny at Castle Black: Bowen Marsh, Othell Yarwyck, Alliser Thorne, and Olly
Jon asks if any of the men have last words to say
Bowen Marsh says Jon shouldn't be alive, stating that it's not right
Jon coldly replies, "Neither was killing me."
Yarwyck pleads Jon to write his mother in White Harbor that he died honorably fighting the Wildlings
Alliser Thorne says he had a choice: to betray his Commander or the Night's Watch
Alliser says Jon bringing the Wildlings was a dangerous problem and had to be stopped
Alliser warns Jon that he will be fighting their battles forever
Jon gives his respect to Alliser for following his convictions even knowing the consequences
Jon moves on to Olly, who says nothing and glares furiously at Jon
Olly glares furiously at Jon
Jon reluctantly cuts down the rope, killing all four traitors
Jon looks at Olly's lifeless body
Jon takes off his cloak and hands it to the confused Edd
Edd is confused as Jon hands him the cloak
Jon says Edd can do whatever he wants with the cloak, unofficially designating Edd as the 999th Lord Commander of the Night's Watch
Jon leaves the courtyards and declares his watch has ended
The rest of the Brothers look on in shock
Samwell, Gilly, and her baby son Sam sail on a ship to Oldtown
Gilly and Samwell experience being on a ship for the first time
Samwell becomes seasick and throws up into a chamber pot
Gilly assures Samwell that it won't be long
Gilly says she can't wait to see Oldtown and mentions the captain's description of the city
Samwell adjusts his plans since he cannot bring Gilly and her child to the Citadel, which only allows men
Samwell decides to take Gilly and Sam to his ancestral home of Horn Hill for their safety
Samwell assures Gilly that his mother and sister are very kind, despite not getting on with his father Randyll Tarly
Samwell wants to protect Gilly and her child from harm
Gilly promises to follow Samwell and let him decide what is best for them, despite some unease
Bran has a vision of the past at the Tower of Joy in Dorne
Brynden Rivers accompanies Bran in the vision
The last two Targaryen Kingsguard stand before the Tower and block the way of a small party of Northmen led by Eddard Stark
Eddard leads a small party of Northmen to the Tower of Joy
Brynden identifies Howland Reed as Meera's father
Eddard tells Ser Arthur Dayne and Lord Commander Gerold Hightower to stand down
Eddard asks why the Kingsguard weren't at the Trident
Ser Arthur states that Rhaegar Targaryen ordered them to guard the tower
Eddard asks where his sister Lyanna is
Ser Arthur wishes Eddard the best in the wars to come
The two Kingsguard draw their swords
Ser Arthur says, "And now it begins"
Eddard replies, "No, now it ends"
The two sides clash
Ser Arthur makes short work of a few of Ned's northmen with two swords
Ser Gerold falls to Eddard's blade
Ser Arthur duels Eddard one-on-one
Bran expresses shock at Ser Arthur's skills
Brynden confirms that Ser Arthur is better than Ned
Ser Arthur gains the upper hand and kicks Ned's sword away
Howland stabs Ser Arthur through the back of the neck
Eddard is visibly uncomfortable at winning this way
Eddard delivers the killing blow to the incapacitated Ser Arthur
Bran is shocked, having heard tales of the duel and never expecting his father to stand for such things
Eddard runs towards the tower after hearing a woman's scream from one of its windows
Bran runs to follow Eddard, calling out to him
The raven tells Bran it's time to go
Eddard looks back for a second, seemingly hearing Bran, but then runs into the Tower
Bran tries to follow Eddard
Brynden forces Bran out of the past and back into the cave
Bran feels both angry and sad
The raven asserts it was for Bran's safety and sternly reminds him that the past cannot be changed
The raven states he has waited a thousand years for Bran to arrive and that Bran will not share his destiny
The raven tells Bran he must learn everything before leaving the cave
Qyburn is in the company of the little birds, children who used to spy for Varys
Qyburn tries to win the favor of the children by attracting them with candied plums from Dorne
Cersei, Jaime Lannister, and the reanimated Ser Gregor Clegane enter
Cersei wants Qyburn to gather information from Dorne, Highgarden, the North, and wherever people are plotting against them
Pycelle complains about Qyburn and his experiments
Kevan Lannister heads the Small Council as Hand of the King
Small Council consists of Kevan, Pycelle, and Mace Tyrell, with Olenna Tyrell in attendance
Olenna Tyrell lobbies for Margaery and Loras Tyrell's release
Cersei, Jaime, and Ser Gregor enter the room, startling Pycelle and Mace
Pycelle and Mace are startled by the entrance
Cersei is questioned about attending the Small Council meeting
Cersei replies that she is the Queen
Olenna corrects Cersei, stating that Margaery is the rightful queen
Jaime argues that, as Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, he has a right to attend
Pycelle admits Ser Gerold Hightower once sat on the council, but dismisses it due to the Mad King's eccentricities
Cersei accuses Kevan of not caring for her daughter Myrcella
Jaime urges Kevan to take action against Ellaria Sand and the Sand Snakes in Dorne
Kevan and the Small Council, displeased with the newcomers, leave the hall
King Tommen Baratheon and his Kingsguard visit the High Sparrow to secure permission for Cersei to visit her daughter's resting place
The High Sparrow replies that Cersei cannot visit because she has not been cleared of her other sins
Tommen criticizes the High Sparrow for his self-righteous treatment of Cersei after her atonement
The High Sparrow claims admiration for Cersei's deep love for her son, Tommen, attributing it to the divine love of the Mother
Tommen asks the High Sparrow why he wants to put his mother through more trials
The High Sparrow replies that he is merely carrying out the will of the Seven
The High Sparrow urges Tommen to turn to the Seven for wisdom
Tommen prepares to leave, feeling less angry and more uncertain
The High Sparrow tells Tommen that the Seven have worked through his grandfather and mother even though they may not acknowledge it
Varys holds court in the Great Pyramid of Meereen
Unsullied bring in Vala, a prostitute allied with the Sons of the Harpy
Vala had seduced and murdered several members of the Second Sons and Unsullied
Vala is defiant and states her opposition to the new regime imposed by the Unsullied and Second Sons
Vala explains she became a prostitute out of patriotic fervor for the old ways
Varys assures Vala he has no interest in torturing her
Varys decides to strike a deal with Vala
Varys offers Vala and her son safe passage to Pentos and a bag of silver in exchange for information on who is funding the Sons of the Harpy
Varys warns Vala that if she refuses, she will be executed and her son will be orphaned
Tyrion engages Missandei and Grey Worm in small talk
Tyrion offers to play games with Missandei and Grey Worm
Missandei and Grey Worm are not interested in playing games
Tyrion prepares to embark on a drinking game
Varys returns and informs the group that the Wise Masters of Yunkai, the Good Masters of Astapor, and the Free City of Volantis have been secretly funding the Sons of the Harpy
Grey Worm advocates attacking the cities
Missandei advises against attacking, since that would leave Meereen unprotected
Tyrion asks Varys to use his little birds to send a message
Daenerys walks with the Dothraki towards Vaes Dothrak
Daenerys is ushered into the Temple of the Dosh Khaleen by Khal Moro's bloodriders
The High Priestess orders the bloodriders to leave
The High Priestess strips Daenerys naked and gives her a robe
Daenerys puts on her new clothes
Daenerys tells the High Priestess she will regret her actions, citing her titles
The High Priestess retorts that she was the wife of a previous Great Khal, Savo
The High Priestess explains her own past ambitions and Savo's death
The High Priestess says she is now content and Daenerys will be too, if allowed to join
Daenerys expresses confusion
The High Priestess points out khaleesi are supposed to return to Vaes Dothrak after their khal's death
The High Priestess explains Daenerys went out into the world instead
The High Priestess explains all the khalasars have gathered to decide the fate of cities, tribes, and Daenerys
The High Priestess says allowing Daenerys to join the Dosh Khaleen is the best outcome
Arya resumes her training with the Waif and Jaqen H'ghar
The Waif beats Arya repeatedly when she lies
Jaqen has Arya mixing potions blindly
Arya continues sparring with the Waif while answering questions about her family and the Hound
Arya admits to leaving the Hound to die instead of killing him, despite wanting his death
The Waif interrogates Arya about the people on her list: Cersei, Gregor Clegane, and Walder Frey
The Waif voices disbelief that Arya's list is so short
Arya counters by asking which name the Waif wants added
Arya insists that she is no one
The Waif does not believe Arya and tries to hit her
Arya deflects the Waif's blow and leaps out of the way, surprising the Waif
Jaqen confirms Arya has mastered blind sparring
Jaqen approaches Arya and offers to give back her sight if she tells him her name
Arya replies that "a girl has no name"
Jaqen leads Arya to the well and scoops up a bowl of water, offering it to her
Arya hesitates to drink, as others have died from it
Jaqen tells Arya that if she is truly "no one" there is nothing to fear
Arya drinks from the bowl and her sight is restored
Jaqen asks Arya who she is
Arya replies that she is no one
The Umbers led by Smalljon Umber travel to Winterfell to meet Ramsay Bolton
Smalljon and Ramsay chat about their experiences with their fathers
Smalljon mentions that his father Greatjon Umber is dead
Smalljon admits he would have killed his father if the opportunity arose
Ramsay denies murdering the late Roose Bolton and claims he was poisoned by his enemies
Smalljon doesn't bother believing Ramsay
Smalljon comes to forge an alliance with the Boltons against Jon Snow and the Wildlings
Smalljon refuses to bow to Ramsay, citing Robb Stark's betrayal at the hands of Roose
Smalljon reveals he has a "gift" for Ramsay
Ramsay asks if the gift is a girl, hoping for Sansa Stark
Smalljon replies otherwise
Umber guards enter the room with two hooded figures
The first hooded figure is revealed to be Osha
The second hooded figure is revealed to be Rickon Stark, Ned Stark's youngest child
Osha and Rickon appear uncomfortable in Ramsay's presence
Ramsay asks Smalljon to verify Rickon's identity
An Umber retainer enters carrying the severed head of the direwolf Shaggydog
Rickon shows an expression of dismay, confirming Shaggydog was his pet
Ramsay, satisfied, sarcastically welcomes Rickon home

Book_of_the_Stranger.action: Septa Unella enters Queen Margaery Tyrell's cell and leads her to a private meeting with the High Sparrow
The High Sparrow asks Margaery what she would do if allowed to leave that day
Margaery replies that she wants to return to her family and her husband Tommen Baratheon
The High Sparrow tells Margaery that attachment leads to sin, citing his experience as a cobbler
Margaery impresses the High Sparrow with her knowledge of The Book of the Stranger
Margaery admits Septa Unella had read The Book of the Stranger at her
The High Sparrow chuckles and agrees that Unella has a habit of reading at people instead of to them
The High Sparrow recounts how he was a womanizer and drunkard in his youth
The High Sparrow undergoes a conversion experience the morning after an orgy
The High Sparrow sees his drunken guests lying naked around his house
The High Sparrow becomes tired of his meaningless existence
The High Sparrow turns his life around and becomes a devoted member of the Faith of the Seven
The High Sparrow leaves his job as a cobbler
The High Sparrow devotes his life to the poor and destitute
The High Sparrow believes he has found a way to reach Margaery
The High Sparrow allows Margaery to visit her brother Loras Tyrell
She finds a dejected Loras lying on the floor of his cell
Loras has lost the will to continue resisting
Margaery tells Loras to stay strong because he is the future of House Tyrell
Loras tells Margaery that he only wants his imprisonment to end and pleads with her to help him
Margaery tells Loras that the Sparrow is trying to use them to break each other and again tells him to stay strong
Cersei walks in on Grand Maester Pycelle advising King Tommen to accommodate the High Sparrow and the Faith Militant
Pycelle advises King Tommen to accommodate the High Sparrow and the Faith Militant
Cersei requests that Pycelle leave the room so she can talk to Tommen alone
Cersei uses the pretext of meeting Tommen for updates about previous Small Council meetings
Tommen proposes that the Iron Throne not antagonize the Sparrows further to avoid endangering Margaery's life
Cersei reminds Tommen about the humiliation she endured at the Sparrows' hands
Cersei exploits Tommen's love for Margaery
Cersei convinces Tommen that the High Sparrow has no respect for kings, queens, and society
Tommen reveals a secret that the High Sparrow shared with him
Cersei barges into a Small Council meeting between Kevan Lannister, Olenna Tyrell, and Jaime Lannister
Kevan and Olenna are initially hostile to Cersei
Cersei convinces Kevan and Olenna to listen to her
Cersei informs the Council that the High Sparrow plans to make Margaery do the walk of atonement
Olenna is horrified that the Sparrows plan to debase her granddaughter
Jaime urges the Council to allow the Tyrells to march their army into King's Landing, crush the Faith Militant, and bring Margaery and Loras back to the Red Keep
Kevan says Tommen expressly forbade him to use the Lannister army against the Great Sept, fearing for Margaery's safety
Jaime suggests Olenna send House Tyrell's army to the city instead
Jaime points out that Tommen only forbade his army from acting, not others, so Kevan wouldn't be breaking commands if his forces stand down
The Tyrell army will surround the Great Sept, intimidating the Sparrows into releasing Margaery and Loras, without bloodshed
Jaime notes that by the time Tommen finds out, he will be happy with the outcome
Kevan's interest is turned, but he still expresses reluctance should they fail
Cersei exploits Kevan's hatred towards the Sparrows for taking Lancel to make him change his mind
Olenna agrees that blood will be shed no matter what, so it's better if it's the Sparrows' blood
Eddison Tollett watches Jon Snow pack up his things
Edd scolds Jon for leaving the Night's Watch after taking an oath
Jon replies that he gave his life for the Night's Watch and can't trust his Watch brothers after what happened
Edd reminds Jon they both saw what happened at Hardhome and that the enemy is still coming
the horns sound for the arrival of travellers
Sansa, Brienne, and Podrick ride through the gates and dismount in the courtyard
Jon rushes out to see Sansa for the first time since their family left Winterfell
Jon and Sansa embrace
Jon and Sansa converse and reminisce on their childhood later that night
Sansa proposes they take Winterfell back from the Boltons
Jon says he's done fighting, especially after hanging Olly
Davos asks Melisandre whether she will stay at Castle Black
Melisandre replies she will do as Jon Snow commands
Davos asks what happened to Stannis
Melisandre replies Stannis was defeated in battle
Davos asks about Shireen
Melisandre is reluctant to speak about Shireen
Brienne interrupts the conversation
Brienne recognizes Davos and Melisandre as Stannis' former entourage
Brienne warns Melisandre that she does not forget or forgive
Brienne tells them she executed Stannis after he admitted using bloodmagic to assassinate Renly

Jon receives a letter from Ramsay Bolton stating that he has Rickon Stark at Winterfell and threatening to slaughter every Wildling at Castle Black, let his men rape Sansa, and feed Jon and Rickon to his dogs if Sansa is not returned
Jon asks Tormund how many men he has available to fight
Tormund replies he has 2,000 able fighters, less than Ramsay's estimated 5,000
Sansa says they have to try to retake their home and rescue their brother, and that the houses of the North will unite behind Jon as the son of Eddard Stark
Jon agrees to take Ramsay down
Robin Arryn trains at archery with Lord Yohn Royce
Robin performs poorly at archery
Petyr Baelish arrives with Arryn guards and a pet falcon as a belated name-day gift for Robin
Robin is delighted by the pet falcon
Royce reminds Baelish that he was supposed to take Sansa to the Fingers, but heard she married Ramsay Bolton
Baelish claims he and Sansa were set upon by Bolton men on the way to the Fingers
Petyr Baelish and Royce confront each other
Petyr accuses Royce of giving away Sansa’s location to House Bolton
Royce accuses Baelish of slander
Petyr reminds Royce that Robin is Lord of the Vale and asks for his judgment
Robin asks Baelish if they should throw Royce from the Moon Door
The surrounding knights, including Royce’s loyalists, muster themselves
Royce swears loyalty to Robin
Baelish recommends letting Royce off without punishment
Royce realizes Littlefinger has control over Robin
Petyr and Robin decide to muster the Vale knights to march North and aid Sansa
Osha is brought before Ramsay Bolton at Winterfell
Ramsay says Rickon Stark has value to him but is unsure of Osha's worth
Osha denounces the Starks, claiming her servitude and loyalty were forced
Osha attempts to seduce Ramsay, climbing onto his lap and kissing him
Osha tries to get the knife Ramsay was using to peel apples
Ramsay claims Theon Greyjoy told him everything, including Osha's seduction of Theon to help Rickon and Bran escape
Osha realizes she has been lured into a trap
Osha tries to stab Ramsay
Ramsay slashes Osha's throat first
Ramsay resumes peeling his apple as Osha dies on the floor
Theon Greyjoy returns home to Pyke
Theon learns of the death of his father, Balon Greyjoy
Yara is unhappy about Theon's return and his earlier refusal to leave the Dreadfort when she tried to rescue him
Theon explains that Ramsay "broke him into a thousand pieces"
Yara affirms Theon's statement, referencing the box containing Theon's penis that she and Balon received
Yara suspects Theon has come back to declare himself King of the Iron Islands
Theon insists he does not want the crown
Theon says he wants Yara to rule and offers to help her in any way he can
Tyrion, Grey Worm, Missandei, and Varys meet with representatives of Astapor, Yunkai, and Volantis
The diplomatic mission enters the harbor
Grey Worm advocates using military force
Tyrion explains he empathizes with slaves because he spent one day as a slave
Missandei counters that Tyrion has not truly experienced slavery
The emissaries are identified as Yezzan zo Qaggaz, Razdal mo Eraz, and Belicho Paenymion
Yezzan marvels that Tyrion has gone from being a nearly-worthless slave to the de facto ruler of Meereen
The slave-trading cities offer Daenerys and her mercenaries a large pot of money if they sail away from Slaver's Bay
Missandei defends Daenerys's actions in liberating slaves
Razdal contends that slavery has existed for centuries
Tyrion brings up that profit is possible without slaves, referencing his wealthy upbringing in Westeros
Razdal and Belicho look at each other, insulted
Yezzan looks away from Tyrion, contemplating his words
Tyrion proposes a counter-offer after hearing their offer
Tyrion offers a seven-year grace period to phase out slavery and compensation for slave owners, on the condition they end support for the Sons of the Harpy
Tyrion cautions them that they will not get a better offer
Tyrion gives them time to consider his offer
Grey Worm and Missandei insist on abolishing slavery
Tyrion and his entourage are confronted by a crowd of former slaves in the Meereen throne room
A freedman demands to know when Daenerys will return
Another freedman is appalled at the idea of Tyrion negotiating with slave traders
Tyrion insists he is doing his best to rule the city in Daenerys's absence
The freedmen do not trust Tyrion and look to Grey Worm and Missandei for reassurance
Grey Worm and Missandei publicly back Tyrion's diplomacy in front of the freedmen
Grey Worm and Missandei privately warn Tyrion that the masters will outmaneuver him
Jorah Mormont and Daario Naharis approach the outskirts of Vaes Dothrak and discard their weapons
Jorah estimates there are 100,000 Dothraki in the city
Jorah and Daario run afoul of Aggo and Iggo within the city
Aggo and Iggo see through Jorah's claims to be a wine merchant
Daario takes care of Iggo
Aggo tackles Jorah
Jorah is nearly undone
Daario saves Jorah at the last minute with the stiletto he smuggled in
Daario smashes Aggo's skull with a rock to disguise his use of a bladed weapon
The High Priestess gives Daenerys a commentary on the other crones
Some of the crones hate Daenerys, thinking the Dothraki should not interbreed with other races
The High Priestess dismisses the crones' concerns, saying the Dothraki have always interbred and never cared about blood purity
The High Priestess indicates another of the khaleen, a Lhazareen girl (Ornela) taken from her village at age twelve
Daenerys asks to relieve herself
The High Priestess sends Ornela with Daenerys
As they walk, Daenerys learns that Ornela's khal died when she was sixteen
Jorah and Daario interrupt the pair and take Ornela hostage
Daenerys, unsurprised, cancels Jorah and Daario's plans for escape
Daenerys tells them she has a plan of her own
Daenerys asks Ornela if she is willing to help, calling her Khaleesi instead of khaleen
Ornela reluctantly agrees to help
The khals argue about how to deal with Iggo and Aggo's deaths
Khal Moro defuses the situation by reminding them that Aggo was a member of his khalasar and dismisses the need to find the killer
Daenerys is brought in by the two priestesses she befriended before
Khal Moro says Daenerys should join the dosh khaleen
Another khal expresses interest in making Daenerys his khaleesi
Another khal suggests giving Daenerys to the Wise Masters in exchange for ten thousand horses
Daenerys dismisses all of them
Daenerys reminds them that her husband declared he would lead a Dothraki army across the Narrow Sea to retake the Iron Throne for his khaleesi
Daenerys points out that all they have done since is raid and plunder nearby villages
Daenerys declares they are all petty and weak, and none of them are fit to lead the Dothraki
