Daenerys declares she will lead the Dothraki herself
Moro and the khals burst into laughter
Moro, disgusted with Daenerys's insolence, decrees she will be raped by the khals, their bloodriders, and then by their horses if alive
Moro mocks the notion of serving Daenerys
Daenerys smiles and seems to relish the thought
Daenerys calmly clamps her hand on the basket of a burning brazier without flinching
The khals' expressions turn to amazement and fear as they realize Daenerys's true identity
Daenerys informs them in a flat tone that they are going to die
Daenerys topples the brazier, setting the floor of the hut alight in seconds
The khals desperately attempt to escape the rapidly spreading fire
The khals find the temple's single door barred, with the bodies of the two men guarding it lying dead outside
Khal Moro is the only one left and turns to face Daenerys
Daenerys pushes the last brazier over in Khal Moro's direction
Khal Moro is engulfed by flames and screams briefly before dying
flames climb higher
dosh khaleen and khalasars gather outside in confusion, watching the temple burn
temple doors collapse
Daenerys emerges, her clothes burnt off but otherwise untouched by the flames
entire leadership present at Vaes Dothrak is incinerated
crowd of thousands bow to Daenerys almost immediately, awed, horrified, and fearful
High Priestess and the rest of the dosh khaleen bow to Daenerys after a moment
Jorah and Daario move to the front of the crowd and bow as well
Jorah, having seen this before, is less surprised
Daario is utterly awestruck

The_Door.action: Sansa receives a message bearing the sigil of House Baelish, asking her to a meeting in Mole's Town
Sansa is accompanied by Brienne to the meeting
Sansa furiously confronts Petyr Baelish, asking if he was aware what Ramsay Bolton was capable of
Baelish deflects, saying he has the knights of the Vale waiting at Moat Cailin to aid Sansa
Sansa is mistrustful of Baelish when he claims he had no idea of Ramsay's abusive treatment
Sansa describes her wedding night and what Ramsay did
Littlefinger looks on in silent horror and apologizes to Sansa, offering to protect her now
Sansa doubts Baelish's ability to protect her or himself, threatening to have Brienne kill him
Baelish says he'll do whatever Sansa asks
Sansa tells Baelish to leave and never come back for her
Baelish obeys, but informs Sansa that her great-uncle Brynden Tully has recaptured Riverrun and recommends she seek him out and the remaining loyal Tully forces
Sansa says she already has an army, her brother Jon Snow's army of wildlings
Baelish clarifies "Half-brother" as he walks away
a war council is called at Castle Black, attended by Sansa, Brienne, Podrick, Jon, Davos, Melisandre, Tormund, and Eddison Tollett
the council discusses the need for more men to defend Castle Black, since the Karstarks and Umbers have declared for Ramsay
Jon points out they can summon the rest of the minor Houses to rival their enemies
Sansa states that "the North remembers" and the people are still loyal to the Starks
Sansa reveals Brynden's successful retaking of Riverrun, claiming she learned via one of Ramsay's ravens
Sansa tasks Brienne and Podrick with securing Brynden's help
the rest of the group leaves Castle Black to start building their army
Sansa presents Jon with a new cloak like their father's, carrying the Stark sigil
The Waif continues to drill Arya and mocks her high-born origins
Jaqen H'ghar explains that the Faceless Men were slaves in Valyria before establishing Braavos and the House of Black and White
Jaqen hands Arya a vial
Jaqen tells Arya that Lady Crane will be the next to receive the Many-Faced God’s “gift”
Arya enjoys the spectacle of the actors re-enacting the War of the Five Kings, playing Baratheons and Lannisters
Arya’s pleasure ceases when her father and his execution are inaccurately caricatured
Eddard is portrayed as a buffoon in the play
The actress playing Sansa bares her breasts to the audience
Arya sneaks into the dressing room after the play to observe her target, the actress playing Cersei
The actress playing Cersei appears to be a clever, decent woman
Arya shares with Jaqen her plan to poison Lady Crane’s rum, which no one else in the troupe drinks
Arya suspects that a jealous younger actress, Bianca, has commissioned the kill
Jaqen cuts Arya off, reminding her a servant does not question
Daenerys is unsure of what to do with Jorah, having banished him twice, seeing him return twice, and him twice saving her life
Jorah confesses his love for Daenerys
Jorah reveals his spreading greyscale infection
Jorah says he needs to leave for good
Jorah starts to leave and states his plan to take his own life before the greyscale envelops him
Daenerys tearfully commands Jorah to find a cure and come back to her side when she conquers Westeros
Jorah departs
Daenerys and Daario Naharis lead the Dothraki horde back to Meereen
Tyrion reminds the rulers of Meereen of the need to convince the Meereenese that everything has been done with Daenerys's blessing
Tyrion warns that the Masters could use his and Varys's foreign status against them to reclaim Slaver's Bay
Tyrion invites Kinvara, High Priestess of the Red Temple of Volantis, to negotiate spreading the word of Daenerys's accomplishments
Kinvara appears highly accommodating in supporting Tyrion's goals, believing Daenerys is the Prince That Was Promised
Varys is skeptical and points out that Melisandre had already declared Stannis Baratheon to be the Prince, only for him to be defeated twice
Kinvara explains that the Lord of Light has a plan, but humans following him occasionally make errors
Kinvara unnerves Varys by revealing she knows Varys was emasculated by a "second-rate sorcerer" and offers to repeat the words he heard in the flames and identify who spoke
Varys is unsettled by Kinvara's revelation
Kinvara assures Tyrion that she will send the preachers and priests best suited to the task
Yara Greyjoy is the first to lay claim to the Salt Throne at the kingsmoot on Old Wyk
Yara argues that raiding the mainland is not enough and calls for military force against the mainlanders
An Ironborn man challenges Yara's candidacy on the grounds that she is a woman and points out Theon Greyjoy's return
Theon endorses Yara and urges the gathering to support her, emphasizing her qualities as a warrior, reaver, and Ironborn
Many shout for Yara to be their Queen
Euron Greyjoy joins the gathering to lay claim to the Salt Throne
Euron openly mocks Theon for his military failures and emasculation
Euron derides Yara as a woman
Yara accuses Euron of killing their King and her father Balon
Euron admits to killing Balon
Euron defends his actions by claiming Balon was leading them to defeat in the North
Euron states his only regret is not killing Balon years earlier
The crowd cheers for Euron
Theon counters that Euron spent years overseas while Yara commanded Ironborn ships and men
Yara suggests building a massive fleet to attack and make their mark on Westeros
Euron announces support for expanding the Ironborn fleet
Euron proposes to marry Daenerys Targaryen and carry her army and dragons back to Westeros
The Ironborn support Euron's plan and declare him king
Aeron Greyjoy leads Euron down to the sea to be drowned and reborn, as is the custom
Yara, Theon, and their loyalists flee the Iron Islands during Euron's coronation, taking much of the Iron Fleet with them
Euron revives himself and is crowned by his brother Aeron
Euron learns that his niece and nephew have fled with their best ships
Euron calls on his followers to build their own ships to pursue and kill Yara and Theon
Brynden Rivers shows Bran a vision of a heart tree amid spirals of standing stones in a lush green valley
Bran sees Leaf and other Children of the Forest talking amongst themselves
Leaf and the Children of the Forest look eagerly at a captive bound to the tree
Leaf approaches the captive and forces a dragonglass dagger into the captive's chest
The captive screams but does not die
The captive becomes the first White Walker
Bran confronts Leaf about creating the White Walkers
Leaf explains they created the White Walkers because they were at war with the First Men and were desperate
Bran is the only one in the cave awake and feels anxious to warg into the weirwood tree
Bran wargs into the tree by himself
Bran sees the Army of the Dead
Bran walks through the army until he comes to a space occupied by four White Walkers, including the Night King
The Night King and the dead become aware of Bran and are able to see him
The Night King grabs Bran’s arm
Bran breaks out of the vision with a scream, waking the others
Brynden tells Bran, Meera, and Hodor that the Night King can now locate Bran, who is marked on his right forearm, and can bypass the magic protecting the cave
Brynden says they must leave immediately and Bran must become the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran asks if he is ready
Brynden says "No"
Bran’s eyes whiten as he enters another vision
Meera and Hodor prepare to leave
Meera and Hodor notice the air is so cold their breath is visible, indicating the arrival of the army of the dead
The Night King and three White Walkers start marching towards the cave
The Children of the Forest try using magic projectiles to fend the Walkers off, but are overwhelmed
The Children of the Forest light a fire around the entrance to prevent the wights from entering
The Walkers extinguish a pathway through the fire and walk into the cave
The wights climb over the Weirwood and drop through roots at the top of the cave
Meera desperately tries to get Bran out of the vision
Meera attempts to get the frightened Hodor to carry Bran away, but fails
Meera kills the first White Walker that enters with a dragonglass-tipped spear
Meera fights alongside the remaining Children of the Forest to fend off the wights until Bran wakes up
Wights begin to swarm the cave, killing all of the Children of the Forest except for Leaf
Meera starts yelling at Bran to warg into Hodor
Bran is at Winterfell watching his father say goodbye to Rickard Stark before being sent to the Vale as a ward
Bran hears Meera's cries to warg into Hodor while still in the vision
Meera cries out to Bran to warg into Hodor
Brynden tells Bran to listen to Meera
Bran wargs into present-day Hodor in the cave
Bran wargs into Wylis, the young version of Hodor in his vision
Hodor puts Bran's body on a sled and hauls him down a tunnel leading out of the cave with Leaf and Meera
Bran's direwolf Summer is killed attacking the wights
The wights close in on Hodor, Bran, Leaf, and Meera
Leaf sacrifices herself, using magic to cause a huge explosion
The explosion buys Hodor, Bran, and Meera enough time to get to a door at the end of the tunnel
Brynden and Bran share a vision
The Night King kills Brynden
Brynden's figure within Bran's vision blows away as ashes and rags
Hodor pushes the door open and pulls Bran through
Hodor comes back to help Meera close the door as the wights approach
Meera grabs Bran's sled and tells Hodor to bar the exit, shouting "Hold the door!"
Wylis (young Hodor) notices Bran inside Bran's vision
Bran links present Hodor to past Wylis
Wylis begins to seize when he enters the mind of his future self
Meera continues to yell "Hold the door!" to present-day Hodor
Wylis falls to the ground convulsing and starts repeating "Hold the door!" during his seizure
"Hold the door" slurs into "Hodor" as Wylis's condition deteriorates
It becomes apparent this is the moment Hodor lost the ability to say any other words, and his destiny was fixed
Hodor braces himself against the door
Meera disappears into the snow with Bran's unconscious body on the sled
wights break through parts of the wood
wights begin scratching and stabbing a visibly distressed Hodor
young Wylis in the past seizes and cries out "Hodor"

Blood_of_My_Blood.action: Meera pulls Bran through the snow as he remains in vision-state
Meera becomes tired
Bran experiences a rush of visions: King Aerys shouting "Burn them all!"; the Red Wedding, his family, wights in Hardhome, wildfire, dragons, Jaime Lannister and the Iron Throne
Pursuing wights are seen
Meera cannot go on any further and tries to wake Bran
Bran comes out of his visions and says "They've found us."
Meera hears wights approaching through the trees
Meera, with no more energy to pull Bran and barely enough to draw her sword, apologises to Bran for failing him
Wights come into sight
A mysterious rider appears as all seems hopeless
The rider attacks the wights with a sickle and a flaming flail
The rider deals with the wights in the immediate vicinity
The rider urges Meera and Bran to come with him because "the dead don't rest"
Meera and Bran escape on horseback with the rider
The group reaches relative safety
The rider prepares a meal
Meera asks the rider why he helped them
The rider reveals he was sent by the former Three-Eyed Raven who "lives again"
Bran wakes up as the rider says this

The rider explains he was stabbed in the gut by a White Walker's sword and left to die
The Children of the Forest rescue the rider and stop the magic that would have turned him into a Wight
He explains that Bran must now be the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran will be waiting for the White Walkers when they come to the realms of men
Sam and Gilly approach Horn Hill, the seat of House Tarly
Sam warns Gilly that they must claim Little Sam is Sam's son
Sam tells Gilly to hide the fact that she is a wildling
Sam explains that his father abhors wildlings
Sam introduces Gilly and Little Sam to his mother, Lady Melessa Tarly, and sister, Lady Talla Tarly

Melessa is delighted to meet her first grandchild
Talla tries to tell Sam about her upcoming betrothal
Melessa shushes Talla
Talla takes Gilly under her wing, offering her a dress and a spare bedroom
They all have dinner with Sam's father, Lord Randyll Tarly, and brother, Dickon Tarly
Sam reveals his plans to become a maester and eventually return to Castle Black
Randyll criticises Sam for his weight
Randyll mocks Sam's attempt to claim hunting and fighting skills
Sam admits the hunting and fighting skills actually belong to Gilly and Jon Snow, respectively
Talla is delighted to hear that Gilly can hunt for herself

Sam and Gilly wince at Talla's suggestion
Gilly defends Sam from Randyll's verbal assault
Gilly mentions Sam's heroism on three different occasions
Gilly inadvertently reveals her origins from North of the Wall
Randyll ignores Gilly's point about Sam's qualities
Randyll realizes that Gilly is a wildling
Randyll reveals his prejudice towards wildlings and his contempt for Sam
Melessa, angered by Randyll's behavior, declares that Sam isn't the one dishonoring House Tarly, but Randyll is
Melessa storms out, taking Talla and Gilly with her
Randyll agrees that Gilly and Little Sam can stay at Horn Hill
Randyll decides Gilly will work in the kitchens and Little Sam will be acknowledged and raised as a bastard
Randyll declares this will be Sam's last night at Horn Hill, forever
Sam apologises to Gilly for not standing up to his father and says he will be leaving at first light
Sam returns to Gilly, declaring he has changed his mind
Sam says they need to stay together and that they are all leaving right now
Sam takes Heartsbane, the family's Valyrian steel sword, on the way out
Tommen speaks with the High Sparrow about Margaery's Walk of Atonement
The High Sparrow offers to let Tommen see Margaery
Tommen enters Margaery's cell
Tommen is surprised to find Margaery speaking positively about the High Sparrow and contritely about her past sins
Margaery speaks positively about the High Sparrow and contritely about her past sins, including her vanity about being seen to help the poor and needy
Tommen finds himself agreeing with Margaery's feelings towards the High Sparrow
Mace Tyrell leads an army through the streets of King's Landing to a waiting Jaime Lannister
Mace Tyrell gives a rather pompous speech
Jaime cringes at Mace Tyrell's speech
Mace Tyrell and Jaime proceed to the Great Sept of Baelor
The High Sparrow, Septa Unella, the Faith Militant, and a large crowd prepare for Margaery's Walk of Atonement outside the Sept

The High Sparrow refuses and fends off Jaime's threats to kill all the Sparrows

The Kingsguard now have a seven-pointed star sigil on their breastplates instead of the usual crown

The crowd bursts into cheers after Tommen's speech
The High Sparrow now has the support of both the crown and the smallfolk
Mace asks what is happening, baffled
Olenna replies in disgust that the High Sparrow has beaten them
Jaime seethes at the High Sparrow's triumphant expression, furious
Tommen strips Jaime of his position in the Kingsguard
Tommen states that "an attack on the faith is an attack on the crown"
Tommen reveals Jaime will not have to face imprisonment or humiliation
Tommen tells Jaime he can continue to serve his King and the Lannisters, just not in King's Landing
Jaime complains to Cersei that he has been ordered to help Walder Frey recapture Riverrun
Jaime intends to have Bronn find as many hired killers as possible
Jaime plans to march into the Great Sept and kill the High Sparrow
Cersei tells Jaime he should go to Riverrun and show everyone how easily a Lannister can take a castle

Cersei says she does not need Jaime in the city for her trial, as it will be a trial by combat and she has the Mountain
Cersei reassures Jaime they will still defeat all their enemies
Jaime and Cersei kiss
Jaime and Cersei kiss
Arya watches the second half of the mummers' performance of The Bloody Hand
Caricature version of Tyrion poisons his nephew and murders his father in the play
Arya displays amusement at the rendition of Joffrey's painful death
The crowd watches Joffrey's death mournfully
Arya is moved by Lady Crane's performance as Cersei

Arya sneaks backstage and poisons Lady Crane's rum, hesitating before doing so
Arya remains backstage as the mummers come in
Lady Crane recognises Arya as having been in the audience earlier
Arya and Lady Crane converse and Arya realises she has much in common with the actor
Arya suggests to Lady Crane that Cersei's final speech is out of character for the real Queen Mother
Lady Crane is impressed with Arya's insight, compliments her, and asks if she enjoys pretending to be someone else
Arya appears uncomfortable or unsettled by the question
Arya makes an excuse that her father is waiting for her and leaves
Lady Crane argues with Izembaro about improving the script
Lady Crane is about to drink her rum
Arya reappears and knocks the rum from Lady Crane's hand
Arya points at Bianca and warns Lady Crane that Bianca wants her dead
Arya runs out

The Waif reports back to Jaqen

Arya retrieves Needle
Arya demonstrates she has forsaken her desire to become a Faceless Man and is embracing her destiny as Arya Stark
Arya hides in a small room
Arya blows out her candle
Arya waits in darkness for the retribution she knows is coming
Walder Frey receives word that Riverrun is lost, retaken by Brynden Tully
Walder Frey is flabbergasted and blames his sons for allowing the Blackfish to escape the Red Wedding

Lothar Frey and Walder Rivers inform Walder Frey that Houses Mallister and Blackwood have risen against House Frey and the Brotherhood Without Banners are rallying the smallfolk and raiding their supply lines and camps
Walder Frey demands that the Tully stronghold be taken back, refusing to be humiliated for failing to hold a castle from rebels
Walder Frey decides to use his trump card to force the Tullys' surrender: Edmure Tully, held as a prisoner since the Red Wedding
Walder Frey brings Edmure Tully into his hall
Walder Frey jokingly tells Edmure Tully to cheer up as he is going home
Daenerys rides with Daario Naharis and her newly acquired khalasar of 100,000

Daenerys tells the khalasar to halt and rides on ahead on her white horse

The shadow of a dragon passes overhead, stopping Daario

Daenerys asks the khalasar if they are willing to cross the Narrow Sea, defeat the armies of the Seven Kingdoms, and rip down the keeps of its lords

Daenerys declares the entire khalasar her bloodriders

The_Broken_Man.action: Sandor Clegane is revealed to have survived his wounds from his battle with Brienne
Sandor lives with a band of villagers
Brother Ray, the leader and a septon, talks with Sandor
Brother Ray recounts how he saved Sandor's life after mistaking him for a corpse
Ray mentions justice
Sandor remarks that if there was justice in the world, he should have been punished
Ray responds that perhaps Sandor has already been punished
A trio of men from the Brotherhood Without Banners arrives at the gathering and attempts to extort the group
The trio leaves after finding out the group has no worthwhile possessions
Sandor warns the septon that the Brotherhood will return and advises Ray to defend himself
Ray tells Sandor that "Violence is a disease—you don’t cure it by spreading it to more people."
Sandor replies that you don't cure it by dying either
Sandor goes out to the forest to chop wood
Sandor returns to find all of the villagers murdered and the septon hanged
Sandor, angered, picks up an axe and heads off
Jaime and Bronn lead the Lannister army to the gates of Riverrun
The Freys attempt to coerce the Blackfish into surrendering the castle by threatening to execute Edmure Tully
The Blackfish calls the Freys' bluff and refuses to surrender
Jaime, disgusted with the Freys' incompetence, takes charge of the siege
Jaime attempts to parley with the Blackfish, warning him that the Lannisters will show no mercy to the Tullys, but if he surrenders, the lives of his men will be spared

The Blackfish warns Jaime that he has two years worth of food in his stronghold, and that while hundreds of his own men may die defending, thousands of Lannister troops will perish as well
Margaery studies The Seven-Pointed Star at the Sept
High Sparrow enters the Sept
Margaery and High Sparrow discuss the passage about the Mother's love and mercy

High Sparrow assures Margaery that desire is not necessary and reminds her of her duty to produce an heir

Margaery returns to the Red Keep

Margaery comes to Unella's defense

Olenna tries to convince Margaery to leave King's Landing and return to Highgarden
Margaery refuses, stating her duty requires her to be at King Tommen's side
Margaery pleads for Olenna to return to Highgarden

Margaery communicates to Olenna that she doesn't mean what she's saying in front of Septa Unella
Olenna's expression changes as she realizes Margaery's message

Olenna finds that there is a rose drawn on it
Olenna seems cheered by the silent message
Olenna understands that the use of the Tyrell sigil indicates Margaery is tricking the Sparrows and her true loyalty is to House Tyrell

Olenna refuses Cersei, noting that Cersei has neither influence nor support and is surrounded by enemies

Jon, Sansa, and Davos begin searching for allies to retake Winterfell from Ramsay
Tormund and Wun Wun help Jon, Sansa, and Davos secure the allegiance of the wildlings and their elders led by Dim Dalba

Jon asks Tormund if he’s sure the Free Folk will join him

Jon, Sansa, and Davos travel to Bear Island

Sansa and Jon try to flatter Lyanna with small talk about her mother Maege and uncle Jeor Mormont
Lyanna is unimpressed and aggressively brushes them off, demanding to know their business
Lyanna initially rejects their request for help and stresses that House Stark is dead and she needs her forces to garrison Bear Island

Lyanna is initially unconcerned about the threat of Ramsay
Before Lyanna can dismiss them, Davos intervenes
Davos briefly discusses his background
Davos convinces Lyanna by warning her about the dangers the White Walkers pose to the living

Jon and Sansa receive a frosty reception from Robett Glover
Robett Glover points out that the late King Robb failed to protect his home from the Ironborn
Robett Glover recalls that Robb did not come to his aid when the Ironborn invaded Deepwood Motte, imprisoned his wife and children, and brutalized his subjects
Sansa tries to highlight that the Glovers had pledged fealty to House Stark
Robett responds that he received them out of respect for their father
Robett warns Jon and Sansa that they have outstayed their welcome
Jon and Sansa recruit only three minor houses: House Mormont, House Hornwood, and House Mazin, adding 405 soldiers to their army
Lyanna and her men are seen among the Stark and wildling forces
Davos deals with a brawl among the wildlings and Northern soldiers
Jon insists on attacking Winterfell as soon as possible before Ramsay rallies more forces and before the weather turns
Sansa disagrees and opts to try recruiting more houses, specifically House Cerwyn
Jon refuses to change his mind about attacking
Sansa begins writing a letter to Littlefinger, taking him up on his offer for troops
Theon and Yara take the Iron Fleet to Volantis to hide and rest
Theon sits despondently inside a brothel
Yara and the others carouse with naked and semi-naked prostitutes
Yara repeatedly tells Theon to drink some ale
Theon repeatedly refuses to drink ale
Yara persuades Theon to regain his former identity and self-confidence
Yara tells Theon she will need his assistance in getting justice and retaking the Iron Islands from Euron
Theon says that if justice were served, he would be burnt for his crimes
Yara responds, "Fuck justice, then. We'll get revenge."
Yara convinces Theon to put his guilt aside and help her because she needs him
Theon drinks some ale
Theon begins to gain some composure
She reveals to Theon that she plans to take the Iron Fleet to Meereen and forge an alliance with Daenerys before Euron does
She goes to have sex with a female prostitute

Arya approaches two Westerosi traders drinking at an outdoor table

Arya negotiates passage to Westeros at dawn using large bags of money

An old woman approaches Arya

The Waif repeatedly stabs Arya in the gut, twisting the knife
Arya escapes by leaping over the handrail and into the river
Arya is left stumbling through the streets of Braavos, leaving a trail of blood droplets and visibly afraid

No_One.action: The acting troupe performs The Bloody Hand with Lady Crane playing Cersei mourning Joffrey’s death
The play is edited as Arya proposed, with Lady Crane’s Cersei promising revenge
Lady Crane walks off stage and sees Arya nursing her injuries
Arya seeks refuge with Lady Crane

Lady Crane asks Arya to join the play company going to Pentos
Arya warns Lady Crane about coming dangers
Lady Crane tries to give Arya milk of the poppy
Arya rejects the milk of the poppy at first
Arya accepts the milk of the poppy after being told sleep is needed for healing
Lady Crane stands on a chair to fetch medicine from a high shelf
Lady Crane sees a man in her room
Arya wakes up, startled by a crash
Arya finds Lady Crane brutally murdered, impaled onto the legs of the chair
The man is revealed to be the Waif, who rebukes Arya for not carrying out her mission
The Waif tells Arya that Lady Crane could have died less violently and now she has come to kill Arya
Arya leaps out the window and flees into the streets
Arya rolls under a wagon and flees into a bathhouse
The Waif chases Arya, closing in on her
Arya barely manages to stay ahead and rolls down a broad flight of stairs, overturning several baskets of vegetables
Several baskets of vegetables are overturned
Arya lands hard and her wound reopens
Arya flees into an alleyway
Arya uses her bloody hand to leave a trail to a doorway, luring the Waif into a darkened room lit by a candle
The Waif catches up with Arya

Arya rises to her feet

Arya slices the top of the candle off with Needle
The room is plunged into total darkness
Jaqen enters the Hall of Faces and finds a trail of blood
Jaqen discovers Arya has murdered the Waif and ripped off her face, placing it on a shelf
Arya gouges out the Waif’s eyes with her sword
Arya confronts Jaqen
Jaqen admits sending the Waif to kill Arya
Jaqen tells Arya she has become "No One" and passed the test to join the Faceless Men
Arya corrects Jaqen, declaring she is Arya Stark of Winterfell and is going home

Arya walks away
red priestesses preach that Daenerys has been sent by the Lord of Light to end slavery
Tyrion and Varys enter the marketplace
Tyrion and Varys debate the merits of Tyrion's hiring of "fanatics" to keep order in Meereen
Varys prepares to leave on a secret expedition because Daenerys needs friends in Westeros
Varys parts with Tyrion before reaching the docks to keep his expedition secret
Tyrion corrects Varys, saying he is "the most famous dwarf in the world."
Tyrion meets up with his advisors Grey Worm and Missandei at the Great Pyramid of Meereen
Tyrion offers to share drinks with Grey Worm and Missandei to celebrate the resurgence of the city
Grey Worm and Missandei express reluctance to drink
Tyrion convinces them to drink in honor of their Queen
Grey Worm complains about the taste of the wine
Tyrion boasts about starting his own vineyard once Daenerys conquers the Seven Kingdoms
Tyrion asks Grey Worm and Missandei to tell jokes
Grey Worm and Missandei admit they do not know how to tell jokes
Tyrion makes a joke about Westeros Lords
Grey Worm expresses puzzlement and does not understand the joke
Missandei explains what a joke is
Missandei realizes she is enjoying the wine and tells her own joke about two translators
Tyrion feigns amusement at Missandei's joke
Grey Worm says it was the worst joke he has ever heard
The others react with confusion
Grey Worm reveals he was making his own joke
Missandei laughs and Grey Worm smiles
Tyrion prepares to tell another joke
They hear the sound of bombardment
They look out the ramparts and see a large fleet of ships from Astapor, Yunkai, and Volantis
Missandei tells Tyrion that the slavers have come to reclaim their property
By evening, the slavers' fleet use trebuchets to bombard the city, causing much damage to Meereen
Tyrion admits that diplomacy has failed and lets Grey Worm take command of the battle
Grey Worm proposes defending the pyramid instead of fighting on the beach
They hear movement on the roofs
They venture out onto the balcony
They discover that Dany has returned with her dragon Drogon
Qyburn informs Cersei that the Faith Militant have been permitted entry to the Red Keep and demand to see her
Lancel, among the Faith Militant, tells Cersei that the High Sparrow would like to speak to her in the Sept of Baelor
Cersei refuses to go with Lancel
Lancel warns Cersei that it is a command, not a request
Cersei retorts that the High Sparrow promised she could stay in the Red Keep until her trial
Lancel replies that no such promise was made
Ser Gregor Clegane threatens the Faith Militant
Lancel tells Cersei to order Gregor aside or there will be violence
Cersei refuses to let the Kingsguard stand down
One of the Faith Militant attacks Gregor in the chest, penetrating his armor
Gregor seems unharmed

Lancel and his men are forced to stand down
Cersei, Qyburn, and Ser Gregor enter the throne room and find a large crowd gathered for a royal announcement
Cersei asks Kevan Lannister why she was not informed about the announcement
Kevan bars Cersei from standing beside her son and tells her to go to the gallery with the other ladies of the court
Cersei reluctantly takes her place in the gallery
The other women hurry aside, not wanting to be near Cersei or Gregor
King Tommen Baratheon says that the Crown and the Faith are the two pillars that hold up the world, and if one collapses, so does the other
King Tommen says that the Father judges everyone, and if they break his laws, they will be punished
King Tommen announces that Loras Tyrell and Cersei's trial will be held in the Sept of Baelor on the first day of the Festival of the Mother
He prays and reflects
He announces that trial by combat will be forbidden in the Seven Kingdoms
He calls trial by combat a scheme made by those who want to escape true judgement from the Gods
He declares that Loras and Cersei will stand trial before seven septons as in the earlier days of the Faith
The King leaves
Qyburn tells a shocked Cersei that his little birds have been investigating an old rumor she told him about
Cersei had been planning on calling for a trial by combat with Gregor as her champion
Qyburn reveals that the rumor was found to be much more than a rumor
Several men including Gatins and Morgan, members of the Brotherhood Without Banners, exchange jokes over a campsite fire
The men joke about sex
Sandor Clegane sets upon the four men with an axe, seeking revenge for the murder of villagers
Sandor kills two men before they can react
Sandor dispatches Morgan after a brief scuffle
Sandor buries his axe in Gatins' groin, spilling his innards on the ground
Sandor demands to know where the one with the yellow cloak is
Gatins responds with "Fuck you!"
Sandor points out those are terrible last words and gives Gatins a chance to do better
Gatins calls Sandor a "c***"
Sandor remarks that Gatins is "shit at dying" before cutting him down
Sandor stumbles upon a forest clearing
Sandor finds seven members of the Brotherhood, including Beric Dondarrion and Thoros of Myr, preparing to hang Lem and two other renegades
The three condemned men stand on blocks with nooses around their necks
Sandor explains the men killed his friend and requests to kill them himself
Sandor wants to inflict a harsher death than hanging
Beric stops Sandor from inflicting a harsher death
Beric agrees to let Sandor kill two of the men out of respect for his loss
Thoros refuses to let Sandor butcher the men
Sandor complains, saying he would have killed all seven in the past
Sandor ultimately agrees to let the men hang
Sandor mutters that hanging is too quick
Sandor kicks the blocks from under the feet of two men, including Lem
Sandor takes Lem’s boots as the body is still twitching
Beric kicks out the block of the third man
Sandor joins the Brotherhood for a meal by a creek
Beric suggests Sandor should join the Brotherhood, saying they are fighting for a greater cause and that "cold winds are rising in the North"
Sandor is skeptical of the Brotherhood's chances of success and distrusts them due to his earlier acrimonious encounter
Beric recognizes Sandor as a warrior and tells him they are fighting against things that seek to destroy all life
Beric tells Sandor it is not too late to help
Brienne and Podrick travel to Riverrun to deliver Sansa's letter to Brynden Tully
Brienne and Podrick arrive at Riverrun and discover the Lannisters and Freys are besieging the castle
Brienne and Podrick are surrounded by several Lannister horsemen
Brienne tells the horsemen she has come to see Ser Jaime Lannister and to return his sword, Oathkeeper
Brienne speaks with Jaime
Podrick waits in the camp
Podrick is put in a choke-hold by an unseen figure, revealed to be Bronn
Bronn is jubilant to see Podrick and playfully ribs him about sex and his fighting skills
Bronn offers to give Podrick a lesson in dirty fighting, instead of the formal kind Brienne has been teaching him
Jaime and Brienne meet discreetly in a tent
Brienne informs Jaime that she has found Sansa, but their situation is complicated by a bounty Cersei has placed on Sansa's head
Cersei places a bounty on Sansa's head
Brienne tells Jaime she has come to secure Brynden's help in seeking reinforcements to retake Winterfell from the Boltons
Jaime is laying siege to Riverrun, making Brynden's help unavailable
Brienne convinces Jaime to let her negotiate with the Blackfish so Brynden and his men can leave the castle at night to travel to Winterfell
