Brienne tries to return Oathkeeper to Jaime, believing her use for it is fulfilled
Jaime tells Brienne to keep Oathkeeper, saying it is hers
Brienne reminds Jaime that if she fails to get the Blackfish to surrender, her oath to Lady Catelyn Stark compels her to fight against him
Jaime, moved by admiration, replies, "Let's hope it doesn't come to that."
Brienne and Podrick enter Riverrun to negotiate with the Blackfish
Brynden refuses to relinquish his ancestral seat
Brienne tells Brynden of her oath to aid his grand-niece Sansa
Brienne shows Brynden Sansa's letter
Brynden is sympathetic to his grand-niece but is unable to help
Brienne tells Podrick to get a maester to fetch a raven and send a message to Sansa about her failure to secure help
Jaime visits the captive Edmure Tully and apologizes for the conduct of the Freys
Edmure remains defiant and insists that his uncle will not surrender Riverrun
Jaime informs Edmure that he has sired a son with Roslin Tully
Jaime offers Edmure and his family comfortable exile at Casterly Rock if Edmure convinces the Blackfish and the garrison to surrender
Jaime offers to give Edmure's son a proper education and some lands when he comes of age
Edmure denounces Jaime as an evil man
Jaime responds that he would let the gods decide
Edmure asks Jaime how he sleeps at night after all the bad things he has done
Jaime recounts his time as a captive of Lady Stark and how she struck him with a stone
Jaime comes to respect Catelyn for her undying love for her children
Jaime compares Catelyn's love for her children to Cersei Lannister's love for her own
Jaime reiterates that he loves Cersei so much that he would kill any Tully that stands in his way
Jaime issues his final ultimatum to Edmure: if the Tullys do not surrender, he will catapult Edmure's son over the wall and kill every Tully who stands between him and Cersei
Jaime leaves to let Edmure make up his mind
Edmure seemingly accepts Jaime's offer and walks back to the castle with a torch
Edmure announces himself as their rightful lord and convinces the guards to lay down the drawbridge despite Brynden's protests
Brynden protests Edmure's actions
guards obey Edmure because of their oath of fealty to Hoster Tully and his son
guards lower the drawbridge
Edmure enters the castle
Lothar Frey criticizes Jaime for losing their most valuable hostage
Edmure climbs the ramparts and orders the Tully garrison to lower the drawbridge and surrender to the Lannisters and Freys
Jaime and the Lannister and Frey hosts march into Riverrun and occupy the castle
Lannister and Frey banners are draped all over the walls
Edmure orders his men to find and put the Blackfish in chains and hand him over to the Freys
The Blackfish escapes before he can be captured
The Blackfish helps Brienne and Podrick into a boat at one of the castle's exits
Brienne tries to convince the Blackfish to come along
The Blackfish insists on not running away from the fight
The Blackfish tells Brienne to continue serving Sansa and assures her she will do a better job than he has done
Brienne and Podrick escape by boat
The Blackfish stays behind for a final stand against several Lannister soldiers
Jaime is informed of the Blackfish's death on the ramparts
Jaime sees Brienne and Podrick rowing away on their boat
Jaime quietly waves goodbye
Brienne waves back

Battle_of_the_Bastards_(episode).action: Daenerys and Tyrion discuss a plan to deal with the Slaver fleet
The pyramid is bombarded with flaming cannonballs
Daenerys wants to slaughter the slaver army
Tyrion suggests talking to the masters about terms of surrender
Tyrion reminds Daenerys about her father's plan to destroy King's Landing with Wildfire and Jaime Lannister killing the Mad King
Daenerys, Tyrion, Missandei, and Grey Worm meet with the masters: Razdal mo Eraz, Belicho Paenymion, and Yezzan zo Qaggaz
The masters tell Daenerys they will let her and Tyrion leave if they hand over the Unsullied and Missandei, and allow her dragons to be slaughtered
Daenerys tells the masters she is offering them a chance to surrender
Drogon flies in to meet Daenerys
Daenerys rides Drogon into the bay
Rhaegal and Viserion break free from captivity beneath the Great Pyramid to join Daenerys and Drogon
The dragons begin to burn a few ships in the attacking fleet
Daario arrives at Meereen with Daenerys's Dothraki horde
Daario and the Dothraki rout the Sons of the Harpy at the city gates
The Masters' soldiers abandon them at Grey Worm's invitation
Tyrion gives an ultimatum that one of the three masters must die as punishment
Yezzan is scapegoated by the other two for not being highborn
Yezzan kneels on the ground and begs Grey Worm not to kill him
Grey Worm slits the throats of Razdal and Belicho with one slash of his dagger
Tyrion tells Yezzan to share what he had seen in Meereen and what Daenerys is capable of
Daenerys and Tyrion meet with Theon and Yara, who have arrived in the city
Theon and Yara offer Daenerys their fleet of a hundred manned ships in exchange for help against Euron and support for their claim to the Iron Islands
Tyrion is wary of Theon based on his actions at Winterfell and his crimes against the Starks
Theon insists he has paid for what he has done
Daenerys agrees to support an independent kingdom of the Iron Islands, on the condition that the Ironborn cease all raiding, reaving, pillaging, and rape
Yara balks at Daenerys’s suggestion
Daenerys says their fathers all left the world worse than they found it, but their alliance can leave it better
Yara is swayed and agrees to Daenerys’s terms
Daenerys and Yara seal their pact
Jon, Sansa, Tormund, Davos, Lyanna, and the other Northern lords have a parley with Ramsay, Harald Karstark, and Smalljon Umber before battle
Ramsay offers surrender terms, promising to pardon Jon and the Northerners if Jon hands over Sansa
Jon refuses Ramsay's terms and offers Ramsay a chance for one-on-one combat
Ramsay rejects the one-on-one combat, expressing confidence in his numerically superior forces
Jon counters that Ramsay's men may not want to fight for him if he will not fight for them
Ramsay threatens Rickon in response
Jon and Sansa are wary of whether Ramsay actually has their brother
Umber throws out Shaggydog's head as proof
Sansa refuses the terms of surrender and tells Ramsay he will die the following day before riding off
Jon discusses the battle plan with Tormund and Davos
Sansa admonishes Jon for attacking too early and insists they should have gathered more men
Jon retorts that this is the largest army they could possibly gather
Sansa warns Jon of Ramsay's cunning and cruel nature
Jon assures Sansa that he has faced worse
Jon promises to protect Sansa from Ramsay
Sansa cynically replies that no one can keep anyone safe
Davos and Tormund discuss their time serving Stannis and Mance, acknowledging they may have served the wrong king
Tormund parts ways with Davos and goes to drink
Davos takes a walk as the sun rises
Davos comes across the pyre where Shireen Baratheon was sacrificed
Davos finds the wooden stag he carved for Shireen in the remains of the pyre
Davos realizes how Shireen died
Jon meets with Melisandre and orders her not to bring him back if he falls in battle
Melisandre contends she will try anyway, saying it is the Lord of Light who decides Jon's fate
Melisandre ponders that the Lord of Light may have brought Jon back only to die in the battle
The armies gather the following morning
Ramsay brings out Rickon
Ramsay menacingly raises a knife
Ramsay cuts Rickon's bonds
Ramsay tells Rickon they are to play a game; the only rule is to run towards his brother
Rickon runs towards Jon
Ramsay pulls out a bow and nocks an arrow
Jon urgently rides out on horseback to try to save Rickon
Ramsay fires arrows at Rickon
Ramsay appears to intentionally miss Rickon with his first shots
Jon approaches Rickon
Rickon is struck in the back with an arrow and killed
Jon is devastated and enraged over his brother's murder
Jon charges at Ramsay and the Bolton forces alone
Davos tells the Stark cavalry to charge
The Bolton archers fire at Jon and strike his horse
Jon is now defenseless in the middle of the battlefield
Ramsay orders the Bolton cavalry to charge at Jon
Jon brandishes Longclaw and prepares to fight to the death
Stark cavalry smashes into the Boltons, narrowly saving Jon from being trampled
Davos belays his order to his archers to release arrows into the melee of infantry
Davos announces that releasing arrows would kill his own men as well as the enemy
Davos eventually sends the archers forward to be of more use in the middle of the battlefield
Ramsay repeatedly and purposefully orders arrow fusillades that kill all combatants in the middle of the field
Ramsay attempts to create a strategic barrier of human death in the center of the battlefield
Stark forces remain unaware of the Bolton strategy and developing trap
Bolton soldiers are ordered forward to create an impenetrable horseshoe formation of tall shields and spears in a phalanx configuration
Bolton soldiers surround three sides of the Stark forces against the death mound and progressively compress them into a helpless mass
Smalljon Umber leads a group of soldiers across the mound of corpses to attack the Stark infantry from behind and complete the envelopment
Wun Wun kills a few Bolton soldiers
Wun Wun fails to break the Bolton phalanx
Panic sets in among the Stark forces
Tormund senses futility and urges his wildlings backward towards the pile and possible escape
Jon is trampled by his own forces and nearly suffocates under a group of men crawling on top of him
Jon is eventually able to get back to his feet
a horn sounds out in the distance as Sansa and Littlefinger arrive with the Knights of the Vale, waving banners bearing the Arryn sigil
Sansa and Littlefinger arrive with the Knights of the Vale
The Knights of the Vale begin to cut down the Bolton soldiers from behind to disrupt the encirclement
Smalljon is momentarily distracted by the arrival of the Arryn forces
Tormund bites out Smalljon's throat
Tormund kills Smalljon, stabbing him multiple times in the face with a dagger
Ramsay sees his soldiers being cut down
Ramsay decides to retreat inside Winterfell
Jon, Wun Wun, and Tormund begin to give chase
Ramsay’s general insists the battle is lost
Ramsay assures his general they still have Winterfell and the Stark Army is too weak for a siege
Wun Wun breaks down Winterfell’s gates
wildling archers pour into the castle
the last vestiges of the Bolton garrison are overwhelmed and slaughtered
Wun Wun is overwhelmed by arrows and killed when Ramsay shoots him through the eye
Ramsay taunts Jon, saying he has reconsidered one-on-one combat
Jon approaches Ramsay
Ramsay fires three arrows at Jon with his bow
Jon blocks all three arrows with a Mormont shield
Jon picks up the Mormont shield from the ground
Jon gets close enough and smacks the bow out of Ramsay’s hands
Jon strikes Ramsay in the chest and knocks him to the ground
Jon pounces on Ramsay and savagely beats him with his fists
Ramsay does not attempt to defend himself and grins maniacally through the beating
Jon continues brutally pounding Ramsay
Jon notices Sansa
Jon realizes Sansa has as much right for revenge as he does
Jon stops beating Ramsay and orders him locked up as a prisoner
The Bolton banners on Winterfell are torn down
The Stark banners are raised in their place
Melisandre looks over the courtyard
Davos glares at Melisandre from below, clutching Shireen's burned stag
Jon orders Rickon's body to be buried next to his father in the crypts
Sansa demands Jon tell her where he is keeping Ramsay prisoner
Sansa goes to Ramsay's kennels to confront him
Ramsay is lashed to a chair within the common area of the kennels
Ramsay goads Sansa, telling her she will never be rid of him because he is "part of her now"
Sansa retorts that Ramsay's deeds and names will disappear from future memory
Ramsay realizes his hounds are slowly approaching him
Jon deliberately leaves the kennel doors open to where Ramsay is bound
Ramsay, still covered in his own blood, denies his peril and claims his dogs are loyal to him
Sansa reminds Ramsay that he has been starving his dogs for a week
The hounds begin sniffing at Ramsay and licking their chops
Ramsay desperately orders the hounds to heel, but they refuse to listen
The hounds attack and begin to tear Ramsay apart
Sansa walks away, smirking with dark satisfaction as the barking of the hounds mingles with Ramsay's screams

The_Winds_of_Winter.action: Jaime and Bronn arrive at the Twins to celebrate their victory at Riverrun and the alliance between the Freys and the Lannisters
Jaime rides north from King's Landing to retake Riverrun from The Blackfish with the persuasion of Edmure Tully
Walder Frey crows over his victory and tries to build rapport with Jaime, stating that they are both "kingslayers"
Jaime is insulted by the comparison
Jaime angrily asks Walder why the Lannisters need House Frey when they cannot control the Riverlands
Jaime leaves the feast and a dumbstruck Walder
Walder Frey eats dinner in his hall
a servant serves Walder pie
Walder questions the servant's identity and slaps her on the behind
Walder sarcastically wonders if the servant is one of his progeny
Walder complains about the tardiness of his sons, Black Walder and Lothar Frey
the servant insists that Walder's sons are already present and directs his attention to the pie
Walder opens the pie and reacts in horror upon seeing a fingertip in the stuffing
the servant removes her face, revealing herself as Arya Stark
Arya reminds Walder of his crimes and declares her intent for revenge
Arya tells Walder that the last thing he will see is a Stark smiling down at him as he dies
Walder is shocked and tries to escape
Arya seizes and holds Walder down
Arya slits Walder's throat and watches him bleed to death with a satisfied smile
Everyone in King's Landing prepares for the trial of Ser Loras Tyrell and Cersei Lannister
The smallfolk enter the Great Sept of Baelor
Queen Margaery Tyrell, Lord Mace Tyrell, and Ser Kevan Lannister enter the Great Sept of Baelor
Grand Maester Pycelle heads to the sept
A small child stops Grand Maester Pycelle and whispers something into his ear
The Sparrows of the Faith Militant bring Ser Loras in to the High Sparrow
The High Sparrow awaits Loras
Loras confesses to his crimes
Loras tells the High Sparrow he wishes to devote his life to the Faith
The High Sparrow tells Loras he must renounce his family name and lordship
Loras tearfully agrees to renounce his name and lordship
The Sparrows seize Loras and carve a seven-pointed star into his forehead
Mace tries to fight his way through the crowd to stop the Sparrows
Margaery, her voice trembling, holds Mace back
Margaery protests to the High Sparrow for mutilating her brother
The High Sparrow replies that he only promised to allow Loras to leave after Cersei faces her trial
King Tommen prepares to depart the Red Keep for the trial of his mother
Ser Gregor Clegane prevents Tommen from leaving his room
Lancel tells the High Sparrow that Cersei has yet to leave the Red Keep
The High Sparrow asks Lancel to go retrieve Cersei
Arthur, one of Qyburn's "little birds", lures Lancel away to the catacombs below the Sept
Pycelle follows Frances, another little bird, into a room where Qyburn awaits him
Qyburn apologizes to Pycelle for the inconvenience
multiple children surround Pycelle and begin stabbing him repeatedly to death
Margaery realizes something is wrong since Cersei and Tommen are not at the Great Sept for the trial
Margaery confronts the High Sparrow
Margaery tells the High Sparrow that it's a trap
The High Sparrow does not listen to Margaery
Lancel follows Arthur below the Sept
Arthur drops the torch he was carrying
Lancel goes to pick up the torch
A child stabs Lancel in the spine and runs off
Lancel, paralyzed from the waist down, looks around and discovers a cache of wildfire about to be set off with three candles
Lancel desperately crawls toward the candles in an attempt to put them out
Margaery tells everyone in the Sept they need to leave immediately
The Faith Militant block the doors, preventing anyone from exiting
Lancel does not make it to the candles before the wildfire ignites
Wildfire ignites, flooding the tunnels with green flame
The gathered nobles in the Sept hear muffled explosions
The fire punches through the floor, burning the High Sparrow down to his bones
The fire builds to such intensity that the Great Sept shatters and crumbles to rubble, flinging debris and levelling the city for a mile around
Cersei looks out one of the windows in her room and smiles at the explosion
screams of the terrified and dying population shatter the air
Tommen stares at the explosion in shock and disbelief from his chambers
Tommen is informed of the explosion and its casualties, including his wife
Tommen takes his crown off his head and sets it down
Tommen calmly steps out of the window to his death
Cersei pays a visit to Septa Unella, who is unconscious and strapped to a table
Cersei awakens Septa Unella by pouring a pitcher of wine on her face as she struggles
Cersei prompts Septa Unella to confess that she enjoyed tormenting her during her imprisonment
Cersei mocks Septa Unella by "confessing" her own crimes, including incest with Jaime, causing Robert's death, and the massacre in the sept
Cersei reminds Septa Unella of her promise that her face would be the last thing Unella saw before death
Septa Unella declares she is not afraid to die
Cersei assures Septa Unella that her torture will last many days and brings in Ser Gregor Clegane
Ser Gregor Clegane removes his helmet and begins torturing Septa Unella
Cersei walks away, closes the door, and echoes the word "shame" three times
Septa Unella screams in horror at her fate
Tommen's body is found
Cersei's triumph is greatly diminished
Cersei stands by Tommen's covered remains with Qyburn
Cersei insists on seeing her son's face one last time
Qyburn asks Cersei what she wants to do about funeral arrangements
Cersei tells Qyburn to burn Tommen's body and bury the ashes where the Great Sept of Baelor once stood
Jaime, Bronn, and the Lannister army arrive back at King's Landing
Jaime and Bronn witness smoke rising from where the Great Sept and its surrounding districts once stood
Cersei and her Kingsguard enter the throne room, flanked by Lannister guards
Cersei ascends the steps and stands before the Iron Throne
Qyburn proclaims Cersei Queen of the Seven Kingdoms and all associated titles
Qyburn places a crown on Cersei's head
Cersei sits down on the Iron Throne
The crowd of nobles glare at Cersei with undisguised hatred, professing loyalty out of fear
Cersei looks only at Jaime, whose expression is stony
Cersei achieves her ambition to become Queen of the Seven Kingdoms
Sam, Gilly, and her son arrive at Oldtown
Sam, Gilly, and her son spot the Hightower
dozens of white ravens are released from the Hightower
the release of the white ravens signals the arrival of winter
Sam informs the maester he has been sent by the Night's Watch's Lord Commander, Jon Snow, to be trained as a maester
The bureaucrat maintains a deadpan expression and is hesitant because Jeor Mormont and Aemon are recorded as Lord Commander and Maester of Castle Black
Sam awkwardly informs the bureaucrat that Jeor Mormont and Aemon died before ravens could be sent out
The bureaucrat arranges for Sam to meet with one of the Archmaesters
The bureaucrat allows Sam access to the Citadel's library
The bureaucrat bars Gilly and her son from following Sam
Sam explores the library by himself
Sam is in awe of the vast collection of books and scrolls
Benjen drops Meera and Bran off at a grove with a weirwood heart tree in sight of the Wall
Benjen explains he can go no farther because the magic of the Wall prevents the dead from crossing
Bran asks Benjen why he cannot go farther
Benjen replies that the magic of the Wall prevents the dead from crossing, such as himself
Benjen states he plans to prepare for the coming war and still fights for the living
Bran thanks his uncle, who rides off on his horse
Bran turns his attention to the weirwood tree
Meera expresses doubts about Bran’s readiness
Bran insists he must be the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran places his hand on the face carved into the tree
Bran has another vision of the events at the Tower of Joy
Bran does not call after his father in the vision
Eddard turns around briefly before rushing into the Tower
Eddard, followed by Bran, discovers Lyanna Stark lying in bed with blood-stained sheets
Handmaidens tend to Lyanna Stark
One of the handmaidens hands a newborn baby to Eddard
Eddard takes the child in his arms
Lyanna whispers to Eddard about the baby's identity
Lyanna begs Eddard to promise he’ll keep her child safe
Eddard looks at his infant nephew
The baby's eyes open, revealing the dark-colored eyes of Jon Snow
Bran realizes Jon is Lyanna’s son
Jon reminisces in Winterfell's main hall
Melisandre is present in the hall with Jon
Ser Davos angrily strides in and tosses Princess Shireen's burned wooden stag to Melisandre
Ser Davos demands that Melisandre explain herself
Melisandre is silent at first
Ser Davos loses his composure
Melisandre admits she convinced King Stannis Baratheon to sacrifice his daughter to the Lord of Light
Melisandre defends herself, saying it needed to be done because Stannis's forces were stranded by snows and Shireen's parents made the decision as well
Ser Davos dismisses Melisandre's defense, pointing out Stannis and his wife died anyway
Ser Davos exclaims that Melisandre's god is evil and tearfully admits he loved Shireen as his own daughter
Melisandre, fighting back tears, concedes she made a mistake in believing Stannis was the Lord's Chosen
Ser Davos retorts that because of Melisandre's mistake, not only did an innocent child die for a lost cause, but many more good soldiers died as well
Davos demands Jon's leave to execute Melisandre for her actions
Jon learns of Davos's demand
Jon asks Melisandre if she has anything to say for herself
Melisandre replies that she only followed the Lord of Light and implies he brought Jon back to life; she insists she can still be of use against the Night King
Jon tells Melisandre to ride south or he will hang her as a murderer
Davos adds that he will personally execute Melisandre if she ever shows up in the North again
Jon and Sansa look on as Melisandre is leaving Winterfell
Jon tells Sansa he has prepared Eddard and Catelyn's master bedroom for her
Sansa insists Jon should have the master bedroom
Jon remarks that Sansa is the Lady of Winterfell and credits her for their survival and reclaiming Winterfell
Sansa apologizes for not telling Jon about Littlefinger's Knights of the Vale
Jon asks Sansa if she trusts Littlefinger
Sansa firmly denies trusting Littlefinger
Jon tells Sansa they have to trust each other because the Starks have many enemies
Jon gently kisses Sansa on the forehead
Sansa informs Jon that a white raven arrived earlier, signaling that winter has finally arrived
Littlefinger approaches Sansa in the godswood
Sansa asks Littlefinger what he wants from her
Littlefinger admits he wants the Iron Throne with Sansa at his side
Littlefinger moves in to kiss Sansa
Sansa stops Littlefinger and walks away
Littlefinger states he has openly declared for House Stark
Sansa reminds Littlefinger he has declared for other houses in the past and served himself
Littlefinger contests that was the past and says he is looking towards the future, adding Sansa is the future of House Stark
Littlefinger asks Sansa who the North should rally behind: a trueborn daughter of Ned and Catelyn Stark, or a motherless bastard born in the south
Sansa considers Littlefinger’s words but keeps walking without turning to look at him
Lord Yohn Royce speaks against allying the Knights of the Vale with wildling invaders
Tormund counters that the wildlings were invited into the realm and are not invaders
Jon reminds the assembly that the Free Folk fought side by side with the Northmen and the Knights of the Vale and won
Jon adds that his father used to say true friends are found on the battlefield
Lord Cley Cerwyn argues that with the Boltons defeated, the war is over and winter has arrived, the coldest in a thousand years according to the maesters
Lord Cley Cerwyn proposes everyone ride home and wait out the coming storms
Jon counters that the war is not over and the true enemy doesn't wait out the storm, he brings the storm
The assembled begin to argue amongst themselves
Lyanna Mormont stands up and speaks to Lord Wyman Manderly about his son being murdered at the Red Wedding, yet Wyman recently refused the Stark summons
Lyanna Mormont turns to Lord Robett Glover and reminds him he swore an oath to House Stark, yet refused to aid them in their hour of greatest need
Lyanna Mormont confronts Lord Cley Cerwyn and reminds him his father was flayed alive by Ramsay Bolton, yet he also refused to aid the Starks
Lyanna Mormont declares that House Mormont and the North remember they have no king but the King in the North, whose name is Stark
Lyanna Mormont states she doesn't care that Jon Snow is a bastard, for he has the blood of Eddard Stark and is her king until his death
The assembled begin to quietly discuss
Lord Wyman stands up and admits Lady Mormont speaks harshly but truly
Lord Wyman adds that after his son died for the Young Wolf, he ignored Jon's summons to keep more Manderlys from dying for a lost cause
Lord Wyman says he was wrong and that Jon has avenged the Red Wedding
Lord Wyman declares Jon is the "White Wolf," the King in the North, bares his sword, and kneels
Lord Robett stands up, states his regret for not fighting beside Jon, and asks for forgiveness
Jon humbly says there is nothing to forgive
Lord Glover turns to the rest of the lords, announces there will be more fights to come, and that House Glover will stand behind House Stark as it had for a thousand years
Lord Glover bares his sword, kneels, and hails Jon as the King in the North
The rest of the assembly, including the Lords of the Vale, stands up, follows the example, and hails Jon as King in the North
Davos joins, declaring his allegiance
Lady Mormont watches her elder, male counterparts and cheers, pleased
Jon stands up and shares a look with a smiling Sansa
Sansa smiles at Jon
Sansa's smile fades as Littlefinger looks at her in a sinister fashion
Ellaria and the Sand Snakes meet with Olenna Tyrell, who is mourning her son and grandchildren
The Sand Snakes attempt to speak, but Olenna shuts them down
Ellaria suggests they must work together for survival, as Cersei has declared war on both factions
Olenna expresses distrust of Ellaria for orchestrating the deaths of Doran and Trystane Martell
Olenna clarifies that her House's future has been taken from her and she is not after survival
Ellaria promises that an alliance will give Olenna her new heart's desire
Olenna sardonically asks what that is
Ellaria states that it is "justice and vengeance"
Varys reveals himself and rephrases: "Fire and Blood."
Daario Naharis meets Queen Daenerys Targaryen and reports that the fleet is nearly ready
Daario expresses eagerness to see how the Dothraki do on the open sea
Daenerys tells Daario he will not be joining them
Daario interprets this as a mission to seize Casterly Rock
Daenerys clarifies that Daario is to stay in Meereen with the Second Sons to keep the peace
Daenerys explains she cannot bring her lover to Westeros, as marriage is her most valuable bargaining chip
Daario begs Daenerys to take him, arguing that queens should have lovers like kings do
Daenerys stands firm in her decision
Daario realizes Tyrion convinced Daenerys to leave him behind, but admits it is politically wise
Daario muses that no woman could compare to Daenerys, though she is sure he will have more lovers
Daenerys assures Daario she will leave instructions for him to govern the Bay of Dragons
Daario takes his leave
Daenerys departs and goes to see Tyrion
Tyrion unsuccessfully tries to console Daenerys
Daenerys thanks Tyrion
Daenerys admits she is not upset about Daario, but frightened by how easily she dismissed him
Tyrion says Daario wasn't the first man to love Daenerys and won't be the last
Daenerys turns the topic to Tyrion's rule of Meereen in her absence
Tyrion defends his actions ruling Meereen
Tyrion says he gave up on believing in himself or anything else, but believes in Daenerys
Daenerys, touched, gives Tyrion a pin: the brooch of the Hand of the Queen
Tyrion, struck with emotion, kneels in front of Daenerys
Drogon, Rhaegal, and Viserion fly over the assembled Dothraki, Ironborn, Dornishmen, men of the Reach, and Unsullied fleet of House Targaryen
Daenerys stands on the deck of the flagship, clad in Targaryen black, with Tyrion, Missandei, and Varys
Daenerys, Tyrion, Missandei, and Varys look ahead to the next round of the game of thrones
Daenerys anticipates either gaining a new ally or facing another massive war within House Targaryen for the Iron Throne

Dragonstone_(episode).action: Walder Frey presides over the second feast he has organized in a fortnight at the Twins
Walder Frey summons every Frey who took part in the Red Wedding under the pretext of revealing his plans
Walder orders the servants to serve wine from the Arbor to his family
Walder harshly tells his latest wife, Kitty Frey, that he is not wasting any wine on "a damn woman"
Walder thanks his family for helping him slaughter the Starks
Walder backhandedly compliments his brood for the violent deaths of King Robb Stark, his wife Talisa Stark, their unborn child, and Lady Catelyn Stark
Walder’s family laughs nervously at his comments
The Freys begin to choke from poisoned wine
Walder chides the Freys for not killing all the Starks
Walder remarks, "Leave one wolf alive, and the sheep are never safe," as his family members die around him
"Walder" reveals himself as a disguised Arya Stark, who has exacted vengeance on those who arranged the massacre of her mother and brother at the Red Wedding
Arya spares Walder's wife and the servants
Arya tells them, "The North remembers. Tell them winter came for House Frey."
Arya acquires a horse and rides south
Arya stumbles upon a group of Lannister soldiers sent to the Twins to keep law and order
The soldiers sing a song and offer to share rabbit meat for dinner
The soldiers ask Arya why she is riding south
Arya tells the soldiers she is traveling to King's Landing
Arya learns from the soldiers that the Great Sept of Baelor and its surroundings have been destroyed and that the Red Keep has been sealed
The young soldiers insist Arya join them around the campfire to eat and rest
Arya asks the soldiers about their adventures
The soldiers admit they are homesick and wish to return to their families
A young soldier offers Arya blackberry wine
Another soldier asks Arya why she is traveling south
Arya tells them she is going to kill the Queen
The soldiers think Arya is joking and burst out laughing
Arya smiles and laughs with them
Sandor Clegane rides north with the Brotherhood Without Banners through thick snow
The Brotherhood decides to take shelter for the night at an abandoned home
Sandor recognizes the house and declares it unsafe
Beric Dondarrion dismisses Sandor's unease and orders that they set up camp for the night
The men enter the house and discover the corpses of the farmer and his daughter Sally
It appears the father killed his daughter and himself to avoid starving to death
Sandor recalls seeing Beric at the tournament at King's Landing and wonders aloud why Beric keeps getting resurrected
Beric confides that he does not know what the Lord of Light is telling him
Sandor remarks that he does not believe in divine justice, citing the fate of the farmer and his daughter
Thoros tells Sandor to look into the fire despite Sandor's fear of flames
Thoros tells Sandor that only the fire can show him what the Lord of Light wants him to see
Sandor looks into the fire and at first can only see burning logs
Sandor sees a Wall of Ice, a castle where the Wall meets the sea, a mountain that looks like an arrowhead, and thousands of the dead marching past
Beric asks Sandor if he now believes that they are here for a reason
Thoros finds Sandor burying the dead farmer and his daughter Sally in the snow
Thoros deduces that Sandor knew the dead
Sandor admits that he doesn't know the dead
Thoros and Sandor bury the bodies together
Sandor attempts to deliver an epitaph to the Seven but can't remember the rest of the verse
Sandor tells the dead that they deserve better
Jon organizes the defense of the North against the Night King and his army
Jon asks all maesters to search for dragonglass, stressing its value
Jon requests Tormund and his people to man Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
Tormund agrees to defend Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
Jon orders all able-bodied men and boys aged 10 to 60 to be trained in combat
Jon orders every woman and girl to be trained and equipped as well
Robett Glover balks at the idea of arming his young granddaughter
Lyanna Mormont volunteers to help and assures every girl on Bear Island will be trained
The older lords are reminded of their place by Lyanna’s words and agree
Jon notes that if the Walkers get past the wall, the first obstacles will be the castles of Last Hearth and Karhold
Lord Yohn Royce opines that the castles should be demolished for their lords' treachery
Sansa interjects that Last Hearth and Karhold committed no crimes and urges Jon to strip the Umbers and Karstarks of their castles as punishment
Jon advocates forgiveness and insists that children will not be punished for the crimes of their fathers
Sansa continues to insist on punishment
Jon insists his decision is final
Sansa reluctantly obliges
Jon summons Ned Umber and Alys Karstark
Ned Umber and Alys Karstark, both children, are clearly terrified
Jon asks Ned Umber and Alys Karstark to reaffirm their loyalty to House Stark
Ned Umber and Alys Karstark oblige and kneel before King Jon
Jon says that the mistakes of the past don't matter anymore
Petyr Baelish watches the proceedings with a smile
Jon chides Sansa for questioning his decision-making in front of the other lords and ladies
Jon tells Sansa that questioning him publicly undermines his position
Sansa responds that Joffrey Baratheon did not tolerate dissent
Jon reassures Sansa that he is not Joffrey
Sansa tells Jon she knows he is nothing like Joffrey and assures him he is good at leadership
Sansa tells Jon she wants him to be wiser than their late father and brother
Sansa confides that their father sought to protect her from the harshness of reality
Maester Wolkan delivers a message from Queen Cersei Lannister
Cersei demands that House Stark submit to her authority
Jon is preoccupied with preparing to fight the Night King
Sansa warns Jon not to underestimate Cersei
Brienne of Tarth and Podrick Payne spar with swords
Podrick struggles during the sparring
Tormund tells Podrick he has a long way to go
Petyr and Sansa watch Brienne and Podrick sparring
Petyr tries to get under Sansa's skin
Sansa dismisses Petyr's attempts, stating she is safe at Winterfell and has Brienne as her sworn shield
Petyr prods Sansa that she looks unhappy
Sansa retorts that she only wants peace and quiet
Brienne notices what is going on with Sansa and Petyr and moves to intervene
Baelish scurries off
Brienne asks Sansa why Petyr is still at Winterfell
Sansa replies that the Knights of the Vale helped turn the tide of the Battle of the Bastards
Sansa is confident that she knows what Petyr wants
A column of White Walkers riding undead horses leads a horde of wights through a snowstorm beyond the Wall, including at least three undead Giants
Bran and Meera reach the gate beneath Castle Black
Bran and Meera are greeted by Acting Lord Commander Eddison Tollett and several armed Black Brothers
Edd asks if Bran and Meera are Wildlings
Meera introduces herself and Bran
Edd asks Bran and Meera to verify their identities
Bran "recognizes" Edd from the conflicts at the Fist of the First Men and Hardhome, and says he has seen the army of the dead
Edd decides to bring Bran and Meera inside, glancing nervously at the lands beyond the Wall
Cersei shows Jaime a giant map of Westeros she is having painted on a floor
Jaime remains silent since his return due to anger with Cersei over Tommen's death
Cersei tells Jaime that Tyrion has returned with Daenerys at the head of an armada
Jaime thinks Daenerys will land her forces at Dragonstone
Jaime reasons that Dragonstone is Daenerys's birthplace, is unoccupied, and has deep water for her fleet
Cersei notes they have enemies in the south, west, and north: Dorne, the Tyrells, and the Starks
Cersei dreams of ushering in a long dynasty
Jaime tells Cersei that they are losing the war and that there is no Lannister line to inherit the Iron Throne
Jaime mentions the deaths of their children, Kevan, and Lancel
Jaime asks Cersei about Tommen
Cersei responds angrily that Tommen betrayed them
Cersei adds that they are the only living Lannisters who count
Jaime tells Cersei that they need allies and reports that House Frey has been exterminated
Cersei angrily reminds Jaime that she has been listening to their father's counsel for forty years and has learned some things
A large Ironborn fleet bearing the sigil of House Greyjoy sails into King's Landing
Cersei tells Jaime that she is planning to forge an alliance with Euron Greyjoy
Jaime criticizes Cersei's decision and points out that the Greyjoys are oath breakers and "bitter killers."
Cersei and Jaime host Euron in the throne room
Cersei and Jaime host Euron in the throne room
Euron seeks revenge against Yara and Theon Greyjoy for running away with part of the fleet
Euron proposes that they join forces to murder their enemies, including Tyrion
Jaime questions Euron's legitimacy and trustworthiness
Euron talks about the Greyjoy Rebellion and praises Jaime's swordsmanship
Euron subtly infers to Cersei that Jaime's loss of a hand makes him inadequate for the wars ahead
Euron adds that he went into exile because of the Greyjoy Rebellion and has returned a feared and experienced pirate captain
Euron boasts that the Iron Fleet is the greatest fleet Westeros has ever seen and a military asset for the Lannisters
Euron proposes to marry Cersei to seal a marriage pact
Cersei declines Euron's proposal due to his history of oath-breaking and his role in murdering Balon Greyjoy
Euron promises Cersei that he will deliver his "finest gift" when he returns to King's Landing
Samwell is assigned menial work at the Citadel in Oldtown
Samwell serves the maesters' meals
Samwell empties the maesters' chamber pots
Samwell shelves books
Samwell browses through the library
Samwell stares at a book behind a locked grate in the restricted section
Samwell helps Archmaester Ebrose dissect a corpse
Samwell asks Archmaester Ebrose if he has read his proposition
Samwell asks for permission to read the restricted section
Samwell says he has truly seen the army of the dead
Archmaester Ebrose responds that Maesters are naturally critical
The Archmaester accepts Samwell's claim because the stories about the Long Night are corroborated in a number of sources
The Archmaester reminds Samwell that Maesters are guardians of knowledge and history
The Archmaester expresses belief that the Wall will not fall because it has stood for millennia
The Archmaester tells Samwell to finish his job
Samwell steals a Maester's key and enters the forbidden section later that night
Samwell studies
Gilly entertains her young son Sam, who is now a toddler
Gilly asks Samwell about the book he is studying, which is about the Long Night
Samwell mentions that Stannis Baratheon told him that dragonglass could be found on Dragonstone
Samwell discovers from the book that there is a mountain of obsidian just outside the castle
Samwell excitedly prepares a letter to Jon Snow relating the discovery
Samwell gathers empty bowls from the cells of the Citadel sanitarium
A shadowy figure whose arm is almost completely covered in greyscale confronts Samwell
The figure asks Samwell if the Dragon Queen has arrived yet
Samwell sputters that he does not know
The figure withdraws his arm
Daenerys lands at Dragonstone island with Tyrion, Varys, Missandei, and Grey Worm
Daenerys's three dragons circle overhead
Daenerys kneels on the ground and touches Westerosi land for the first time since her birth
Daenerys and her entourage climb the steps to Dragonstone castle
Daenerys, Grey Worm, and Unsullied guards silently enter the unguarded main gates and deserted courtyard
Daenerys enters the castle and studies the fiery stag on one of Stannis's banners
Daenerys rips down Stannis's banner with a firm tug
Daenerys enters the empty throne room and gazes upon the Targaryen throne
Grey Worm moves to stand beside Daenerys
Missandei holds Grey Worm back, waiting for Daenerys
Daenerys strides past the throne and enters the Chamber of the Painted Table
Daenerys surveys the discarded markers from Stannis's last use of the Table
Daenerys turns to Tyrion and says "Shall we begin?"

Stormborn.action: A thunderstorm occurs at Dragonstone
Tyrion, Varys, and Daenerys talk about the stormy circumstances of Daenerys's birth
Varys informs Daenerys that Cersei's position on Westeros is tenuous
Tyrion counsels Daenerys not to burn the Seven Kingdoms
Tyrion proposes that Daenerys forge alliances with Houses not aligned with Cersei
Daenerys questions Varys about his previous service to her father and King Robert Baratheon
Varys confides that King Robert had little interest in ruling but seemed better than Aerys II
Daenerys confronts Varys about his role in hiring assassins to target her
Varys reminds Daenerys that his choices were limited to feigning loyalty to Robert or losing his head
Daenerys distrusts Varys due to his history of switching allegiances
Varys refuses to be intimidated and points out that incompetent rulers do not deserve blind loyalty
Varys recounts his origins as a castrated slave and claims his true loyalty is to the oppressed masses
Varys tells Daenerys that if she only wants blind loyalty, she may as well have him killed, but he is more useful alive
Varys states he believes Daenerys is the last hope for the people
Daenerys accepts Varys’s fealty on the condition that he not conspire against her
Daenerys has Varys swear to tell her if he thinks she is failing the people and to be honest with her
Varys agrees to Daenerys’s condition
Daenerys warns Varys that if he betrays her, she will burn him alive
Varys replies that he would expect nothing less from the "Mother of Dragons"
Melisandre visits Daenerys and suggests she might be the Prince That Was Promised
Varys questions Melisandre about her previous service to King Stannis Baratheon
Daenerys initially does not believe she could be the Prince
Missandei reveals that the High Valyrian word for "prince" or "princess" is gender neutral
Daenerys asks Melisandre if she believes the prophecy applies to her
Melisandre replies that the prophecy could refer to Daenerys or Jon Snow
Tyrion vouches for Jon Snow and convinces Daenerys that Jon will make a good ally
Daenerys accepts Tyrion's advice
Daenerys tells Tyrion to invite Jon to Dragonstone, where Jon must "bend the knee"
Yara Greyjoy and Ellaria Sand advocate an immediate assault on King's Landing
Tyrion advises against laying waste to the capital and causing the deaths of innocents
Ellaria belittles Tyrion for his lack of stomach for war
Tyrion retorts by deriding Ellaria for poisoning Myrcella Baratheon for petty vengeance
Ellaria does not deny poisoning Myrcella and expresses regret over Oberyn Martell's death
Daenerys rebukes Ellaria and tells her to respect her Hand
Daenerys insists she doesn't wish to be known as the Queen of the Ashes
Olenna Tyrell sarcastically ponders if Daenerys will take the Iron Throne by asking politely
Olenna reminds Daenerys of Margaery Tyrell's fate at the hands of Cersei
Tyrion proposes using a combination of Tyrell and Dornish forces to lay siege to King's Landing
Olenna and Ellaria balk at the thought of using their own soldiers
Tyrion insists that using the Unsullied and Dothraki would arouse the patriotism of Westerosi bannermen, who would see them as foreign invaders and potentially close ranks behind Cersei
Tyrion proposes sending Grey Worm and the Unsullied to take Casterly Rock instead
Tyrion explains this would cut off Cersei's retreat and deal a massive blow to the morale of the Western army
The Greyjoy fleet escorts Ellaria back to Sunspear to rally the Dornish forces
The Greyjoy fleet ferries the Dornish forces up the coast to King's Landing, bypassing the Stormlands
Daenerys accepts Tyrion's proposal
Daenerys reminds the council that she will not be attacking King's Landing
Daenerys requests consenting approval from everyone
Everyone on the council gives their approval
Daenerys dismisses everyone except Lady Olenna
Daenerys tells Olenna she knows Olenna's motives are based on revenge for Cersei, not love for Daenerys
Daenerys insists she will usher in an era of peace
Olenna reflects that there was never lasting peace under the Mad King or any Targaryen
Olenna tells Daenerys that Tyrion is clever but she has outlasted many clever men by not listening to their advice
Olenna says the lords of Westeros are sheep but Daenerys is not a sheep
Olenna counsels Daenerys to be a dragon if she is to rule Westeros
Missandei visits Grey Worm to bid him farewell before his departure to Casterly Rock
Grey Worm confides that Missandei is his weakness
Missandei expresses confusion
Grey Worm explains how the Good Masters of Astapor tortured the Unsullied by exposing them to their worst fears
Grey Worm recalls he was the most fearless warrior until he met Missandei
Missandei replies that she also has to deal with fear
Missandei kisses Grey Worm
Missandei strips naked and proceeds to undress Grey Worm
Grey Worm is reluctant to remove his trousers but eventually acquiesces to Missandei's wishes
Missandei and Grey Worm share an intimate sexual encounter
Cersei gives a speech urging nobles from the Reach, including Lord Randyll Tarly, to reaffirm their allegiance to the Iron Throne and not support House Tyrell or Daenerys
Cersei warns that Daenerys's Dothraki and Unsullied would pillage lands, homes, and rape women
Lord Randyll Tarly points out that Daenerys has three dragons
Maester Qyburn replies that he is at work on a solution
Lord Tarly meets in private with Jaime Lannister after the audience with Queen Cersei
Jaime wants to appoint Tarly as his general
Tarly is unwilling to break his fealty to Lady Olenna Tyrell
Tarly expresses wariness of the Lannisters, referencing their reputation for cutting rivals' throats at weddings
Jaime reminds Tarly that he also swore an oath to the Iron Throne
Jaime tells Tarly that Cersei is preferable to Daenerys and her foreign armies
Jaime promises that Cersei will make Tarly the new Warden of the South
Qyburn leads Cersei to the dragon skulls beneath the Red Keep
Qyburn tells Cersei that his spies reported one of Daenerys' dragons was wounded by a spear at Meereen
Qyburn shows Cersei that dragons are not invincible
Qyburn displays a scorpion, a long-range artillery weapon, to Cersei
Qyburn reassures Cersei that they can hurt dragons
Cersei tests the scorpion on the skull of Balerion the Black Dread
The scorpion's bolt pierces through the massive, hard skull
Cersei is pleased by the result
Jon, Sansa, and Davos discuss the letter they received from Tyrion
Jon asks for Sansa's opinion about the letter
Sansa remembers that Tyrion was kind to her and unlike the rest of his family
Sansa wonders if the message is truly from Tyrion
Jon confirms the letter's authenticity by referencing a line Tyrion told him when they first met
Sansa worries it is too big a risk to respond
Jon concedes that now is not the right time to go to Dragonstone
Davos opines that Daenerys will make a good ally in the war against the White Walkers
Jon told Davos that wights are killed by fire
Davos points out that dragons breathe fire
Maester Wolkan brings Jon a message from Sam in the Citadel
Jon gathers the Northern lords in the main hall
Jon announces that Sam's letter reveals there is a vein of dragonglass beneath Dragonstone
Jon adds that Lord Tyrion has invited him to Dragonstone to meet with Queen Daenerys
Jon announces his decision to travel to Dragonstone to convince Daenerys to join their fight against the White Walkers
Jon declares that he plans to travel with Davos to White Harbor and sail to Dragonstone island
Lords Yohn Royce and Robett Glover voice their opinions that a Targaryen cannot be trusted, citing the atrocities committed by Daenerys's father
Lady Lyanna Mormont urges Jon to stay at home
Jon accepts that he is taking a risk but stresses that the fight against the White Walkers is more important, and they need Daenerys' aid if the North is to be saved
Jon emphasizes that the North is his home, it is part of him, and he will never stop fighting for it
Sansa reiterates her objection to Jon's leaving
Jon tells Sansa that he is appointing her as ruler of the North in his absence as she is his sister, regent, and a Stark
Sansa accepts
Petyr Baelish finds Jon Snow in the catacombs beneath Winterfell, visiting his father's tomb
Petyr vouches that Tyrion can be trusted
Petyr tells Jon that he and Eddard both loved Catelyn Stark despite their differences
Petyr notes that Catelyn wasn't fond of Jon
Petyr muses that Catelyn underestimated Jon's potential, as Jon is now the North's best chance against the coming storm
Jon tells Petyr he shouldn't be there and that they have nothing to say to each other
Petyr replies that Jon should be grateful for being saved from Ramsay Bolton
Petyr insists he is not Jon's enemy
Petyr tells Jon that he loves Sansa, as he loved Sansa's mother
Jon, angered, seizes Petyr by the throat and slams him into a wall
Jon threatens Petyr, saying "Touch my sister and I'll kill you myself."
Jon, Davos, and several horsemen prepare to ride south to White Harbor
Sansa watches from the castle battlements
Jon and Sansa bid each other farewell before Jon leaves
Petyr emerges from the catacombs and gazes at Sansa
Arya encounters Hot Pie at the Inn at the Crossroads and accosts him to give her some pie and ale
Hot Pie tells Arya that he has become a seasoned pie-master
Arya mentions she has had some experience baking two pies, omitting the details about Lothar Frey and Walder Rivers
Hot Pie asks Arya if Brienne of Tarth had found her
Arya gives an affirmative answer
Hot Pie asks Arya where she is traveling to
Arya replies that she is traveling to King's Landing for the Queen
Hot Pie tells Arya that Queen Cersei has blown up the Great Sept of Baelor and wonders why Arya is not heading to Winterfell
Arya replies that the Boltons occupy the castle
Hot Pie informs Arya that the Boltons are dead, killed during the Battle of the Bastards by Jon Snow, who has been named King in the North
Arya decides to head north
Hot Pie tells Arya not to worry about paying for the meal since she is a friend
Arya leaves
Hot Pie remarks that Arya is pretty for someone he thought was a boy
Arya rides her horse into the frost-covered forest
Arya prepares to set up camp in a snowy, forested area
Arya's horse is startled by several wolves
Wolves approach Arya, preparing to attack
Wolves are silenced by their pack leader, Nymeria
Nymeria, Arya's direwolf, appears and silences the wolves
Nymeria is initially apprehensive towards Arya
Nymeria recognizes Arya as her former mistress
Arya tells Nymeria she is going home and invites her to come
Nymeria turns and leaves with the pack
Arya watches in sadness and remarks, "That's not you."
Archmaester Ebrose and Sam examine Jorah Mormont, whose greyscale has spread to his torso
Archmaester Ebrose tells Jorah he should have cut off his lower arm instead of letting the infection spread
Archmaester Ebrose states that Jorah is beyond saving
Archmaester Ebrose explains that infected smallfolk are usually sent to live with the Stone Men
Archmaester Ebrose gives Jorah one more day, implying he could choose to commit suicide
Sam asks about sending condolences to Jorah's family at House Mormont
Jorah replies that he is estranged from his family
The Archmaester lectures Tarly about the art of writing history
Sam refuses to give up on Jorah
Sam mentions that a previous Maester, Pylos, successfully treated two cases of advanced greyscale
Ebrose responds that Pylos contracted the disease himself and died of it
Ebrose explains that because of the risk, the procedure was declared forbidden
Sam decides to treat Jorah and secretly visits his quarters after dark
Sam tells Jorah, "You're not dying today"
Sam reveals himself to be a sworn brother of the Night's Watch
Sam tells Jorah he served under Jorah's father, Jeor Mormont, and was present when he died
Sam gives Jorah rum to drink after taking a swig himself
Sam tells Jorah the process will be painful
Sam provides Jorah with a thick strip of leather as a mouthguard
Sam explains he is performing the procedure without permission
Sam prepares to use a scalpel to separate and cut away Jorah's infected skin and apply a medicinal ointment
Jorah gives Sam a look that tells him not to bother explaining
Jorah nods his consent for Sam to continue the debridement despite the pain
Yara and Theon Greyjoy travel aboard their portion of the Iron Fleet with their Dornish allies Ellaria Sand, Tyene, Obara, and Nymeria Sand
The Sand Snakes lie in bed and argue about killing Lannisters
Ellaria drinks Ironborn liquor with Yara and Theon, comparing it unfavorably to Dornish wine
Ellaria asks Yara if she has ever been to Dorne
Yara responds that she has been there a few times
Ellaria says there is a boy in every port in Dorne
Yara tells Ellaria, "A boy, a girl. Depends on the port."
Ellaria and Yara begin to flirt with one another
Ellaria motions for Theon to get her more wine
Yara explains Theon is not a servant but will be her advisor and protector once she's killed her uncle
Yara puts her feet up on the table
Ellaria bends over Yara and spreads her legs apart across the table
Ellaria moves her hand up Yara's leg towards her crotch
Ellaria invites Theon to have a threesome with her and Yara
Yara tells Ellaria, "Leave him be."
Yara and Ellaria begin kissing
Yara's ship is struck and lurches to the side
They move topside and discover their fleet has been ambushed by Euron Greyjoy's portion of the Iron Fleet
Euron's flagship Silence rams Yara's ship and lands a boarding party, with Euron at the forefront
Yara and her men fight fiercely against Euron's warriors
Yara and her men are overwhelmed by Euron's warriors
Tyene retreats below decks to protect her mother Ellaria on Yara's orders
Obara and Nymeria fight against Euron's Ironborn
Obara fights Euron
Euron kills Obara by impaling her with her own spear
Nymeria attacks Euron with her whip
Euron strangles Nymeria to death with her own whip
Tyene kills several Ironborn
Tyene and Ellaria are overwhelmed
Ellaria tells the Ironborn to kill her and Tyene quickly
Tyene and Ellaria are taken captive instead
Yara and Euron fight with axes as Yara's fleet burns in the night
Euron captures Yara
Euron goads Theon to come save Yara
Theon is about to come to his sister's aid but panics when Euron's men begin mutilating the wounded sailors
Theon is reminded of the tortures he suffered at Ramsay Bolton's hands
Theon jumps overboard to Yara's dismay
Euron laughs with cruel glee
Theon clings to driftwood
Theon sees Obara and Nymeria's corpses impaled and hanged from the prow
Euron's fleet starts sailing towards King's Landing
Yara's fleet burns in the Narrow Sea
Theon watches helplessly as the ship carrying Yara disappears into the night

The_Queen%27s_Justice.action: Euron parades the captives Yara, Ellaria, and Tyene through the streets of King's Landing, reveling in his victory
The crowd pelt Yara, Ellaria, and Tyene with rotting fruit
Cersei is impressed with Euron's "gift" and agrees to an alliance with him, praising him as a true friend to the Crown
Ellaria spits at Cersei's feet while Ser Gregor Clegane is present among the Queensguard
Euron demands his "reward" from Cersei
Cersei responds that Euron will get his reward once the war is over
Cersei appoints Euron as commander of her naval forces
Cersei appoints Jaime as commander of her armies
Jaime is privately hostile towards Euron and tells him that his head belongs on a spike
Euron tells Jaime that he desires Cersei and asks for tips about her sexual tastes
Jaime is silently furious at Euron's remarks
Cersei confronts Ellaria and Tyene in their cell, where both are gagged and chained to opposite walls
Cersei is accompanied by Ser Gregor Clegane and Qyburn
Cersei remembers Oberyn Martell's showdown with Ser Gregor during Tyrion's second trial by combat
Cersei sadistically recalls the Red Viper's savage death to Ellaria and how Ellaria screamed at the sight of it
Cersei provokes Ellaria's sorrow by recalling Oberyn's fearsome skill with a spear and how it didn't stop Gregor from killing him
Cersei infers that Oberyn brought his death on himself by taunting Gregor instead of leaving him to die
Cersei remembers raising Myrcella and the fact that Ellaria murdered her
Cersei praises Tyene's beauty to Ellaria and suggests Tyene is Ellaria's favorite
Cersei contemplates how to execute Ellaria or Tyene, cruelly suggesting Ser Gregor kill them as he killed Oberyn
Cersei decides that would be too fast a death
Cersei kisses Tyene with the Long Farewell, citing poetic justice for Myrcella's murder
Cersei torments Ellaria by having Qyburn confirm the uncertainty and inevitability of Tyene's death
Qyburn explains the Long Farewell's effects and uncertainty of timing
Cersei tells Ellaria she will be left alive to witness Tyene's death and to watch her daughter's face decay
Cersei says Ellaria will be forced to contemplate her actions and be force-fed if she tries to starve herself
Cersei orders torches to be routinely replaced so Ellaria doesn't miss a moment of Tyene's demise
Ellaria frantically struggles against her chains in a futile effort to reach her daughter
Jaime drinks wine for supper
Cersei arrives and kisses Jaime
Jaime initially rebuffs Cersei's advances
Jaime ultimately consents to sex with Cersei
Cersei and Jaime have sex
Cersei answers the door for a servant the next morning
Servant announces that a visitor from Braavos has arrived
Cersei acknowledges the message and requests fresh sheets
Cersei meets with Tycho Nestoris, a representative from the Iron Bank of Braavos
Tycho offers condolences to Cersei for the loss of her son Tommen Baratheon
Tycho thanks Cersei for eradicating the Faith Militant, describing it as superstition
Cersei realizes the Iron Bank wants to bet on the strongest faction
Cersei tells Tycho that Lord Euron controls the sea and is an ally for now
Cersei convinces Tycho to side with the Lannisters by denouncing Daenerys as a revolutionary
Cersei promises the Lannisters will pay their debts, unlike the Dothraki and former slaves
Cersei vows to pay the Crown's debts in full within a fortnight
Cersei invites Tycho to stay in King's Landing
Tycho is pleased and describes Cersei as her "father's daughter"
Sansa and Petyr Baelish learn from Maester Wolkan that they have about 4,000 bushels of wheat
Sansa realizes they don't have enough food for the coming winter
Sansa advocates building granaries to stockpile for a famine
Sansa orders Yohn Royce to see that the armor made for their armies is outfitted with leather to keep warm
Baelish and Sansa walk and talk about the threat of Cersei
Petyr urges Sansa to fight every battle and look for threats in every corner
A guard interrupts and tells Lady Stark she has received a visitor
Sansa learns her visitor is her younger brother Bran Stark, accompanied by Meera Reed
The two siblings have a tearful reunion
Sansa and Bran retreat to the Godswood
Sansa tells Bran she wishes Jon were at Winterfell
Bran agrees and notes he needs to speak to Jon
Sansa points out Bran is the rightful Lord of Winterfell
Bran refuses the position, stating he is the Three-Eyed Raven and can't be any sort of Lord
Sansa begs Bran to explain what being the Three-Eyed Raven means
Bran demonstrates his power by recalling details from the night of Sansa's marriage to Ramsay Bolton
Sansa is startled and walks away in shock and tears
Ebrose examines Jorah Mormont's wounds and surmises that the infection is no longer active
Ebrose realizes that somebody treated Jorah
Jorah claims that rest and the climate healed him
Ebrose lets Jorah go
Ebrose orders a private audience with Samwell Tarly later that evening
Jorah and Sam realize Ebrose is upset with Sam
Sam says he'll find out how much trouble he's in that evening
Jorah tells Sam he is returning to Daenerys because she gave him hope and a sense of purpose
Jorah thanks Sam for saving him
Sam says it's the least he could do given everything Jeor Mormont did for him
Jorah is moved when he takes Sam's offered farewell handshake, having expected to never touch another person again
Ebrose chastises Sam for embarking on an illegal healing procedure due to the high risk of infection
Ebrose praises Sam for his success, noting the operation was extraordinarily difficult
Ebrose asks Sam for the secret of his success
Sam replies that he simply read the books and followed the instructions
Ebrose "congratulates" Sam by telling him to make fresh copies of several old manuscripts and scrolls
Ebrose explains that Sam's reward is not being expelled from the Citadel
Jon and Davos arrive at Dragonstone
Tyrion and Missandei greet Jon and Davos
Tyrion addresses Jon as the bastard of Winterfell
Jon addresses Tyrion as the dwarf of Casterly Rock
Jon and Tyrion share a friendly grin

Jon discusses his predicament with Tyrion

Tyrion thinks the Lannisters will put up a fierce fight for Casterly Rock
Tyrion tells Daenerys that the gates and walls of Casterly Rock are impregnable to siege

Scenes of the Unsullied trying to storm the fortress are shown

Jaime Lannister, Randyll Tarly, and Bronn lead the Lannister and rebel forces towards Highgarden
Olenna Tyrell looks out from a balcony

Jaime pours two glasses of wine

Jaime explains that a token garrison was left behind and the Rock's stores were emptied before they fled
Jaime states Euron's Iron Fleet will destroy the attacking fleet, leaving the Unsullied trapped in Westeros at the mercy of Lannister forces
Jaime explains the main Lannister army would be far away from the main attack, a strategy he learned from Robb Stark's attack at the Whispering Wood
Olenna wonders why Tywin Lannister didn't just take Highgarden when Casterly Rock's mines first ran out of gold

Jaime is shocked into silence, realizing Olenna killed Joffrey and Tyrion took the blame

Olenna insists Jaime tell Cersei that she murdered her son

The_Spoils_of_War.action: Littlefinger meets with the recently-returned Bran Stark at Winterfell
Littlefinger tries to ingratiate himself with Bran, hoping to exploit disunity among the Stark children
Littlefinger gives Bran the Valyrian steel dagger that was used in the attempt on Bran’s life
Bran remains emotionally detached and generally uninterested due to his visions as the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran absentmindedly asks who the dagger belonged to
Littlefinger responds that the question of the dagger’s owner started the War of the Five Kings
Catelyn took the dagger south to King’s Landing to find its owner, convinced the Lannisters sent the cutthroat
Catelyn left the dagger with Ned, from whom Littlefinger later recovered it
Littlefinger tries to manipulate Bran by remarking on the chaos he must have lived through
Bran looks at Littlefinger and says, "Chaos is a ladder," quoting Littlefinger’s own words
Littlefinger is visibly unnerved and startled by Meera Reed entering the room

Littlefinger leaves
Meera notices Bran's new wheelchair

Bran responds that he isn't really "Bran Stark" anymore, but the Three-Eyed Raven
Bran explains he remembers Bran Stark's life, but now remembers vast amounts of other memories
Bran says everything that once affected Bran Stark now seems distant and trivial
Meera, in horror, cries that Bran "died" in the cave and leaves

Sansa instantly realizes it must be Arya and knows where she has gone

Sansa and Arya are happy to see each other but feel awkward at first
Sansa and Arya are happy to see each other but feel awkward at first
Arya asks if she has to call Sansa "Lady Stark" now

Sansa and Arya smile and hug, though still a bit unsure
Sansa and Arya smile and hug, though still a bit unsure
Arya notes that Jon left Sansa in charge
Arya smiles when Sansa says she hopes Jon will be back soon
Sansa says she hopes Jon will be back soon
Sansa remembers how happy Jon was to see her when they were reunited

Arya says the statue doesn't really look like Ned

Arya points out that she and Sansa are not dead
Arya asks Sansa if she killed Joffrey as everyone believes
Sansa explains she didn't kill Joffrey, though she wished she had
Arya remarks that Joffrey was always at the top of her "list"

Arya explains she kept a list of everyone she was going to kill
Arya and Sansa laugh together
Arya and Sansa laugh together
Sansa asks Arya how she got back
Arya says her road wasn't a pleasant one
Sansa says her road wasn't pleasant either
Arya and Sansa hug earnestly and warmly
Arya and Sansa hug earnestly and warmly
Sansa informs Arya that Bran is home too
Arya is elated to hear Bran is home
Arya's face falls when Sansa makes no mention of Rickon, realizing Rickon is dead
Sansa brings Arya to Bran in the Godswood
Bran is lost in thought by the Weirwood heart tree

Sansa looks down, baffled and concerned at Arya's deadly skill level
Arya glares at a surprised Littlefinger
Cersei is happy to learn of the gold taken from the Sack of Highgarden
Lannister army transports the gold from Highgarden to King's Landing
Lannister army remains in the north of the Reach seizing grain supplies

Daenerys and Missandei make their way down to the beach to meet Jon Snow

Jon leads Daenerys on a tour of the long-abandoned Valyrian dragonglass mine

Jon has a reason for asking Daenerys to come to the mine, beyond its history
Jon leads Daenerys deeper into the mines by torchlight
Jon shows Daenerys cave paintings left by the Children of the Forest, filled with arcane magical symbols
Daenerys is stunned that the mythical Children stood where they are standing thousands of years ago
Jon explains that this isn’t exactly true and leads Daenerys into another chamber

Jon asks Daenerys to ally with him to defeat the Night King
Daenerys says she will fight for Jon and the North after he bends the knee
Jon tells Daenerys that the North won't accept a southern ruler again after everything that has happened to them
Daenerys urges that the North will accept her if their king does

Daenerys argues that if Jon's point in showing her the cave drawings was that she should swallow her pride and ally with him because of the White Walkers, then by the same token, the North should submit to her because their survival should outweigh their pride
Daenerys, as she exits the cave to the beach, is joined by Varys and Tyrion with grave looks on their faces
Varys and Tyrion inform Daenerys that the Unsullied succeeded in capturing Casterly Rock
Tyrion reveals the disaster at Highgarden and that his military strategy fell apart
Tyrion explains that Casterly Rock was a feint and the Lannisters sacked Highgarden
Daenerys becomes furious and struggles to keep her temper
Daenerys snaps at Tyrion, blaming his cautious strategy for losing Dorne, the Iron Islands, and the Reach
Daenerys accuses Tyrion of wanting to use a light hand against the Lannisters because they are his own people
Tyrion insists they still have enough ships to shuttle the Dothraki from Dragonstone to the mainland
Tyrion points out that their combined Unsullied/Dothraki forces are still the largest single army in Westeros
Daenerys fumes that her numbers are useless if she cannot feed them, as the Reach was the true strategic value
Daenerys decides to abandon clever plans and use the Dothraki and her dragons in a direct assault
Daenerys desires to assault the Red Keep with all three dragons, intending to raze it to the ground
Daenerys asks Jon for his opinion
Jon argues against using dragons to destroy castles and cities, warning Daenerys not to become like her tyrannical ancestors
Jon and Davos Seaworth proceed back up to the castle
Davos remarks that Jon seems attracted to Daenerys
Jon says Daenerys has a good heart
Jon brushes Davos' observation aside, saying he doesn't have time for that
Jon says he has seen the Night King and his horde of the undead
Jon stresses that they need to ally with Daenerys
Jon explains the North is largely depleted of soldiers after years of war and the Red Wedding
Jon estimates they have maybe 10,000 soldiers or less left
Davos corrects Jon, saying "fewer"
They run into Missandei again
Missandei politely enquires why Jon's surname is "Snow" despite House Stark ruling the North for centuries
Jon explains that he is bastard-born and uses the regional surname 'Snow' because his parents weren't married
Davos asks Missandei if there are similar customs for bastards on Naath
Missandei explains that marriage does not exist in Naath, so the concept of a "bastard" is alien to her

They see a lone Ironborn ship approaching the island, a survivor of Yara Greyjoy's fleet
Theon and some of his men come to shore on a rowboat
Theon disembarks and is stunned to see Jon Snow
Jon is stunned for a moment as well
Theon walks up to Jon and asks if Sansa is all right
Jon angrily grabs Theon by his coat and shakes him, remembering Theon's past treachery
Jon tells Theon that saving Sansa is the only reason he is not killing him and releases him
Theon is visibly relieved
Theon informs them that Euron Greyjoy attacked their fleet and took Yara prisoner
Theon says he has returned to ask Daenerys's help in saving Yara
Jon grudgingly tells Theon that Daenerys has just left
Jaime coordinates the soldiers loading up the spoils of war for transport at Highgarden
The Sack of Highgarden allows the Lannisters to seize all of House Tyrell's gold stores
The gold is sent ahead to King's Landing
Jaime and the soldiers move on to securing grain shipments
Jaime procures a large bag of gold coins and gives it to Bronn as payment
Bronn is annoyed that this isn't the full reward he was promised, which included a wife and a castle
Bronn half-seriously asks Jaime why he doesn't just grant him Highgarden
Jaime replies that they are at war, Highgarden requires upkeep, and Daenerys could arrive at any moment
Bronn remarks on the riches Jaime is packing up
Jaime says the gold is not his and will be used to repay the Iron Bank
Bronn is not satisfied with Jaime's answer
Jaime says when they win the war, Bronn can choose any castle he likes
Jaime has Bronn assist Randyll Tarly and Dickon Tarly in seizing grain supplies in the northern Reach
Jaime's army advances farther north and reaches the Blackwater Rush
Lord Randyll Tarly tells Jaime they should hurry to get all the grain wagons over the Blackwater river to King's Landing
Randyll warns they will be vulnerable if caught on both sides of the river
Jaime agrees with Randyll
Randyll suggests flogging the stragglers to motivate them
Jaime urges that his soldiers fought well at Highgarden and should be given a fair warning first
Jaime and Bronn encounter Dickon Tarly
Jaime asks Dickon what he thought of his first taste of battle
Dickon nervously claims it was glorious, then admits he was conflicted
Dickon explains House Tarly had been loyal vassals of the Tyrells and he knew many of the men they killed
Jaime replies those men didn't deserve to die but blames Olenna Tyrell for siding with Daenerys against Cersei
Dickon confesses the aftermath was horrible, especially the stench of corpses
Bronn playfully taunts Dickon about learning that men empty their bowels when they die
The Lannister army and wagon train continue east, approaching a river
Jaime and Bronn are surprised to hear what sounds like distant thunder
Jaime and Bronn realize the sound is approaching cavalry
Jaime and Randyll shout for their soldiers to form up
The soldiers form up before the enemy crests over the horizon
A massive, 100,000-strong horde of Dothraki cavalry appears, armed and baying for blood
Bronn advises Jaime to ride ahead to King's Landing
Jaime insists he will not abandon his men
Bronn tells Jaime the Dothraki will quickly overwhelm the Lannister lines and Jaime is too valuable to stay and fight
Jaime insists they have a chance if they hold
Jaime and Bronn look to the sky in awe and horror
a massive roar drowns out all other sound
the Lannister army sees a huge adult dragon, Drogon, part the cloud cover and head straight for their lines
Daenerys rides Drogon above the approaching Dothraki
Drogon outpaces the Dothraki
Daenerys commands "Dracarys"
Drogon blasts a torrent of fire through the Lannister ranks, punching a hole in their formation
the Dothraki light cavalry sweep through the hole and wheel around to envelop the Lannister lines
The Lannister-Tarly lines rally under the leadership of Jaime and Randyll
The Dothraki charge into the Lannister lines head-on
The Lannister spear wall holds firm, meeting the Dothraki charge with stiff resistance
Lannister soldiers, though outnumbered, fight as better-armed, highly disciplined, battle-hardened veterans
Dothraki horse-archers let off shots before charging into the Lannister ranks
Tarly archers return fire at the Dothraki, doing as much or more damage due to the Dothraki's lighter armor
The Lannister army holds their line for a time
The enemy uses their advantage in numbers to turn the tide against the Lannister army
The sheer tide of horses smashes through the shield wall, opening gaps for others to charge through
Jaime appears to be able to force the Dothraki to a standstill for a moment
Daenerys wheels out of the air again, devastating the Lannister formations
Daenerys switches to outflanking the Lannister ranks, burning a vast horizontal swath of men from left to right
Daenerys obliterates much of the Lannister wagon train in the process
Men are flash-burned into ash or cooked alive in their armor as they struggle to rip it off and douse themselves in the river
Jaime rallies his remaining forces a second time
Jaime takes command of surviving Lannister and Tarly archers
Jaime directs the archers to concentrate their fire at the dragon
Archers fire arrows at Drogon and Daenerys
Daenerys sees the attack coming

Drogon blasts the archer formations with fire
Jaime avoids the flames
Jaime is surrounded by enemies

Jaime commands Bronn to reach Qyburn's anti-dragon scorpion-bolt launcher
Bronn hesitates for a moment

A Dothraki warrior cuts off Bronn's horse's leg, sending him sprawling

Bronn fires a bolt into the Dothraki warrior, flinging him twenty feet and pinning his corpse to another wagon
Bronn reloads the scorpion as fast as he can
Bronn spots the dragon in the distance and fires, but misses
Daenerys is startled by the scorpion-bolt flying a few feet from her head
Daenerys scans the battlefield and spots Bronn on the scorpion as he reloads
Daenerys becomes enraged at the attack on Drogon
Daenerys leads Drogon in a direct charge, diving straight down at Bronn on the scorpion launcher
Bronn waits for Daenerys to approach close enough before firing
Bronn fires another shot, which impales Drogon's right shoulder
Drogon lets out an awful screech and collapses in shock
Drogon drops into a free-fall from hundreds of feet in the air
Bronn is elated
Daenerys quickly breaks Drogon out of his fall before they hit the ground
Daenerys and Drogon avoid being killed in the fall
Surviving Lannister soldiers realize the dragon is not dead, but in pain and extremely angry
Drogon hovers above the river
Drogon sets his sights on the device (scorpion) that injured him
Drogon angrily sends a fire-blast at the scorpion, destroying it
Bronn narrowly jumps out of the way to safety
The scorpion bolt causes only superficial damage to the dragon's armored scales
The flesh wound grounds Drogon
Drogon howls in pain and fury
Drogon destroys the burning scorpion with a swipe of his tail
Drogon burns alive any enemy soldiers that get near him
Daenerys dismounts Drogon
Daenerys desperately tries to remove the scorpion bolt
The dragon and the Dothraki overwhelm the Lannister lines
The scorpion is destroyed, eliminating the Lannisters' last hope
The battle turns into a rout and massacre
The Dothraki cut down fleeing Westerosi mercilessly on and off horseback
Tyrion views the battle from a nearby hill with Qhono
Qhono remarks that Tyrion's people can't fight
Tyrion looks gravely on the battlefield, realizing his own countrymen are being massacred
Burning and dying men rush to the river to try to extinguish the flames
Jaime sees that Daenerys is grounded and immobilized
Jaime realizes he still has one chance to end her invasion by killing Daenerys
Jaime grabs a spear
Jaime charges his horse across the flaming battlefield to make a death-run for Daenerys as she tends to her dragon
Tyrion swears under his breath, calling his brother a "fucking idiot"
Tyrion is angry that Jaime is going to get himself killed
Jaime charges toward Daenerys during the chaos of battle
Drogon notices Jaime amidst the chaos
Drogon shields Daenerys behind his head
Drogon opens his mouth and his throat glows, preparing to unleash fire
Drogon unleashes flames at Jaime
Bronn rides in and tackles Jaime off his horse
Jaime and Bronn fall into the river
Jaime is weighed down by his armor and sinks beneath the waters, in danger of drowning
Their horses are consumed by Drogon's flames

Eastwatch.action: Bronn pulls Jaime from the depths of Blackwater Rush and onto the shore after Jaime's failed attempt to slay Daenerys
Bronn tells Jaime he rescued him only because he wants his reward from the Lannisters
Jaime, shellshocked, remarks on Drogon's destructive power
Jaime realizes they are in great peril if Daenerys deploys all three dragons in future battles
Bronn assures Jaime he won't be around for such an assault
Jaime laments his duty to report what happened to Cersei
Bronn thinks it would be safer for Jaime to jump back into the river than face Cersei's wrath
Tyrion assesses the carnage of the battle, seeing ashes of wagons, horses, and Lannister soldiers
Dothraki herd prisoners of war to Daenerys
Drogon perches behind Daenerys, appearing menacing and majestic
Daenerys appeals to the prisoners, addressing rumors spread by Cersei and warnings of her brutality
Daenerys assures she is not here to murder, but to destroy the wheel of power
Daenerys offers the prisoners a choice: bend the knee and join her, or refuse and die
Tyrion looks at Daenerys apprehensively
Only a few prisoners kneel at first
Drogon bellows at the prisoners, causing most to quickly yield
Randyll Tarly, Dickon, and a handful of men refuse to kneel
Daenerys summons Randyll forward
Randyll insists he already has a Queen
Tyrion recalls Randyll didn’t pledge to Cersei until recently and that she murdered the rightful Queen
Tyrion observes Randyll’s allegiances are "flexible"
Randyll answers there are no easy choices in war
Randyll tells Tyrion that Cersei is at least a true Westerosi, and that Tyrion is a kinslayer and supporter of a foreigner
Daenerys accepts Randyll’s answer and prepares to carry out a sentence
Tyrion intervenes, suggesting sending Randyll to the Wall instead of death
