Randyll replies Daenerys cannot send him to the Wall as she is not his Queen
Daenerys signals for three Dothraki men to apprehend Randyll and Dickon
Dickon suddenly speaks up, insisting he will have to be killed too
Randyll tries to silence his son, horrified
Tyrion reminds Dickon that he is the future of House Tarly and insists that he submit, reminding him what happened to House Tyrell
Randyll silently nods in agreement to Dickon, indicating his tacit approval to bend the knee
Dickon refuses to relent on his decision
Tyrion proposes to Daenerys about having them committed to the cells for a time instead
Daenerys refuses Tyrion’s proposal, not wishing to grant the reputation of putting traitors in chains
Daenerys resumes her sentencing for Randyll and Dickon as they hold hands
Drogon unleashes his dragonfire, burning Randyll and Dickon alive and reducing them to ash
The remaining soldiers, terrified, instantly kneel
Tyrion reflects uneasily over the execution
Bran wargs into a flock of ravens at the Godswood in Winterfell
Bran flies the ravens over the Wall into the Land of Always Winter
Bran sees the army of the dead led by the White Walkers and the Night King traveling south towards Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
The Night King sees the ravens
Bran severs the connection with the ravens
Bran tells Maester Wolkan that they need to send ravens at once
Arya observes Sansa presiding over a meeting of the Northern lords in Winterfell's council chamber
Sansa presides over a meeting of the Northern lords
Lord Robett Glover and Lord Yohn Royce imply they made a mistake in their choice of ruler and suggest Sansa take over power in Jon Snow's absence
Sansa insists Jon Snow is their true ruler and that she is his regent
Sansa confides her frustration in the Northern lords to Arya after the meeting
Arya calls Sansa out for diplomatically handling the lords' concerns instead of shutting them down
Arya suggests Sansa should not let the lords insult Jon and hints at assassinating them
Sansa disagrees with Arya's approach
Arya accuses Sansa of using their parents' chambers and thinking she is better than everyone else, suggesting Sansa wants the support of the northern lords to seize the North
Sansa, disturbed and hurt by Arya's accusations, tells Arya she has "work to do"
Arya stalks Littlefinger as he walks through the grounds of Winterfell
Arya sees Littlefinger speaking with a servant, but is too far to hear their conversation
Arya follows Littlefinger to his personal quarters
Arya sees Littlefinger chatting at the door with Maester Wolkan
Arya eavesdrops on Littlefinger asking Wolkan if he is sure that "this" is the only copy
Wolkan assures Littlefinger that it is the only copy
Littlefinger replies that "Lady Stark thanks him for his services"
Littlefinger briefly enters his room, then leaves again
Arya enters Littlefinger’s chamber and rummages through his study and furniture
Arya finds a scroll hidden under Littlefinger’s mattress
Arya discovers the scroll Sansa wrote to Robb, urging him to yield to Joffrey
Arya looks horrified, unaware Sansa wrote the letter under duress from Cersei
Arya rolls up the scroll and sneaks out of the room
Littlefinger grins as he watches Arya from behind a wall
Maesters read Bran Stark's message warning of the army of the dead but are dismissive of his account
Samwell Tarly, present to swap out books, vouches for Bran and tells the Maesters that Bran survived in the wilds alone
One Maester mockingly tells Samwell to practice inscribing instead of entertaining myths and fables
Sam counters that the Maesters should use their position to warn people to prepare for the coming Night
Archmaester Ebrose suggests Bran's message could be genuine, but could also be disinformation from Queen Daenerys
Maesters agree to send a letter to Winterfell to investigate Bran's claims further
Maesters remain skeptical of Bran's claims, regarding White Walkers as legendary beings
Sam leaves the room
Another Archmaester asks if Sam's father and brother were recently burned alive
Ebrose confirms this and admits he hasn't told Sam yet
Gilly reads High Septon Maynard's diary
Gilly tells Samwell that the High Septon issued an annulment for "Prince Ragger" so he could marry another woman in Dorne
Gilly indicates that any children Rhaegar may have with his new wife are trueborn and not bastards
Gilly is ignorant of Robert's Rebellion
Samwell is preoccupied with his own misery
The enormity of the discovery is lost on both Gilly and Samwell
Samwell complains about having to inscribe the bodily functions of High Septon Maynard
Samwell, exasperated, stalks out of the room
Samwell goes to the library
Samwell grabs several books and other items
Samwell contemplates the atrium of the room
Samwell meets Gilly in the courtyard of the Citadel, where she and Sam wait in a wagon
Gilly asks Samwell if he is sure he wants to give up his studies
Samwell replies that he is "tired of reading about the achievements of better men", quoting his father
Samwell and Gilly ride off into the night
Jon Snow walks on the grounds of Dragonstone island
Daenerys arrives on the back of her dragon Drogon
Drogon roars at Jon and stretches out his head to face him
Drogon calms down and recognizes Jon as a friend, allowing Jon to stroke his snout
Jon strokes Drogon's snout
Daenerys is surprised by Drogon's reaction
Jon, under slight duress, agrees that the dragons are beautiful beasts
Daenerys responds that the dragons are her children
Jon observes that Daenerys was not gone for long
Daenerys curtly replies that she was not, and now has fewer enemies to deal with
Daenerys asks Jon about the Battle of the Bastards and him "taking a knife in the heart for his people"
Jon deflects Daenerys's question and replies that Davos likes to embellish things
Their conversation is interrupted by the return of a recently-healed Jorah Mormont
Daenerys introduces Jorah to Jon
Jon says he worked under Jorah's father in the Night's Watch
Jorah reaffirms his allegiance to Daenerys
Daenerys accepts Jorah's offer of service and hugs him
Tyrion and Varys talk about the deaths of Randyll and Dickon in the Reach
Varys tells Tyrion how he distanced himself from the Mad King's role in killing Rickard and Brandon Stark
Tyrion insists that Daenerys is not her father
Varys agrees, but warns that Daenerys has a growing ruthless streak that must be curtailed by Tyrion's counsel
Tyrion and Varys discuss a sealed scroll containing Bran Stark's message from the North
Varys reads Bran Stark's message
Jon tells Daenerys about Bran and Arya's return to Winterfell
Jon expresses his desire to return home to Winterfell
Jon shares Bran's vision warning that the army of the dead is getting closer
Jon worries the undead army will make it past the Wall and advance on the North
Daenerys notes that Jon doesn't have enough men to fight the army of the dead
Jon asks Daenerys again to join him
Daenerys expresses concern that Cersei will take advantage if she focuses on the army of the dead
Tyrion proposes bringing a wight south to King's Landing to prove the army of the dead is real and convince Cersei to agree to a truce
Varys opines that it is suicide to try to appeal to Cersei
Tyrion argues that he can persuade Jaime
Davos reluctantly agrees to smuggle Tyrion into King's Landing, but thinks the mission is risky
Jorah volunteers to go north to help capture a Wight
Jon volunteers to lead the expedition north
Jon explains the Free Folk won't follow Jorah and that he has the most experience facing their enemy
Daenerys, apparently on the verge of tears, responds that she did not give Jon permission to leave
Jon reminds Daenerys that he is the King in the North
Jon tells Daenerys she has the power of life and death over him but that he trusted her even though she was a stranger
Jon pleads with Daenerys to return the favor by trusting him
Davos and Tyrion chat about smuggling before their mission to infiltrate King's Landing
Davos plans to find Gendry
Tyrion wants to secretly meet with Jaime to find a peaceful solution to the war
Davos and Tyrion return with Gendry to Dragonstone
Jon supervises the diggings and excavations at the dragonglass mines
Gendry meets Jon and remarks that Jon is a lot shorter than he expected
Gendry blurts out his true parentage to Jon
Gendry volunteers to accompany Jon on his mission to the North
Jon and his party, including Jorah Mormont, prepare to depart on boats for Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
Daenerys and her entourage arrive to bid Jon and his party farewell
Daenerys quips to Jorah that they should be used to saying farewell to each other
Jon arrives at the beach as Daenerys and Jorah are saying goodbye
Jon playfully quips to Daenerys that if he doesn’t return, she won’t have to deal with the King in the North anymore
Daenerys responds that she’s grown used to Jon, hinting at her feelings for him
Jon wishes Daenerys good fortune in the wars to come before he leaves
Daenerys and Tyrion watch as Jon and his party depart on their boats for Eastwatch
Tyrion watches as Jon and his party depart on their boats for Eastwatch
Jaime returns to King's Landing to inform Cersei of the defeat
Jaime insists that the Lannisters have no chance of defeating Daenerys, even with mercenaries
Jaime explains that Qyburn's scorpion only angered Drogon
Jaime states that neither Lannister soldiers nor mercenaries can match the Dothraki horde
Jaime notes that the Dothraki killed Lannister men as if it were sport, not war
Cersei snidely asks Jaime if they are expected to surrender to Daenerys
Cersei mocks Jaime, remarking that Tyrion could intercede for them with Daenerys as an apology for killing Joffrey and Tywin
Jaime reveals to Cersei that Tyrion is innocent of Joffrey's murder, telling her Olenna Tyrell confessed to it
Cersei is dismissive of Tyrion's innocence as usual
Jaime talks Cersei through Olenna's motives, asking if Olenna would prefer Margaery to marry Joffrey or Tommen
Jaime explains Olenna would have become the true ruler of the Seven Kingdoms behind the scenes
Cersei feels cheated of vengeance and can barely contain her fury, lamenting listening to Jaime and saying Olenna ought to have died screaming
Jaime says Olenna is dead, along with the rest of House Tyrell, and warns that they will go the same way unless careful
Jaime observes that Tyrion now stands against his siblings with Daenerys, who has a large army and three dragons
Cersei dismisses Jaime's protests and makes it clear she intends to fight to the death rather than surrender her throne, expecting the same from Jaime and the Lannister military
Bronn leads Jaime beneath the Red Keep, claiming to take him to a sparring session among the skulls of the Targaryen dragons
Jaime discovers the meeting is actually with his estranged brother Tyrion
Jaime is initially angry that Bronn arranged the secret meeting
Tyrion compliments Jaime on abandoning Casterly Rock shrewdly and unsentimentally
Jaime expresses a wish for Tyrion’s death for killing their father Tywin
Tyrion emotionally points out that Tywin knew he was innocent but still condemned him to death, hating him for being a dwarf
Jaime demands to know what Tyrion wants
Tyrion tells Jaime that Daenerys will win the Last War and is willing to make peace with Cersei under certain conditions
Tyrion tells Jaime about Daenerys’s terms
Davos visits Flea Bottom and finds Gendry in a smithy on the Street of Steel
Davos jokes that he thought Gendry might still be rowing
Gendry confirms that no one has given him a second glance
Gendry realizes Davos has come to bring him for something important
Gendry explains he was never happy serving the family who murdered his father and tried to kill him
Gendry says he has been preparing for such a moment and agrees to come with Davos
Davos warns it will be dangerous and suggests Gendry take a sword
Gendry points out he was never taught to fight properly and is only good with a hammer
Gendry retrieves a war hammer with a stag's head design from the back of the shop
Davos and Gendry prepare to leave on the shores of Blackwater Bay, with Gendry placing his hammer in Davos' boat
They are spotted by a pair of Gold Cloaks
Davos pretends to be a common smuggler and bribes the guards with coin
Davos strikes up a conversation with the guards
When asked about his cargo, Davos shows the guards fermented crab, claiming it is an aphrodisiac popular with the city's brothels
Davos uncovers the crab while pulling the cloth aside to hide the war hammer and gives each guard a sample
The guards are satisfied, if slightly disgusted, and prepare to depart
Tyrion arrives at that moment and walks past the guards to the boat while looking downward
One of the guards recognizes Tyrion by his scar and asks him to stop
The guards realize he is Tyrion Lannister and see through Davos' deception
Gendry kills both guards by smashing their skulls in with his hammer before they can react
Davos, slightly exasperated, introduces Gendry to Tyrion
Tyrion wryly observes "he'll do"
Qyburn visits Cersei at the Red Keep
Jaime enters Cersei's chambers
Jaime tells Cersei that he met with Tyrion
Cersei asks if Daenerys wants to negotiate a surrender
Jaime tells Cersei that Daenerys is seeking an armistice due to the threat of the army of the dead
Cersei reveals she knows Bronn secretly organized the meeting between Tyrion and Jaime
Cersei suggests that an alliance with Daenerys may be a wiser move
Cersei expresses her determination to destroy any force that stands against her
Cersei asks Jaime if he plans to punish Bronn for arranging the meeting with Tyrion
Cersei reveals she is pregnant with another of Jaime's children
Cersei reflects on their late father's advice that "the lion does not concern himself with the opinions of the sheep"
Cersei hugs Jaime and whispers in his ear that he is never to betray her again
Jon and his band land at Eastwatch and meet with the wildling Tormund
Tormund thinks Jon's plan is suicidal and mockingly asks which Queen they need to convince
Jon replies that they need to convince both Queens
Davos volunteers to stay behind at Eastwatch due to his age and lack of fighting ability
The group learns that the Night's Watch has detained members of the Brotherhood Without Banners, including Beric Dondarrion, Thoros, and Sandor Clegane
Jon and his team meet with the prisoners in the icy cells beneath Eastwatch
The group establishes their reasons for hating each other
Gendry opines that they should not trust Thoros because the Brotherhood sold him to the Red Witch
Davos and Jon are angered by Gendry's statement
Thoros is surprised to see Jorah, an old comrade and rival from the Siege of Pyke
The Wildlings, Mormont, and the Brotherhood Without Banners distrust each other
The Wildlings, Mormont, and the Brotherhood Without Banners decide to put aside their differences to fight against the army of the dead
Sandor quips that the cells are freezing
Jon, Jorah, Tormund, Gendry, Sandor, Beric, and Thoros are freed
Jon, Jorah, Tormund, Gendry, Sandor, Beric, and Thoros exit Eastwatch's gate
Jon, Jorah, Tormund, Gendry, Sandor, Beric, and Thoros set out into the lands beyond the Wall on their mission to capture a Wight

Beyond_the_Wall_(episode).action: Arya talks to Sansa about borrowing Bran's bow and arrow
Arya tells Sansa she practiced several times until she hit the bullseye
Arya recalls Eddard watching and clapping his hands in praise of her accomplishments
Eddard claps his hands in praise of Arya's accomplishments
Arya reasons that Eddard knew the rules were wrong but that she was in the right
Arya confronts Sansa about her alleged role in Eddard's death
Arya presents the letter Sansa wrote to Robb urging him to bend the knee to Joffrey
Sansa replies that Cersei forced her to write the letter under duress
Arya counters that Sansa was not tortured and mentions seeing Sansa at Ned's execution
Sansa retorts that Arya did nothing to stop their father's execution
Arya chastises Sansa for betraying their family
Sansa responds that they only returned to Winterfell because of her, while Arya pursued her own agenda
Sansa adds that Jon was saved when Petyr Baelish and the Knights of the Vale rescued him
Sansa insists Arya would not have survived the torments she endured at the hands of Joffrey and Ramsay
Sansa demands to know where Arya found the letter
Sansa chides Arya that Cersei would be pleased to see them fighting
Arya remains bitter towards Sansa
Arya realizes Jon would understand the difficult circumstances Sansa was under when she wrote the letter
Arya realizes Sansa is afraid the Northern lords will discover the letter and turn on her, including Lyanna Mormont
Arya adds that Lyanna is younger than Sansa was when she wrote the letter
Arya argues Lyanna wouldn't agree with Sansa's defense that she was a child at the time
Arya recognizes Sansa wrote the letter out of fear
Arya says she prefers to embrace anger over fear
Sansa asks Petyr Baelish about where Arya got the letter from
Sansa is unaware that Baelish orchestrated the entire incident
Sansa tells Petyr that she is commanding 20,000 men who answer to Jon but not to her
Petyr tells Sansa that the men will trust her because she can rule
Sansa expresses distrust in the loyalty of the Northern lords, citing their history of switching sides
Sansa says the discovery of the letter will turn her liege lords and men against her
Sansa confides in Petyr about her strained relations with Arya
Petyr suggests that Sansa talk to Brienne of Tarth because she has sworn to protect both of Lady Catelyn Stark's daughters
Sansa trusts Baelish and accepts his advice
Maester Wolkan informs Sansa that they have received a letter from Queen Cersei
Sansa meets with Brienne
Brienne advises Sansa not to leave Winterfell
Sansa decides to send Brienne as her representative
Sansa reasons that Brienne could reason with Jaime Lannister
Brienne warns that it is too dangerous for her to leave Sansa alone at Winterfell with Petyr
Sansa insists that her guards and men are loyal to her
Brienne warns that Petyr might be bribing Sansa's guards behind her back
Brienne offers to leave her squire Podrick Payne, whose swordsmanship has improved
Sansa insists that she can take care of herself
Sansa enters Arya's quarters
Sansa opens a leather case containing several "faces," including Walder Frey's face
Arya catches Sansa pilfering through her personal effects
Sansa tells Arya that her men are loyal to her
Arya mockingly retorts that Sansa's men are not here
Arya tells Sansa she obtained the faces from the Faceless Men of Braavos
Arya admits she spent time training to be a Faceless Man
Arya forces Sansa to play the lying game
Arya asks Sansa if she thinks Jon is the rightful King
Sansa demands that Arya tell her what the "faces" are
Arya replies that they always wanted to pretend to be other people
Sansa wanted to be a queen
Arya wanted to be a knight
Neither Arya nor Sansa got what they wanted
Arya says the faces allow her to become someone else
Arya toys with the idea of assuming Sansa's face and status
Arya approaches Sansa with her dagger
Arya muses about the possibility of becoming the Lady of Winterfell
Arya relents and leaves Sansa alone with the dagger
Sansa is left disturbed
Daenerys and Tyrion chat in the Chamber of the Painted Table at Dragonstone
Daenerys tells Tyrion she appreciates that he is not a hero
Daenerys compares Tyrion favorably to heroes such as Khal Drogo, Ser Jorah, Daario, and Jon Snow
Tyrion notes that all those individuals fell in love with Daenerys
Daenerys dismisses the implication that Jon Snow is in love with her
Tyrion sarcastically quips that Jon must be staring at Daenerys because he wants a military alliance
Daenerys remarks that Jon is too little for her tastes, then apologizes to Tyrion for insulting his height
Daenerys recognizes that Tyrion is no coward
Daenerys and Tyrion turn their attention to their impending meeting with Cersei in King's Landing
Tyrion admits Cersei cannot be trusted and could be setting a trap
Tyrion says Daenerys will have the Unsullied, the Dothraki, and her dragons at hand
Tyrion warns that if anyone tries to harm Daenerys, King's Landing will be leveled in response
Daenerys wonders whether they should plan to double-cross Cersei, given Cersei's likely deceit
Tyrion firmly opposes any deceit
Tyrion counsels Daenerys that she needs some fear to instill respect, but cannot rule through fear alone
Tyrion cites Cersei, Tywin, and Joffrey as examples of rulers hated for ruling through fear
Tyrion reminds Daenerys of her vision to "break the wheel" and not imitate prior rulers like Aegon Targaryen
Tyrion cautions Daenerys about her temper and impulsiveness, citing the executions of Randyll and Dickon Tarly
Daenerys asserts that executing the Tarlys was necessary
Tyrion argues Daenerys should have tried more merciful means or kept the Tarlys alive longer
Daenerys becomes increasingly agitated by Tyrion's remarks
Tyrion reassures Daenerys that he supports her vision and ideals
Tyrion proposes Daenerys consider a succession plan due to her belief she cannot bear children
Daenerys refuses to consider a succession plan until she is queen
Daenerys coldly blames Tyrion's caution for the loss of Highgarden, Ellaria Sand, and Yara Greyjoy
Jon and his ranging party travel through the lands beyond the wall on their mission to capture a wight
Gendry complains about the bitter cold
Gendry asks Tormund about life as a wildling
Tormund confides with Jon about the foolhardy nature of their mission
Jon discusses his difficult negotiations with Daenerys
Tormund points out that the pride of the Northmen may cost them too many lives, citing Mance Rayder and the Wildlings as an example
Gendry confronts the Brotherhood Without Banners about selling him off as a sacrifice to Melisandre
Sandor Clegane sneers that Gendry should be grateful that he is still alive and points out that Beric Dondarrion has been killed six times
Gendry, unsure what to think, accepts a drink from Thoros's wineskin
Jon and Jorah Mormont walk and chat about their relationships with their fathers, Eddard Stark and Jeor Mormont
Jon and Jorah say their fathers were good and honorable men who did not deserve their deaths
Jon tells Jorah about the brutal death of Jeor at the hands of the Mutineers
Jon tells Jorah that Eddard was beheaded
Jon tries to return Longclaw, Jeor's Valyrian sword, to Jorah
Jorah tells Jon he is not worthy to bear his father's sword, having forfeited any claim to it when he brought shame into his House
Jorah says his father gave the sword to Jon and gives his blessing for Jon and his future children to keep it
Sandor and Tormund trade jabs about sex
Sandor takes offense when Tormund asks about how he burnt his face
Tormund confides in Sandor about his infatuation for Brienne of Tarth
Tormund jokes about having "monstrous" babies with Brienne, to Sandor's bewilderment
Beric and Jon talk about Eddard Stark and their resurrections by worshipers of the Lord of Light
Beric tells Jon he is fighting for life because death is the first and last enemy
Beric warns Jon they must work together to fight death and defend those who cannot defend themselves
Jon reflects on his Night's Watch oath about being the "shield that guards the realms of men" and agrees
Sandor sees the mountain from his vision lying ahead and steers the group in that direction
Jon and his party trudge through a snowstorm
Jon and his party sight a massive snow bear with blue eyes approaching
The snow bear, resurrected by a White Walker, approaches, showing rotting flesh and bone
The snow bear mauls and kills three members of the company
Beric sets the snow bear alight with his flaming sword
The snow bear continues attacking despite being on fire
Thoros puts himself in the bear's path to protect Sandor
Sandor is too terrified of the flames to defend himself
Thoros is caught in the bear's jaws
Jorah kills the snow bear with a dragonglass dagger
Beric cauterizes Thoros' wounds with his flaming sword
The group presses on, following the bear's tracks northward
Jorah asks Thoros about his experience charging with a flaming sword during the Siege of Pyke
Jorah commends Thoros for his drunken bravery
Thoros admits he was so drunk he didn't remember any of the battle
Thoros learns of his exploits from comrades afterwards
The ranging party navigates through the mountain
The party sees a White Walker leading a column of Wights through the canyon below
The party plants a fire and ambushes the White Walker and Wights
Jon kills the White Walker with Longclaw
Most of the Wights associated with the White Walker disintegrate
The rest of the group captures the only unaffected wight
The captured wight screeches for help
Sandor slaps his gloved hand over the wight's mouth
The wight wrenches its face away, tearing off mouth skin into Sandor's glove
Sandor and Jorah are disgusted
Jorah pulls out a bag from his jacket and secures it over the wight's head
Sandor and Tormund bind the wight with a coil of rope
The group realizes the captive's howls have drawn a massive host of wights their way
The group hastens their work
Jon sends Gendry back to Eastwatch-by-the-Sea to bring news to Daenerys, over Gendry's protest
Gendry protests Jon's order
Gendry gives his war hammer to Tormund
Tormund hands the war hammer to Sandor
Sandor carries the wight slung over his shoulder
The wights pursue the group over a lake of weak ice as they flee toward a large stone island
One of the party trips and falls behind
A few of the horde tackle the fallen party member, breaking the ice and falling through
The breaking ice causes a chain reaction around the rock
The horde slows to a stop to avoid the water
Jon and his comrades wait in the middle of the ice, encircled by the army of the undead throughout the night
Gendry reaches Eastwatch's outer gates but collapses from exhaustion
Davos Seaworth and several guards attend to Gendry
Davos asks Gendry what happened
Gendry tells Davos to prepare a raven
Davos orders to bring the maester
Jon and his company awake to find that Thoros has died from his wounds
Beric and Sandor pay their last respects to Thoros
Beric prays for the Lord of Light to guard them
Sandor assures Beric that freezing to death is said to be one of the better ways to die
Jon insists they burn Thoros's body with Beric's flaming sword so Thoros can't be reanimated
The wights watch while their captive Wight struggles under its hood and restraints
Jorah wonders why all the wights but one died when Jon slew the White Walker
Jon guesses the Walker was the one who raised them all and killing him ended the magic that kept the wights animated
Jorah proposes that if they target the White Walkers, they might be able to fight their way free
Jon insists they have to get the wight back as evidence and hopes a raven has reached Daenerys for rescue
Beric offers another solution: to kill the Night King, who has just arrived on horseback, watching them
Beric argues that killing the Night King might destroy all the wights
Jon insists the Night King is too dangerous to risk such a plan
Beric counters that the Lord of Light has not resurrected Jon for no reason
Sandor reminds Beric that they have just lost their priest and Beric is now down to his last life
Beric responds that he's been waiting for death for a long time and wonders if the Lord of Light brought him north to find it
Sandor dismisses Beric's words scornfully
Tyrion receives Gendry's message from Eastwatch
Tyrion implores Daenerys not to leave for the North because it is too risky
Tyrion insists that Jon and his companions knew the risks of their mission
Tyrion warns that if Daenerys dies beyond the Wall, everything they've done will be for nothing
Daenerys dons a cold-weather version of her Targaryen garb
Daenerys counters that Tyrion told her to do nothing before and she lost
Daenerys, dissatisfied with Tyrion's advice, leaves with all three dragons to aid Jon Snow and his ranging party beyond the Wall
Sandor hurls a rock at one of the undead minions, knocking its jaw off
Sandor throws a second rock, which misses and skids across the ice
Both the party and the undead realize the ice is strong enough to support their weight
The horde attacks the group's position in ever-increasing numbers
Sandor holds the undead back with Gendry's hammer
Jon and the other members of the ranging party join in, wielding weapons of fire, dragonglass, or Valyrian steel
Beric sets several of the wights alight with his flaming sword
The ranging party hack and slash at the wights with their blades but are unable to stem the tide
Jon orders the group to fall back to the highest part of the island
Tormund is overwhelmed by several wights and is nearly dragged under the ice
Sandor saves Tormund by dragging him back onto the island
The group continues fighting against the wights
One of the Wildlings falls off a ledge and is ripped apart by the creatures
The creatures begin to scramble up the ledge towards the living
Daenerys arrives with her dragons
Daenerys's dragons attack the wights with dragonfire
Hundreds of wights are burned to ashes
Other wights collapse under the ice, which is melted by dragonfire
Jon and his party rush to Daenerys and her dragon Drogon, dragging their captive wight with them
Viserion and Rhaegal provide covering fire from above
Night King obtains an icy javelin from one of his lieutenants
Night King hurls the javelin at Viserion, scoring a direct hit
Viserion is struck in the neck and plunges helplessly into freefall, shrieking in agony as blood and fire pour from the fatal wound
Drogon and Rhaegal cry out for their brother, but are powerless to help him
Daenerys watches in horror and sorrow
Viserion crashes onto the frozen lake, shattering the ice, and slowly sinks beneath it
The Night King readies another spear
Jon hollers at Daenerys and company to leave with her remaining dragons
Jon is dragged under the ice by two wights
Daenerys and the survivors flee with Drogon and Rhaegal before the Night King can kill them
The Night King hurls the second javelin
Drogon narrowly dodges the javelin
The Night King and his army leave the scene
Jon climbs out of the ice and regains Longclaw
Jon is spotted and pursued by a large horde
Benjen Stark appears on horseback with his flaming flail
Benjen tells Jon to flee on his horse while he stays behind to buy time
Jon flees on Benjen's horse
Jon watches his uncle being overwhelmed by the undead while riding away
Sandor carries the struggling Wight into a boat
Tormund and Beric tell Sandor they will meet again
Sandor retorts he hopes not
Daenerys sends Drogon and Rhaegal to scour the surrounding mountains for Jon
Jorah tells Daenerys it is time to leave
Daenerys insists on waiting a bit longer
a horn blows, signaling a rider approaching
Daenerys looks down from the battlements and sees a wounded Jon Snow approaching on horseback
Davos and Gendry remove the frozen-stiff garments from Jon Snow and tend to his injuries aboard their ship
Daenerys notes the massive scars on Jon’s chest from his previous fatal wounds
Jon wakes to find Daenerys watching over him in his chambers
Jon apologizes for the disastrous ranging party and Viserion's death
Daenerys tells Jon not to apologize because she now knows the army of the dead is real
Daenerys, overcome with emotion, tells Jon the dragons are the only children she will ever have
Daenerys vows that she and Jon will together destroy the Night King
Jon thanks Daenerys for her support, addressing her as "Dany"
Daenerys realizes the last person to call her "Dany" was her brother Viserys, whom she remembers as not being a good person
Jon apologizes and asks if "My Queen" would be more appropriate
Daenerys asks Jon what the Northern lords will make of him bending the knee
Jon assures Daenerys the Northern lords will come to see her as a good person, as he already has
Daenerys, touched by Jon's statement, gently takes Jon's hand
Jon and Daenerys gaze into each other's eyes for a long moment
Daenerys suddenly pulls away and tells Jon to get some rest, then leaves him alone
Hundreds of Wights use several large chains to drag Viserion's corpse out of the frozen lake
The Night King and one of his lieutenants watch the Wights drag Viserion's corpse
The Night King walks over to Viserion's corpse and places his hand upon Viserion's snout
All is quiet for a moment
Viserion's eyes snap open, shining a depthless, icy blue

The_Dragon_and_the_Wolf.action: The Unsullied, led by Grey Worm, line up in tight formation outside the walls of King's Landing
Grey Worm leads the Unsullied
Jaime and Bronn discuss Bronn's discomfort with soldiers without genitalia
Bronn expresses that the idea of soldiers who fight for no promise of sex is alien to him
Hordes of Dothraki ride in, contrasting with the disciplined Unsullied
The Unsullied remain unflinching as hundreds of horses flood past them
The two ancient enemies (Unsullied and Dothraki) are united in cause today
Tyrion, Varys, Missandei, Jon, and Davos nervously sail past Euron Greyjoy's fleet
Sandor Clegane goes below decks to check on their "guest"
"Guest" is riled up as ever
Qyburn informs Cersei of the arrival of Daenerys's party, with no sign of Daenerys herself
Cersei tells Gregor Clegane that if anything goes wrong at the summit, he is to kill Daenerys, Tyrion, and Jon in that order, then dispose of the rest as he pleases
The group arrives and are escorted to the Dragonpit, the location of the summit
Tyrion reconnects with Bronn
Bronn concedes it is good to see Tyrion again
Tyrion and Bronn greet Podrick Payne
Podrick had arrived earlier with Brienne of Tarth
Podrick goes off with Bronn
Brienne hangs back to talk with Sandor
Brienne and Sandor acknowledge they only fought to protect Arya
Brienne tells Sandor that Arya doesn't need looking out for anymore
Brienne tells Sandor to reserve his concern for whoever stands in Arya's way
Sandor seems amused by Brienne's comment
various factions meet at the Dragonpit
Cersei, Jaime, Qyburn, and Euron represent the Iron Throne
Jon, Davos, and Brienne represent the North
Daenerys's court is present
Cersei demands to know where her rival is
Daenerys makes a dramatic entrance on Drogon's back, with Rhaegal flying overhead
Sandor comes face-to-helmet with his brother Gregor for the first time in years
Sandor wonders what has happened to Gregor, then dismisses it as irrelevant
Sandor promises Gregor that he still intends to end him
Sandor leaves to retrieve the captured wight
Tyrion begins to address the summit
Euron postures, threatens to kill Yara unless Theon yields, and derides Tyrion's dwarfism
Tyrion and Theon answer Euron's taunts with their own
Euron remarks that Tyrion would have been killed at birth in the Iron Islands
Jaime orders Euron to sit down
Euron disregards the warning
Cersei repeats the warning to Euron
Euron returns to his seat, subdued
Tyrion and Jon attempt to convince Cersei of the greater threat coming from the North
Tyrion and Jon attempt to convince Cersei of the greater threat coming from the North
Tyrion states that one million people live crammed together in the city
Cersei callously responds that becoming wights will be an improvement for most city dwellers
Cersei’s flippancy noticeably angers Jon
Cersei refuses to believe the claims and dismisses them as a ploy
Sandor returns with the crate containing the wight
Sandor opens the crate and is visibly frightened as he peers inside
Jaime is worried by Sandor’s reaction
Cersei watches the proceedings with a smug smirk
Sandor kicks the crate and knocks it over
The wight clambers out and charges toward Cersei
Cersei and her allies recoil in horror
Sandor pulls the wight back on a chain, then slices it in half when it attacks him
The Stark and Targaryen delegations watch emotionlessly
The Lannisters look on in shock as the wight’s upper half continues to move
The wight tries to crawl toward Sandor, who slices off one of its hands
Qyburn briefly examines the severed hand
Jon takes the wight’s severed hand and uses a torch to demonstrate fire’s effect
Davos provides Jon with a torch
Jon uses a dragonglass dagger to kill the wight’s upper half
Jon states that if they don’t win, everyone in Westeros will share this fate
Daenerys states she didn’t believe in wights until she saw them herself
Jaime, horrified, asks how many wights are coming
Daenerys tells Jaime the army of the dead numbers at least 100,000
Euron asks if wights can swim
Jon responds that wights cannot swim
Euron announces to Cersei his intention to withdraw his fleet to the Iron Isles
Cersei demands an explanation from Euron
Euron declares nothing has terrified him until now
Euron tells Daenerys to retreat to her island and to find him if they are the only two left alive
Euron departs
Cersei admits Euron is right to be afraid, but a fool to think running and hiding will accomplish anything
Cersei agrees the army of the dead is a threat that supersedes all other concerns
Cersei offers terms: she will not withdraw her troops, but guarantees they will not hinder Targaryen or Northern forces during the battle against the White Walkers
Cersei refuses to deal with Daenerys
Cersei calls on Jon, as King in the North and Eddard Stark's son, to keep the truce and stay out of any future conflict between Cersei and Daenerys
Jon says he cannot serve two queens and reveals he has already declared for Daenerys
All three Lannisters present are infuriated by Jon's declaration
Cersei declares there will be no truce if it is just her and Daenerys
Cersei storms out, content to let the Starks and Targaryens battle the undead alone and then deal with the victor
Brienne grabs Jaime and begs him to reconsider, saying what they've seen goes beyond Houses, honor, and thrones
Jaime doesn't disagree, but walks away, not knowing what to say to convince Cersei
Daenerys criticizes Jon for declaring his allegiance in public
Tyrion criticizes Jon for declaring his allegiance in public
Tyrion suggests that learning to lie a little might be a good skill
Daenerys points out that leaving without an alliance would make their sacrifices, including Viserion's death, worthless
Jon responds that honesty may or may not have contributed to his father's death, but if no one speaks the truth, everyone's word is worthless and lies won't help them win
Tyrion reluctantly decides to go and try to talk reason into Cersei alone
Daenerys protests, fearing Cersei may have Tyrion killed
Jon protests, fearing Cersei may have Tyrion killed
Tyrion insists it's the only way if they don't want everything they've done to be for nothing and bids them wait
Tyrion, escorted by Ser Gregor, meets Jaime in the Red Keep
Jaime confirms he believes the threat of the dead but has been unable to convince Cersei
Jaime talks at Cersei until she denounces him as an idiot and throws him out of her chambers
Tyrion quips that he is the bigger idiot for willingly entering a room with a woman who has tried to have him killed before
Jaime wishes Tyrion luck, aware this could be their final meeting, and steps aside to let Tyrion enter Cersei's office
Gregor closes the door behind Tyrion
Cersei and Tyrion trade barbs once alone
Cersei mocks Tyrion's affection for another "foreign whore"
Tyrion quips that Cersei must be frustrated Daenerys isn't someone she can "beat, abduct or intimidate"
Cersei angrily blames Tyrion's murder of Tywin for the deaths of her younger children and the destruction of House Lannister's future
Tyrion maintains Tywin deserved to die for a lifetime of abuse and for trying to execute him on false charges
Tyrion insists he loved Myrcella and Tommen almost as much as Cersei and regrets what happened to them
Tyrion calls Cersei's bluff, claiming Gregor should kill him if she truly blames him
Cersei refrains from giving the order to kill Tyrion
Tyrion, relieved, heads straight for the wine
Cersei angrily accuses Tyrion of plotting to bring down House Lannister with Jon and Daenerys
Tyrion loses patience, retorting he didn't know Jon had sworn allegiance to Daenerys and that without his intervention, Daenerys would have razed the Red Keep
Cersei questions why Tyrion stopped Daenerys
Tyrion replies he believes Daenerys will make the world better and serves her to curb her excesses, unlike Cersei
Cersei retorts her only interest is protecting those she cares about, musing about Euron fleeing somewhere safe
Tyrion realizes that Cersei is pregnant
Daenerys and Jon discuss the dragons and how her ancestors caged them, causing the dragons to become less impressive
Jon questions Daenerys's assertion of infertility
Daenerys admits she never got an informed opinion about her condition except from Mirri Maz Duur
Their conversation is interrupted by the return of all three Lannisters
Cersei agrees to work with Daenerys
Cersei decides the Lannister army will march north to fight alongside the Starks and Targaryens
Jaime meets with his commanders to discuss the logistics of moving the army north
Cersei enters the map room and asks Jaime what he is doing
Cersei dismisses the commanders
Cersei tells Jaime he really is the stupidest Lannister
Jaime is shocked and listens as Cersei explains Euron’s true intentions
Cersei explains that Euron has gone to Essos to ferry the Golden Company back to Westeros
Cersei reveals she intentionally leaked her pregnancy to Tyrion
Cersei states her plan to let their enemies exhaust themselves against the army of the dead, then use the Golden Company to clean up
Jaime becomes furious that Cersei and Euron plotted behind his back
Cersei angrily accuses Jaime of plotting with Tyrion in favor of her enemies
Jaime, reeling from the accusation, reminds Cersei that someone will win the battle in the North and come south afterwards
Jaime explains the consequences if the dead or the living win
Cersei is indifferent to Jaime’s warnings
Jaime finally sees Cersei for the power-mad narcissist she is, recalling Olenna Tyrell’s warnings
Jaime declares he will fight to honor his pledge
Jaime tries to leave but finds his way blocked by Ser Gregor
Cersei furiously insists she will kill Jaime as a traitor if he tries to leave
Jaime calls Cersei’s bluff and storms out
Cersei does not give the order to kill Jaime
Jaime rides out of King's Landing, having discarded his Lannister armour for rougher, less recognisable travelling garb
Jaime realizes how conspicuous he is and nervously pulls a glove over his golden hand
A single snowflake lands on Jaime's glove
Jaime looks up and sees snow begin to fall from the grey sky, carried by a chill wind
Snow begins to cover King's Landing: the streets, houses, ruins of the Great Sept, little skulls in the Dragonpit, and Cersei's map in the Red Keep
Jaime rides away from King's Landing
Winter arrives in the south
Sansa discusses the potential threat of Arya with Petyr Baelish
Petyr Baelish tries to manipulate Sansa, encouraging her to think as he does
Petyr Baelish tells Sansa to ask herself what Arya's worst possible motivation is
Sansa appears overcome with horror at the thought that Arya would want to take her place and reign as Lady of Winterfell
Sansa seems to decide to do something about Arya
Petyr Baelish is quietly delighted by Sansa's reaction
Sansa spends a long time reflecting on her course of action on the battlements
Sansa orders Arya be brought to the great hall
Sansa and Bran are seated at the great table in the hall
The hall is lined with Stark and Arryn men and key lords such as Yohn Royce and Baelish
Baelish watches the proceedings with a malicious little smile
Arya is brought into the hall
Arya asks Sansa if she really wants to do this
Sansa replies that it is about justice, not what she wants
Sansa rattles off a list of crimes perpetrated against House Stark
Sansa asks Baelish how he intends to answer the charges
All eyes turn towards Baelish
Baelish is thrown and tries to figure out what is going on
Sansa reveals Baelish’s murder of Lysa Arryn and his use of Lysa to murder Jon Arryn
Sansa uses Baelish’s own words against him
Sansa accuses Baelish of orchestrating the conflict between the Starks and the Lannisters, including the betrayal and death of Eddard Stark
Baelish tries to deny Bran's accusation
Bran uses the Sight to recall the exact words Baelish said as he held the knife to Ned's throat
Baelish realizes he has lost control of the situation and the trial is a drumhead
Baelish demands Lord Royce take him to safety as Lord Protector of the Vale
Lord Royce refuses Baelish with the words "I think not"
Baelish falls to his knees and pleads for his life, insisting he loved Cat and now loves Sansa
Sansa, unswayed, sentences Baelish to death and promises never to forget his lessons, sharing a look with Arya
Baelish tries to speak
Arya walks up and slits Baelish's throat with the Valyrian steel dagger
Baelish bleeds out and slumps dead to the floor
Northern and Vale men and women watch dispassionately
The War of the Five Kings ends and justice is meted out on Baelish
Sansa and Arya discuss Littlefinger's plots and how much they have changed as people
Arya tells Sansa that she wouldn't have been able to survive what Sansa did
Sansa disagrees, saying Arya is the strongest person she knows
Sansa and Arya muse on another of their father's sayings about lone wolves and wolf packs
Sansa and Arya realize the truth of their father's words and that their remaining family needs to stick together
Samwell Tarly and Gilly arrive at Winterfell
Sam hears that Bran is back and calls on him
Sam recalls meeting Bran at the Nightfort years earlier
Bran is glad to see Sam but is surprised to find him at Winterfell
Sam reiterates his loyalty to Jon
Bran reveals the truth of Jon's origins: he was born to Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark at a Tower in Dorne, and is not a Snow but a Sand
Sam realizes that isn't true, recalling the entry from High Septon Maynard's private journal about annulling Rhaegar's marriage
Bran expresses his doubts
Sam reiterates the private nature of the journal and encourages Bran to use the Sight to confirm it
Bran uses the Sight and finds the wedding of Lyanna and Rhaegar
Bran sees Lyanna wedding Rhaegar in a Faith of the Seven ceremony before a heart tree
Bran wargs forward to the Tower of Joy and hears Lyanna's dying words
Bran pieces the truth together and declares that Rhaegar never raped Lyanna, and that she loved him and bore him a son: Aegon Targaryen
Bran realizes that Robert's Rebellion, the deaths of his grandfather and uncle, and the reign of House Baratheon was all for nothing and built on a lie, and that Jon is the heir to the Iron Throne
Daenerys and her court discuss logistics in the Chamber of the Painted Table
It is established that it will take the Dothraki a fortnight to reach Winterfell
The plan is made for Jon and the Unsullied to cross the sea by ship and meet at White Harbor
Jorah points out that the North is not really safer for Daenerys than anywhere else
Jorah suggests Daenerys fly to Winterfell to avoid potential danger
Jon counters that Daenerys should ride with them so the North can see her as a liberator and ally
Daenerys considers Jon’s suggestion
Daenerys decides to sail to Winterfell with Jon
Jorah throws Daenerys a look, suspecting a different reason for her decision
Daenerys notices Jorah’s look but avoids it
Theon talks privately with Jon after the meeting
Theon tells Jon that Jon always seemed to know the right thing to do growing up at Winterfell
Jon explains he has made mistakes and done things he regrets

Theon replies that Jon hasn't made mistakes compared to him and confides he has always felt torn between his Greyjoy heritage and Stark upbringing, tried to do the right thing, but was uncertain about the right step
Jon expresses anger about Theon's betrayal against his family
Jon tells Theon that Ned Stark was more a father to Theon than Balon Greyjoy ever was
Jon relents and tells Theon he never lost Ned, Ned is part of both of them, and Theon is both a Greyjoy and a Stark
Jon says he can forgive Theon for what is in his power to forgive
Theon confides that when he was Ramsay's prisoner, his sister Yara tried to save him and he needs to save her now
Jon encourages Theon to save Yara
Theon returns to the remaining Ironborn, who regard him with disgust
Theon tells his men that Euron has his sister and that they must go back for her
Harrag shuts Theon down, declaring him a coward
Harrag proclaims that they are going to go where they can rape and pillage as usual
Theon reminds them that Yara has forbidden their old ways
Harrag threatens Theon and begins to assault him, telling him to stay down
Theon is knocked down but barely reacts to the blows
Theon gets up to attack
Harrag continues to beat Theon, insisting he stay down or be killed
Theon defies Harrag and attacks him
Harrag grabs Theon and kicks him several times in the crotch
Theon smiles as he gets the upper hand, smashing Harrag’s face and beating him into submission
The other Ironborn relent, accepting Theon and his mission
Theon wades into the sea to wash his wounds in salt water, embracing his identity as an ironborn
Jon knocks on the door of Daenerys's cabin
Daenerys answers the door and meets Jon's gaze without words
Jon enters the cabin, eyes still locked with Daenerys, and shuts the door
Jon and Daenerys, unaware of their biological relationship, give into their passion and consummate their relationship
Jon and Daenerys, unaware of their biological relationship, give into their passion and consummate their relationship
Tyrion is on his way to speak with his queen
Tyrion sees Jon enter the cabin
Tyrion becomes silently concerned about the possible implications
Tyrion walks away
Tormund and Beric Dondarrion review the defenses atop the Wall at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea
Tormund remarks that the crows say he'll get used to the height, but admits it'll probably be a while
Tormund and Beric see movement at the edge of the Haunted Forest
A White Walker emerges atop an undead horse
A horde of wights follows the White Walker
More White Walkers emerge
The Night Watch's horns sound three times
The army of the dead stops some distance from the foot of the Wall
Tormund looks relieved
Everyone on the Wall stops in horror as they hear a screeching roar and the thumping of huge wings
A screeching roar mixed with the heavy thumping of huge wings is heard
Viserion swoops down with the Night King riding on his back
Viserion demolishes some of the scouting platforms atop the Wall with blue flame
Viserion turns his flames against the Wall itself
Tormund shouts for everyone to run
Night's Watch brothers and wildlings desperately try to evacuate as ice melts and rock crumbles
Tormund and Beric watch in horror as the eastern extremity of the Wall collapses under Viserion's breath and crashes into the sea
The Wall collapses, leaving a gap between the Wall and the sea
White Walkers direct the wights to begin their march into the North
Wights, giants, and other monstrosities march by the thousands into the North
Viserion and the Night King fly overhead to lead the march
The Great War begins

Winterfell_(Game_of_Thrones).action: Jon, Daenerys, and the Targaryen army pass through the winter town outside Winterfell
The inhabitants warily watch Daenerys's foreign army of Unsullied and Dothraki
Arya smiles when she spots Jon
Arya appears confused and worried when she sees the Hound with them
Arya brightens up again when she sees Gendry
Jon tells Daenerys he had warned her Northerners are wary of outsiders
Daenerys's dragons fly above them
The people flee in fear
Arya looks up in awe
Daenerys smiles at her dragons overhead
Sansa warily watches from Winterfell's battlements as Drogon and Rhaegal circle above the castle
Jon sees his younger brother Bran, whom he last saw in a coma, and quickly dismounts
Jon kisses Bran's forehead
Jon embraces Sansa, who keeps a wary eye on his queen
Sansa keeps a wary eye on Daenerys
Jon asks about Arya's whereabouts
Sansa replies that Arya is lurking
Jon introduces Daenerys and Sansa to one another
Sansa formally welcomes Daenerys to Winterfell
Daenerys thanks Sansa and remarks that Jon told her about the beauty of the North and Sansa
Bran interrupts, saying there is no time for this
Bran informs them that the Night King has Daenerys's dragon, Viserion, and that the Army of the Dead has breached the Wall and is marching south
Jon tries to organize the defense against the undead in the Great Hall
Lyanna Mormont voices the anger of many lords toward Jon bending the knee to Daenerys
Jon answers that he said he would protect the North and that they need allies
Tyrion insists they'd all be dead already without Jon
Tyrion says the remaining Lannister army will soon arrive to reinforce them
lords resume their objections
Tyrion insists they must fight together against the White Walkers and their undead army
Sansa asks how they are meant to feed Daenerys's army
Sansa says she ensured their stores would last through winter but didn't account for Dothraki, Unsullied, and two dragons
Sansa scoffs and asks what dragons eat
Daenerys quips, "Whatever they want"
Jon visits Winterfell's godswood and stands in front of the heart tree
Arya appears seemingly out of nowhere and startles Jon
Arya says Jon used to be taller
Jon turns and asks Arya how she was able to sneak up on him
Arya asks Jon how he survived a knife to the heart
Jon responds, "I didn't"
Arya runs to Jon and they embrace, Jon lifting Arya in his arms
Jon remarks that Arya still has Needle
Arya unsheathes Needle
Jon asks Arya if she's ever had to use Needle
Arya quietly responds, "Once or twice."
Jon shows Arya Longclaw and facetiously asks if she’s jealous
Arya admires Longclaw but remarks it's too heavy for her
Jon confides he could have used Arya's help with Sansa in the Great Hall
Arya surmises that Sansa doesn't like Daenerys
Jon says Sansa thinks she's smarter than everyone
Arya defends Sansa, stating she is the smartest person she knows and is defending her family
Arya tells Jon not to forget he's family too and hugs him
Tyrion encounters Sansa in the courtyard
Tyrion notes he hasn't seen Sansa since Joffrey's wedding, during which Joffrey was assassinated and Sansa was whisked away by Littlefinger
Tyrion reflects that Sansa fleeing after the assassination made him look more incriminating
Tyrion and Sansa discuss the soon to arrive Lannister Army
Sansa points out she used to think Tyrion was the most clever man she'd ever met
Sansa tells Tyrion he is foolish to trust his sister Cersei
Tyrion, Varys, and Davos observe Jon and Daenerys talking from a distance
Tyrion, Varys, and Davos remark that a marriage-alliance might help cement their alliance
Daenerys and Jon inspect Winterfell's defenses
Daenerys and Jon discuss Sansa
Daenerys mentions that Sansa doesn't like her
Jon assures Daenerys that Sansa just doesn't know her yet and used to dislike him
Dothraki scouts ride up to tell Daenerys that her dragons are not eating much
Jon and Daenerys ride out to see the dragons
Daenerys says the dragons are upset to be in the North
Daenerys mounts her black dragon Drogon
Daenerys asks Jon if he'd like to ride her green dragon, Rhaegal
Jon hesitantly climbs on top of Rhaegal
Rhaegal accepts Jon as a rider
Jon and Daenerys go on a lengthy, romantic flight over the countryside
Jon initially struggles to fly on Rhaegal
Jon and Daenerys land at a waterfall and share an intimate moment
Arya visits the castle forge
Gendry finishes making a new dragonglass-axe for Sandor
Arya and the Hound are surprised to see each other
Arya and the Hound exchange gruff-but-toying insults
The Hound departs on good terms
Arya speaks with Gendry again
Gendry is flustered by how Arya has grown up
Arya says it is good to see Gendry
Sansa informs Jon that Lord Glover has chosen to remain at Deepwood Motte and not join the muster at Winterfell
Lord Glover holds off the Army of the Dead at Deepwood Motte in protest of Jon bending the knee to Daenerys
Jon becomes furious at the news
Sansa responds that Glover is angry over Jon's decision and that she shares this anger
Jon tells Sansa that defeating the Army of the Dead is impossible without allies and that Daenerys will be a good queen
Sansa questions Jon if he bent the knee to save the North or because he loves Daenerys
Daenerys goes to the library with Jorah Mormont to thank Samwell Tarly for saving Jorah's life
Sam brings up that he stole his father's Valyrian steel sword, Heartsbane
Daenerys awkwardly explains that she defeated Sam's father in battle and executed him when he refused to bend the knee
Sam has a mixed reaction to the news about his father
Sam says the sword would then pass to his younger brother Dickon, who is a good and honorable man
Daenerys explains that Dickon refused to leave his father's side and was executed as well
Sam, stunned and devastated, rushes out of the room in tears
Sam stumbles into Bran Stark, who is waiting in the courtyard "for an old friend"
Bran urges Sam to tell Jon of his real parentage, as Jon trusts Sam more than anyone
Sam finds Jon in the crypts, where Jon is visiting the tomb of his father Eddard Stark
Sam tells Jon that Daenerys executed his father and brother
Jon sympathizes with Sam
Jon defends Daenerys's decision, explaining he has also had to execute people who disobeyed him, and so must she as queen
Sam is not appeased
Sam reveals to Jon that Jon is the rightful heir to the Iron Throne, not Daenerys
Sam explains that Jon's mother was Lyanna Stark and his father was Rhaegar Targaryen
Jon is stunned and refuses to believe this
Jon insists that Eddard was the most honorable man he ever knew and wouldn't lie
Sam counters that Eddard promised Lyanna to keep Jon safe and hide his identity
Sam explains that Robert Baratheon would have killed Jon if he knew Rhaegar's heir lived
Sam and Bran discover that Jon should be "Aegon Targaryen, Sixth of his Name" and the true ruler of the Seven Kingdoms
A hooded stranger rides into the Winterfell courtyard
Jaime is revealed as the hooded stranger, fulfilling his promise to fight the White Walkers
Cersei decides to abandon her enemies to the mercy of the undead
Jaime looks around the courtyard
Jaime is surprised to see Bran waiting for him
Bran waits for Jaime in the courtyard
Jaime remembers that he shoved Bran out a window and crippled him in this castle years ago
Euron sails the Iron Fleet back to King's Landing from the Free Cities
Euron transports the Golden Company, an elite sellsword company, across the Narrow Sea
Euron keeps his niece Yara Greyjoy as a prisoner on his ship
Euron taunts Yara
He presents Cersei Lannister with the Company's commander, Harry Strickland
Harry Strickland says he has brought 20,000 men and 2,000 horses to King's Landing, despite a few deaths on the voyage
Euron kills a few men who either cheated him at dice, or caught him cheating at dice
Cersei expresses confusion about the lack of elephants
Harry Strickland explains that elephants are ferocious but not fit for long sea voyages
Strickland leaves
Euron arrogantly propositions Cersei to have sex as a reward for his completed task
Cersei is not amused by Euron's brashness and tells him so
Cersei accepts Euron's proposition because she needs her remaining allies
Cersei and Euron have sex
Cersei is still bitter about her lack of elephants after they have finished
Cersei sips at a glass of wine
Euron asks Cersei how he compared to her former husband King Robert Baratheon
Cersei says Robert was only interested in his own pleasure and did not know his way around a woman's body, while Euron was more adventurous
Euron asks how he did compared to Cersei's brother Jaime
Cersei refuses to answer Euron's question about Jaime
Euron leaves, boasting that he will soon impregnate Cersei with a prince
Theon and his small remaining crew stage a sneak attack on Euron's ship, the Silence, taking advantage of Euron's absence
Theon and his crew silently pick off most of the guards with bows and arrows
Theon bursts into Yara's chamber with an axe and frees her
Yara headbutts Theon for letting her get captured
Yara embraces Theon
Theon and Yara flee the ship
Yara says she intends to sail back to the Iron Islands to rally her remaining supporters and retake the home islands
Yara notices Theon wants her permission to leave to help the Starks defend Winterfell
Yara says she will secure the Iron Islands as a fallback position for Daenerys
Yara grants Theon's request to leave
Yara and Theon embrace
Theon departs
Bronn gets ready to have sex with three prostitutes: Marei, Dirah, and Craya
The prostitutes gossip about how many Lannister men the Targaryen dragon burned alive in the last battle
Bronn points out that he wounded the dragon during the battle using a scorpion-launcher
Marei quips that Bronn was "brave" and brusquely shoves him down and mounts him
The prostitutes continue to gossip
Qyburn interrupts Bronn and the prostitutes
Qyburn apologizes and says Cersei told him to hurry
Qyburn tells Bronn that Cersei wants to re-hire him for a new assassination
Qyburn points out that Bronn’s previous rewards were taken away and Cersei wants to correct that mistake
Qyburn tells Bronn there is a wagon laden with gold waiting outside as advance payment
Qyburn presents Bronn with Joffrey Baratheon’s former crossbow
Qyburn says Cersei wants Bronn to use the crossbow to kill Tyrion and Jaime if they survive the undead assault
Tormund, Beric Dondarrion, and the survivors of the garrison at Eastwatch-by-the-Sea make their way south to Last Hearth
They find the castle totally abandoned, with damage from a battle but no bodies, only blood
The environment implies the White Walkers already raised the dead as undead wights
They investigate the castle tensely
They stumble into and nearly fight Eddison Tollett
Eddison Tollett, following Jon Snow's message, leads the remaining garrison from Castle Black down to Winterfell
Tormund and Beric ask Edd if he found anyone
Edd leads Tormund and Beric to the main hall
Edd shows them Lord Ned Umber's corpse pinned to a wall, surrounded by a spiral design made of severed limbs
Tormund, Beric, and Edd realize it is a warning from the Night King
Tormund and Beric worry that the White Walkers' army will reach Winterfell first
Edd's men bring horses so they can outpace the wights
Umber re-animates as a wight and tries to grab Tormund but cannot free himself from the wall
Beric sets his sword on fire to kill the wight
The wight bursts into flame at the sword's touch, igniting the spiral symbol

A_Knight_of_the_Seven_Kingdoms_(Game_of_.action: Jaime stands still while Daenerys speaks
Daenerys recounts that her brother told her a bedtime story about the man who murdered their father
Daenerys says her brother told her other stories about what they would do to that man once they took back the Seven Kingdoms
Daenerys remarks that Cersei pledged to send her army north, but only Jaime arrived, so it appears Cersei lied
Jaime makes it clear that Cersei lied to him as well and never intended to send her army north
Jaime tells Daenerys that Cersei has Euron Greyjoy's fleet and 20,000 fresh troops from the Golden Company
Jaime explains that even if they defeat the Army of the Dead, Cersei will have plenty of men to kill the survivors
Daenerys asks Jaime why he came
Jaime states he promised to fight for the living and intends to keep that promise
Tyrion tries to step in
Daenerys quickly stops Tyrion
Tyrion argues that Jaime’s choice to come to Winterfell means something
Daenerys suggests Jaime came north to worm his way into her confidence and potentially assassinate her
Sansa surprisingly takes Daenerys’s side, saying they can’t trust Jaime and listing his past crimes
Jaime retorts that if they want an apology, they won’t get it, and says he did everything for his house and family and would do it again
Bran interjects: "The things we do for love."
Daenerys asks Jaime why he’s abandoned House Lannister
Jaime quotes Brienne: "This goes beyond loyalty. This is about survival."
Brienne stands and steps forward on Jaime's behalf
Brienne tells Daenerys that Jaime is a man of honor
Brienne explains Jaime defended her from Locke and the Boltons at the cost of his hand
Brienne tells Sansa that without Jaime, she wouldn't be alive
Brienne says Jaime armed and armored her, and sent her to find Sansa because he swore an oath to Catelyn
Sansa agrees that they should let Jaime stay, without seeking Daenerys's approval
Daenerys looks at Jon and asks his advice
Jon says they need every man they can get
Daenerys acquiesces
Tyrion sighs in relief
Grey Worm gives Jaime back his sword
Jaime thanks Daenerys
Sansa leaves
Daenerys turns to face Jon, but Jon avoids her
Jon awkwardly avoids Daenerys
Daenerys leaves, followed by Tyrion, Jorah, and Varys
Tyrion, Jorah, and Varys follow Daenerys
Daenerys vents her anger about Cersei's betrayal to Tyrion
Daenerys accuses Tyrion of knowing Cersei was lying or being duped
Daenerys and Tyrion agree Tyrion was a fool in this matter
Daenerys threatens to find another Hand if Tyrion cannot help her claim the Iron Throne
Tyrion opines to Jorah and Varys that one of them might get his position soon
The men are hard at work forging Dragonglass weapons at the Forge
Gendry is busy working and does not see Arya watching him at first
Arya watches Gendry with piqued interest
Gendry asks Arya if she has something better to do
Arya replies, "Have you made my weapon yet?"
Gendry remarks he'll get to Arya's weapon after making a few thousand more arrowhead style weapons
Arya insists Gendry make her weapon first and that it should be stronger than the one he's currently working on
Gendry says it will be safer down in the crypt anyway
Arya asks if Gendry will be in the crypt
Gendry says no
Arya questions, "but you're a fighter?"
Gendry says he's done his share and fought a few
Gendry describes the Army of the Dead to Arya
Gendry tells Arya that the Army of the Dead are much different than rapers or murderers, that they are simply 'death'
Arya says coyly that she knows 'death'
Arya lifts a dragonglass arrowhead weapon and throws it like a dagger, hitting a door frame in the near distance
Arya throws another dagger, landing it right beside the first
Gendry is surprised
Arya says, "I look forward to seeing this one"
Arya throws the last dagger, landing it exactly beside the other two weapons
Arya walks past Gendry
Arya asks one last time for her weapon
Gendry hastily replies, "I'll get right on it."
Bran sits in his wheelchair beneath the heart tree in the snow-covered godswood
Jaime slowly approaches Bran
Jaime apologizes to Bran for what he did
Bran replies that Jaime wasn't sorry then
Bran adds that Jaime was protecting his family
Jaime says he isn't that person anymore
Bran states that Jaime still would be if he hadn't pushed him out the window, and that he'd still be Brandon Stark
Jaime asks Bran if he isn't Brandon Stark
Bran replies that he isn't, and that he is something else now
Jaime asks why Bran isn't angry at him
Bran says he isn't angry at anyone
Jaime asks why Bran didn't tell anyone the truth
Bran replies that Jaime wouldn't have been able to help them in the war if they had been able to murder him first
Jaime asks about 'afterwards'
Bran says, "how do you know there is an afterwards?"
Tyrion walks about the castle grounds inspecting everything
Jaime approaches Tyrion
Tyrion and Jaime greet each other with "here we are"
Tyrion and Jaime greet each other with "here we are"
One of the Stark military men spits down near the Lannister brothers from a staircase
Tyrion quips "and the masses rejoice"
Jaime asks Tyrion what he thinks of their new Queen Daenerys
Tyrion reminds Jaime that Daenerys is now his queen as well
Tyrion remarks that people remember what happened the last time Targaryens brought dragons north
Tyrion states people will come around once they see Daenerys is different
Jaime asks Tyrion if he is sure about Daenerys
Tyrion says yes
Jaime states Daenerys didn't seem to be sure of Tyrion
Tyrion agrees and says he doesn't blame her
Tyrion finds out through Jaime that Cersei's pregnancy wasn't a lie
Tyrion admits that Cersei told him she hoped her pregnancy could be a second chance for her and Jaime
Tyrion admits he allowed himself to be swayed by Cersei
Jaime tells Tyrion to not be too hard on himself, saying that Cersei fooled him more than anybody
Tyrion states that Cersei never fooled him
Tyrion says Jaime always knew what Cersei was and loved her anyway
Tyrion tells Jaime he never imagined he'd die at Winterfell, referencing a past joke between them
Tyrion finds black humor in the idea that being torn apart by the undead will deny Cersei the satisfaction of having him killed
Jaime is distracted and walks away to look at Brienne in the distance
Brienne oversees sword practicing in the yard
Jaime walks out of the castle gate onto the grounds beyond the walls
Men, including Podrick Payne, practice with swords, build siege engines, or lay traps for the Army of the Dead
Jaime reaches Brienne and they greet one another
Jaime tells Brienne she is commanding the left flank during battle
Brienne states the ground is great and the rise would give good advantage
Brienne turns to Jaime and asks what he's doing, noting they've never had such a long conversation without him insulting her
Jaime admits he came to Winterfell because he's not the fighter he used to be and says he'd be honored to serve under Brienne's command, if she'd have him
Jorah enters the castle to speak to Daenerys on Tyrion's behalf
Jorah tells Daenerys she should give Tyrion a second chance
Jorah and Daenerys remark on why Jorah isn't Daenerys's Hand of the Queen
Daenerys remarks that if Jorah hadn't gone away, he would have become her Hand instead of Tyrion
Jorah insists Daenerys made the right choice in naming Tyrion her Hand
Jorah explains that Tyrion has made mistakes but learns from them and has Daenerys' best interests at heart
Jorah advises Daenerys to forgive Tyrion
Jorah offers another suggestion of advice
Daenerys finds Sansa conversing with Lord Royce in Winterfell's library
Lord Royce advocates for sealing the gates as soon as troops are deployed outside the walls
Sansa favors keeping the gates open as long as possible to let refugees reach Winterfell
Daenerys requests a private audience with Sansa
Lord Royce leaves
Daenerys remarks she thought she and Sansa were about to agree regarding Jaime
Daenerys asks Sansa why she changed her mind
Sansa replies that she trusts Brienne's judgement completely
Daenerys responds that she wishes she could have faith like that in her own advisors
Sansa tells Daenerys that Tyrion is a good man and was decent to her in King's Landing
Daenerys replies she chose Tyrion as Hand for his intelligence and ruthlessness, not just his goodness
Daenerys adds that Tyrion never should have trusted Cersei
Sansa replies that Daenerys never should have trusted Cersei either
Daenerys acknowledges she and Sansa have similar experiences as women rulers with complicated families
Sansa agrees with Daenerys
Daenerys notes they are still at odds and realizes it is because of Sansa's brother
Sansa admits she is wary of Daenerys because Jon loves her and men do foolish things for love
Daenerys replies she has only known one goal— the Iron Throne— until she met Jon
Daenerys explains she brought her armies north to fight the Army of the Dead
Sansa responds she should have thanked Daenerys when she arrived
Daenerys confides she is in the North because she loves Jon and trusts him completely
Sansa asks Daenerys who the first man she trusted was
Daenerys replies, "Someone taller," making both Sansa and Daenerys laugh
Sansa asks what will happen to the North if Daenerys takes the Iron Throne, since she vowed never to bow to another ruler
Maester Wolkan interrupts before Daenerys and Sansa can discuss further
Theon arrives at Winterfell
Theon waits in Winterfell's Great Hall with a detachment of Ironborn
Theon informs Daenerys of Yara's plans to reclaim the Iron Islands from Euron in her name
Theon states he has come back to fight to defend Winterfell if Lady Stark will have him
Sansa embraces Theon emotionally
Missandei experiences racial prejudice from hostile Northerners
Grey Worm asks Missandei if she would want to stay in Westeros once Daenerys has the Iron Throne
Grey Worm says he will fight for his queen until the war is done but there is no place for them in Westeros
Grey Worm asks Missandei if she wants to grow old in Westeros or see something else
Missandei admits she'd like to see the beaches of Naath again
Grey Worm promises to take Missandei back to Naath and have the Unsullied protect the natives
The sound of a horn blowing brings Jon out into the courtyard
Jon goes out into the courtyard
Jon finds Edd, Beric, and the rest of the Night's Watch dismounting from their horses
Edd, Beric, and the Night's Watch dismount from their horses
Jon moves to greet them
Tormund tackles Jon, ecstatic to see his "little crow" again

The new arrivals bring bad news: Last Hearth has fallen, the Umbers are now part of the army of the dead, as is anyone who has not reached Winterfell by now, and the undead will reach Winterfell before daybreak
The defenders gather to make their plans for the battle to come as night falls
Jon notes that the army of the dead outnumbers the living too heavily for a conventional victory, even with dragonglass, Valyrian steel, and defenses
Jaime asks what they can do
Jon suggests making the Night King their primary target, reasoning that killing him will destroy his army
Jaime predicts the Night King will never risk himself on the battlefield
Bran interjects that the Night King will come for him, as he has tried to kill the Three-Eyed Raven many times before
Sam asks why the Night King wants to kill Bran
Bran explains the Night King wants to erase all memory of the world of men, which Bran possesses as the Three-Eyed Raven
Jon states they will place Bran in the crypts for his safety
Bran refuses and declares he will wait in the godswood to lure the Night King out
Sansa and Arya balk at the prospect of Bran using himself as bait
Sansa and Arya balk at the prospect of Bran using himself as bait
Theon declares he and his men will defend Bran as atonement for his previous capture of Winterfell
Tyrion questions how the Night King will know where to find Bran
Bran reveals the claw marks the Night King left on him allow the creature to track him anywhere
Tyrion declares he and Davos will signal Jon and Daenerys to ignite the trench around Winterfell at the right time
Daenerys overrides Tyrion, declaring he will be in the crypts for his own safety and she will need him if the battle is won
Davos suggests the dragons will be helpful on the battlefield
Jon notes they need the dragons near the godswood to protect Bran and will hold them back to avoid suspicion
Jon explains they will strike with the dragons when the Night King makes his move for Bran
Arya asks if dragonfire will stop the Night King
Bran replies he has no idea, as no one has ever tried that
everyone leaves the meeting
Tyrion pulls up a chair next to Bran
Tyrion and Bran exchange stories
Tyrion and Bran exchange stories
Tyrion and Jaime drink and talk at the fireplace in Winterfell’s main hall
Tyrion says he'd love to see their father's face when they decided to defend Winterfell
Podrick and Brienne walk in and begin to drink
Davos joins them to warm himself by the fire
Tormund joins the group and focuses on Brienne
Tormund brags about killing a giant at the age of 10
Tormund tells the story of being nursed by a giant for 3 months and getting the name 'Giantsbane'
The rest of the group looks on, unsure how to react
Tormund guzzles down his milk from a horn
Arya approaches the Hound on the upstairs rooftop's walkway
Beric arrives and sits with Arya and the Hound
Sandor threatens to throw Beric from the battlements when Beric starts sermonizing about the Lord of Light
Arya begins to walk off
Arya remarks she'd rather be anywhere than with the two 'miserable s*its' drinking together
Arya begins target practicing with her bow and arrow
Gendry arrives with Arya’s new weapon he forged for her
Arya asks Gendry what the Red Woman wanted from him years ago
Gendry explains the Red Woman wanted his blood, used leeches for a spell, and reveals he is the bastard son of Robert Baratheon
Arya asks Gendry how many partners he has slept with
Gendry answers 3
Arya says she’d like to know what it’s like before they possibly die
Arya and Gendry kiss, disrobe, and have sex
Arya and Gendry kiss, disrobe, and have sex
The group of Brienne, Tormund, Davos, Pod, Jaime, and Tyrion continue to drink and regale stories by the fireplace
Tyrion jokingly speculates that, as veterans of major battles, they might make it out alive
Tormund, unsure about knighthood, asks Brienne why she isn't a knight
Brienne replies that it is because of "tradition"
Jaime refills his drink and mentions aloud that the tradition isn't necessarily true
Jaime states that any knight can make a knight
Brienne is unsure what to do at first
Pod gives Brienne a faint smile and a nod
Brienne stands and moves away from the group so she can kneel
Jaime brings his sword and gives Brienne her knighthood
Jaime says "arise Brienne of Tarth, a Knight of the Seven Kingdoms"
The group claps for Brienne
Brienne stands and gives a tearful smile to the small group in the room
Jorah speaks to his cousin Lyanna in the courtyard
Everyone prepares for the imminent arrival of the army of the dead
Lyanna and Jorah have an argument
Jorah advises Lyanna to remain in the crypt with the other women and children
Jorah tells Lyanna she is the future of House Mormont
Lyanna refuses to stay in the crypt
Lyanna insists on fighting with the rest of the men and women
Jorah allows Lyanna to make up her own mind
Sam approaches Jorah and gives him the sword 'Heartsbane'
Sam explains he wants Jorah to wield the sword in honor of Jeor
Jorah accepts the sword and says he will use it in his father's memory
Sam says "I hope we win" before leaving
Tyrion asks the small group if anyone can sing
No one answers Tyrion's question
Pod begins singing "Jenny of Oldstones" softly
Various other characters are shown on their last night before the coming battle
Samwell, Gilly, and Sam lie in bed together
Sansa eats a bowl of soup in the castle courtyard with Theon
Theon eats a bowl of soup with Sansa in the castle courtyard
Gendry and Arya are in bed after sleeping together
Gendry is fast asleep
Arya stares off in deep thought
Missandei gives Grey Worm a heavy and deep kiss goodbye
Grey Worm prepares to leave with the rest of the Unsullied army
Jorah rides with a detachment of Dothraki, scanning the horizon for any sign of the foe
Jon stares at a statue of Lyanna Stark in the crypts
Daenerys finds Jon and approaches him
Daenerys wraps an arm around Jon’s waist as he holds her hand
Daenerys asks Jon who the statue is of
Jon explains the statue is of Lyanna Stark
Daenerys expresses disbelief that her brother Rhaegar raped Lyanna, saying it was out of character
Jon turns to face Daenerys and tells her the truth
Jon says Rhaegar didn’t rape Lyanna, but loved her and they were secretly wed
Jon adds that Rhaegar and Lyanna had a son, and if Robert Baratheon had found out, the baby would have been killed
Jon explains Eddard Stark took his sister’s child and raised him as his own, pretending the child was a bastard
Jon reveals the last words spoken by Lyanna were the name of her son
Jon tells Daenerys that he is that child, and his true name is Aegon Targaryen
Daenerys finds it nearly impossible to believe, especially since the truth came from two men close to Jon
