Daenerys realizes and says aloud that Jon is the last living male heir of the Targaryens
a horn is heard sounding in the distance
Jon runs to the roof walkways
Daenerys runs to the roof walkways
Jon and Daenerys see the Army of the Dead standing in formation in the distance
Jon and Daenerys see the Army of the Dead standing in formation in the distance
the Army of the Dead arrives and stands ready for battle

The_Long_Night.action: Night falls at Winterfell
Samwell Tarly receives a pair of dragonglass daggers from an unseen benefactor
Tyrion Lannister takes several wine casks from the provisions
Tyrion brings the wine casks to the crypts below, where the non-combatants are hiding
Bran Stark is wheeled to the Godswood to wait as bait
Theon Greyjoy, his detachment of Ironborn, and Alys Karstark accompany Bran to the Godswood
Sansa Stark oversees one section of the castle wall
Arya Stark oversees one section of the castle wall
Davos commands another section of the castle wall
Lyanna Mormont commands defense of the main courtyard
Brienne of Tarth commands the left flank outside the gates
Jaime Lannister accompanies Brienne on the left flank
Podrick Payne, Sandor Clegane, Gendry, Eddison Tollett, and Samwell Tarly join Brienne and Jaime on the left flank
Grey Worm leads the formations of Unsullied
Jorah Mormont and Qhono ride with the Dothraki cavalry, accompanied by Ghost
Daenerys Targaryen rides her dragon away from the castle
Jon Snow rides his dragon away from the castle
The moonless darkness is impenetrable and eerily silent
Melisandre appears as a lone rider
Melisandre rides up to Jorah and asks him to command the Dothraki in their language to raise their arakhs
Jorah commands the Dothraki to raise their arakhs
Melisandre prays to the Lord of Light in High Valyrian
The army’s arakhs burst into flame, sweeping from one side of the field to the other
Melisandre withdraws within Winterfell
Davos is about to kill Melisandre for Shireen
Melisandre tells Davos not to bother, as she expects to be dead before dawn
Melisandre stares intently at Arya for a few moments before departing
Dothraki cavalry charge ahead into the night with fiery arakhs
Dothraki crash into the undead horde of wights
Infantry around Winterfell hear Dothraki shouts and watch in the distance
Dothraki war cries turn to screams of terror
Flames from Dothraki swords are quickly extinguished
A stray horse or rider flees back toward the castle
Jorah, looking haggard, flees back toward the castle
Army of the Dead advances in earnest
The Unsullied form phalanxes
Catapults start shooting flaming barrels into the night
The fires reveal the true size of the undead horde
The undead horde rushes down from the north and slams into the defenders around Winterfell
The defenders are quickly overrun by the wights
Wights swarm over and on top of each other and infantry formations like a tidal wave
A savage melee sees most defenders surrounded
Defenders kill wights with their dragonglass weapons
The living dead drag down and overwhelm the defenders through sheer numbers
Daenerys views the battle from the west and is upset at the slaughter of her Dothraki
Daenerys wants to strafe the wights
Jon tries to stop Daenerys because the plan is to locate the Night King
Bran lures the Night King into the godswood
Jon and Daenerys plan to attack the Night King when he appears
Daenerys shakes off Jon's arm and tells him, "The dead are already here"
Daenerys takes off on Drogon
Jon follows Daenerys on Rhaegal
Two dragon-riders make strafing runs on the undead army, just behind the faltering human forces
Thousands of wights are incinerated
The tide of wights is stemmed for a short time, but more keep coming
The White Walkers stay in a mounted line far behind the front, not engaging
The White Walkers throw wave after wave of undead pawns at the castle
Jon sees the White Walkers and tries to make a bombing run at them
The White Walkers magically summon a huge snowstorm, blasting Jon with cold air and forcing him back
Both dragons can barely stay in the air and nearly fly into each other during the whiteout
Jon calls Daenerys's name as they struggle to find each other in the storm
Jon and Daenerys reunite above the storm clouds
Jon and Daenerys reunite above the storm clouds
The Night King appears riding Viserion and pursues Daenerys and Drogon
Jon tries to follow the Night King but is knocked out of the way
Daenerys manages to evade the Night King's attack
Jon and Daenerys find each other again above the clouds
Jon and Daenerys find each other again above the clouds
The Night King is gone
The Night King makes his way to Winterfell
The remaining infantry is badly affected
The Unsullied hold in their phalanxes, resisting the wights
The Unsullied are slowly but steadily consumed by the scrambling mass of wights
A full retreat into the castle is ordered
Grey Worm commands the Unsullied to form up a rearguard to protect the withdrawal of their few remaining forces
Samwell Tarly is nearly killed in the retreat
Dolorous Edd saves Samwell Tarly
Dolorous Edd is stabbed from behind and killed
The wights gradually force their way forward in slaughter
Unsullied reinforcements step forward to take the place of each that falls
The Unsullied are on the verge of being overwhelmed
The last of the main host gets back through the gates
Grey Worm orders the entrenchments around the castle to be set on fire
Grey Worm remains with the last rearguard at the earthworks
Arya and other archers attempt to shoot the pitch with flaming arrows
Severe winds blow away the flaming arrows
The snowstorm prevents the dragonriders from seeing the signal to light the pitch
Melisandre appears and lays her hands on the trenchworks, praying to the Lord of Light
The entire trench ringing Winterfell bursts into flames at the last moment
Grey Worm and his remaining men withdraw behind the castle gates, which close
The Wights halt, sensing the danger
Arya tells Sansa to flee to the crypts
Sansa protests that she can't abandon her people
Arya gives Sansa a dragonglass dagger to defend herself
Sansa says she doesn't know how to use the dagger
Arya advises Sansa to "stick them with the pointy end"
Sansa goes to the crypts and meets other women, children, the infirm, and non-combatants including Tyrion and Varys
Tyrion laments that he should be up on the battlements, even if he can't fight well
Tyrion suggests he and Varys could use their intellects as commanders
Sansa chides Tyrion that witty remarks won't make a difference
Sansa says the bravest thing they can do is "look the truth in the face"
Tyrion is moved and quips that maybe they should have stayed married
Sansa responds, "You were the best of them."
Tyrion remarks that is a terrifying thought
Sansa says a marriage wouldn't work because of Tyrion's loyalty to Daenerys
Missandei defends Daenerys, saying without the Dragon Queen they'd all be dead
Theon hears the approach of the undead army in the godswood
Theon starts to apologize to Bran
Bran politely cuts Theon off, saying everything Theon did led him to where he is now: "Home"
Theon is moved by Bran's words
Bran wargs into flocks of ravens around Winterfell
Bran, through the ravens' eyes, comes across the Night King on Viserion as he orders his army to press their attack
The Night King psychically commands his Wights to smother the trench across multiple spots
Many Wights burn in the trench
The flames are quickly extinguished, breaching the outer defenses
Thousands of Wights swarm around the base of Winterfell's great stone walls
Wights climb over each other in a mass, using their strength to grip into crevices in the stone
The Night King makes another run towards the godswood
Jon and Daenerys predict where the Night King is headed
Jon spots the Night King and pursues him
The Night King approaches the castle walls and is about to strafe Winterfell
Jon attacks the Night King from atop Rhaegal
Rhaegal clashes with his undead brother
A battle between the two factions ensues in the skies above Winterfell
The castle's walls act as a force multiplier for the defenders, allowing them to kill many more hundreds of wights as they reach the battlements
More wights keep coming as the snowstorm reduces the castle to a whiteout amidst the flames
Jaime is nearly overwhelmed by a dozen wights
Brienne carves her way to Jaime using her Valyrian steel sword and saves him
Jorah Mormont saves Samwell Tarly using Heartsbane, the ancestral Valyrian steel sword of House Tarly
Arya picks off many wights with flaming arrows
Sandor Clegane is left frozen due to his fear of fire as Beric Dondarrion urges him to keep fighting
Sandor pleads that they can't beat death itself
Beric points to Arya and says, "Tell her that."
The tide of battle turns against the defenders
Lyanna Mormont tries to organize the defense of the main courtyard
A large wight-giant bashes its way through the main gate of Winterfell
More wights flow in behind the giant, leading the advance
The wight-giant knocks Lyanna to the side and takes out any of her soldiers still standing
Lyanna recovers, grabs a dragonglass blade, and charges the undead giant while letting out a battle cry
The wight-giant snatches Lyanna up in his hand and holds her up to his one remaining blue eye
The wight-giant clenches his fist, crushing Lyanna's chest as her bones snap
Lyanna, with one last defiant cry, stabs the dragonglass into the wight-giant's eye
The wight-giant instantly dies and topples to the ground
Lyanna falls free of the giant's grasp, but dies from her injuries
The entire courtyard is overrun and all remaining order to the siege collapses
Wights spread to surround any remaining pockets of resistance around the castle
Arya, who was knocked out against a stone wall, recovers some time later
Arya stumbles into the Winterfell library
The fighting moves away from this part of the castle
Wights shamble about looking for survivors to pick off
Arya uses her stealth training from the Faceless Men
Arya slowly sneaks past the wights
Wights burst through the door after Arya as soon as she leaves
Arya runs into Sandor and Beric, who are also fleeing wights
Sandor and Beric help Arya fight off the wights
Arya, Sandor, and Beric make a running retreat through the narrow hallways of the castle's interior
Beric is stabbed multiple times and barely manages to drag himself after Arya and Sandor into the great hall
The heavy doors of the great hall hold after they shut them
Beric succumbs to his injuries once inside the great hall
Arya and Sandor find Melisandre waiting in the hall
Melisandre remarks that Beric has fulfilled the purpose the Lord of Light had for him by saving Arya
Arya recalls that she and Melisandre met before, and Melisandre said they would meet again
Melisandre reminds Arya that she said Arya would close many eyes forever, including "blue" ones
Melisandre asks Arya what they say to the god of Death
Arya responds, "Not today."
Arya departs, newly determined in her goal
The dragons are locked in battle in the sky above Winterfell
Jon and the Night King fight in fierce close quarters mid-air brawl on their dragons
Viserion badly claws Rhaegal's belly
The Night King readies another dragon-killing crystal spear, but can't get a clear shot due to the chaotic swirling
Rhaegal, despite his wounds, lands a bite on Viserion and tears the left half of his face off
The Night King is significantly distracted and doesn't notice Daenerys's approach on Drogon
Daenerys and Drogon collide with Viserion, spilling the Night King from his mount
Drogon tears into Viserion
Rhaegal, badly wounded, makes a forced landing and drops Jon on the ground before fleeing
Drogon sends the badly injured Viserion crashing into the castle
Jon regains himself on the ground
Jon finds he is on the abandoned outer battlefield
Jon notices he is not far from the Night King
Night King proceeds on foot to the breach in the walls leading to the godswood and Bran
Daenerys reaches the Night King
Daenerys commands "Dracarys!"
Drogon blasts the Night King with dragonfire
The smoke dissipates, revealing the Night King is undamaged
Bran's warning about dragonfire is proven correct
Night King readies an ice javelin to throw at Drogon
Daenerys flees and narrowly dodges the shot
Jon pursues the Night King
The Night King silently turns to Jon
The Night King raises his arms, resurrecting all of Winterfell's fallen defenders (including Dolorous Edd, Lyanna Mormont, and Qhono) into wights
Jon finds himself surrounded by the new wights
Jon struggles to fight off the new wights
The Night King continues into the godswood
Living men desperately pound on the heavy doors to be let into the crypts
Sansa and the others hear the men’s cries but do not open the doors, fearing a flood of wights
The men’s cries are quickly silenced
Wights cannot find a way into the crypts
White Walkers reanimate dead bodies already in the crypts
Many interred corpses rise as wights and start clawing their way out of their tombs
Wights attack the unarmed women and children, and many succumb
Sansa and Tyrion hide behind one tomb
Sansa and Tyrion contemplate helping the women and children with the dragon glass they were given
Jon fights off the surrounding wights
Daenerys appears with Drogon and burns up the wights from the air
Jon calls to Daenerys, planning to pursue the Night King
Daenerys tells Jon to go
Jon follows the Night King
Daenerys and Drogon land but stay on the ground for moments too long
Drogon is swarmed by dozens of wights
Drogon barely manages to fly away still covered in wights
Daenerys is knocked off Drogon's back in the process
Jorah appears and helps Daenerys fight off the wights
Daenerys picks up a dragonglass dagger from the ground
Daenerys and Jorah are both exhausted and more wights surround them again
Jon fights his way back into the courtyard of the castle on foot
Viserion pins Jon down in the courtyard
Jon considers stabbing Viserion with his Valyrian steel sword but finds it too dangerous to approach
Viserion, badly wounded, leaks bursts of fire and crashes through the rubble
Jon dives for cover as Viserion catches sight of him and unleashes a torrent of fire
Viserion incinerates the courtyard, barely missing Jon
Jon emerges from his hiding place, facing certain death
Jon bellows defiance at Viserion as the undead dragon's jaws gape open
Wights advance through Winterfell's godswood
Theon's archers pick off advancing wights
Each of the defenders is killed, including Alys Karstark
Theon takes out a dozen more wights until he runs out of arrows
Wights stop and form a ring around the Weirwood tree
Wights step aside to make a path for the Night King
Night King enters, followed by other White Walkers and a horde of wights
Night King temporarily halts some wights
Bran turns to Theon and says "You are a good man. Thank you."
Theon grabs a spear and courageously charges at the Night King
Night King blocks Theon's blow, snaps the spear, and impales Theon
Theon slowly dies on the ground
Night King walks slowly towards Bran at the base of the tree
Bran looks up at the Night King with pity
Night King stares down at Bran (the Three Eyed Raven) in triumph
The Night King draws the sword sheathed on his back for the killing blow
A wisp of wind catches the attention of one of the Night King's lieutenants
Arya leaps at the Night King from behind, her Valyrian steel dagger drawn
The Night King spins around and catches Arya by the throat in one hand and her left wrist in the other
The Night King leers at Arya
Arya drops the dagger out of her left hand and catches it with her right hand
Arya drives the blade into the Night King's chest, shattering him into icy shards
The Night King is destroyed
The other White Walkers shatter
Every wight falls
The few surviving and surrounded defenders at Winterfell see the wights suddenly drop and fall apart
The magic animating the wights' dead flesh and sinews departs
Viserion dies for the final time in the courtyard, saving Jon Snow
wights attacking Jorah and Daenerys drop dead
Jorah is fatally wounded defending Daenerys
Jorah dies in the arms of Daenerys
Daenerys cries while holding Jorah
Drogon curls himself around Daenerys and Jorah to comfort her as she mourns
wights fall in the crypts, saving Tyrion, Sansa, and the others
The ancient threat of the White Walkers is destroyed forever
Dawn breaks over the devastated castle
Melisandre walks out of the gates, removing her cloak
Davos follows Melisandre, ready to kill her with his dagger
Melisandre removes her magical choker and drops it on the ground, the glow from its gem fading out
Davos is astonished to see from a distance that Melisandre is now a centuries-old woman
Melisandre takes a few faltering steps, then collapses
Melisandre’s body crumbles away into dust

The_Last_of_the_Starks.action: Winterfell is cleared of thousands of corpses in and around it
The fallen are gathered into dozens of funeral pyres
Jon gives a eulogy about the duty of the survivors to keep the fallen alive in memory
Jon quotes the Night's Watch funeral speech, announcing the fallen were the shields that guarded the realms of men and their watch is ended
The assembled leaders go forward with torches to light the pyres
Sansa weeps over Theon Greyjoy's body
Sansa takes a pin with the Stark direwolf sigil off her own gown and adds it to Theon's chest
Daenerys lights Jorah Mormont's pyre
Jon lights Lyanna Mormont's pyre
Arya lights Beric Dondarrion's pyre
Samwell lights Dolorous Edd's pyre
The survivors hold a victory feast in the great hall of Winterfell
The mood is somber due to many deaths
Gendry gets up to look for Arya
Daenerys calls on Gendry as he passes the high table
Daenerys notes that Gendry is Robert Baratheon's son, the man who overthrew her family
Gendry humbly responds that he didn't know Robert was his father until after Robert died
Daenerys asks Gendry who the Lord of Storm's End is now that Robert is dead
Gendry says he doesn't know
Daenerys asks the entire hall who the lord of Storm's End is now
Daenerys declares she will make Gendry the new Lord of Storm's End as a reward for his heroism
Gendry is shocked and says he cannot be lord as he is only Robert's bastard son
Daenerys announces she legitimizes Gendry as "Gendry Baratheon," rightful son of Robert and new lord of Storm's End
Davos Seaworth toasts the newly made Gendry Baratheon
The rest of the hall toasts Gendry
Tyrion quietly notes to Daenerys that her move was politically wise, not just altruistic
Tyrion explains this makes Gendry a rival claimant to the Stormlands instead of Cersei and pacifies a potential rival to the Iron Throne
Sansa looks over at Daenerys and Tyrion, silently distressed
The mood is lightened and the hall becomes more celebratory as everyone starts drinking heavily and boasting about their deeds in the battle
Tyrion talks to Davos
Davos tells Tyrion about Melisandre's death
Davos wonders about the meaning of Melisandre's connection to a higher power and why it left after the great danger passed
Tyrion says pondering the subject won't make him happier
Davos says he's not trying to be happy
Tyrion remarks that they have defeated the White Walkers but still have themselves to deal with
Tyrion speaks to Bran Stark, pointing out that as Ned Stark's last trueborn son he should be the lawful heir to Winterfell and the North
Bran says he won't ever be a lord now, as he is the Three-Eyed Raven and removed from human affairs
Tyrion says he envies Bran for his powers
Bran says Tyrion wouldn't envy him, as he mostly just lives in memories of the past now
Tormund and Sansa encourage Jon to chug an entire horn of wine
Jon declines, saying "Vomiting is not celebrating"
Tormund replies, "Yes, it is," and raises his horn, "To the Dragon Queen!"
The hall cheers
Daenerys stands up and makes a toast, "To Arya Stark, the hero of Winterfell!"
The hall cheers again
The Hound smiles at Daenerys's toast
Jon and Daenerys share a smile
Sansa notices Jon and Daenerys sharing a smile and leaves the table
Tyrion starts playing his "I Have Never"-style drinking game with Jaime, Brienne, and Podrick
Jaime and Brienne playfully argue over whether she told him she was an only child
Brienne ends up having to take a drink
Jaime tells Brienne she danced with Renly Baratheon
Brienne looks at Pod, who smiles and shrugs
Tyrion tells Brienne, "Drink!"
Tormund, drunk, laughs and drinks, praising Jon and recounting their history
Everyone laughs off Tormund's story as drunken boasting
Tormund declares, "He climbed on a fucking dragon and fought. What kind of person climbs on a fucking dragon? A madman or a king!"
Jon turns to look at Daenerys
Daenerys smiles and raises her glass to Jon
Jon returns Daenerys's smile and turns back to the conversation
Daenerys becomes more downcast as she looks around the hall at the groups of people
Daenerys looks at Tyrion, Brienne, Pod, and Jaime playing their drinking game
Daenerys looks at Jon, Tormund, Davos, and some wildlings
Varys notices Daenerys's mood and isolation, becoming concerned
Daenerys gets up to leave the hall
Tyrion, Jaime, Brienne, and Pod continue their drinking game
Brienne correctly guesses that Tyrion was married to someone else before Sansa
Brienne guesses that Tyrion prefers ale over wine
Tyrion yells "No!"
Brienne takes another drink
Brienne and Jaime exchange smiles
Brienne and Jaime exchange smiles
Tyrion, now very drunk, tells Brienne, "You're a virgin."
Brienne's smile fades
Pod takes a drink
Tyrion drunkenly guesses that Brienne has never slept with anyone
Brienne doesn't answer, gets up, and says she must relieve herself
Tormund drunkenly propositions Brienne, whom he is smitten with

Jaime intervenes, further rebuffing Tormund
Tormund, comically heartbroken, commiserates with Sandor Clegane
Northern serving girls start hitting on Tormund
Tormund leaves with the serving girls
Sandor stays drinking alone
Sandor shooes away another serving girl who is interested in him
Sansa comes to Sandor's table to sit with him
Sandor recalls their last conversation during the Battle of the Blackwater
Sandor mentions he heard Sansa was taken by Littlefinger
Sandor comments that Sansa was "broken in rough" by marrying Ramsay Bolton
Sansa says that without Littlefinger and the rest, she would have stayed a "little bird"
Sansa says the abuse from Ramsay made her strong
Sandor asks how Sansa had Ramsay executed
Sansa says she killed Ramsay with "hounds"
Sandor laughs at Sansa's answer
Gendry slips out of the hall
Gendry finds Arya practicing archery alone in the stables
Gendry happily announces to Arya that he is no longer a bastard and has been made "Gendry Baratheon" and the new lord of Storm's End by Daenerys
Arya is very happy for Gendry
Gendry says he doesn't know how to be a lord and it won't mean anything if Arya isn't by his side
Gendry gets down on one knee and begs Arya to marry him and become the new Lady of Storm's End
Arya is deeply moved and pulls Gendry in for a passionate kiss
Arya says that while Gendry deserves a wonderful wife, she doesn't think she'll ever be a proper "Lady" or get married
Gendry is left crestfallen
Jaime shows up at Brienne's chamber in the castle
Jaime starts undressing
Brienne starts undressing
Brienne admits she's never slept with anyone before
Jaime and Brienne have sex
Daenerys arrives at Jon's bedchambers
Jon expresses regret over Jorah's death and says Jorah would have chosen to die defending Daenerys

Jon and Daenerys passionately kiss and begin to undress
Jon stops himself from having sex with Daenerys
Daenerys laments that she wishes Jon never told her the truth about his parentage
Daenerys expresses her feelings about seeing people look at Jon with admiration
Jon insists he doesn't want the throne
Daenerys argues that it doesn't matter what Jon wants, people will press his claim
Jon gets down on one knee and insists Daenerys is his queen
Daenerys tells Jon to never tell anyone who he really is
Daenerys holds Jon's hand and says she wants things to be the way they were
Jon clasps Daenerys's hand and says he must tell Sansa and Arya
Daenerys warns Jon that Sansa will press his claim over hers
Jon insists Sansa won't do that
Daenerys warns Jon that Sansa isn't the same girl he grew up with
Jon says he owes Sansa and Arya the truth
Daenerys begs Jon not to tell them, saying the truth will destroy them
Jon insists the truth won't destroy them and reaffirms Daenerys is his queen
Daenerys tells Jon they can live together, but only if he keeps the secret
Daenerys, Jon, and their remaining leaders hold a war council around a large map of Westeros
Grey Worm reports that about half of the Unsullied were killed in the battle
Jon says that about half of the Northern/Vale armies were destroyed
Varys updates the council on the situation in the south using map markers
The Golden Company arrives in King's Landing, delivered by Euron Greyjoy's fleet
Cersei's position is reinforced by the arrival of the Golden Company
Daenerys says they must attack King's Landing and rip out Cersei root and stem
Tyrion warns Daenerys that a direct assault would cause many civilian casualties and make the Smallfolk hate her
Tyrion suggests they should encircle Cersei and starve out the city until the commoners revolt
Varys points out that Daenerys is gaining allies elsewhere
Varys says the new Prince of Dorne has declared for Daenerys and against Cersei
Varys reports that Yara Greyjoy has retaken the Iron Islands while Euron's fleet is away
Daenerys insists they must attack Cersei because she only has power by controlling the Iron Throne
Daenerys says they must take the capital city to defeat Cersei, as without it she is nothing
Sansa counters that they are not in a strong position to immediately mount a new full-scale offensive
Sansa explains that the men are exhausted and wounded, and will fight better after rest
Daenerys asks Sansa how much time is needed
Sansa says she needs to talk to the officers first
Daenerys tells Sansa she came north to fight at great cost and now Sansa wants to postpone
Sansa reminds Daenerys that both the Northern armies and the Unsullied are exhausted
Daenerys warns that the longer they wait, the more time Cersei has to gather strength and allies
Jon interjects that the North will honor its allegiance to Queen Daenerys and will march south with a smaller army
Sansa and Arya exchange a look
It is decided that Jon and Davos will lead the surviving Northmen/Vale and Dothraki/Unsullied forces south
A smaller force of Unsullied will return via ship from White Harbor to Dragonstone with Daenerys and her dragons
From Dragonstone, Daenerys can blockade Cersei by sea and stage a final attack
Brienne wishes to stay at Winterfell to defend the Stark sisters
Jaime decides to stay with Brienne at Winterfell
Daenerys declares to the war council that this will be the last war in the Seven Kingdoms
Daenerys and Sansa exchange a tense look
Daenerys and her advisors leave
Arya asks Jon for a word in private
The four surviving Stark children gather in private in the godswood
Jon is irritated with his sisters
Jon says they'd all be dead if not for Daenerys
Sansa replies that Arya killed the Night King
Jon reminds Sansa that Daenerys's men gave their lives to fight the undead
Sansa says she won't forget that but doesn't want to bend the knee to Daenerys
Jon says he pledged allegiance to Daenerys
Arya says she respects Jon's decision but doesn't trust Daenerys
Jon tells Arya she doesn't know Daenerys yet
Arya insists she'll never know Daenerys and says "She's not one of us."
Jon replies that if you only trust the people you grew up with, you won't make many allies
Arya says she doesn't need allies, they're family
Arya stresses that they are the last of the Starks and must remain loyal to each other
Jon, weighed down by the truth, says he has never been a Stark
Sansa urges Jon that he is as much Ned Stark's child as they are
Arya insists Jon is her brother and says she loves him completely
Jon looks at Bran, unable to bear the lie
Bran quietly says it's Jon's choice to tell them
Jon makes Sansa and Arya swear, before the weirwood heart tree, to never tell anyone else the secret
Sansa and Arya swear
Sansa and Arya swear
Jon gives Bran permission to tell them
Jaime tells Tyrion about his new relationship with Brienne
Tyrion, very drunk, jokes about mocking Jaime for being with a taller woman
Bronn bursts through the front door, pointing Joffrey's crossbow at Jaime and Tyrion
Bronn tells Jaime and Tyrion that Cersei hired him to assassinate both of them
Tyrion, too drunk to care, does not take Bronn seriously
Bronn punches Tyrion in the nose, nearly breaking it
Bronn sits at the table, still pointing the crossbow at them
Bronn says he would have killed them already if that was his only option
Bronn explains he doesn't think Cersei will win the war after seeing Daenerys's dragons
Tyrion realizes Bronn is referring to his old offer to double any bribe to kill him
Bronn says Cersei offered him Riverrun as a reward

Tyrion suggests Highgarden as a reward
Jaime says no cutthroat like Bronn would be accepted as lord of Highgarden
Bronn scoffs, saying all Great Houses started with cutthroats seizing land
Tyrion assures Bronn he will be granted Highgarden
Bronn leaves, promising to shoot first next time if Tyrion doesn't keep his word
Jaime asks Tyrion if they are seriously going to give Bronn Highgarden
Tyrion says he won't worry about it for now, as it assumes they survive the war against Cersei
Sandor Clegane rides away from Winterfell down the Kingsroad
Sandor realizes he is being followed by Arya Stark
Arya rides up to Sandor’s side on her horse
Sandor says he has unfinished business in the south (his brother, Gregor Clegane)
Arya says she has unfinished business too (killing Cersei)
Sandor says he doesn't think he'll be coming back to Winterfell
Arya says she doesn't think she'll return either
Sansa stands on the castle walls, still in shock from the revelation of Jon's parentage
Sansa gazes at Daenerys's dragons flying in the distance
Sansa is visibly teary-eyed and only half-aware as Tyrion approaches her
Tyrion approaches Sansa
Sansa, still numb, asks "Why her?"
Tyrion insists that Daenerys will be a good queen and that Jon will be Warden of the North under her
Tyrion says with Jon in the capital, Sansa will be the true power in the North
Tyrion expresses he would feel better if Sansa and Daenerys were allies
Sansa wonders what Tyrion is worried about, given Daenerys's dragons and armies
Tyrion says Sansa doesn't have to be Daenerys's friend but shouldn't provoke her
Sansa accuses Tyrion of being afraid of Daenerys
Tyrion responds that all capable leaders must inspire some fear
Sansa anxiously says she doesn't want Jon to go to King's Landing because her family members died there
Tyrion weakly says he believes in Sansa
Sansa, with a pained look, asks Tyrion what he'd say if there was "someone else, someone better?"
Jon gets ready to leave south with the army in the castle courtyard
Tormund and Samwell Tarly come to say goodbye to Jon
Tormund says he is leaving, returning north with his people
The surviving Free Folk plan to travel to Castle Black, then return Beyond the Wall after winter
Jon says the free folk are welcome in the North
Tormund says the lands north of the Wall are their home
Jon says this is farewell
Tormund says, "You never know. You've got the north in you. The real north."
Jon and Tormund hug good-bye
Ghost, Jon's direwolf, is present, badly wounded but survived
Jon tells Tormund to take Ghost with him
Jon says a direwolf has no place in the southern kingdoms and would be happier in the northern forests
Gilly says goodbye to Jon with a hug
Jon realizes Gilly is pregnant again with Samwell's child
Gilly says if it's a boy, she hopes to name it "Jon"
Jon responds that he hopes it's a girl
Jon and Sam share a long hug and tell each other they were the best friend each other had
Jon rides out of the castle as Tormund, Ghost, Sam, and Gilly look on
Daenerys's fleet sails south
Tyrion meets with Varys in a chamber below decks
Tyrion repeats what Sansa told him: Jon is the secret legitimate son of Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark, and the true rightful heir to the Iron Throne
Tyrion wonders aloud about the wars reverberating from Lyanna choosing Rhaegar instead of Robert Baratheon
Tyrion and Varys wonder what would happen if the truth came out
Varys acknowledges that the North would switch support to Jon as rightful heir
Varys says Sansa would make sure the Vale would support Jon as well
Tyrion asks why they don't pursue a marriage-alliance
Varys rebukes that Daenerys is Jon's aunt
Tyrion points out that incestuous marriages never deterred a Targaryen before
Varys insists Jon would never agree to it because he grew up in the North
Varys says he is starting to worry about Daenerys's state of mind and her growing isolation
Missandei and Grey Worm hold hands as Dragonstone island comes into view
Daenerys soars over the fleet on Drogon, accompanied by Rhaegal
Daenerys is happy to catch sight of her birthplace
A gigantic scorpion bolt appears out of nowhere, burying itself deep in Rhaegal's chest
Euron Greyjoy's fleet mounts a surprise attack with ships equipped with huge ballistas
The new weapons punch deep through Rhaegal's scales
A second bolt hits Rhaegal's wing
Rhaegal roars in agony
A third bolt impales Rhaegal through the throat
Rhaegal plunges into the sea, sinks, and drowns
Daenerys, distraught and enraged, tries to attack Euron's ship with Drogon
All of the scorpions start raining bolts at Daenerys
Daenerys barely manages to turn away and escape with her life
Euron's ships target their ballistae at Daenerys's fleet, smashing them to splinters
Grey Worm urges Missandei to flee to safety by taking the skiff to shore
Grey Worm and Missandei are overwhelmed and separated in the chaos
Tyrion barely dodges ballista bolts on the ship's deck as it is blown to pieces
Tyrion survives by jumping overboard, is hit in the head, and passes out
Survivors of the destroyed fleet drag themselves onto the nearby shore of Dragonstone, including Tyrion and Varys
Grey Worm stands ashore and realizes in horror that Missandei isn't with them
Cersei gloats with Euron over their victory
Cersei watches the city gates as thousands of refugees flood into King's Landing
Thousands of refugees flood into the city
Qyburn acknowledges that Cersei's true intention is proceeding as planned
Cersei uses the commoners as human shields against Daenerys's army
Cersei anticipates Daenerys will be deterred from a direct assault due to risk of civilian casualties
Cersei feels the Northern and Targaryen armies are exhausted after fighting the White Walkers
Cersei gains strength with new allies such as the Golden Company
Golden Company helps crew Euron's ships in the attack
Cersei declares the Lion will rule the land and the Kraken will rule the sea, and their child shall rule it all
Euron looks to Qyburn, surprised
Qyburn nods in acknowledgement that Cersei is pregnant
Cersei turns to Missandei, taken captive by Euron and brought before her in chains
Cersei sarcastically gloats that Daenerys is the 'Breaker of Chains' indeed
Daenerys meets with her remaining advisors in the Chamber of the Painted Table at Dragonstone, furious and bereaved
Grey Worm grimly suggests they should storm the capital city at once
Varys warns that Cersei has filled the city with refugees to use as human shields
Varys pleads that attacking would be an act of tyranny and turn the people of Westeros against Daenerys
Daenerys declares her purpose is to remove tyrants and vows to tear out Cersei root and stem
Tyrion agrees with Varys and insists they must offer Cersei the option to flee Westeros with her life
Daenerys, restraining her tears and rage, relents to offering Cersei a chance to surrender
Daenerys reasons that offering surrender will make it clear Cersei is to blame when King's Landing is destroyed
Tyrion and Varys offer no other military option
Tyrion and Varys offer no other military option
Tyrion and Varys meet alone in the throne room
Varys questions if Daenerys is fit to rule and suggests Jon Snow might be a better monarch
Tyrion tries to argue that Daenerys must be strong and instill fear in her enemies
Varys says he will always act in the interest of the people and warns that thousands of innocents will die if Daenerys attacks Cersei
Tyrion asks if Daenerys would step aside if Jon wanted the Iron Throne
Varys silently implies they should assassinate Daenerys if she attacks King's Landing
Tyrion, horrified, mutters that Daenerys will make the right choice
Jaime arrives in the courtyard at Winterfell
Sansa and Brienne receive a messenger-raven from Varys about the attack on Daenerys's fleet and the situation in the south
Cersei kills one of Daenerys's dragons and takes her close advisor captive
Sansa tells Jaime that Daenerys will execute Cersei and won't let her surrender with her life
Jaime gets up from the bed he shares with Brienne at night and thinks long and hard
Brienne awakes to find Jaime gone
Brienne rushes to catch Jaime outside as he saddles a horse to return south to Cersei
Brienne starts crying and pleads with Jaime not to go to Cersei, saying he is a good person now
Jaime tells Brienne that he isn't a good person, listing the terrible things he did for Cersei
Brienne says that Cersei is hateful
Jaime responds, "So am I," and rides out of the castle
Brienne is left weeping
A small contingent of Unsullied escort Daenerys and her advisors to approach one of the gates of King's Landing
Daenerys and her advisors approach the landward gate of King's Landing
They see that the city wall defenses have been drastically improved with anti-dragon ballista weapons on each tower
The city wall towers display improved anti-dragon ballista weapons developed by Qyburn
Drogon hangs back behind Daenerys and her group on the ground, stalking back and forth in anger
Cersei stands atop the gatehouse with Euron Greyjoy, Gregor Clegane, and the chained Missandei
Tyrion approaches the gate to parley
Qyburn comes out alone to speak with Tyrion
Tyrion says Daenerys asks for Cersei's unconditional surrender
Qyburn counters that Cersei sent him to ask for Daenerys's unconditional surrender
Qyburn points out that the Northern/Targaryen armies have been badly depleted and exhausted by fighting the White Walkers
Qyburn states that Cersei has reinforced King's Landing with the Golden Company
Qyburn explains that the new defenses make it risky to attack with the dragon and would kill many innocent people
Tyrion walks closer to plead with Cersei again
Cersei's archers get ready to shoot Tyrion
Cersei pauses and then motions for her archers to stand down
Tyrion begs Cersei, appealing to her love for her children and warning her about condemning her unborn baby
Cersei pauses and thinks on Tyrion's words
Cersei quietly tells Missandei that if she has any last words, now is the time
Missandei shouts "Dracarys!"
Cersei gives a nod to Gregor
Gregor decapitates Missandei with a single swing of his sword
Missandei's head and body fall onto the ground below
Grey Worm is horrified and turns his face away
Daenerys, seething with rage, silently turns away and withdraws from the city

The_Bells.action: Varys writes letters about the truth of Jon Snow's heritage
One of Varys's spies walks in and tells Varys that Daenerys has not eaten in days
Varys responds that they'll try again at supper
Martha tells Varys that Daenerys's soldiers are constantly on alert
Varys assures Martha that the higher the risk, the greater the reward
Varys sends Martha back to the kitchen
Varys gets word of the arrival of Jon and his men
Jon briefs Varys on the Northern army's progress
Jon asks Varys how Daenerys is doing
Varys responds that Daenerys is refusing to eat or leave her chambers
Jon replies that Daenerys shouldn't be alone
Varys admires that Jon is worried for Daenerys
Jon asks Varys if he isn't worried as well
Varys says he is worried for them all and speaks of Daenerys's mental state
Varys quotes, "They say that when a Targaryen is born, the gods flip a coin."
Varys says he knows not what side Daenerys's coin will land on, but knows which side Jon's will land on, implying he wants Jon to take the Iron Throne
Jon vehemently refuses to take the Iron Throne
Varys says he has advised kings and rulers for many years and knows how Daenerys will end up
Jon insists that Daenerys is his Queen now and forever
Tyrion meets with Daenerys in Dragonstone's war room
Daenerys self-isolates for days, becoming paranoid
Daenerys surmises that someone has betrayed her
Tyrion confirms Daenerys's suspicion of betrayal
Daenerys names Jon Snow as the traitor
Tyrion reveals that it was actually Varys who betrayed her
Daenerys confirms that Varys knows the truth about Jon
Daenerys tells Tyrion that he told Varys, and traces the information back to Sansa and Jon
Daenerys feels Jon has betrayed her
Tyrion explains he needed to know so he and Varys could be prepared
Daenerys becomes suspicious of Sansa's motives for telling Tyrion
Tyrion admits it was a mistake to tell Varys without Daenerys's permission
Tyrion says Sansa told him because she trusts him
Daenerys accuses Tyrion of spreading secrets that could destroy her
Tyrion asks for forgiveness, saying their intentions were good
Daenerys dismisses Tyrion's justification, saying it doesn't matter now
Varys writes letters
Varys hears the approach of guards
Varys burns the letter he is currently writing
Grey Worm and his men enter Varys's room with shackles
Varys is taken to the shores of Dragonstone without resistance
Daenerys, Jon, and Tyrion wait for Varys at the shore
Tyrion tells Varys that he sold out his treachery to Daenerys
Varys states he hopes he is wrong about Daenerys
Varys bids farewell to Tyrion
Drogon burns Varys at Daenerys's command
Tyrion looks on unhappily
Jon looks at Daenerys in concern
Jon comes to see Daenerys in their room
Daenerys asks Jon what she said would happen if he told his sister about her parentage
Jon replies he doesn't want the crown and that he told this to Varys
Daenerys says Sansa betrayed Jon's trust and claims Sansa killed Varys as much as she did
Daenerys says this was a victory for Sansa and now Sansa knows what happens when people hear the truth about Jon
Daenerys confides that the people of Westeros love Jon more than her and that all she has is fear, not love
Jon tells Daenerys he loves her and she will always be his Queen regardless of how others feel
Daenerys asks Jon if that is all she is to him: his queen
Jon and Daenerys start to kiss
Jon breaks off the kiss, unable to get over their blood ties
Daenerys accepts this and solemnly says, "Alright then. Let it be fear."
Tyrion consults with Daenerys in the throne room of Dragonstone
Daenerys orders Grey Worm and the Unsullied to sack King's Landing
Tyrion argues that the citizens of King's Landing are innocent and not Daenerys's enemy
Daenerys counters that the slaves in Meereen liberated themselves by turning against their masters
Tyrion responds that the smallfolk are afraid of Cersei because she punishes rebellious acts
Daenerys states that the smallfolk are hostages in a tyrant's grip
Tyrion begs Daenerys not to burn the city, warning that thousands of children will die
Daenerys counters that Cersei is using mercy as a weakness, but insists mercy is their strength for future generations of Westeros
Tyrion bargains with Daenerys, offering to talk to Cersei and convince her to surrender
Tyrion says, "Cersei's followers will abandon her if they know the war is lost. Give them that chance."
Tyrion explains he will ring the bells of King's Landing to indicate Cersei's unconditional surrender
Tyrion pleads with Daenerys not to attack unless he has rung the bells
Daenerys reluctantly agrees to Tyrion's request
Daenerys informs Tyrion that Jaime was caught by her men trying to get past their lines
Daenerys warns Tyrion that the next time he fails her, it will be his last
Ser Davos and his men prepare for the siege outside King's Landing
Jon and Tyrion arrive at the camp
Tyrion asks Davos if he can smuggle something specific for him
Tyrion gets past the guards surrounding Jaime's tent
Tyrion enters Jaime's tent and sees his brother shackled to a post
Tyrion reveals he smuggled the key to Jaime's shackles and unchains him
Tyrion tells Jaime to go to Cersei and convince her to surrender
Tyrion arranges with Davos a boat for Jaime and Cersei to escape to Pentos
Jaime agrees to the plan but argues that Daenerys will kill Tyrion for this
Tyrion replies that his life is a fair exchange if it allows Daenerys to take the city without slaughtering thousands
Tyrion thanks Jaime for everything he has ever done for him
Tyrion and Jaime share a tearful goodbye for the last time
The united army of the Unsullied, Dothraki, and Northmen wait outside the walls of King's Landing
Tyrion instructs Jon to call off his men when they hear the bells ringing
Jon receives Tyrion's instructions
A select few citizens are crowded in the Red Keep to protect them from the coming invasion
Cersei overlooks the city and sees her plan coming to fruition
Cersei intends to keep a vast swath of innocent smallfolk in the path of the Red Keep
Cersei waits to see if Daenerys will burn the city and all the inhabitants with it
The Iron Fleet waits in silence in Blackwater Bay
Daenerys, riding atop Drogon, ambushes the Iron Fleet by diving directly out of the sun
The Ironborn fire their scorpions at Daenerys and Drogon
The Ironborn are unable to land a hit because Drogon is too fast
Drogon destroys the Iron Fleet with his flames, including the Silence
Euron Greyjoy escapes by jumping into the sea
armies wait at the gates for the bells to ring
Daenerys and Drogon burst through the gates in a fiery blast, scattering the Golden Company
Grey Worm and the united army charge at the gate and attack the remains of the Golden Company
Grey Worm impales a fleeing Captain Strickland in the back with his spear, killing him
The united army takes to the streets of King's Landing, taking out enemy soldiers
Daenerys and Drogon strafe the city walls, destroying the remaining scorpions
Daenerys and Drogon make their way to the gates of the innermost part of the city
Lannister soldiers wait at the gates
The two opposing armies come to an intense standstill
Daenerys perches Drogon atop one of the walls
Lannister soldiers throw down their weapons and surrender
Tyrion is visibly relieved
Jon is visibly relieved
Citizens of the city cry out for the bells to be rung
The bells are rung after a long amount of time passes, signaling the end of the sack of the city
The bells keep ringing
Daenerys gazes at the Red Keep with a look of pure hatred and becomes more unstable, succumbing to Targaryen Madness
Daenerys takes Drogon to the skies without uttering a word
Daenerys begins to burn the city, incinerating entire buildings and neighborhoods, killing both soldiers and innocent civilians
Tyrion is horrified by what he has witnessed outside the city
Grey Worm takes his spear and throws it at the Lannister Captain, killing him
Targaryen, Stark, and Arryn forces begin attacking and killing surrendered forces
Jon orders his own men back and tries to get them to stop fighting
The Northmen join their new allies in the slaughter
The Unsullied, Northmen, and Valemen charge into the innermost parts of the city, killing soldiers and civilians, and raping women
Jon is caught up in the disorder, deflecting attacks and shouting at his men to stop fighting
Davos tries to usher people to safety
Cersei looks on from the Red Keep in shock as the carnage continues and fire comes down from above
Jon finds one of his own men grabbing an innocent woman and intervenes before the soldier can rape her
The soldier, blinded by rage and vengeance, attacks Jon
Jon is forced to kill the soldier
Daenerys begins destroying parts of the Red Keep from the air, sending tons of debris falling to the ground below
Jaime sneaks around the cliffs on which the Red Keep is situated
Jaime encounters Euron, who survived his brush with death
Jaime urges Euron to help him save Cersei
Euron points out that the city and Cersei are lost
Euron and Jaime begin fighting
Euron says he will deliver Jaime's severed head to Cersei so she can kiss it one last time
Euron stabs Jaime multiple times
Jaime overpowers Euron and impales him through the stomach with his sword
Jaime walks away, severely injured
Euron, while smiling, utters: "I'm the man who killed Jaime Lannister."
Euron dies from his wounds
Arya and the Hound sneak into the Red Keep and reach Maegor's Holdfast as the walls crumble around them
Sandor urges Arya to go home, arguing that Cersei is as good as dead
Arya insists that she is going to kill Cersei
Sandor grabs Arya by the arm and urges her to choose life over vengeance, sharing his own experience with revenge
Sandor warns Arya that if she goes with him, she'll also die
Sandor goes to deal with his brother
Arya calls Sandor by his name for the first time and thanks him
Arya and Sandor go their separate ways
Qyburn convinces Cersei that all is lost
Qyburn evacuates Cersei and her Queensguard from the Red Keep
Cersei, Qyburn, and the Queensguard come across Sandor before reaching Maegor's Holdfast
Sandor (the Hound) effortlessly dispatches the Queensguard
Sandor stands face-to-face with his brother Gregor
Sandor taunts Gregor, breaking Cersei and Qyburn's control over him
Cersei orders Gregor to protect her
Gregor (the Mountain) defies Cersei, wanting to fight his brother
Qyburn repeats the order to Gregor
Gregor smashes Qyburn's head against a wall, killing him
Cersei flees as the Clegane brothers stare each other down
Arya continues to escape through King's Landing
Arya is nearly trampled by civilians trying to escape Daenerys and her forces
Sandor attacks his brother Gregor, which has little effect
Sandor knocks Gregor's helmet off, revealing Gregor's face
Sandor mocks Gregor
Gregor is provoked, unsheathes his sword, and begins to madly fight Sandor
Sandor proves to be a better fighter and drives his sword into Gregor's chest, though it does not kill him
Sandor pulls the sword out and discards most of his armor
Gregor beats down Sandor and lifts him up against the wall, putting him in a choke-hold
Sandor tries to free himself by stabbing Gregor several times with a knife, which proves ineffective
Gregor is about to gouge out Sandor's eyes
Sandor stabs Gregor clean in the left eye
Gregor begins pulling the knife from his eye, undeterred
Sandor realizes none of his attacks will kill Gregor and knows what he must do
Sandor musters up courage, tackles Gregor, and shoves them both through the badly damaged wall
Sandor and Gregor plummet hundreds of feet into the fiery remains of the city
Davos and Jon reel at the carnage and fire surrounding them as caches of wildfire explode
Daenerys and Drogon burn up the city
Davos and Jon share a look
Jon orders his forces to fall back
Jon and Davos rush people out of the city as fire rains down from above
Cersei is alone in the Red Keep and sees Jaime, bloodied and badly wounded
Jaime appears, bloodied and badly wounded following his fight with Euron
Cersei and Jaime go deep into the lower chambers of the Red Keep
Cersei and Jaime follow Tyrion's instructions to find a boat waiting for them on the beach
Cersei and Jaime find their path blocked by rubble
Cersei begins to cry in fear, saying she does not want to die
Jaime tells Cersei not to look at the crumbling keep and reassures her
The weakened structure above them begins to cave in
Cersei and Jaime embrace for the last time as the ceiling caves in, crushing them
Cersei and Jaime embrace for the last time as the ceiling caves in, crushing them
Arya regains consciousness after being incapacitated by a concussive blast
Arya gets into a building full of women and children
Arya convinces the women and children to keep moving
The women and children begin to escape
Most of the women and children, except a mother and her young daughter, are killed by the roving Dothraki
The mother collapses and tells Arya to take her daughter to safety
Arya grabs the child and begins to run
The girl refuses to leave her mother and goes to her side
Daenerys and Drogon swoop down and unleash another torrent of fire upon the innocent civilians
Arya awakes and sees the devastation around her
Arya is devastated to find that the mother and daughter were burned alive
Arya stumbles around the city
Arya sees the dead bodies of many innocent men, women, and children
innocent men, women, and children are killed by Arya's cousin's lover
Arya spots a white horse among the dead
Arya goes up to the horse and calms it
Arya mounts the horse and rides out of the city to an uncertain future
King's Landing burns as the Mad King's last words, "Burn them all," are fulfilled by his daughter

The_Iron_Throne.action: Ash falls over the wreckage of King's Landing
Jon, Davos, and Tyrion walk through the ash-covered devastation
Jon, Davos, and Tyrion walk through the ash-covered devastation
Tyrion leaves Jon and Davos
Tyrion walks alone towards the Red Keep
Tyrion heads towards the cellar inside the Red Keep
Tyrion takes the same escape route Jaime had taken him through previously
Tyrion walks through the rubble and remains of the Red Keep, searching for his siblings
Tyrion spots Jaime's golden hand under the rubble
Tyrion clears away the rubble stone by stone to find the corpse of Cersei Lannister
Tyrion is visibly shaken by finding Cersei's corpse
Tyrion continues to clear away broken rock, knowing Jaime is next to her
Tyrion begins to weep when he finds Jaime's corpse
Jaime's death-wish is fulfilled: he died in the arms of the woman he loved
Jon finds Grey Worm about to execute the remainder of the Lannister forces and tries to stop him, telling him it's over
Grey Worm tells Jon it won't be over until Daenerys's enemies are defeated
Davos tries to get Grey Worm to stop
Grey Worm reminds Davos that he only obeys the queen's commands and not his
Jon asks Grey Worm what those commands are
Grey Worm reveals he has orders to kill all who follow Cersei and that the captured soldiers chose to fight for her
Grey Worm takes out his knife to begin the execution
Jon grabs Grey Worm by the arm in an attempt to stop him
All of the Unsullied present aim their spears at Jon and the Northmen
The Northmen, except for Jon, draw their swords in self-defense
Grey Worm scowls at Jon for attempting to intervene
Davos diffuses the confrontation, telling Jon they should speak with Daenerys
Jon and Davos leave
Grey Worm starts to slit the prisoners' throats one by one
Dothraki celebrate their victory outside the gates of the city
Arya appears from the ruined city covered in ash, blood, and grime, looking stony-faced
Jon pushes his way through the crowd of Dothraki and goes toward the gate
A huge Targaryen banner hangs on the gate
Jon reaches the top of the gate
Grey Worm appears and exchanges a tense look with Jon
Drogon appears with Daenerys and lands behind Jon and Grey Worm
Daenerys walks out to address her army amidst much cheering
Daenerys tells the Dothraki they gave her the Seven Kingdoms
Daenerys promotes Grey Worm to Commander of all her forces and Master of War
Daenerys praises the Unsullied and calls her forces 'liberators'
Jon watches Daenerys in concern
Tyrion arrives at the scene, devastated by his brother's death
Daenerys declares her intent to 'liberate' all of Westeros and the world
Arya looks up balefully at Daenerys from behind the cheering armies, angry about Cersei's death
Daenerys accuses Tyrion of treason for freeing Jaime
Tyrion replies that he freed his brother and Daenerys slaughtered a city
Tyrion resigns as Hand of the Queen, removing and discarding his brooch
Daenerys orders Tyrion arrested
The Unsullied lead Tyrion away
Tyrion makes eye contact with Jon
Jon turns to face Daenerys
Jon and Daenerys share a tense silence
Daenerys leaves with some of the Unsullied
Arya silently appears beside Jon while he watches Daenerys leave
Jon is surprised that Arya is in King's Landing
Jon notices Arya's bloody face and asks what happened to her
Arya tells Jon she came to kill Cersei, but Daenerys got to her first
Jon tries to convince Arya that Daenerys is "everyone's queen" now
Arya coldly suggests Jon try telling that to Sansa, knowing Sansa will never bend the knee to Daenerys
Jon tells Arya to wait for him outside the city gates
Arya warns Jon that Daenerys knows who he really is and will view him as a threat
Arya tells Jon, "I know a killer when I see one," in reference to Daenerys, before leaving
Jon visits Tyrion in prison
Tyrion asks Jon if he brought wine
Tyrion tells Jon that their queen doesn't keep prisoners for long and that there's a crude kind of justice
Tyrion tells Jon he betrayed Varys and watched him burn to death
Tyrion notes that once he's executed, Varys's ashes will be able to tell his ashes "I told you so" about Daenerys
Tyrion asks Jon if there is life after death
Jon tells Tyrion, "Not that I've seen."
Tyrion notes that oblivion is the best he could hope for
Tyrion goes over his past sins, including murdering his lover and father and betraying their queen
Jon tries to tell Tyrion he didn't betray Daenerys
Tyrion insists he did and that he'd do it again, now that he's seen what Daenerys is capable of
Tyrion tells Jon, "I chose my fate, the people of King's Landing did not."
Jon, disturbed by what Daenerys did and unable to justify it, insists the war is over now
Tyrion questions Jon about Daenerys, asking if she sounded like someone who's done fighting
Tyrion says Daenerys liberated the people of Slaver's Bay and King's Landing, and will continue until she rules everyone
Jon angrily retorts that Tyrion has been at Daenerys's side, counselling her every step
Tyrion agrees, admitting Varys was right and he was wrong, and that it was vanity to think he could guide Daenerys
Tyrion states that Daenerys's nature is fire and blood
Jon is upset by the idea that blood defines who they are, and tells Tyrion that Daenerys is not her father, just as Tyrion is not his father
Tyrion admits Tywin and Cersei were evil, but reminds Jon that Daenerys has killed more people than Tywin, Cersei, and any other evil people they know
Jon tries to defend Daenerys by mentioning her traumas and Cersei's actions
Tyrion reminds Jon that Daenerys burned King's Landing
Jon argues that it is easy to judge Daenerys's actions from afar, but hesitates when Tyrion asks if he would have done the same
Tyrion presses Jon, suggesting that Jon wouldn't have done the same thing if he had Daenerys's power
Tyrion realizes Jon doesn't want to betray Daenerys and reviews her history of killing evil men and gaining support
Tyrion reveals Daenerys is convinced it is her destiny to make the world better, no matter how many must die
Jon sinks down, unable to argue against Tyrion
Tyrion sympathetically tells Jon he knows Jon loves Daenerys and that he loved her too
Jon reflects on Maester Aemon's words: "Love is the death of duty."
Tyrion agrees with Jon's reflection
Tyrion says that sometimes duty must be the death of love
Tyrion tells Jon he is the shield that guards the realms of men and has tried to do the right thing
Tyrion asks Jon who is the greatest threat to the people now
Tyrion acknowledges that assassinating Daenerys is terrible but says it is the right thing
Jon struggles with the idea of killing Daenerys
Tyrion says Jon won't be the last man Daenerys executes and asks who is more dangerous than the rightful heir to the Iron Throne
Jon accepts being executed by Daenerys if she chooses and apologizes to Tyrion
Tyrion tries again to persuade Jon and reveals Sansa and Arya won't bend the knee, putting House Stark in danger
Jon insists his sisters will be loyal to the throne
Tyrion responds that Sansa told him about Jon's parentage because she doesn't want Daenerys to be queen
Jon says that's not up to Sansa
Tyrion agrees but tells Jon he needs to make a choice and must choose now
Jon leaves with a rattled look
Jon walks through the ruins of the Red Keep, troubled
Drogon appears from under the pile of ash
Drogon comes face to face with Jon
Drogon goes back to sleep
Daenerys comes to the throne room and sees the Iron Throne for the first time
Daenerys is visibly overwhelmed by her emotions
Daenerys goes to touch the throne
Jon appears in the throne room
Daenerys tells Jon about her childhood memories of the Iron Throne and her brother Viserys's stories
Daenerys asks Jon what a thousand swords look like to a little girl
Daenerys tells Jon she imagined a mountain of swords too high to climb
Jon confronts Daenerys over Grey Worm executing Lannister prisoners
Daenerys defends the executions as necessary
Jon protests, mentioning children being burned
Daenerys responds that she tried to make peace with Cersei, but Cersei used innocence against her
Jon urges Daenerys to forgive Tyrion and the people of King's Landing
Jon pleads with Daenerys to show mercy and prove she is not the person they fear
Daenerys refuses, stating she can't give in to small mercies
Jon urges Daenerys that the world needs mercy
Daenerys tries to convince Jon that her way is the way to a better world
Jon asks Daenerys how she knows what is good
Daenerys assures Jon that she knows what is good and so does he
Jon says he doesn't know and asks about others who think they know what's good
Daenerys replies that others don't get to choose
Daenerys embraces Jon and asks him to join her in breaking the wheel
Jon declares Daenerys is his queen, now and always
Jon and Daenerys kiss
Jon drives his dagger through Daenerys's heart
Daenerys collapses, stunned, as Jon catches her
Daenerys dies in Jon's arms as he weeps over her body
Drogon arrives from behind as Jon holds Daenerys's body
Jon holds Daenerys's body while snow falls
Snow falls
Jon carefully lays Daenerys down when Drogon approaches
Drogon tries to wake Daenerys up to no avail
Drogon realizes Daenerys is dead and becomes enraged
Drogon faces Jon, who prepares for the dragon's flames and awaits his impending death
Drogon roars and breathes fire in several directions in grief
Drogon does not kill Jon
Drogon channels his rage towards the Iron Throne and melts it down
Drogon gently picks up Daenerys's body
Drogon flies away in the gloomy sky, still bellowing in grief
Grey Worm escorts a disheveled and grimy Tyrion to the Dragonpit
Sansa, Bran, Arya, Yara Greyjoy, Brienne, Davos, Gendry Baratheon, Sam Tarly, Yohn Royce, Robin Arryn, Edmure Tully, and the new Prince of Dorne are present at the Dragonpit
Jon is imprisoned for regicide but is not brought forth with Tyrion
Sansa demands Grey Worm to say where Jon is
Grey Worm replies that Jon is their prisoner
Sansa protests that Tyrion is also Grey Worm's prisoner and both Jon and Tyrion should be brought together
Grey Worm firmly tells Sansa that he decides what to do with their prisoners and that King's Landing is their city now
Sansa warns Grey Worm that thousands of Northmen are outside the city and harming Jon would not be in his interest
Grey Worm responds that there are also thousands of Unsullied who think otherwise
Yara sides with Grey Worm because she swore to follow Daenerys
Sansa accuses Yara of agreeing to follow a tyrant
Yara argues that Daenerys freed Westeros from a tyrant, that Cersei is gone because of her, and Jon killed Daenerys
Yara demands that the Unsullied be allowed to execute Jon
Arya threatens to cut Yara's throat if she speaks another word of harm towards Jon
Davos immediately defuses the situation
Davos gives Grey Worm's men credit for their aid in the war against the undead
Davos suggests Grey Worm and the Unsullied take land in the Reach and start their own house
Davos states, "We've had enough war. Thousands of you, thousands of them. You know how it ends. We need to find a better way."
Grey Worm rejects Davos's suggestion and states they do not need payment but justice for Daenerys's assassination
Grey Worm insists Jon cannot go free
Grey Worm tries to silence Tyrion
Tyrion reminds Grey Worm that he does not get to decide Jon's fate, as it rests with their new queen/king
Yohn Royce points out that there isn't a new queen/king
Tyrion says the most powerful lords and ladies gathered should pick a new ruler from their number
Grey Worm relents and tells the assembly to decide the new ruler
The assembly start looking at each other
Edmure Tully rises from his seat and starts to give a speech putting himself forward for the position
Sansa sternly asks Edmure to sit down
Edmure, visibly embarrassed, sits back down
Sam suggests the new ruler should be chosen by everyone through a vote, not just the lords
The assembly responds with scornful laughter
Edmure and Yohn mockingly compare giving votes to the smallfolk with giving votes to horses and dogs
Tyrion is told that perhaps he wants the crown himself
Tyrion disagrees, saying he is hated by half the people for serving Daenerys and the other half for betraying her
Davos asks Tyrion who he thinks should be king
Tyrion responds that stories unite people and are powerful things
Tyrion proposes Bran as king, citing his story and experiences
Sansa objects, mentioning that Bran is not interested in kingship and cannot father children
Tyrion replies that it is good, since sons of kings are often cruel and stupid, referencing Joffrey
Tyrion turns to Grey Worm and tells him that it is the wheel Daenerys wants to break
Tyrion proposes an elective monarchy, declaring that future monarchs will be chosen by the high lords and ladies in the Dragonpit
Tyrion asks Bran if he will accept the crown, even though he does not want it
Bran replies, "Why do you think I came all this way?"
Tyrion waits for the response of the other leaders
The other leaders, starting with Sam, slowly say "aye" one by one, accepting Bran as their new ruler
Sansa hesitates and tells Bran that the North has suffered too much to ever kneel before another ruler
Sansa declares that the North will once again be an independent kingdom
Bran gives his assent to Sansa's request
Tyrion and the assembly hail Bran as Bran the Broken, First of His Name, King of the Andals and the First Men, Lord of the Six Kingdoms, and Protector of the Realm
As Tyrion turns to leave, Bran stops him and says he wants him to be his Hand
Tyrion is flattered but refuses to take up the position, saying he doesn't deserve it
Tyrion advises Bran to choose Davos or someone else as Hand
Bran refuses to change his mind
Grey Worm objects, saying Tyrion deserves justice
Bran reveals that Tyrion being his Hand is the justice he gets, but acknowledges Tyrion has made many mistakes and will spend his life rectifying them
Grey Worm shakes his head, insisting it is not enough
Tyrion visits a dejected Jon in prison
Tyrion informs Jon that a compromise has been reached to avoid another war
Tyrion tells Jon he is going to be sent to the Night's Watch
Jon presumes the Night's Watch was disbanded after the extinction of the White Walkers
Tyrion explains the Night's Watch continues as a quarantine for the banished, poor, and unfortunate
Sansa and Arya want Jon to be pardoned
The Unsullied want Jon executed
King Bran makes a compromise that satisfies neither party
Sansa and Arya understand Bran needs to make peace
Jon shakily asks if what he did was right
Tyrion corrects Jon, saying "What they did."
Jon says killing Daenerys doesn't feel right
Tyrion tells Jon to ask him again in ten years
Jon believes they won't see each other again
Tyrion says a few years as Hand of the King would make anyone want to leave
Jon is shown leaving King's Landing, defeated
Jon is brought to the port to set sail for Castle Black
The city is bustling with people again, with much of it rebuilt
Jon passes Grey Worm's ship
Jon and Grey Worm's eyes meet as Grey Worm gives him a fierce look
Grey Worm and the Unsullied leave the harbor to sail to Naath
Grey Worm keeps his promise to Missandei of protecting her people
Sansa, Arya, and King Bran meet Jon at the harbor before he leaves
Sansa asks Jon for his forgiveness
Jon hesitates to forgive Sansa
Jon tells Sansa she has gained independence for the North
Sansa replies that the North has lost its king
Jon tells Sansa that Ned Stark's daughter will speak for the North and she's the best they could ask for
Sansa and Jon share an emotional hug
Jon tells Arya she can come see him at Castle Black
Arya gives Jon a sad smile and tells him she's not coming back North
Jon asks Arya where she's going
Arya asks Jon "What's west of Westeros?" and reveals she is going beyond the Sunset Sea
Jon returns Arya's sad smile and says he doesn't know
Arya replies that no one knows what's beyond West, since it's where all the maps stop, and that's where she is going
Jon asks Arya if she has her sword Needle
Arya replies that she has Needle with her and starts to cry
Jon wipes away Arya's tear
Jon and Arya share a long hug
Jon turns to King Bran and kneels before him, apologizing for not being there when he needs him
Bran responds, "You were exactly where you needed to be," in his usual quizzical fashion
Jon gets up
Jon looks at his siblings one last time
Jon leaves for the boat that is waiting to take him to Castle Black
The Stark siblings separate
The Stark siblings never meet again
Brienne becomes the first Lady Commander of Bran's Kingsguard
Brienne goes through the Book of Brothers
Brienne finishes Jaime Lannister's record
Brienne reluctantly adds "Died protecting his Queen" to Jaime's record, with tears in her eyes
Tyrion is met by Davos and Bronn in the Small Council chambers
Bronn, now Lord of Highgarden, arrives
Sam arrives as Grand Maester and presents a book called A Song of Ice and Fire, written by himself and Archmaester Ebrose
Sam explains the book details the wars after Robert's Rebellion
Tyrion asks if he is mentioned in the book in a positive or negative light
Sam informs Tyrion he is not mentioned at all, amusing everyone
Brienne and King Bran arrive
Bran tells his advisors they lack Masters of Whisperers, War, and Laws
Tyrion replies that suitable candidates will be brought in the coming weeks
Bran asks about the whereabouts of Drogon
Sam replies Drogon was last seen flying east to Volantis
Bronn cuts Sam off, saying "The farther away, the better."
Bran decides to search for Drogon himself and leaves the room with Podrick
Tyrion asks Bronn, now Master of Coin, to provide food supplies from Highgarden
Bronn assents
Davos asks for funds to rebuild the navy
Tyrion talks about building a good sewer system to improve city hygiene
Bronn talks about rebuilding brothels
Tyrion and Bronn start squabbling, signaling a return to normalcy
Jon reaches Castle Black while wearing the black garb of the Night's Watch
Jon sees Tormund on the castle walls
Tormund gives Jon a sympathetic look
Jon reunites with Ghost, who is now missing an ear and visibly scarred
Sansa returns to Winterfell
Sansa is crowned Queen in the North
Arya stands on the bow of her own ship, looking content
Arya sails forth towards unknown lands and an uncertain fate
The ship flies the Stark banner
Jon and Tormund lead the wildlings outside the gates of Castle Black
Jon and Tormund lead the wildlings outside the gates of Castle Black
Jon silently watches the gate close behind them
Jon looks at the wildling men, women, and children as they make their way
Jon has a brief look of peace
Jon rides among the group as they progress to the true North
The wildlings are finally free from what has haunted them for thousands of years
A piece of grass emerges from the thawing snow
The first signs of spring begin to slowly appear in Westeros
